{"content":"1: name: inductor-nightly\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   pull_request:\n5:     paths:\n6:       - .github/workflows/inductor-nightly.yml\n7:       - benchmarks/dynamo/ci_expected_accuracy/dynamic_cpu_max_autotune_inductor_amp_freezing_huggingface_inference.csv\n8:       - benchmarks/dynamo/ci_expected_accuracy/dynamic_cpu_max_autotune_inductor_amp_freezing_timm_inference.csv\n9:       - benchmarks/dynamo/ci_expected_accuracy/dynamic_cpu_max_autotune_inductor_amp_freezing_torchbench_inference.csv\n10:   workflow_dispatch:\n11:   schedule:\n12:     # Run every day at 7:00 AM UTC\n13:     - cron: 0 7 * * *\n14: \n15: concurrency:\n16:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n17:   cancel-in-progress: true\n18: \n19: permissions:\n20:   id-token: write\n21:   contents: read\n22: \n23: jobs:\n24:   get-default-label-prefix:\n25:     name: get-default-label-prefix\n26:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n27:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n28:     with:\n29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n31: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-nightly.yml%3FL1-30"}
338-usertool_resultmcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file
{"content":"1: name: inductor-A100-perf-compare\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   push:\n5:     tags:\n6:       - ciflow/inductor-perf-compare/*\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\n8: \n9: concurrency:\n10:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\n11:   cancel-in-progress: true\n12: \n13: permissions:\n14:   id-token: write\n15:   contents: read\n16: \n17: jobs:\n18:   get-default-label-prefix:\n19:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n20:     name: get-default-label-prefix\n21:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n22:     with:\n23:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n24:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n25:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n26:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n27:       opt_out_experiments: lf\n28: \n29:   build:\n30:     name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\n31: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-perf-compare.yml%3FL1-30"}
339-usertool_resultmcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file
{"content":"1: name: inductor-perf-b200\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   schedule:\n5:     - cron: 0 7 * * 1-6\n6:     - cron: 0 7 * * 0\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\n8:     inputs:\n9:       training:\n10:         description: Run training (on by default)?\n11:         required: false\n12:         type: boolean\n13:         default: true\n14:       inference:\n15:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\n16:         required: false\n17:         type: boolean\n18:         default: true\n19:       default:\n20:         description: Run inductor_default?\n21:         required: false\n22:         type: boolean\n23:         default: false\n24:       dynamic:\n25:         description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\n26:         required: false\n27:         type: boolean\n28:         default: false\n29:       cppwrapper:\n30:         description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\n31: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-b200.yml%3FL1-30"}
340-usertool_resultmcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file
{"content":"1: name: inductor-perf-nightly-aarch64\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   schedule:\n5:     # Does not perform max_autotune on CPU, so skip the weekly run setup\n6:     - cron: 0 7 * * *\n7:   # NB: GitHub has an upper limit of 10 inputs here\n8:   workflow_dispatch:\n9:     inputs:\n10:       training:\n11:         # CPU for training is not typical, but leave the option open here\n12:         description: Run training (off by default)?\n13:         required: false\n14:         type: boolean\n15:         default: false\n16:       inference:\n17:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\n18:         required: false\n19:         type: boolean\n20:         default: true\n21:       default:\n22:         description: Run inductor_default?\n23:         required: false\n24:         type: boolean\n25:         default: true\n26:       dynamic:\n27:         description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\n28:         required: false\n29:         type: boolean\n30:         default: false\n31: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-aarch64.yml%3FL1-30"}
341-usertool_resultmcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file
{"content":"1: name: inductor-perf-nightly-h100\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   schedule:\n5:     - cron: 15 0 * * 1-6\n6:     - cron: 0 7 * * 0\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\n8:     inputs:\n9:       training:\n10:         description: Run training (on by default)?\n11:         required: false\n12:         type: boolean\n13:         default: true\n14:       inference:\n15:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\n16:         required: false\n17:         type: boolean\n18:         default: true\n19:       default:\n20:         description: Run inductor_default?\n21:         required: false\n22:         type: boolean\n23:         default: false\n24:       dynamic:\n25:         description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\n26:         required: false\n27:         type: boolean\n28:         default: false\n29:       cppwrapper:\n30:         description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\n31: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-h100.yml%3FL1-30"}
342-usertool_resultmcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file
{"content":"1: name: Lint\n2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.\n3: \n4: on:\n5:   pull_request:\n6:     branches-ignore:\n7:       - nightly\n8:   push:\n9:     branches:\n10:       - main\n11:       - release/*\n12:       - landchecks/*\n13:     tags:\n14:       - ciflow/pull/*\n15:       - ciflow/trunk/*\n16:   workflow_dispatch:\n17: \n18: permissions: read-all\n19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.\n20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.\n21: jobs:\n22:   get-label-type:\n23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n24:     name: get-label-type\n25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n26:     with:\n27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n30: \n31:   get-changed-files:\n32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n33:     name: Get changed files\n34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml\n35:     with:\n36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}\n37: \n38:   lintrunner-clang:\n39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\n40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD\n41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}\n42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]\n43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy 
... (truncated)

{"content":"1: name: trunk\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   push:\n5:     branches:\n6:       - main\n7:       - release/*\n8:       - landchecks/*\n9:     tags:\n10:       - ciflow/trunk/*\n11:   workflow_dispatch:\n12:   schedule:\n13:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\n14: \n15: concurrency:\n16:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n17:   cancel-in-progress: true\n18: \n19: permissions:\n20:   id-token: write\n21:   contents: read\n22: \n23: jobs:\n24:   llm-td:\n25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n26:     name: before-test\n27:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n28:     permissions:\n29:       id-token: write\n30:       contents: read\n31: \n32:   target-determination:\n33:     name: before-test\n34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n35:     needs: llm-td\n36:     permissions:\n37:       id-token: write\n38:       contents: read\n39: \n40:   get-label-type:\n41:     name: get-label-type\n42:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n43:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n44:     with:\n45:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n46:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n47:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n48:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n49: \n50:   libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-debug-build:\n51:     name: libtorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-debug\n52:     u

URGENT: Fix 60 incomplete workflow files listed in /tmp/small_files.txt

These files currently have fewer than 20 lines (they are stubs). You must fetch the complete content from Sourcegraph and write to /workspace.

For each file in /tmp/small_files.txt:
1. Call sg_read_file(repo=github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78, path=<file_path>)
2. Call Write(file_path=/workspace/<file_path>, content=<full content>)

Process ALL 60 files in this single batch. Do not stop until all are complete.
Use rapid successive tool calls. Process files line-by-line from the /tmp/small_files.txt file.

3. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_commit_search @ -
Input
{
  "messageTerms": [
    "162493",
    "2.9",
    "release"
  ],
  "repos": [
    "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
  ]
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"06b0307\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"Rename pytorch-triton package to triton (#169888)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/163963\\nMakes naming more consistent with official triton release\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169888\\nApproved by: https://github.com/malfet, https://github.com/njriasan\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Rename pytorch-triton package to triton (#169888)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/06b03073b5fe2fb26260323a69a9d5ab23bbc9cd\"},{\"author\":\"Wang, Chuanqi <chuanqi.wang@intel.com>\",\"commit\":\"365a6c8\",\"date\":\"2025-12-05\",\"message\":\"[BE] Upgrade XPU support package to 2025.3 (#166829)\\n\\nFollows #166723. Including below changes,\\n\\n- Add XPU support package 2025.3 build and test in CI for both Linux and Windows\\n- Keep XPU support package 2025.2 build in CI to ensure no break issue until PyTorch 2.10 release\\n- Upgrade XPU support package from 2025.2 to 2025.3 in CD for both Linux and Windows\\n- Update XPU runtime pypi packages dependencies of CD wheels\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166829\\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[BE] Upgrade XPU support package to 2025.3 (#166829)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/365a6c84db516f244b7234b7aa3c8843af52936b\"},{\"author\":\"Shangdi Yu <shangdiy@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"d2eff5d\",\"date\":\"2025-10-29\",\"message\":\"Add python stack trace to AOTI generated code (#160539)\\n\\nSummary:\\nWe add a thread_local KernelContext object so Strobelight (and other potential profilers) can read the stack trace information of the running kernel.\\n\\nThis will bring extra overhead, so we guard this behind the `cpp.enable_kernel_profile` flag.\\n\\nExample output code:\\n\\n```cpp\\n#include <torch/csrc/inductor/aoti_runtime/kernel_context_tls.h>\\nnamespace torch::aot_inductor {\\nthread_local KernelContext* tls_kernel_context = nullptr;\\n}\\n// Other code .....\\nvoid AOTInductorModel::run_impl(\\n    AtenTensorHandle*\\n        input_handles, // a... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Add python stack trace to AOTI generated code (#160539)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/d2eff5d454ab2cb0a5ccdfb5eb6e7d6dcc75e097\"},{\"author\":\"Will Feng <yfeng.us@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"2ec8eda\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"[CI] Fix NVSHMEM errors in H100 symm mem job (#169879)\\n\\nExample error:\\n```\\n2025-12-08T12:46:36.6787890Z distributed/test_nvshmem.py::NVSHMEMSymmetricMemoryTest::test_alloc I1208 09:18:18.140000 4559 site-packages/torch/testing/_internal/common_distributed.py:1857] Testing class NVSHMEMSymmetricMemoryTest on 4 cuda\\n2025-12-08T12:46:36.6790166Z /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/gpgpu/toolkit/r12.9/main_nvshmem/src/host/team/team_internal_nvls.cpp:258: non-zero status: 401 /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/gpgpu/toolkit/r12.9/main_nvshmem/src/host/team/team_internal_nvls.cpp:258: non-zero status: 401 /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[CI] Fix NVSHMEM errors in H100 symm mem job (#169879)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/2ec8eda3f6be4251c174bfc1f2d6028bf5d3ddf1\"},{\"author\":\"zpcore <zpcore@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"a5f36a8\",\"date\":\"2025-11-19\",\"message\":\"[DTensor] Fix deadlock after fast cache clear (#168069)\\n\\nThis is the necessary fix for https://github.com/meta-pytorch/autoparallel/issues/256.\\n\\n### Issue:\\nwhen we call `_clear_fast_path_sharding_prop_cache()`, and then `get_thread_local_native_sharding_propagator_cache()`, the code will stuck due to deadlock.\\n\\n### Cause:\\nWhen you assign to a Python dict key that already exists:\\n```C++\\nthread_dict[\\\"__DTensor_fastpath_thread_cache_cleanup\\\"] = old_capsule  // capsule #1 stored\\n...\\nclear_DTensor_sharding_propagator_cache() // call to clean up the cache\\n...\\nget_thread_local_native_sharding_propaga... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[DTensor] Fix deadlock after fast cache clear (#168069)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/a5f36a8fda588eca2b15edefbf416cd92f081f30\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"f633119\",\"date\":\"2025-11-11\",\"message\":\"Enable Doc builds for: Minor Releases RCs. Minor and Patch Releases final RC (#167478)\\n\\nEnable Doc builds for\\n1. Minor Releases RCs\\n2. Minor and Patch Releases final RC\\n\\nThis is done to prevent publishing doc for patch releases when building rcs.\\nSee:\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/docs/pull/57\\n\\nFollowup after: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153973\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/167478\\nApproved by: https://github.com/svekars, https://github.com/seemethere\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Enable Doc builds for: Minor Releases RCs. Minor and Patch Releases final RC (#167478)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/f6331192b4b105bb8a20823dc02e33b55e5c91e2\"},{\"author\":\"Xinya Zhang <Xinya.Zhang@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"77acc66\",\"date\":\"2025-11-17\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][CI] Upgrade ROCm CI to 7.1 (#166743)\\n\\nUpgrade all the ROCm docker images to ROCm 7.1 release version.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166743\\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman, https://github.com/jeffdaily\\n\\nCo-authored-by: Jeff Daily <jeff.daily@amd.com>\\nCo-authored-by: Prachi Gupta <prachi.gupta@amd.com>\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][CI] Upgrade ROCm CI to 7.1 (#166743)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/77acc66df917a2b9f6305d089ac88b8975786552\"},{\"author\":\"Andrey Talman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"3a2d75a\",\"date\":\"2025-11-06\",\"message\":\"Change template 'Release highlight for proposed Feature'->'New Feature for Release' (#167145)\\n\\nMakes it simpler and more clear\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/167145\\nApproved by: https://github.com/huydhn\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Change template 'Release highlight for proposed Feature'->'New Feature for Release' (#167145)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/3a2d75a0869b3ef2344ab0501c19787924442c3e\"},{\"author\":\"karthickai <karthickps@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"929776a\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"[Inductor] Fix combo kernels variable collision with ND tiled reductions (#168946)\\n\\nFixes: #168945\\n\\nFix combo kernels crash when fusing op with multi-dim reductions (ND tiling). It caused variable name collisions in generated triton code when multiple reduction dimensions existed.\\n\\n```python\\ndef triton_per_fused_1(in_out_ptr0, in_ptr0, out_ptr0, XBLOCK : tl.constexpr):\\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\\n    if pid % 1 == 0:\\n        pid_offset = pid // 1\\n        xnumel = 4\\n        r0_numel = 2\\n        RBLOCK_0: tl.constexpr = 2\\n        R0_BLOCK_0: tl.constexpr = 2\\n        r1_numel = 8\\n        RBLOCK_0:... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[Inductor] Fix combo kernels variable collision with ND tiled reductions (#168946)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/929776a1b44da3ee58b7666fce5df8d7ef1671ec\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"a36e1d3\",\"date\":\"2025-12-04\",\"message\":\"Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\\n\\nRequired for release 2.10\\n\\nRocm wheel build fix provided by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169369\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168096\\nApproved by: https://github.com/njriasan, https://github.com/malfet, https://github.com/huydhn\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/a36e1d39ebbf60976fec5a0d8a96763e6adfbea3\"},{\"author\":\"Jithun Nair <37884920+jithunnair-amd@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"1efc14a\",\"date\":\"2025-11-19\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][CI] Update concurrency setting for docker-cache-rocm.yml (#168104)\\n\\nWe only want to cache the latest CI docker image for `main` and `release` branches in cases where multiple `docker-builds` workflow runs get triggered in quick succession. This is because the latest run will anyway overwrite the cached images, since we do not maintain a cached image per-SHA, instead it's only one-per-branch (to minimize cache size and docker load times at runner bringup).\\n\\nAlso removing `workflow_dispatch` as a trigger since it won't work (needs artifacts from `docker-builds` run)\\n\\nPull Request resolved... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][CI] Update concurrency setting for docker-cache-rocm.yml (#168104)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/1efc14a50d56701e3ad0639849bc26da59e7a3c3\"},{\"author\":\"Paul Mullowney <Paul.Mullowney@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"f6bf70b\",\"date\":\"2025-12-10\",\"message\":\"[ROCm] roll kernel as grid stride loop (#169474)\\n\\nReimplement the roll kernel as a grid stride loop. On AMD devices, we see launch failures in the original version when gridDim.x*blockDim.x exceeds 4294967295. This implementation should work and be performant on both AMD and Nvidia devices. The issue can be seen on AMD devices with the following small repro:\\n\\nimport torch\\nN = 21913096\\ninput_tensor_torch = torch.randn(1, 2, N, 98, device='cuda')\\noutput = input_tensor_torch.roll(-1, dims=1)\\ninput_tensor_torch_cpu = input_tensor_torch.cpu()\\noutput_cpu = input_tensor_torch_cpu.roll(-1, dims=1)\\nass... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[ROCm] roll kernel as grid stride loop (#169474)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/f6bf70bd12b1a860b01d34b8fd8425829bfdcbed\"},{\"author\":\"Anatoliy Litvinenko <alitvine@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"add6ad1\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"[ROCm] Bump to aotriton 0.11.1b (#169696)\\n\\nRestore Navi31 support with Alternative Wheel.\\nFix incompatibility with earlier version of GNU linker\\n-This was not detected because the released runtime tarballs are always built with gcc-13 from SCL\\nAdd gfx1103 support\\n\\n[AOTriton 0.11.1b Release Page](https://github.com/ROCm/aotriton/releases/tag/0.11.1b) for more\\ndetails.\\nThis is copy of PR https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168275\\n\\nAs reference, pr for checking test state: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169676\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169696\\nApp... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[ROCm] Bump to aotriton 0.11.1b (#169696)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/add6ad154249523fccba6e81be280b26d1c46d40\"},{\"author\":\"Kathryn-cat <jinqic@alumni.cmu.edu>\",\"commit\":\"8da5d29\",\"date\":\"2025-12-04\",\"message\":\"[DLPack] C Functions for DLPack Speed Exchange and Stream Handling (#165483)\\n\\n## Addressed Issue\\n\\nIssue #162845\\n\\n## Summary of Changes\\n\\nThis PR introduces a unified `DLPackExchangeAPI` struct as described in proposal [175](https://github.com/dmlc/dlpack/issues/175). This new convention replaces the previous mechanism of separate function pointers, and aligns with the latest DLPack standard as shown in PR [174](https://github.com/dmlc/dlpack/pull/174).\\n\\nSpecifically, the new `DLPackExchangeAPI` struct is exposed as `torch.Tensor.__c_dlpack_exchange_api__`, which stores and exposes the following... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[DLPack] C Functions for DLPack Speed Exchange and Stream Handling (#165483)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/8da5d29de7feb165047246464d09c4c2b2318987\"},{\"author\":\"Andrey Talman The change reflects a lower instruction count for the `basic_modules_ListOfLinears_eager` benchmark.\\n\\nthis reflects improvement after: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169553  that is outside the tolerance and fails the CI\\n\\nhttps://hud.pytorch.org/hud/pytorch/pytorch/e81262f1b9caff41164a1ce30f33665c12091634/1?per_page=50&name_filter=pr_time_benchmarks&mergeEp... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Update expected results for basic_modules_ListOfLinears_eager (#169895)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/27bf9eb7786dcce72b5174db82408d816ba2d1d4\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"cc8bfd1\",\"date\":\"2025-11-04\",\"message\":\"Docker release build: Use 13.0.0 nvidia docker (#166904)\\n\\nForward fix for failing Docker release builds\\nRelated to: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166897\\n\\nNightly Docker build failure https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/18900508440/job/53946606434\\nDue to missing base image:\\n```\\nERROR: failed to build: failed to solve: docker.io/nvidia/cuda:13.0.2-devel-ubuntu22.04: not found\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166904\\nApproved by: https://github.com/tinglvv, https://github.com/malfet\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Docker release build: Use 13.0.0 nvidia docker (#166904)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/cc8bfd1206f4bff26bd86ce584f6c16b6401ef50\"},{\"author\":\"mori360 <yifanmao@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"add5fc9\",\"date\":\"2025-12-08\",\"message\":\"[DTensor] update redistribute_cost, add disable_graph_based_transform (#169304)\\n\\n### Background\\nDTensor has 2 different utilities\\n* redistribute_cost() API is used to compute a cost value for each possible redistribution when generating a sharding strategy.  DTensor dispatch then minimizes over these cost values to select the strategy before performing a redistribution\\n* redistribute_tensor APIs are given a src/target placement, but need to find the fastest set of operations to perform this redistribution.  This logic uses different cost modeling than `redistribute_cost`\\n\\nThe redistribute_tens... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[DTensor] update redistribute_cost, add disable_graph_based_transform (#169304)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/add5fc9c2d7496be643dbf0e059f7432c9d82e18\"},{\"author\":\"Yu, Guangye <guangye.yu@intel.com>\",\"commit\":\"c8d790b\",\"date\":\"2025-11-18\",\"message\":\"[xpu][fix] Fix empty cache on mempool (#168074)\\n\\n# Motivation\\nThis is definitely a bug: we were attempting to release cached memory back to the system without proper **synchronization**. Callers must ensure that all accesses to memory blocks allocated by SYCL APIs have completed before invoking `sycl::free`.\\n\\nFor a simple example, in the following code:\\n```python\\npool = torch.xpu.MemPool()\\nwith torch.xpu.use_mem_pool(pool):\\n    input = torch.randn(100, device='xpu')\\nsum = input.sum()\\ndel pool\\nprint(sum)\\n```\\n`sum` may exhibit undefined behavior because `input.sum()` might not have finished exec... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[xpu][fix] Fix empty cache on mempool (#168074)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/c8d790b56d7bbb4ee266976944ab920f2d513b40\"},{\"author\":\"Huy Do <huydhn@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"e1f5147\",\"date\":\"2025-12-08\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"Create a mock benchmark results for torchao cudagraphs_low_precision (#123419)\\\" (#169753)\\n\\nThis reverts commit cd3c1132a969412bcaa1ed6288e83fee25666dc3.\\n\\nThis was originally planned as a way to apply quantization in DynamoBench, but it didn't lead anywhere.  And torchao is running its own benchmark on the repo now.  We can clean this up.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/169663\\n\\n### Testing\\n\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/19985987464\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169753\\nApproved by: https://github.com/masnesral, https://... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"Create a mock benchmark results for torchao cudagraphs_low_precision (#123419)\\\" (#169753)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/e1f51479c89dc67dd0aecc1adac8cd9400beedab\"},{\"author\":\"atalman <atalman@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"93d0d68\",\"date\":\"2025-12-02\",\"message\":\"Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\\n\\nRequired for release 2.10\\n\\nRocm wheel build fix provided by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169369\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168096\\nApproved by: https://github.com/njriasan, https://github.com/malfet\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/93d0d6838c56af59b0dba794e6aa08f0c1c7799c\"},{\"author\":\"Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"d19f36b\",\"date\":\"2025-11-06\",\"message\":\"[BE][Ez]: Update fmtlib submodule to 12.1.0 (#166983)\\n\\nFixed some compiler idiosyncrasies, improves CPP support, bugfixes, and performance optimizations. This is a header only minor library change so should be low risk and improve the performance of our formatting/loggers. Also allows fmtlib to be used in more constexpr contexts.\\n\\nFull changelog here: https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt/releases/tag/12.1.0\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166983\\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[BE][Ez]: Update fmtlib submodule to 12.1.0 (#166983)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/d19f36bea1c47d47ace6bae43d3b34dd858de145\"},{\"author\":\"johannes <johannes.zeiser5@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"f3fa560\",\"date\":\"2025-11-03\",\"message\":\"Integrate NVIDIA cuSolver backend into ATen/Linalg (initial implementation for eig/eigval) (#166715)\\n\\n### Summary\\n\\nAdds support for NVIDIA\u2019s cuSolver backend to torch.linalg.eig and torch.linalg.eigvals within the ATen/Linalg framework.\\n\\n### Motivation\\n\\nExtending PyTorch\u2019s Linalg backends with NVIDIA\u2019s cuSolver enables faster execution of torch.linalg.eig and torch.linalg.eigvals, complementing existing MAGMA and CPU implementations.\\n\\nThe speedup observed on consumer hardware (RTX4070/Ryzen 5700x) is in the order of **2x**, with preliminary testing on HPC hardware (H100, EPYC 9454) sugge... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Integrate NVIDIA cuSolver backend into ATen/Linalg (initial implementation for eig/eigval) (#166715)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/f3fa560dec727380b3e9c074efe05f0ce715a5ca\"},{\"author\":\"Yun Wu <sunnyunwu@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"a45a17f\",\"date\":\"2025-11-06\",\"message\":\"Fix boxcox to return same result for same input in one batch (#166986)\\n\\nSummary:\\nThe SIMD path is using SLEEF version of pow which is slightly different from std::pow. The fix is to use the same vectorized code (with partial load and store) for the trailing data as well to ensure consistency between results.\\n\\nDeploy:\\nNeed to make a hotfix in waas to monitor release signals, since this diff can cause testing failures in veloski and waas release correctness tests.\\n\\nTest Plan: Sandcastle.\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D86218207\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166986\\nAppr... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Fix boxcox to return same result for same input in one batch (#166986)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/a45a17f65ed21232bc702e59c66fcad6be69ff73\"},{\"author\":\"Wei Wang <weiwan@nvidia.com>\",\"commit\":\"ce5e7e3\",\"date\":\"2025-12-02\",\"message\":\"[CI][CUDA][Distributed] Update NCCL to 2.28.9 for CUDA13 (#168091)\\n\\nThis PR updates the NCCL version for CUDA13 from 2.27.7 to 2.28.9.\\n\\n2.28.9 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.9-1\\n2.28.7 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.7-1\\n2.28.3 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.3-1\\n\\nCUDA 12 remains at 2.27.5 and is untouched by this PR.\\n\\nReference PR: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166174\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168091\\nApproved by: <pianpwk@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"e51bd3c\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"[DebugMode] more selective silencing of profiler::record_function (#169716)\\n\\nPreviously DebugMode would ignore all `profiler::record_function` logs, but a lot of these are useful for contextualizing logs. Sadly compile doesn't preserve profiler ops, so these are only respected outside compiled regions.\\n\\ne.g. in aot_eager/inductor we get the backward start for free:\\n```\\n      aten::detach(t: f32[2, 4])  ->  t: f32[2, 4]\\n    aten::ones_like(t: f32[], pin_memory=False, memory_format=torch.preserve_format)  ->  t: f32[]\\n  [record function] backward._backward_impl (dynamo_timed)\\n    aten::expand(t:... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[DebugMode] more selective silencing of profiler::record_function (#169716)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/e51bd3c68d821117ddd7920914466b72b8cb175b\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"7c593b9\",\"date\":\"2025-12-08\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"Support for vector of Tensors in autograd::Function (#169155)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit cdb521f3ad8259c282b64cd1dcdd82af0ad719f5.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169155 on behalf of https://github.com/vishal9-team due to Breaking internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169155#issuecomment-3627839884))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"Support for vector of Tensors in autograd::Function (#169155)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/7c593b99eb9bcb1949924a2257ad6a063c08476f\"},{\"author\":\"bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"50e1cc3\",\"date\":\"2025-12-05\",\"message\":\"[precompile] disable dispatch when deepcloning in PrecompileContext.record_artifact (#169242)\\n\\nI ran into this when trying to precompile simplefsdp and realizing that deepcloning\\na DeviceMesh within a fake mode causes the following error:\\n\\n```\\n[rank0]:   File \\\"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/spc.py\\\", line 111, in <module>\\n[rank0]:     .aot_compile(((input_tensor, d_input_tensor), {\\\"mesh\\\": mesh}))\\n[rank0]:   File \\\"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py\\\", line 800, in aot_compile\\n[rank0]:     return aot_compile_fullgraph(\\n[rank0]:   File \\\"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/torch/_dynamo... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[precompile] disable dispatch when deepcloning in PrecompileContext.record_artifact b4e4ee81d386db922d8f63359f9870eff1f44052.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166968 on behalf of https://github.com/malfet due to It might have caused deadlock/test timeouts, see https://hud.pytorch.org/hud/pytorch/pytorch/d4dcd0354c4affcd90417f213785fc762e1b2b2f/1?per_page=50&name_filter=trunk%20%2F%20linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11%20%2F%20test&mergeEphemeralLF=true <37884920+jithunnair-amd@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"0fcec25\",\"date\":\"2025-12-09\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][CI] Use test runners for MI300 shadow workflow (#169427)\\n\\nTests out new MI3xx capacity via test labels.\\n\\n* Use `trunk-rocm-mi300` as a \\\"shadow\\\" workflow to test any new ROCm CI capacity to run on every commit to main/release branches\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169427\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][CI] Use test runners for MI300 shadow workflow (#169427)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/0fcec25922443c24d4bde5abfecd71a889417543\"},{\"author\":\"Joona Havukainen <jhavukainen@apple.com>\",\"commit\":\"8f8082d\",\"date\":\"2025-11-21\",\"message\":\"Fix memory leak test for SDPA op call (#168040)\\n\\nFixes #158330\\n\\nCurrently the test fails in the nightly because it keeps committing more work without waiting for previous work to finish and clean-up. So it is not testing the correct thing. Each iteration adds 2MB to the driver allocated memory as it holds on to the needed inputs, outputs and graph definition while the computation is in-flight.\\n\\nEither waiting for about ~0.2s after each op call or calling device to synchronize allows the computation to wrap-up and the underlying memory to get released, displaying the expected result that the me... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78\",\"title\":\"Fix memory leak test for SDPA op call (#168040)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/8f8082d7f077db8fc5d55a601fd8a1f3a4b236f7\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:162493 OR message:2.9 OR message:release) repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78 count:50\",\"totalCount\":42}",

      {
        "author": "atalman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "06b0307",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "Rename pytorch-triton package to triton (#169888)\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/163963\nMakes naming more consistent with official triton release\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169888\nApproved by: https://github.com/malfet, https://github.com/njriasan",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Rename pytorch-triton package to triton (#169888)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/06b03073b5fe2fb26260323a69a9d5ab23bbc9cd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Wang, Chuanqi <chuanqi.wang@intel.com>",
        "commit": "365a6c8",
        "date": "2025-12-05",
        "message": "[BE] Upgrade XPU support package to 2025.3 (#166829)\n\nFollows #166723. Including below changes,\n\n- Add XPU support package 2025.3 build and test in CI for both Linux and Windows\n- Keep XPU support package 2025.2 build in CI to ensure no break issue until PyTorch 2.10 release\n- Upgrade XPU support package from 2025.2 to 2025.3 in CD for both Linux and Windows\n- Update XPU runtime pypi packages dependencies of CD wheels\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166829\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[BE] Upgrade XPU support package to 2025.3 (#166829)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/365a6c84db516f244b7234b7aa3c8843af52936b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Shangdi Yu <shangdiy@meta.com>",
        "commit": "d2eff5d",
        "date": "2025-10-29",
        "message": "Add python stack trace to AOTI generated code (#160539)\n\nSummary:\nWe add a thread_local KernelContext object so Strobelight (and other potential profilers) can read the stack trace information of the running kernel.\n\nThis will bring extra overhead, so we guard this behind the `cpp.enable_kernel_profile` flag.\n\nExample output code:\n\n```cpp\n#include <torch/csrc/inductor/aoti_runtime/kernel_context_tls.h>\nnamespace torch::aot_inductor {\nthread_local KernelContext* tls_kernel_context = nullptr;\n}\n// Other code .....\nvoid AOTInductorModel::run_impl(\n    AtenTensorHandle*\n        input_handles, // a... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Add python stack trace to AOTI generated code (#160539)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/d2eff5d454ab2cb0a5ccdfb5eb6e7d6dcc75e097"
      },
      {
        "author": "Will Feng <yfeng.us@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "2ec8eda",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "[CI] Fix NVSHMEM errors in H100 symm mem job (#169879)\n\nExample error:\n```\n2025-12-08T12:46:36.6787890Z distributed/test_nvshmem.py::NVSHMEMSymmetricMemoryTest::test_alloc I1208 09:18:18.140000 4559 site-packages/torch/testing/_internal/common_distributed.py:1857] Testing class NVSHMEMSymmetricMemoryTest on 4 cuda\n2025-12-08T12:46:36.6790166Z /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/gpgpu/toolkit/r12.9/main_nvshmem/src/host/team/team_internal_nvls.cpp:258: non-zero status: 401 /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/gpgpu/toolkit/r12.9/main_nvshmem/src/host/team/team_internal_nvls.cpp:258: non-zero status: 401 /dvs/p4/build/sw/rel/... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[CI] Fix NVSHMEM errors in H100 symm mem job (#169879)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/2ec8eda3f6be4251c174bfc1f2d6028bf5d3ddf1"
      },
      {
        "author": "zpcore <zpcore@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "a5f36a8",
        "date": "2025-11-19",
        "message": "[DTensor] Fix deadlock after fast cache clear (#168069)\n\nThis is the necessary fix for https://github.com/meta-pytorch/autoparallel/issues/256.\n\n### Issue:\nwhen we call `_clear_fast_path_sharding_prop_cache()`, and then `get_thread_local_native_sharding_propagator_cache()`, the code will stuck due to deadlock.\n\n### Cause:\nWhen you assign to a Python dict key that already exists:\n```C++\nthread_dict[\"__DTensor_fastpath_thread_cache_cleanup\"] = old_capsule  // capsule #1 stored\n...\nclear_DTensor_sharding_propagator_cache() // call to clean up the cache\n...\nget_thread_local_native_sharding_propaga... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[DTensor] Fix deadlock after fast cache clear (#168069)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/a5f36a8fda588eca2b15edefbf416cd92f081f30"
      },
      {
        "author": "atalman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "f633119",
        "date": "2025-11-11",
        "message": "Enable Doc builds for: Minor Releases RCs. Minor and Patch Releases final RC (#167478)\n\nEnable Doc builds for\n1. Minor Releases RCs\n2. Minor and Patch Releases final RC\n\nThis is done to prevent publishing doc for patch releases when building rcs.\nSee:\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/docs/pull/57\n\nFollowup after: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153973\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/167478\nApproved by: https://github.com/svekars, https://github.com/seemethere",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Enable Doc builds for: Minor Releases RCs. Minor and Patch Releases final RC (#167478)",

      },

        "commit": "77acc66",

        "author": "karthickai <karthickps@meta.com>",
        "commit": "929776a",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "[Inductor] Fix combo kernels variable collision with ND tiled reductions (#168946)\n\nFixes: #168945\n\nFix combo kernels crash when fusing op with multi-dim reductions (ND tiling). It caused variable name collisions in generated triton code when multiple reduction dimensions existed.\n\n```python\ndef triton_per_fused_1(in_out_ptr0, in_ptr0, out_ptr0, XBLOCK : tl.constexpr):\n    pid = tl.program_id(0)\n    if pid % 1 == 0:\n        pid_offset = pid // 1\n        xnumel = 4\n        r0_numel = 2\n        RBLOCK_0: tl.constexpr = 2\n        R0_BLOCK_0: tl.constexpr = 2\n        r1_numel = 8\n        RBLOCK_0:... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[Inductor] Fix combo kernels variable collision with ND tiled reductions (#168946)",

      },
      {
        "author": "atalman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "a36e1d3",
        "date": "2025-12-04",
        "message": "Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\n\nRequired for release 2.10\n\nRocm wheel build fix provided by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169369\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168096\nApproved by: https://github.com/njriasan, https://github.com/malfet, https://github.com/huydhn",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",

      {
        "author": "Jithun Nair <37884920+jithunnair-amd@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "1efc14a",
        "date": "2025-11-19",
        "message": "[ROCm][CI] Update concurrency setting for docker-cache-rocm.yml (#168104)\n\nWe only want to cache the latest CI docker image for `main` and `release` branches in cases where multiple `docker-builds` workflow runs get triggered in quick succession. This is because the latest run will anyway overwrite the cached images, since we do not maintain a cached image per-SHA, instead it's only one-per-branch (to minimize cache size and docker load times at runner bringup).\n\nAlso removing `workflow_dispatch` as a trigger since it won't work (needs artifacts from `docker-builds` run)\n\nPull Request resolved... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[ROCm][CI] Update concurrency setting for docker-cache-rocm.yml (#168104)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/1efc14a50d56701e3ad0639849bc26da59e7a3c3"
      },
      {
        "author": "Paul Mullowney <Paul.Mullowney@amd.com>",
        "commit": "f6bf70b",
        "date": "2025-12-10",
        "message": "[ROCm] roll kernel as grid stride loop (#169474)\n\nReimplement the roll kernel as a grid stride loop. On AMD devices, we see launch failures in the original version when gridDim.x*blockDim.x exceeds 4294967295. This implementation should work and be performant on both AMD and Nvidia devices. The issue can be seen on AMD devices with the following small repro:\n\nimport torch\nN = 21913096\ninput_tensor_torch = torch.randn(1, 2, N, 98, device='cuda')\noutput = input_tensor_torch.roll(-1, dims=1)\ninput_tensor_torch_cpu = input_tensor_torch.cpu()\noutput_cpu = input_tensor_torch_cpu.roll(-1, dims=1)\nass... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[ROCm] roll kernel as grid stride loop (#169474)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/f6bf70bd12b1a860b01d34b8fd8425829bfdcbed"
      },
      {
        "author": "Anatoliy Litvinenko <alitvine@amd.com>",
        "commit": "add6ad1",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "[ROCm] Bump to aotriton 0.11.1b (#169696)\n\nRestore Navi31 support with Alternative Wheel.\nFix incompatibility with earlier version of GNU linker\n-This was not detected because the released runtime tarballs are always built with gcc-13 from SCL\nAdd gfx1103 support\n\n[AOTriton 0.11.1b Release Page](https://github.com/ROCm/aotriton/releases/tag/0.11.1b) for more\ndetails.\nThis is copy of PR https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168275\n\nAs reference, pr for checking test state: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169676\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169696\nApp... [truncated]",

        "title": "[ROCm] Bump to aotriton 0.11.1b (#169696)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/add6ad154249523fccba6e81be280b26d1c46d40"
      },
      {
        "author": "Kathryn-cat <jinqic@alumni.cmu.edu>",
        "commit": "8da5d29",
        "date": "2025-12-04",
        "message": "[DLPack] C Functions for DLPack Speed Exchange and Stream Handling (#165483)\n\n## Addressed Issue\n\nIssue #162845\n\n## Summary of Changes\n\nThis PR introduces a unified `DLPackExchangeAPI` struct as described in proposal [175](https://github.com/dmlc/dlpack/issues/175). This new convention replaces the previous mechanism of separate function pointers, and aligns with the latest DLPack standard as shown in PR [174](https://github.com/dmlc/dlpack/pull/174).\n\nSpecifically, the new `DLPackExchangeAPI` struct is exposed as `torch.Tensor.__c_dlpack_exchange_api__`, which stores and exposes the following... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[DLPack] C Functions for DLPack Speed Exchange and Stream Handling (#165483)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/8da5d29de7feb165047246464d09c4c2b2318987"
      },
      {
        "author": "Andrey Talman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "b4e4ee8",
        "date": "2025-11-04",

        "author": "Basil Wong <basilwong@meta.com>",
        "commit": "a4b91a3",
        "date": "2025-12-10",
        "message": "[partitioner] New configuration for considering custom operations in knapsack optimization (#167083) (#169636)\n\nSummary:\n\nCreates new configuration: `include_custom_and_triton_kernel_wrappers_in_knapsack_optimization`\n\n ---\n\nIf config toggle is set to true, then nodes meeting the following criteria no longer just recomputed by default:\n\n```\nop.namespace not in {\"aten\", \"prim\", \"prims\"}\n```\n\nor\n\n```\nnode.target == torch.ops.higher_order.triton_kernel_wrapper_functional\n```\n\nTest Plan:\n# E2E Testing\n\n### Baseline\n\nHere is the [tlparse link](https://manifold.edge.x2p.facebook.net/v0/read/tree/log...       {
        "author": "atalman <atalman@fb.com>",
        "commit": "cc8bfd1",
        "date": "2025-11-04",
        "message": "Docker release build: Use 13.0.0 nvidia docker (#166904)\n\nForward fix for failing Docker release builds\nRelated to: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/166897\n\nNightly Docker build failure https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/18900508440/job/53946606434\nDue to missing base image:\n```\nERROR: failed to build: failed to solve: docker.io/nvidia/cuda:13.0.2-devel-ubuntu22.04: not found\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166904\nApproved by: https://github.com/tinglvv, https://github.com/malfet",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Docker release build: Use 13.0.0 nvidia docker (#166904)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/cc8bfd1206f4bff26bd86ce584f6c16b6401ef50"
      },
      {
        "author": "mori360 <yifanmao@meta.com>",
        "commit": "add5fc9",
        "date": "2025-12-08",
        "message": "[DTensor] update redistribute_cost, add disable_graph_based_transform (#169304)\n\n### Background\nDTensor has 2 different utilities\n* redistribute_cost() API is used to compute a cost value for each possible redistribution when generating a sharding strategy.  DTensor dispatch then minimizes over these cost values to select the strategy before performing a redistribution\n* redistribute_tensor APIs are given a src/target placement, but need to find the fastest set of operations to perform this redistribution.  This logic uses different cost modeling than `redistribute_cost`\n\nThe redistribute_tens... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[DTensor] update redistribute_cost, add disable_graph_based_transform (#169304)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/add5fc9c2d7496be643dbf0e059f7432c9d82e18"
      },
      {
        "author": "Yu, Guangye <guangye.yu@intel.com>",
        "commit": "c8d790b",
        "date": "2025-11-18",
        "message": "[xpu][fix] Fix empty cache on mempool (#168074)\n\n# Motivation\nThis is definitely a bug: we were attempting to release cached memory back to the system without proper **synchronization**. Callers must ensure that all accesses to memory blocks allocated by SYCL APIs have completed before invoking `sycl::free`.\n\nFor a simple example, in the following code:\n```python\npool = torch.xpu.MemPool()\nwith torch.xpu.use_mem_pool(pool):\n    input = torch.randn(100, device='xpu')\nsum = input.sum()\ndel pool\nprint(sum)\n```\n`sum` may exhibit undefined behavior because `input.sum()` might not have finished exec... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[xpu][fix] Fix empty cache on mempool (#168074)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/c8d790b56d7bbb4ee266976944ab920f2d513b40"
      },
      {
        "author": "Huy Do <huydhn@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "e1f5147",
        "date": "2025-12-08",
        "message": "Revert \"Create a mock benchmark results for torchao cudagraphs_low_precision (#123419)\" (#169753)\n\nThis reverts commit cd3c1132a969412bcaa1ed6288e83fee25666dc3.\n\nThis was originally planned as a way to apply quantization in DynamoBench, but it didn't lead anywhere.  And torchao is running its own benchmark on the repo now.  We can clean this up.\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/169663\n\n### Testing\n\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/19985987464\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169753\nApproved by: https://github.com/masnesral, https://... [truncated]",

        "message": "Triton 3.6 pin update (#168096)\n\nRequired for release 2.10\n\nRocm wheel build fix provided by: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169369\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168096\nApproved by: https://github.com/njriasan, https://github.com/malfet",

      {
        "author": "Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "d19f36b",
        "date": "2025-11-06",
        "message": "[BE][Ez]: Update fmtlib submodule to 12.1.0 (#166983)\n\nFixed some compiler idiosyncrasies, improves CPP support, bugfixes, and performance optimizations. This is a header only minor library change so should be low risk and improve the performance of our formatting/loggers. Also allows fmtlib to be used in more constexpr contexts.\n\nFull changelog here: https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt/releases/tag/12.1.0\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166983\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[BE][Ez]: Update fmtlib submodule to 12.1.0 (#166983)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/d19f36bea1c47d47ace6bae43d3b34dd858de145"
      },
      {
        "author": "johannes <johannes.zeiser5@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "f3fa560",
        "date": "2025-11-03",
        "message": "Integrate NVIDIA cuSolver backend into ATen/Linalg (initial implementation for eig/eigval) (#166715)\n\n### Summary\n\nAdds support for NVIDIA\u2019s cuSolver backend to torch.linalg.eig and torch.linalg.eigvals within the ATen/Linalg framework.\n\n### Motivation\n\nExtending PyTorch\u2019s Linalg backends with NVIDIA\u2019s cuSolver enables faster execution of torch.linalg.eig and torch.linalg.eigvals, complementing existing MAGMA and CPU implementations.\n\nThe speedup observed on consumer hardware (RTX4070/Ryzen 5700x) is in the order of **2x**, with preliminary testing on HPC hardware (H100, EPYC 9454) sugge... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Integrate NVIDIA cuSolver backend into ATen/Linalg (initial implementation for eig/eigval) (#166715)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/f3fa560dec727380b3e9c074efe05f0ce715a5ca"
      },
      {
        "author": "Yun Wu <sunnyunwu@meta.com>",
        "commit": "a45a17f",
        "date": "2025-11-06",
        "message": "Fix boxcox to return same result for same input in one batch (#166986)\n\nSummary:\nThe SIMD path is using SLEEF version of pow which is slightly different from std::pow. The fix is to use the same vectorized code (with partial load and store) for the trailing data as well to ensure consistency between results.\n\nDeploy:\nNeed to make a hotfix in waas to monitor release signals, since this diff can cause testing failures in veloski and waas release correctness tests.\n\nTest Plan: Sandcastle.\n\nDifferential Revision: D86218207\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166986\nAppr... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Fix boxcox to return same result for same input in one batch (#166986)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/a45a17f65ed21232bc702e59c66fcad6be69ff73"
      },
      {
        "author": "Wei Wang <weiwan@nvidia.com>",
        "commit": "ce5e7e3",
        "date": "2025-12-02",
        "message": "[CI][CUDA][Distributed] Update NCCL to 2.28.9 for CUDA13 (#168091)\n\nThis PR updates the NCCL version for CUDA13 from 2.27.7 to 2.28.9.\n\n2.28.9 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.9-1\n2.28.7 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.7-1\n2.28.3 release notes: https://github.com/NVIDIA/nccl/releases/tag/v2.28.3-1\n\nCUDA 12 remains at 2.27.5 and is untouched by this PR.\n\nReference PR: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166174\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/168091\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[CI][CUDA][Distributed] Update NCCL to 2.28.9 for CUDA13 (#168091)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/ce5e7e3bf1f4b69a4f4f93d288ba75b906df492a"
      },
      {
        "author": "Xinya Zhang <Xinya.Zhang@amd.com>",
        "commit": "57f36c9",
        "date": "2025-11-18",
        "message": "[ROCm][CI] Upgrade ROCm CI to 7.1 (#166743)\n\nUpgrade all the ROCm docker images to ROCm 7.1 release version.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166743\nApproved by: https://github.com/atalman\n\nCo-authored-by: Jeff Daily <jeff.daily@amd.com>\nCo-authored-by: Prachi Gupta <prachi.gupta@amd.com>",

      {
        "author": "Pian Pawakapan <pianpwk@meta.com>",
        "commit": "e51bd3c",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "[DebugMode] more selective silencing of profiler::record_function (#169716)\n\nPreviously DebugMode would ignore all `profiler::record_function` logs, but a lot of these are useful for contextualizing logs. Sadly compile doesn't preserve profiler ops, so these are only respected outside compiled regions.\n\ne.g. in aot_eager/inductor we get the backward start for free:\n```\n      aten::detach(t: f32[2, 4])  ->  t: f32[2, 4]\n    aten::ones_like(t: f32[], pin_memory=False, memory_format=torch.preserve_format)  ->  t: f32[]\n  [record function] backward._backward_impl (dynamo_timed)\n    aten::expand(t:... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[DebugMode] more selective silencing of profiler::record_function (#169716)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/e51bd3c68d821117ddd7920914466b72b8cb175b"
      },

        "commit": "7c593b9",

        "author": "bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>",
        "commit": "50e1cc3",
        "date": "2025-12-05",
        "message": "[precompile] disable dispatch when deepcloning in PrecompileContext.record_artifact (#169242)\n\nI ran into this when trying to precompile simplefsdp and realizing that deepcloning\na DeviceMesh within a fake mode causes the following error:\n\n```\n[rank0]:   File \"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/spc.py\", line 111, in <module>\n[rank0]:     .aot_compile(((input_tensor, d_input_tensor), {\"mesh\": mesh}))\n[rank0]:   File \"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py\", line 800, in aot_compile\n[rank0]:     return aot_compile_fullgraph(\n[rank0]:   File \"/home/bobren/local/a/pytorch/torch/_dynamo... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[precompile] disable dispatch when deepcloning in PrecompileContext.record_artifact (#169242)",

        "date": "2025-11-05",
        "message": "Update triton to 3.5.1 release (#166968)\n\nThis includes sm103 https://github.com/triton-lang/triton/pull/8485 fix\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166968\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/njriasan",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Update triton to 3.5.1 release (#166968)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/89165c0a2b5d3c147c19a492437291c8ff18aa7f"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "9c2c3db",
        "date": "2025-11-05",
        "message": "Revert \"Update triton to 3.5.1 release (#166968)\"\n\nThis reverts commit b4e4ee81d386db922d8f63359f9870eff1f44052.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166968 on behalf of https://github.com/malfet due to It might have caused deadlock/test timeouts, see https://hud.pytorch.org/hud/pytorch/pytorch/d4dcd0354c4affcd90417f213785fc762e1b2b2f/1?per_page=50&name_filter=trunk%20%2F%20linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11%20%2F%20test&mergeEphemeralLF=true ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/166968#issuecomment-3492399396))",

      },
      {
        "author": "Jithun Nair <37884920+jithunnair-amd@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "0fcec25",
        "date": "2025-12-09",
        "message": "[ROCm][CI] Use test runners for MI300 shadow workflow (#169427)\n\nTests out new MI3xx capacity via test labels.\n\n* Use `trunk-rocm-mi300` as a \"shadow\" workflow to test any new ROCm CI capacity to run on every commit to main/release branches\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/169427\nApproved by: https://github.com/jeffdaily",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "[ROCm][CI] Use test runners for MI300 shadow workflow (#169427)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78/-/commit/0fcec25922443c24d4bde5abfecd71a889417543"
      },
      {
        "author": "Joona Havukainen <jhavukainen@apple.com>",
        "commit": "8f8082d",
        "date": "2025-11-21",
        "message": "Fix memory leak test for SDPA op call (#168040)\n\nFixes #158330\n\nCurrently the test fails in the nightly because it keeps committing more work without waiting for previous work to finish and clean-up. So it is not testing the correct thing. Each iteration adds 2MB to the driver allocated memory as it holds on to the needed inputs, outputs and graph definition while the computation is in-flight.\n\nEither waiting for about ~0.2s after each op call or calling device to synchronize allows the computation to wrap-up and the underlying memory to get released, displaying the expected result that the me... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
        "title": "Fix memory leak test for SDPA op call (#168040)",

      }

Otherwise\n+          # If doing this in release/2.10 or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise\n           # use ECR\n           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n",

            "body": " \n       - name: \"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\"\n         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n         continue-on-error: true\n         with:\n           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\n \n         # Setup the environment\n       - name: Checkout PyTorch\n-        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n+        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n         with:\n           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n \n",

            "body": " \n       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n         id: install-nvidia-driver\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\n         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\n \n       - name: configure aws credentials\n",

          {
            "body": "       - name: Calculate docker image\n         id: calculate-docker-image\n         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}\n           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n           working-directory: pytorch\n \n       - name: Pull Docker image\n         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n \n",

            "body": "           done\n \n       - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\n \n       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n       - name: Checkout PyTorch\n-        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n+        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n \n       - name: Get workflow job id\n         id: get-job-id\n         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n         if: always()\n         with:\n           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n \n       - name: Setup Python\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@release/2.10\n         with:\n           python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}\n           pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\n",

          {
            "body": "       PLATFORM: 'manylinux_2_28_x86_64'\n     steps:\n       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n         with:\n           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n \n       - name: Checkout PyTorch\n-        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n+        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n         with:\n           submodules: false\n \n       - name: Setup Linux\n         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n \n       - name: Pull Docker image\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-image: ${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\n \n",

          {
            "body": "       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n       # deep clone (fetch-depth 0) required for git merge-base\n       - name: Checkout PyTorch\n-        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@main\n+        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n \n       - name: Setup Linux\n         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n \n       - name: Build docker image\n         id: build-docker-image\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-image-name: ci-image:${{ matrix.docker-image-name }}\n           always-rebuild: true\n           push: true\n \n       - name: Pull docker image\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-image: ${{ steps.build-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n \n",
            "newRange": {

          {
            "body": "       - name: Checkout PyTorch\n         uses: actions/checkout@v4\n         with:\n-          ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n           submodules: recursive\n           path: pytorch\n           show-progress: false\n",

          {
            "body": "           role-duration-seconds: 18000\n       - name: Calculate docker image\n         id: calculate-docker-image\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\n           docker-image-name: libtorch-cxx11-builder\n           custom-tag-prefix: rocm7.0\n           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\n           working-directory: pytorch\n       - name: Pull Docker image\n-        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@main\n+        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n         with:\n           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n       - name: Test Pytorch binary\n",
