pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n     with:\\n       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n\n.github/workflows/_win-build.yml\n.github/workflows/_win-test.yml\n.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml\n.github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\n.github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml\n.github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\n.github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml\n.github/workflows/build-manywheel-images-s390x.yml\n.github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml\n.github/workflows/build-triton-wheel.yml\n.github/workflows/build-vllm-wheel.yml\n.github/workflows/check-labels.yml\n.github/workflows/close-nonexistent-disable-issues.yml\n.github/workflows/create_release.yml\n.github/workflows/docker-builds.yml\n.github/workflows/docker-cache-rocm.yml\n.github/workflows/docker-release.yml\n.github/workflows/dynamo-unittest.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-libtorch-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-linux-s390x-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-macos-arm64-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-wheel-nightly.yml\n.github/workflows/h100-cutlass-backend.yml\n.github/workflows/h100-distributed.yml\n.github/workflows/h100-symm-mem.yml\n.github/workflows/inductor-micro-benchmark.yml\n.github/workflows/inductor-nightly.yml"
}
11. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       check_experiments:\\n7:         required: false\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: |\\n10:           List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.\\n11:       opt_out_experiments:\\n12:         required: false\\n13:         type: string\\n14:         description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.\\n15:       triggering_actor:\\n16:         required: true\\n17:         type: string\\n18:         description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor\\n19:       issue_owner:\\n20:         required: true\\n21:         type: string\\n22:         description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login\\n23:       curr_branch:\\n24:         required: true\\n25:         type: string\\n26:         description: Current branch or tag.\\n27:       curr_ref_type:\\n28:         required: false\\n29:         type: string\\n30:         default: branch\\n31:         description: The value of \\\"github.ref_type\\\", \\\"branch\\\" or \\\"tag\\\"\\n32:       issue_number:\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         type: string\\n35:         default: \\\"5132\\\"\\n36:         description: |\\n37:           Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra\\n38:           Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\\n39: \\n40:     outputs:\\n41:       label-type:\\n42:         description: Type of runners to use\\n43:         value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}\\n44: \\n45: jobs:\\n46:   runner-determinator:\\n47:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n48:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n49:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n50:     outputs:\\n51:       label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}\\n52:     env:\\n53:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n54:       ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}\\n55:       TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}\\n56:       ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}\\n57:       CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}\\n58:       OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}\\n59:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n60:     steps:\\n61:       # - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n62:       #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n63:       #   with:\\n64:       #     fetch-depth: 1\\n65:       #     submodules: true\\n66: \\n67:       # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below\\n68:       # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners\\n69:       # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py\\n70:       - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script\\n71:         id: hardcode-script\\n72:         run: |\\n73:           cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py\\n74:           # flake8: noqa: G004\\n75: \\n76:           # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml\\n77:           #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:\\n78:           #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py\\n79: \\n80:           \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n81:           This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a\\n82:           GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default\\n83:           https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration\\n84:           of which runners should be used to run which job.\\n85: \\n86:           The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,\\n87:           separated by a line containing \\\"---\\\".  If the line is not present, the\\n88:           settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user\\n89:           list, defined.\\n90: \\n91:           The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be\\n92:           used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.\\n93:           It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.\\n94: \\n95:           The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.\\n96:           The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or\\n97:           experiments which the user could be opted in to.\\n98: \\n99:           The user list has the following rules:\\n100: \\n101:           - Users are GitHub usernames, which must start with the @ prefix\\n102:           - Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable\\n103:           - A \\\"#\\\" prefix opts the user out of all experiments\\n104: \\n105:           Example config:\\n106:               # A list of experiments that can be opted into.\\n107:               # This defines the behavior they'll induce when opted into.\\n108:               # Expected syntax is:\\n109:               #   [experiment_name]: # Name of the experiment. Also used for the label prefix.\\n110:               #      rollout_perc: [int] # % of workflows to run with this experiment when users are not opted in.\\n111: \\n112:               experiments:\\n113:                 lf:\\n114:                   rollout_percent: 25\\n115:                   all_branches: false\\n116:                   default: true\\n117:               ---\\n118: \\n119:               # Opt-ins:\\n120:               # Users can opt into the LF fleet by adding their GitHub username to this list\\n121:               # and specifying experiments to enable in a comma-separated list.\\n122:               # To always opt out of an experiment, prefix it with a \\\"-\\\".\\n123:               # Experiments should be from the above list.\\n124: \\n125:               @User1,-lf,split_build\\n126:               @User2,lf\\n127:               @User3,split_build\\n128:           \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n129: \\n130:           import json\\n131:           import logging\\n132:           import os\\n133:           import random\\n134:           import re\\n135:           import sys\\n136:           from argparse import ArgumentParser\\n137:           from collections.abc import Iterable\\n138:           from functools import cache\\n139:           from logging import LogRecord\\n140:           from typing import Any, NamedTuple\\n141:           from urllib.request import Request, urlopen\\n142: \\n143:           import yaml\\n144:           from github import Auth, Github\\n145:           from github.Issue import Issue\\n146: \\n147: \\n148:           DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX = \\\"\\\"  # use meta runners\\n149:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF = \\\"lf.\\\"  # use runners from the linux foundation\\n150:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF_CANARY = \\\"lf.c.\\\"  # use canary runners from the linux foundation\\n151: \\n152:           GITHUB_OUTPUT = os.getenv(\\\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n153:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_AMI = \\\"runner-ami\\\"\\n154:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE = \\\"label-type\\\"\\n155:           OPT_OUT_LABEL = \\\"no-runner-experiments\\\"\\n156: \\n157:           SETTING_EXPERIMENTS = \\\"experiments\\\"\\n158: \\n159:           LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT = \\\"lf\\\"\\n160:           CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX = \\\".c\\\"\\n161: \\n162: \\n163:           class Experiment(NamedTuple):\\n164:               rollout_perc: float = (\\n165:                   0  # Percentage of workflows to experiment on when user is not opted-in.\\n166:               )\\n167:               all_branches: bool = (\\n168:                   False  # If True, the experiment is also enabled on the exception branches\\n169:               )\\n170:               default: bool = (\\n171:                   True  # If True, the experiment is enabled by default for all queries\\n172:               )\\n173: \\n174:               # Add more fields as needed\\n175: \\n176: \\n177:           class Settings(NamedTuple):\\n178:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n179:               Settings for the experiments that can be opted into.\\n180:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n181: \\n182:               experiments: dict[str, Experiment] = {}\\n183: \\n184: \\n185:           class ColorFormatter(logging.Formatter):\\n186:               \\\"\\\"\\\"Color codes the log messages based on the log level\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n187: \\n188:               COLORS = {\\n189:                   \\\"WARNING\\\": \\\"\\\\033[33m\\\",  # Yellow\\n190:                   \\\"ERROR\\\": \\\"\\\\033[31m\\\",  # Red\\n191:                   \\\"CRITICAL\\\": \\\"\\\\033[31m\\\",  # Red\\n192:                   \\\"INFO\\\": \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\",  # Reset\\n193:                   \\\"DEBUG\\\": \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\",  # Reset\\n194:               }\\n195: \\n196:               def format(self, record: LogRecord) -> str:\\n197:                   log_color = self.COLORS.get(record.levelname, \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\")  # Default to reset\\n198:                   record.msg = f\\\"{log_color}{record.msg}\\\\033[0m\\\"\\n199:                   return super().format(record)\\n200: \\n201: \\n202:           handler = logging.StreamHandler()\\n203:           handler.setFormatter(ColorFormatter(fmt=\\\"%(levelname)-8s: %(message)s\\\"))\\n204: \\n205:           log = logging.getLogger(os.path.basename(__file__))\\n206:           log.addHandler(handler)\\n207:           log.setLevel(logging.INFO)\\n208: \\n209: \\n210:           def set_github_output(key: str, value: str) -> None:\\n211:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n212:               Defines outputs of the github action that invokes this script\\n213:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n214:               if not GITHUB_OUTPUT:\\n215:                   # See https://github.blog/changelog/2022-10-11-github-actions-deprecating-save-state-and-set-output-commands/ for deprecation notice\\n216:                   log.warning(\\n217:                       \\\"No env var found for GITHUB_OUTPUT, you must be running this code locally. Falling back to the deprecated print method.\\\"\\n218:                   )\\n219:                   print(f\\\"::set-output name={key}::{value}\\\")\\n220:                   return\\n221: \\n222:               with open(GITHUB_OUTPUT, \\\"a\\\") as f:\\n223:                   log.info(f\\\"Setting output: {key}='{value}'\\\")\\n224:                   f.write(f\\\"{key}={value}\\\\n\\\")\\n225: \\n226: \\n227:           def _str_comma_separated_to_set(value: str) -> frozenset[str]:\\n228:               return frozenset(\\n229:                   filter(lambda itm: itm != \\\"\\\", map(str.strip, value.strip(\\\" \\\\n\\\\t\\\").split(\\\",\\\")))\\n230:               )\\n231: \\n232: \\n233:           def parse_args() -> Any:\\n234:               parser = ArgumentParser(\\\"Get dynamic rollout settings\\\")\\n235:               parser.add_argument(\\\"--github-token\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub token\\\")\\n236:               parser.add_argument(\\n237:                   \\\"--github-issue-repo\\\",\\n238:                   type=str,\\n239:                   required=False,\\n240:                   default=\\\"pytorch/test-infra\\\",\\n241:                   help=\\\"GitHub repo to get the issue\\\",\\n242:               )\\n243:               parser.add_argument(\\n244:                   \\\"--github-repo\\\",\\n245:                   type=str,\\n246:                   required=True,\\n247:                   help=\\\"GitHub repo where CI is running\\\",\\n248:               )\\n249:               parser.add_argument(\\n250:                   \\\"--github-issue\\\", type=int, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub issue number\\\"\\n251:               )\\n252:               parser.add_argument(\\n253:                   \\\"--github-actor\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub triggering_actor\\\"\\n254:               )\\n255:               parser.add_argument(\\n256:                   \\\"--github-issue-owner\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub issue owner\\\"\\n257:               )\\n258:               parser.add_argument(\\n259:                   \\\"--github-branch\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"Current GitHub branch or tag\\\"\\n260:               )\\n261:               parser.add_argument(\\n262:                   \\\"--github-ref-type\\\",\\n263:                   type=str,\\n264:                   required=True,\\n265:                   help=\\\"Current GitHub ref type, branch or tag\\\",\\n266:               )\\n267:               parser.add_argument(\\n268:                   \\\"--eligible-experiments\\\",\\n269:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\\n270:                   required=False,\\n271:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n272:                   help=\\\"comma separated list of experiments to check, if omitted all experiments marked with default=True are checked\\\",\\n273:               )\\n274:               parser.add_argument(\\n275:                   \\\"--opt-out-experiments\\\",\\n276:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\\n277:                   required=False,\\n278:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n279:                   help=(\\n280:                       \\\"comma separated list of experiments to opt-out of. If unset, no opt-outs will occur. \\\"\\n281:                       \\\"If the same experiment is listed both here and in '--eligible-experiments' opt-out will take priority.\\\"\\n282:                   ),\\n283:               )\\n284:               parser.add_argument(\\n285:                   \\\"--pr-number\\\",\\n286:                   type=str,\\n287:                   required=False,\\n288:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n289:                   help=\\\"the optional PR number where this is run\\\",\\n290:               )\\n291: \\n292:               return parser.parse_args()\\n293: \\n294: \\n295:           def get_gh_client(github_token: str) -> Github:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\\n296:               auth = Auth.Token(github_token)\\n297:               return Github(auth=auth)\\n298: \\n299: \\n300:           def get_issue(gh: Github, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> Issue:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\\n301:               repo = gh.get_repo(repo)\\n302:               return repo.get_issue(number=issue_num)\\n303: \\n304: \\n305:           def get_potential_pr_author(\\n306:               github_token: str, repo: str, username: str, ref_type: str, ref_name: str\\n307:           ) -> str:\\n308:               # If the trigger was a new tag added by a bot, this is a ciflow case\\n309:               # Fetch the actual username from the original PR. The PR number is\\n310:               # embedded in the tag name: ciflow/<name>/<pr-number>\\n311: \\n312:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\\n313: \\n314:               if username == \\\"pytorch-bot[bot]\\\" and ref_type == \\\"tag\\\":\\n315:                   split_tag = ref_name.split(\\\"/\\\")\\n316:                   if (\\n317:                       len(split_tag) == 3\\n318:                       and split_tag[0] == \\\"ciflow\\\"\\n319:                       and split_tag[2].isnumeric()\\n320:                   ):\\n321:                       pr_number = split_tag[2]\\n322:                       try:\\n323:                           repository = gh.get_repo(repo)\\n324:                           pull = repository.get_pull(number=int(pr_number))\\n325:                       except Exception as e:\\n326:                           raise Exception(  # noqa: TRY002\\n327:                               f\\\"issue with pull request {pr_number} from repo {repository}\\\"\\n328:                           ) from e\\n329:                       return pull.user.login  # type: ignore[no-any-return]\\n330:               # In all other cases, return the original input username\\n331:               return username\\n332: \\n333: \\n334:           def is_exception_branch(branch: str) -> bool:\\n335:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n336:               Branches that get opted out of experiments by default, until they're explicitly enabled.\\n337:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n338:               return branch.split(\\\"/\\\")[0] in {\\\"main\\\", \\\"nightly\\\", \\\"release\\\", \\\"landchecks\\\"}\\n339: \\n340: \\n341:           def load_yaml(yaml_text: str) -> Any:\\n342:               try:\\n343:                   data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)\\n344:                   return data\\n345:               except yaml.YAMLError:\\n346:                   log.exception(\\\"Error loading YAML\\\")\\n347:                   raise\\n348: \\n349: \\n350:           def extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\\n351:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n352:               Extracts the text with settings, if any, and the opted in users from the rollout state.\\n353: \\n354:               If the issue body contains \\\"---\\\" then the text above that is the settings\\n355:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\\n356: \\n357:               If it doesn't contain \\\"---\\\" then the settings are empty and the rest is the users.\\n358:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n359:               rollout_state_parts = rollout_state.split(\\\"---\\\")\\n360:               if len(rollout_state_parts) >= 2:\\n361:                   return rollout_state_parts[0], rollout_state_parts[1]\\n362:               else:\\n363:                   return \\\"\\\", rollout_state\\n364: \\n365: \\n366:           class UserOptins(dict[str, list[str]]):\\n367:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n368:               Dictionary of users with a list of features they have opted into\\n369:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n370: \\n371: \\n372:           def parse_user_opt_in_from_text(user_optin_text: str) -> UserOptins:\\n373:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n374:               Parse the user opt-in text into a key value pair of username and the list of features they have opted into\\n375: \\n376:               Users are GitHub usernames with the @ prefix. Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable.\\n377:                   - Example line: \\\"@User1,lf,split_build\\\"\\n378:                   - A \\\"#\\\" prefix indicates the user is opted out of all experiments\\n379: \\n380: \\n381:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n382:               optins = UserOptins()\\n383:               for user in user_optin_text.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\"):\\n384:                   user = user.strip(\\\"\\\\r\\\\n\\\\t -\\\")\\n385:                   if not user or not user.startswith(\\\"@\\\"):\\n386:                       # Not a valid user. Skip\\n387:                       continue\\n388: \\n389:                   if user:\\n390:                       usr_name = user.split(\\\",\\\")[0].strip(\\\"@\\\")\\n391:                       optins[usr_name] = [exp.strip(\\\" \\\") for exp in user.split(\\\",\\\")[1:]]\\n392: \\n393:               return optins\\n394: \\n395: \\n396:           def is_valid_experiment_name(experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n397:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:               Check if the experiment name is valid.\\n399:               A valid name:\\n400:                   - Contains only alphanumeric characters and the special characters \\\"_\\\" & \\\"-\\\"\\n401:                   - The special characters \\\"_\\\" & \\\"-\\\" shouldn't be the first or last characters\\n402:                   - Cannot contain spaces\\n403:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n404: \\n405:               valid_char_regex = r\\\"^[a-zA-Z0-9]([\\\\w-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$\\\"\\n406:               valid = bool(re.match(valid_char_regex, experiment_name))\\n407: \\n408:               if valid:\\n409:                   return True\\n410: \\n411:               log.error(\\n412:                   f\\\"Invalid experiment name: {experiment_name}. Experiment names should only contain alphanumeric characters, '_', and '-'. They cannot contain spaces, and the special characters '_' and '-' cannot be the first or last characters.\\\"\\n413:               )\\n414:               return False\\n415: \\n416: \\n417:           def parse_settings_from_text(settings_text: str) -> Settings:\\n418:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n419:               Parse the experiments from the issue body into a list of ExperimentSettings\\n420:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n421:               try:\\n422:                   if settings_text:\\n423:                       # Escape the backtick as well so that we can have the settings in a code block on the GH issue\\n424:                       # for easy reading\\n425:                       # Note: Using ascii for the backtick so that the cat step in _runner-determinator.yml doesn't choke on\\n426:                       #       the backtick character in shell commands.\\n427:                       backtick = chr(96)  # backtick character\\n428:                       settings_text = settings_text.strip(f\\\"\\\\r\\\\n\\\\t{backtick} \\\")\\n429:                       settings = load_yaml(settings_text)\\n430: \\n431:                       # For now we just load experiments. We can expand this if/when we add more settings\\n432:                       experiments = {}\\n433: \\n434:                       for exp_name, exp_settings in settings.get(SETTING_EXPERIMENTS).items():\\n435:                           if not is_valid_experiment_name(exp_name):\\n436:                               # Exclude invalid experiments from the list. We log an error, but don't raise an exception so that other experiments can still be processed.\\n437:                               continue\\n438: \\n439:                           valid_settings = {}\\n440:                           for setting in exp_settings:\\n441:                               if setting not in Experiment._fields:\\n442:                                   log.warning(\\n443:                                       f\\\"Unexpected setting in experiment: {setting} = {exp_settings[setting]}\\\"\\n444:                                   )\\n445:                               else:\\n446:                                   valid_settings[setting] = exp_settings[setting]\\n447: \\n448:                           experiments[exp_name] = Experiment(**valid_settings)\\n449:                       return Settings(experiments)\\n450: \\n451:               except Exception:\\n452:                   log.exception(\\\"Failed to parse settings\\\")\\n453: \\n454:               return Settings()\\n455: \\n456: \\n457:           def parse_settings(rollout_state: str) -> Settings:\\n458:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n459:               Parse settings, if any, from the rollout state.\\n460: \\n461:               If the issue body contains \\\"---\\\" then the text above that is the settings\\n462:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\\n463: \\n464:               If it doesn't contain \\\"---\\\" then the settings are empty and the default values are used.\\n465:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n466:               settings_text, _ = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\\n467:               return parse_settings_from_text(settings_text)\\n468: \\n469: \\n470:           def parse_users(rollout_state: str) -> UserOptins:\\n471:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n472:               Parse users from the rollout state.\\n473: \\n474:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n475:               _, users_text = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\\n476:               return parse_user_opt_in_from_text(users_text)\\n477: \\n478: \\n479:           def is_user_opted_in(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n480:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n481:               Check if a user is opted into an experiment\\n482:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n483:               return experiment_name in user_optins.get(user, [])\\n484: \\n485: \\n486:           def is_user_opted_out(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n487:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n488:               Check if a user explicitly opted out of an experiment\\n489:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n490:               # if the experiment is prefixed with a \\\"-\\\", then it's an opt-out\\n491:               experiment_optout = \\\"-\\\" + experiment_name\\n492:               if experiment_optout not in user_optins.get(user, []):\\n493:                   return False\\n494: \\n495:               if is_user_opted_in(user, user_optins, experiment_name):\\n496:                   log.warning(\\n497:                       f\\\"User {user} is opted into experiment {experiment_name}, but also opted out of it. Defaulting to opting out\\\"\\n498:                   )\\n499: \\n500:               return True\\n501: \\n502: \\n503:           def get_runner_prefix(\\n504:               rollout_state: str,\\n505:               workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],\\n506:               branch: str,\\n507:               eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\\n508:               opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\\n509:               is_canary: bool = False,\\n510:           ) -> str:\\n511:               settings = parse_settings(rollout_state)\\n512:               user_optins = parse_users(rollout_state)\\n513: \\n514:               fleet_prefix = \\\"\\\"\\n515:               prefixes = []\\n516:               for experiment_name, experiment_settings in settings.experiments.items():\\n517:                   if not experiment_settings.all_branches and is_exception_branch(branch):\\n518:                       log.info(\\n519:                           f\\\"Branch {branch} is an exception branch. Not enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n520:                       )\\n521:                       continue\\n522: \\n523:                   if opt_out_experiments:\\n524:                       if experiment_name in opt_out_experiments:\\n525:                           opt_out_exp_list = \\\", \\\".join(opt_out_experiments)\\n526:                           log.info(\\n527:                               f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as this workflow has opted-out (opted out experiments are: {opt_out_exp_list})\\\"\\n528:                           )\\n529:                           continue\\n530: \\n531:                   if eligible_experiments:\\n532:                       if experiment_name not in eligible_experiments:\\n533:                           exp_list = \\\", \\\".join(eligible_experiments)\\n534:                           log.info(\\n535:                               f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not in the eligible_experiments list: {exp_list}\\\"\\n536:                           )\\n537:                           continue\\n538:                   elif not experiment_settings.default:\\n539:                       log.info(\\n540:                           f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not a default experiment\\\"\\n541:                       )\\n542:                       continue\\n543: \\n544:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted out to this experiment?\\n545:                   opted_out_users = [\\n546:                       requestor\\n547:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\\n548:                       if is_user_opted_out(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\\n549:                   ]\\n550: \\n551:                   if opted_out_users:\\n552:                       log.info(\\n553:                           f\\\"{', '.join(opted_out_users)} have opted out of experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n554:                       )\\n555:                       continue\\n556: \\n557:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted in to this experiment?\\n558:                   opted_in_users = [\\n559:                       requestor\\n560:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\\n561:                       if is_user_opted_in(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\\n562:                   ]\\n563: \\n564:                   enabled = False\\n565:                   if opted_in_users:\\n566:                       log.info(\\n567:                           f\\\"{', '.join(opted_in_users)} have opted into experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n568:                       )\\n569:                       enabled = True\\n570: \\n571:                   elif experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\\n572:                       # If no user is opted in, then we randomly enable the experiment based on the rollout percentage\\n573:                       if random.uniform(0, 100) <= experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\\n574:                           log.info(\\n575:                               f\\\"Based on rollout percentage of {experiment_settings.rollout_perc}%, enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n576:                           )\\n577:                           enabled = True\\n578: \\n579:                   if enabled:\\n580:                       label = experiment_name\\n581:                       if experiment_name == LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT:\\n582:                           # We give some special treatment to the \\\"lf\\\" experiment since determines the fleet we use\\n583:                           #  - If it's enabled, then we always list it's prefix first\\n584:                           #  - If we're in the canary branch, then we append \\\".c\\\" to the lf prefix\\n585:                           if is_canary:\\n586:                               label += CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX\\n587:                           fleet_prefix = label\\n588:                       else:\\n589:                           prefixes.append(label)\\n590: \\n591:               if len(prefixes) > 1:\\n592:                   log.error(\\n593:                       f\\\"Only a fleet and one other experiment can be enabled for a job at any time. Enabling {prefixes[0]} and ignoring the rest, which are {', '.join(prefixes[1:])}\\\"\\n594:                   )\\n595:                   prefixes = prefixes[:1]\\n596: \\n597:               # Fleet always comes first\\n598:               if fleet_prefix:\\n599:                   prefixes.insert(0, fleet_prefix)\\n600: \\n601:               return \\\".\\\".join(prefixes) + \\\".\\\" if prefixes else \\\"\\\"\\n602: \\n603: \\n604:           def get_rollout_state_from_issue(github_token: str, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> str:\\n605:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n606:               Gets the first comment of the issue, which contains the desired rollout state.\\n607: \\n608:               The default issue we use - https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\\n609:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n610:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\\n611:               issue = get_issue(gh, repo, issue_num)\\n612:               return str(issue.get_comments()[0].body.strip(\\\"\\\\n\\\\t \\\"))\\n613: \\n614: \\n615:           def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:\\n616:               for _ in range(num_retries):\\n617:                   try:\\n618:                       req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)\\n619:                       content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n620:                       return json.loads(content)\\n621:                   except Exception as e:\\n622:                       log.warning(f\\\"Could not download {url}: {e}\\\")\\n623: \\n624:               log.warning(f\\\"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed\\\")\\n625:               return {}\\n626: \\n627: \\n628:           @cache\\n629:           def get_pr_info(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n630:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n631:               Dynamically get PR information\\n632:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n633:               github_api = f\\\"https://api.github.com/repos/{github_repo}\\\"\\n634:               headers = {\\n635:                   \\\"Accept\\\": \\\"application/vnd.github.v3+json\\\",\\n636:                   \\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"token {github_token}\\\",\\n637:               }\\n638:               json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(\\n639:                   url=f\\\"{github_api}/issues/{pr_number}\\\",\\n640:                   headers=headers,\\n641:               )\\n642: \\n643:               if not json_response:\\n644:                   log.warning(f\\\"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}\\\")\\n645:                   return {}\\n646: \\n647:               return json_response\\n648: \\n649: \\n650:           def get_labels(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> set[str]:\\n651:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n652:               Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request\\n653:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n654:               pr_info = get_pr_info(github_repo, github_token, pr_number)\\n655:               return {\\n656:                   label.get(\\\"name\\\") for label in pr_info.get(\\\"labels\\\", []) if label.get(\\\"name\\\")\\n657:               }\\n658: \\n659: \\n660:           def main() -> None:\\n661:               args = parse_args()\\n662: \\n663:               runner_label_prefix = DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX\\n664: \\n665:               # Check if the PR is opt-out\\n666:               if args.pr_number:\\n667:                   labels = get_labels(args.github_repo, args.github_token, int(args.pr_number))\\n668:                   if OPT_OUT_LABEL in labels:\\n669:                       log.info(\\n670:                           f\\\"Opt-out runner determinator because #{args.pr_number} has {OPT_OUT_LABEL} label\\\"\\n671:                       )\\n672:                       set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\\n673:                       sys.exit()\\n674: \\n675:               try:\\n676:                   rollout_state = get_rollout_state_from_issue(\\n677:                       args.github_token, args.github_issue_repo, args.github_issue\\n678:                   )\\n679: \\n680:                   username = get_potential_pr_author(\\n681:                       args.github_token,\\n682:                       args.github_repo,\\n683:                       args.github_actor,\\n684:                       args.github_ref_type,\\n685:                       args.github_branch,\\n686:                   )\\n687: \\n688:                   is_canary = args.github_repo == \\\"pytorch/pytorch-canary\\\"\\n689: \\n690:                   runner_label_prefix = get_runner_prefix(\\n691:                       rollout_state,\\n692:                       (args.github_issue_owner, username),\\n693:                       args.github_branch,\\n694:                       args.eligible_experiments,\\n695:                       args.opt_out_experiments,\\n696:                       is_canary,\\n697:                   )\\n698: \\n699:               except Exception as e:\\n700:                   log.error(\\n701:                       f\\\"Failed to get issue. Defaulting to Meta runners and no experiments. Exception: {e}\\\"\\n702:                   )\\n703: \\n704:               set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\\n705: \\n706: \\n707:           if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n708:               main()\\n709: \\n710:           EOF\\n711: \\n712:           cat runner_determinator.py\\n713: \\n714:       - name: Install dependencies\\n715:         run: python3 -m pip install urllib3==1.26.18 PyGithub==2.3.0\\n716: \\n717:       - name: Get the workflow type for the current user\\n718:         id: set-condition\\n719:         run: |\\n720:           curr_branch=\\\"${{ inputs.curr_branch }}\\\"\\n721:           curr_ref_type=\\\"${{ inputs.curr_ref_type }}\\\"\\n722:           echo \\\"Current branch is '$curr_branch'\\\"\\n723: \\n724:           python3 runner_determinator.py \\\\\\n725:             --github-token \\\"$GITHUB_TOKEN\\\" \\\\\\n726:             --github-issue \\\"$ISSUE_NUMBER\\\" \\\\\\n727:             --github-branch \\\"$curr_branch\\\" \\\\\\n728:             --github-actor \\\"$TRIGGERING_ACTOR\\\" \\\\\\n729:             --github-issue-owner \\\"$ISSUE_OWNER\\\" \\\\\\n730:             --github-ref-type \\\"$curr_ref_type\\\" \\\\\\n731:             --github-repo \\\"$GITHUB_REPOSITORY\\\" \\\\\\n732:             --eligible-experiments \\\"$CHECK_EXPERIMENTS\\\" \\\\\\n733:             --opt-out-experiments \\\"$OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS\\\" \\\\\\n734:             --pr-number \\\"${PR_NUMBER}\\\"\\n735: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners\n2: \n3: on:\n4:   workflow_call:\n5:     inputs:\n6:       check_experiments:\n7:         required: false\n8:         type: string\n9:         description: |\n10:           List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.\n11:       opt_out_experiments:\n12:         required: false\n13:         type: string\n14:         description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.\n15:       triggering_actor:\n16:         required: true\n17:         type: string\n18:         description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor\n19:       issue_owner:\n20:         required: true\n21:         type: string\n22:         description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login\n23:       curr_branch:\n24:         required: true\n25:         type: string\n26:         description: Current branch or tag.\n27:       curr_ref_type:\n28:         required: false\n29:         type: string\n30:         default: branch\n31:         description: The value of \"github.ref_type\", \"branch\" or \"tag\"\n32:       issue_number:\n33:         required: false\n34:         type: string\n35:         default: \"5132\"\n36:         description: |\n37:           Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra\n38:           Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\n39: \n40:     outputs:\n41:       label-type:\n42:         description: Type of runners to use\n43:         value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}\n44: \n45: jobs:\n46:   runner-determinator:\n47:     # Don't run on forked repos\n48:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n49:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n50:     outputs:\n51:       label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}\n52:     env:\n53:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n54:       ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}\n55:       TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}\n56:       ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}\n57:       CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}\n58:       OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}\n59:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n60:     steps:\n61:       # - name: Checkout PyTorch\n62:       #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n63:       #   with:\n64:       #     fetch-depth: 1\n65:       #     submodules: true\n66: \n67:       # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below\n68:       # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners\n69:       # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py\n70:       - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script\n71:         id: hardcode-script\n72:         run: |\n73:           cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py\n74:           # flake8: noqa: G004\n75: \n76:           # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml\n77:           #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:\n78:           #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py\n79: \n80:           \"\"\"\n81:           This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a\n82:           GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default\n83:           https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration\n84:           of which runners should be used to run which job.\n85: \n86:           The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,\n87:           separated by a line containing \"---\".  If the line is not present, the\n88:           settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user\n89:           list, defined.\n90: \n91:           The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be\n92:           used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.\n93:           It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.\n94: \n95:           The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.\n96:           The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or\n97:           experiments which the user could be opted in to.\n98: \n99:           The user list has the following rules:\n100: \n101:           - Users are GitHub usernames, which must start with the @ prefix\n102:           - Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable\n103:           - A \"#\" prefix opts the user out of all experiments\n104: \n105:           Example config:\n106:               # A list of experiments that can be opted into.\n107:               # This defines the behavior they'll induce when opted into.\n108:               # Expected syntax is:\n109:               #   [experiment_name]: # Name of the experiment. Also used for the label prefix.\n110:               #      rollout_perc: [int] # % of workflows to run with this experiment when users are not opted in.\n111: \n112:               experiments:\n113:                 lf:\n114:                   rollout_percent: 25\n115:                   all_branches: false\n116:                   default: true\n117:               ---\n118: \n119:               # Opt-ins:\n120:               # Users can opt into the LF fleet by adding their GitHub username to this list\n121:               # and specifying experiments to enable in a comma-separated list.\n122:               # To always opt out of an experiment, prefix it with a \"-\".\n123:               # Experiments should be from the above list.\n124: \n125:               @User1,-lf,split_build\n126:               @User2,lf\n127:               @User3,split_build\n128:           \"\"\"\n129: \n130:           import json\n131:           import logging\n132:           import os\n133:           import random\n134:           import re\n135:           import sys\n136:           from argparse import ArgumentParser\n137:           from collections.abc import Iterable\n138:           from functools import cache\n139:           from logging import LogRecord\n140:           from typing import Any, NamedTuple\n141:           from urllib.request import Request, urlopen\n142: \n143:           import yaml\n144:           from github import Auth, Github\n145:           from github.Issue import Issue\n146: \n147: \n148:           DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX = \"\"  # use meta runners\n149:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF = \"lf.\"  # use runners from the linux foundation\n150:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF_CANARY = \"lf.c.\"  # use canary runners from the linux foundation\n151: \n152:           GITHUB_OUTPUT = os.getenv(\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\", \"\")\n153:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_AMI = \"runner-ami\"\n154:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE = \"label-type\"\n155:           OPT_OUT_LABEL = \"no-runner-experiments\"\n156: \n157:           SETTING_EXPERIMENTS = \"experiments\"\n158: \n159:           LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT = \"lf\"\n160:           CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX = \".c\"\n161: \n162: \n163:           class Experiment(NamedTuple):\n164:               rollout_perc: float = (\n165:                   0  # Percentage of workflows to experiment on when user is not opted-in.\n166:               )\n167:               all_branches: bool = (\n168:                   False  # If True, the experiment is also enabled on the exception branches\n169:               )\n170:               default: bool = (\n171:                   True  # If True, the experiment is enabled by default for all queries\n172:               )\n173: \n174:               # Add more fields as needed\n175: \n176: \n177:           class Settings(NamedTuple):\n178:               \"\"\"\n179:               Settings for the experiments that can be opted into.\n180:               \"\"\"\n181: \n182:               experiments: dict[str, Experiment] = {}\n183: \n184: \n185:           class ColorFormatter(logging.Formatter):\n186:               \"\"\"Color codes the log messages based on the log level\"\"\"\n187: \n188:               COLORS = {\n189:                   \"WARNING\": \"\\033[33m\",  # Yellow\n190:                   \"ERROR\": \"\\033[31m\",  # Red\n191:                   \"CRITICAL\": \"\\033[31m\",  # Red\n192:                   \"INFO\": \"\\033[0m\",  # Reset\n193:                   \"DEBUG\": \"\\033[0m\",  # Reset\n194:               }\n195: \n196:               def format(self, record: LogRecord) -> str:\n197:                   log_color = self.COLORS.get(record.levelname, \"\\033[0m\")  # Default to reset\n198:                   record.msg = f\"{log_color}{record.msg}\\033[0m\"\n199:                   return super().format(record)\n200: \n201: \n202:           handler = logging.StreamHandler()\n203:           handler.setFormatter(ColorFormatter(fmt=\"%(levelname)-8s: %(message)s\"))\n204: \n205:           log = logging.getLogger(os.path.basename(__file__))\n206:           log.addHandler(handler)\n207:           log.setLevel(logging.INFO)\n208: \n209: \n210:           def set_github_output(key: str, value: str) -> None:\n211:               \"\"\"\n212:               Defines outputs of the github action that invokes this script\n213:               \"\"\"\n214:               if not GITHUB_OUTPUT:\n215:                   # See https://github.blog/changelog/2022-10-11-github-actions-deprecating-save-state-and-set-output-commands/ for deprecation notice\n216:                   log.warning(\n217:                       \"No env var found for GITHUB_OUTPUT, you must be running this code locally. Falling back to the deprecated print method.\"\n218:                   )\n219:                   print(f\"::set-output name={key}::{value}\")\n220:                   return\n221: \n222:               with open(GITHUB_OUTPUT, \"a\") as f:\n223:                   log.info(f\"Setting output: {key}='{value}'\")\n224:                   f.write(f\"{key}={value}\\n\")\n225: \n226: \n227:           def _str_comma_separated_to_set(value: str) -> frozenset[str]:\n228:               return frozenset(\n229:                   filter(lambda itm: itm != \"\", map(str.strip, value.strip(\" \\n\\t\").split(\",\")))\n230:               )\n231: \n232: \n233:           def parse_args() -> Any:\n234:               parser = ArgumentParser(\"Get dynamic rollout settings\")\n235:               parser.add_argument(\"--github-token\", type=str, required=True, help=\"GitHub token\")\n236:               parser.add_argument(\n237:                   \"--github-issue-repo\",\n238:                   type=str,\n239:                   required=False,\n240:                   default=\"pytorch/test-infra\",\n241:                   help=\"GitHub repo to get the issue\",\n242:               )\n243:               parser.add_argument(\n244:                   \"--github-repo\",\n245:                   type=str,\n246:                   required=True,\n247:                   help=\"GitHub repo where CI is running\",\n248:               )\n249:               parser.add_argument(\n250:                   \"--github-issue\", type=int, required=True, help=\"GitHub issue number\"\n251:               )\n252:               parser.add_argument(\n253:                   \"--github-actor\", type=str, required=True, help=\"GitHub triggering_actor\"\n254:               )\n255:               parser.add_argument(\n256:                   \"--github-issue-owner\", type=str, required=True, help=\"GitHub issue owner\"\n257:               )\n258:               parser.add_argument(\n259:                   \"--github-branch\", type=str, required=True, help=\"Current GitHub branch or tag\"\n260:               )\n261:               parser.add_argument(\n262:                   \"--github-ref-type\",\n263:                   type=str,\n264:                   required=True,\n265:                   help=\"Current GitHub ref type, branch or tag\",\n266:               )\n267:               parser.add_argument(\n268:                   \"--eligible-experiments\",\n269:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\n270:                   required=False,\n271:                   default=\"\",\n272:                   help=\"comma separated list of experiments to check, if omitted all experiments marked with default=True are checked\",\n273:               )\n274:               parser.add_argument(\n275:                   \"--opt-out-experiments\",\n276:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\n277:                   required=False,\n278:                   default=\"\",\n279:                   help=(\n280:                       \"comma separated list of experiments to opt-out of. If unset, no opt-outs will occur. \"\n281:                       \"If the same experiment is listed both here and in '--eligible-experiments' opt-out will take priority.\"\n282:                   ),\n283:               )\n284:               parser.add_argument(\n285:                   \"--pr-number\",\n286:                   type=str,\n287:                   required=False,\n288:                   default=\"\",\n289:                   help=\"the optional PR number where this is run\",\n290:               )\n291: \n292:               return parser.parse_args()\n293: \n294: \n295:           def get_gh_client(github_token: str) -> Github:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\n296:               auth = Auth.Token(github_token)\n297:               return Github(auth=auth)\n298: \n299: \n300:           def get_issue(gh: Github, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> Issue:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\n301:               repo = gh.get_repo(repo)\n302:               return repo.get_issue(number=issue_num)\n303: \n304: \n305:           def get_potential_pr_author(\n306:               github_token: str, repo: str, username: str, ref_type: str, ref_name: str\n307:           ) -> str:\n308:               # If the trigger was a new tag added by a bot, this is a ciflow case\n309:               # Fetch the actual username from the original PR. The PR number is\n310:               # embedded in the tag name: ciflow/<name>/<pr-number>\n311: \n312:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\n313: \n314:               if username == \"pytorch-bot[bot]\" and ref_type == \"tag\":\n315:                   split_tag = ref_name.split(\"/\")\n316:                   if (\n317:                       len(split_tag) == 3\n318:                       and split_tag[0] == \"ciflow\"\n319:                       and split_tag[2].isnumeric()\n320:                   ):\n321:                       pr_number = split_tag[2]\n322:                       try:\n323:                           repository = gh.get_repo(repo)\n324:                           pull = repository.get_pull(number=int(pr_number))\n325:                       except Exception as e:\n326:                           raise Exception(  # noqa: TRY002\n327:                               f\"issue with pull request {pr_number} from repo {repository}\"\n328:                           ) from e\n329:                       return pull.user.login  # type: ignore[no-any-return]\n330:               # In all other cases, return the original input username\n331:               return username\n332: \n333: \n334:           def is_exception_branch(branch: str) -> bool:\n335:               \"\"\"\n336:               Branches that get opted out of experiments by default, until they're explicitly enabled.\n337:               \"\"\"\n338:               return branch.split(\"/\")[0] in {\"main\", \"nightly\", \"release\", \"landchecks\"}\n339: \n340: \n341:           def load_yaml(yaml_text: str) -> Any:\n342:               try:\n343:                   data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)\n344:                   return data\n345:               except yaml.YAMLError:\n346:                   log.exception(\"Error loading YAML\")\n347:                   raise\n348: \n349: \n350:           def extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\n351:               \"\"\"\n352:               Extracts the text with settings, if any, and the opted in users from the rollout state.\n353: \n354:               If the issue body contains \"---\" then the text above that is the settings\n355:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\n356: \n357:               If it doesn't contain \"---\" then the settings are empty and the rest is the users.\n358:               \"\"\"\n359:               rollout_state_parts = rollout_state.split(\"---\")\n360:               if len(rollout_state_parts) >= 2:\n361:                   return rollout_state_parts[0], rollout_state_parts[1]\n362:               else:\n363:                   return \"\", rollout_state\n364: \n365: \n366:           class UserOptins(dict[str, list[str]]):\n367:               \"\"\"\n368:               Dictionary of users with a list of features they have opted into\n369:               \"\"\"\n370: \n371: \n372:           def parse_user_opt_in_from_text(user_optin_text: str) -> UserOptins:\n373:               \"\"\"\n374:               Parse the user opt-in text into a key value pair of username and the list of features they have opted into\n375: \n376:               Users are GitHub usernames with the @ prefix. Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable.\n377:                   - Example line: \"@User1,lf,split_build\"\n378:                   - A \"#\" prefix indicates the user is opted out of all experiments\n379: \n380: \n381:               \"\"\"\n382:               optins = UserOptins()\n383:               for user in user_optin_text.split(\"\\n\"):\n384:                   user = user.strip(\"\\r\\n\\t -\")\n385:                   if not user or not user.startswith(\"@\"):\n386:                       # Not a valid user. Skip\n387:                       continue\n388: \n389:                   if user:\n390:                       usr_name = user.split(\",\")[0].strip(\"@\")\n391:                       optins[usr_name] = [exp.strip(\" \") for exp in user.split(\",\")[1:]]\n392: \n393:               return optins\n394: \n395: \n396:           def is_valid_experiment_name(experiment_name: str) -> bool:\n397:               \"\"\"\n398:               Check if the experiment name is valid.\n399:               A valid name:\n400:                   - Contains only alphanumeric characters and the special characters \"_\" & \"-\"\n401:                   - The special characters \"_\" & \"-\" shouldn't be the first or last characters\n402:                   - Cannot contain spaces\n403:               \"\"\"\n404: \n405:               valid_char_regex = r\"^[a-zA-Z0-9]([\\w-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$\"\n406:               valid = bool(re.match(valid_char_regex, experiment_name))\n407: \n408:               if valid:\n409:                   return True\n410: \n411:               log.error(\n412:                   f\"Invalid experiment name: {experiment_name}. Experiment names should only contain alphanumeric characters, '_', and '-'. They cannot contain spaces, and the special characters '_' and '-' cannot be the first or last characters.\"\n413:               )\n414:               return False\n415: \n416: \n417:           def parse_settings_from_text(settings_text: str) -> Settings:\n418:               \"\"\"\n419:               Parse the experiments from the issue body into a list of ExperimentSettings\n420:               \"\"\"\n421:               try:\n422:                   if settings_text:\n423:                       # Escape the backtick as well so that we can have the settings in a code block on the GH issue\n424:                       # for easy reading\n425:                       # Note: Using ascii for the backtick so that the cat step in _runner-determinator.yml doesn't choke on\n426:                       #       the backtick character in shell commands.\n427:                       backtick = chr(96)  # backtick character\n428:                       settings_text = settings_text.strip(f\"\\r\\n\\t{backtick} \")\n429:                       settings = load_yaml(settings_text)\n430: \n431:                       # For now we just load experiments. We can expand this if/when we add more settings\n432:                       experiments = {}\n433: \n434:                       for exp_name, exp_settings in settings.get(SETTING_EXPERIMENTS).items():\n435:                           if not is_valid_experiment_name(exp_name):\n436:                               # Exclude invalid experiments from the list. We log an error, but don't raise an exception so that other experiments can still be processed.\n437:                               continue\n438: \n439:                           valid_settings = {}\n440:                           for setting in exp_settings:\n441:                               if setting not in Experiment._fields:\n442:                                   log.warning(\n443:                                       f\"Unexpected setting in experiment: {setting} = {exp_settings[setting]}\"\n444:                                   )\n445:                               else:\n446:                                   valid_settings[setting] = exp_settings[setting]\n447: \n448:                           experiments[exp_name] = Experiment(**valid_settings)\n449:                       return Settings(experiments)\n450: \n451:               except Exception:\n452:                   log.exception(\"Failed to parse settings\")\n453: \n454:               return Settings()\n455: \n456: \n457:           def parse_settings(rollout_state: str) -> Settings:\n458:               \"\"\"\n459:               Parse settings, if any, from the rollout state.\n460: \n461:               If the issue body contains \"---\" then the text above that is the settings\n462:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\n463: \n464:               If it doesn't contain \"---\" then the settings are empty and the default values are used.\n465:               \"\"\"\n466:               settings_text, _ = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\n467:               return parse_settings_from_text(settings_text)\n468: \n469: \n470:           def parse_users(rollout_state: str) -> UserOptins:\n471:               \"\"\"\n472:               Parse users from the rollout state.\n473: \n474:               \"\"\"\n475:               _, users_text = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\n476:               return parse_user_opt_in_from_text(users_text)\n477: \n478: \n479:           def is_user_opted_in(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\n480:               \"\"\"\n481:               Check if a user is opted into an experiment\n482:               \"\"\"\n483:               return experiment_name in user_optins.get(user, [])\n484: \n485: \n486:           def is_user_opted_out(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\n487:               \"\"\"\n488:               Check if a user explicitly opted out of an experiment\n489:               \"\"\"\n490:               # if the experiment is prefixed with a \"-\", then it's an opt-out\n491:               experiment_optout = \"-\" + experiment_name\n492:               if experiment_optout not in user_optins.get(user, []):\n493:                   return False\n494: \n495:               if is_user_opted_in(user, user_optins, experiment_name):\n496:                   log.warning(\n497:                       f\"User {user} is opted into experiment {experiment_name}, but also opted out of it. Defaulting to opting out\"\n498:                   )\n499: \n500:               return True\n501: \n502: \n503:           def get_runner_prefix(\n504:               rollout_state: str,\n505:               workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],\n506:               branch: str,\n507:               eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\n508:               opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\n509:               is_canary: bool = False,\n510:           ) -> str:\n511:               settings = parse_settings(rollout_state)\n512:               user_optins = parse_users(rollout_state)\n513: \n514:               fleet_prefix = \"\"\n515:               prefixes = []\n516:               for experiment_name, experiment_settings in settings.experiments.items():\n517:                   if not experiment_settings.all_branches and is_exception_branch(branch):\n518:                       log.info(\n519:                           f\"Branch {branch} is an exception branch. Not enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\"\n520:                       )\n521:                       continue\n522: \n523:                   if opt_out_experiments:\n524:                       if experiment_name in opt_out_experiments:\n525:                           opt_out_exp_list = \", \".join(opt_out_experiments)\n526:                           log.info(\n527:                               f\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as this workflow has opted-out (opted out experiments are: {opt_out_exp_list})\"\n528:                           )\n529:                           continue\n530: \n531:                   if eligible_experiments:\n532:                       if experiment_name not in eligible_experiments:\n533:                           exp_list = \", \".join(eligible_experiments)\n534:                           log.info(\n535:                               f\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not in the eligible_experiments list: {exp_list}\"\n536:                           )\n537:                           continue\n538:                   elif not experiment_settings.default:\n539:                       log.info(\n540:                           f\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not a default experiment\"\n541:                       )\n542:                       continue\n543: \n544:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted out to this experiment?\n545:                   opted_out_users = [\n546:                       requestor\n547:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\n548:                       if is_user_opted_out(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\n549:                   ]\n550: \n551:                   if opted_out_users:\n552:                       log.info(\n553:                           f\"{', '.join(opted_out_users)} have opted out of experiment {experiment_name}.\"\n554:                       )\n555:                       continue\n556: \n557:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted in to this experiment?\n558:                   opted_in_users = [\n559:                       requestor\n560:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\n561:                       if is_user_opted_in(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\n562:                   ]\n563: \n564:                   enabled = False\n565:                   if opted_in_users:\n566:                       log.info(\n567:                           f\"{', '.join(opted_in_users)} have opted into experiment {experiment_name}.\"\n568:                       )\n569:                       enabled = True\n570: \n571:                   elif experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\n572:                       # If no user is opted in, then we randomly enable the experiment based on the rollout percentage\n573:                       if random.uniform(0, 100) <= experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\n574:                           log.info(\n575:                               f\"Based on rollout percentage of {experiment_settings.rollout_perc}%, enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\"\n576:                           )\n577:                           enabled = True\n578: \n579:                   if enabled:\n580:                       label = experiment_name\n581:                       if experiment_name == LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT:\n582:                           # We give some special treatment to the \"lf\" experiment since determines the fleet we use\n583:                           #  - If it's enabled, then we always list it's prefix first\n584:                           #  - If we're in the canary branch, then we append \".c\" to the lf prefix\n585:                           if is_canary:\n586:                               label += CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX\n587:                           fleet_prefix = label\n588:                       else:\n589:                           prefixes.append(label)\n590: \n591:               if len(prefixes) > 1:\n592:                   log.error(\n593:                       f\"Only a fleet and one other experiment can be enabled for a job at any time. Enabling {prefixes[0]} and ignoring the rest, which are {', '.join(prefixes[1:])}\"\n594:                   )\n595:                   prefixes = prefixes[:1]\n596: \n597:               # Fleet always comes first\n598:               if fleet_prefix:\n599:                   prefixes.insert(0, fleet_prefix)\n600: \n601:               return \".\".join(prefixes) + \".\" if prefixes else \"\"\n602: \n603: \n604:           def get_rollout_state_from_issue(github_token: str, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> str:\n605:               \"\"\"\n606:               Gets the first comment of the issue, which contains the desired rollout state.\n607: \n608:               The default issue we use - https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\n609:               \"\"\"\n610:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\n611:               issue = get_issue(gh, repo, issue_num)\n612:               return str(issue.get_comments()[0].body.strip(\"\\n\\t \"))\n613: \n614: \n615:           def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:\n616:               for _ in range(num_retries):\n617:                   try:\n618:                       req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)\n619:                       content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode(\"utf-8\")\n620:                       return json.loads(content)\n621:                   except Exception as e:\n622:                       log.warning(f\"Could not download {url}: {e}\")\n623: \n624:               log.warning(f\"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed\")\n625:               return {}\n626: \n627: \n628:           @cache\n629:           def get_pr_info(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:\n630:               \"\"\"\n631:               Dynamically get PR information\n632:               \"\"\"\n633:               github_api = f\"https://api.github.com/repos/{github_repo}\"\n634:               headers = {\n635:                   \"Accept\": \"application/vnd.github.v3+json\",\n636:                   \"Authorization\": f\"token {github_token}\",\n637:               }\n638:               json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(\n639:                   url=f\"{github_api}/issues/{pr_number}\",\n640:                   headers=headers,\n641:               )\n642: \n643:               if not json_response:\n644:                   log.warning(f\"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}\")\n645:                   return {}\n646: \n647:               return json_response\n648: \n649: \n650:           def get_labels(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> set[str]:\n651:               \"\"\"\n652:               Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request\n653:               \"\"\"\n654:               pr_info = get_pr_info(github_repo, github_token, pr_number)\n655:               return {\n656:                   label.get(\"name\") for label in pr_info.get(\"labels\", []) if label.get(\"name\")\n657:               }\n658: \n659: \n660:           def main() -> None:\n661:               args = parse_args()\n662: \n663:               runner_label_prefix = DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX\n664: \n665:               # Check if the PR is opt-out\n666:               if args.pr_number:\n667:                   labels = get_labels(args.github_repo, args.github_token, int(args.pr_number))\n668:                   if OPT_OUT_LABEL in labels:\n669:                       log.info(\n670:                           f\"Opt-out runner determinator because #{args.pr_number} has {OPT_OUT_LABEL} label\"\n671:                       )\n672:                       set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\n673:                       sys.exit()\n674: \n675:               try:\n676:                   rollout_state = get_rollout_state_from_issue(\n677:                       args.github_token, args.github_issue_repo, args.github_issue\n678:                   )\n679: \n680:                   username = get_potential_pr_author(\n681:                       args.github_token,\n682:                       args.github_repo,\n683:                       args.github_actor,\n684:                       args.github_ref_type,\n685:                       args.github_branch,\n686:                   )\n687: \n688:                   is_canary = args.github_repo == \"pytorch/pytorch-canary\"\n689: \n690:                   runner_label_prefix = get_runner_prefix(\n691:                       rollout_state,\n692:                       (args.github_issue_owner, username),\n693:                       args.github_branch,\n694:                       args.eligible_experiments,\n695:                       args.opt_out_experiments,\n696:                       is_canary,\n697:                   )\n698: \n699:               except Exception as e:\n700:                   log.error(\n701:                       f\"Failed to get issue. Defaulting to Meta runners and no experiments. Exception: {e}\"\n702:                   )\n703: \n704:               set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\n705: \n706: \n707:           if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n708:               main()\n709: \n710:           EOF\n711: \n712:           cat runner_determinator.py\n713: \n714:       - name: Install dependencies\n715:         run: python3 -m pip install urllib3==1.26.18 PyGithub==2.3.0\n716: \n717:       - name: Get the workflow type for the current user\n718:         id: set-condition\n719:         run: |\n720:           curr_branch=\"${{ inputs.curr_branch }}\"\n721:           curr_ref_type=\"${{ inputs.curr_ref_type }}\"\n722:           echo \"Current branch is '$curr_branch'\"\n723: \n724:           python3 runner_determinator.py \\\n725:             --github-token \"$GITHUB_TOKEN\" \\\n726:             --github-issue \"$ISSUE_NUMBER\" \\\n727:             --github-branch \"$curr_branch\" \\\n728:             --github-actor \"$TRIGGERING_ACTOR\" \\\n729:             --github-issue-owner \"$ISSUE_OWNER\" \\\n730:             --github-ref-type \"$curr_ref_type\" \\\n731:             --github-repo \"$GITHUB_REPOSITORY\" \\\n732:             --eligible-experiments \"$CHECK_EXPERIMENTS\" \\\n733:             --opt-out-experiments \"$OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS\" \\\n734:             --pr-number \"${PR_NUMBER}\"\n735: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml"
  }
}

landchecks/*\n12:     tags:\n13:       - ciflow/pull/*\n14:   workflow_dispatch:\n15:   schedule:\n16:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\n17: \n18: concurrency:\n19:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n20:   cancel-in-progress: true\n21: \n22: permissions:\n23:   id-token: write\n24:   contents: read\n25: \n26: jobs:\n27:   llm-td:\n28:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n29:     name: before-test\n30:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n31:     permissions:\n32:       id-token: write\n33:       contents: read\n34: \n35:   target-determination:\n36:     name: before-test\n37:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n38:     needs: llm-td\n39:     permissions:\n40:       id-token: write\n41:       contents: read\n42: \n43:   get-label-type:\n44:     name: get-label-type\n45:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n46:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && {
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\nimport re\n\n# Read the diff output\nwith open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/b6ec2735-e7f2-4093-acb1-67b55f7ecbd4/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1772417294614.txt', 'r') as f:\n    content = f.read()\n\ntry:\n    data = json.loads(content)\n    print(f\"Total files modified: {data['totalModified']}\")\n    print(f\"Total added lines: {data['totalAdded']}\")\n    print(f\"Total deleted lines: {data['totalDeleted']}\")\n    print(f\"\\nFiles changed ({len(data['files'])}):\")\n    \n    # Group files by type\n    workflow_files = []\n    other_files = []\n    \n    for f in data['files']:\n        path = f['path']\n        if '.github/workflows/' in path:\n            workflow_files.append(path)\n        else:\n            other_files.append(path)\n    \n    print(f\"\\nWorkflow files: {len(workflow_files)}\")\n    print(f\"Other files: {len(other_files)}\")\n    \n    print(\"\\nOther files:\")\n    for f in other_files:\n        print(f\"  {f}\")\n    \n    print(\"\\nFirst 20 workflow files:\")\n    for f in workflow_files[:20]:\n        print(f\"  {f}\")\n        \nexcept json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n    print(f\"Error: {e}\")\n    print(f\"Content length: {len(content)}\")\n    print(f\"First 500 chars: {content[:500]}\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}
