18. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nwith open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/b6ec2735-e7f2-4093-acb1-67b55f7ecbd4/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1772417294614.txt', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.loads(f.read())\n\n# Track all replacements needed\nreplacements = defaultdict(set)\n\nfor f_data in data['files']:\n    path = f_data['path']\n    \n    for hunk in f_data['hunks']:\n        body = hunk['body']\n        \n        # Find lines that are removed (start with -)\n        for line in body.split('\\n'):\n            if line.startswith('-') and not line.startswith('---'):\n                removed_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '-'\n                # Extract the change pattern\n                if '@main' in removed_line:\n                    replacements[f_data['path']].add(('remove_old', removed_line.strip()))\n            elif line.startswith('+') and not line.startswith('+++'):\n                added_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '+'\n                if '@release/2.10' in added_line:\n                    replacements[f_data['path']].add(('add_new', added_line.strip()))\n\n# Show summary\nprint(\"=== Summary of Changes ===\\n\")\n\n# Find the most common replacements\nfrom collections import Counter\n\nremoval_patterns = Counter()\naddition_patterns = Counter()\n\nfor path, changes in replacements.items():\n    for change_type, line_text in changes:\n        if change_type == 'remove_old':\n            removal_patterns[line_text] += 1\n        else:\n            addition_patterns[line_text] += 1\n\nprint(\"\\nMost common removals (top 10):\")\nfor pattern, count in removal_patterns.most_common(10):\n    print(f\"  [{count}x] {pattern[:80]}\")\n\nprint(\"\\nMost common additions (top 10):\")\nfor pattern, count in addition_patterns.most_common(10):\n    print(f\"  [{count}x] {pattern[:80]}\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}
Output
"Error: Exit code 1\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"<stdin>\", line 14, in <module>\nKeyError: 'hunks'\n\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"<stdin>\", line 14, in <module>\nKeyError: 'hunks'"
19. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom collections import Counter\n\nwith open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/b6ec2735-e7f2-4093-acb1-67b55f7ecbd4/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1772417294614.txt', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.loads(f.read())\n\n# Track all replacements needed\nremoval_patterns = Counter()\naddition_patterns = Counter()\nfile_changes = defaultdict(list)\n\ndef defaultdict(type_):\n    from collections import defaultdict as dd\n    return dd(type_)\n\nfile_changes = {}\n\nfor f_data in data['files']:\n    path = f_data['path']\n    \n    if 'hunks' not in f_data:\n        print(f\"SKIP {path}: no hunks field\")\n        continue\n        \n    for hunk in f_data['hunks']:\n        if 'body' not in hunk:\n            continue\n            \n        body = hunk['body']\n        \n        # Find lines that are removed (start with -)\n        for line in body.split('\\n'):\n            if line.startswith('-') and not line.startswith('---'):\n                removed_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '-'\n                if '@' in removed_line or 'ref:' in removed_line:\n                    removal_patterns[removed_line.strip()] += 1\n                    if path not in file_changes:\n                        file_changes[path] = []\n                    file_changes[path].append(('remove', removed_line.strip()))\n                    \n            elif line.startswith('+') and not line.startswith('+++'):\n                added_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '+'\n                if '@' in added_line:\n                    addition_patterns[added_line.strip()] += 1\n                    if path not in file_changes:\n                        file_changes[path] = []\n                    file_changes[path].append(('add', added_line.strip()))\n\nprint(\"=== Top Removals ===\")\nfor pattern, count in removal_patterns.most_common(15):\n    print(f\"[{count:2d}] {pattern[:100]}\")\n\nprint(\"\\n=== Top Additions ===\")\nfor pattern, count in addition_patterns.most_common(15):\n    print(f\"[{count:2d}] {pattern[:100]}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n=== Total files with changes: {len(file_changes)} ===\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}
Output

20. Bash @ -

{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\nfrom collections import Counter, defaultdict\n\nwith open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/b6ec2735-e7f2-4093-acb1-67b55f7ecbd4/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1772417294614.txt', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.loads(f.read())\n\n# Track all replacements needed\nremoval_patterns = Counter()\naddition_patterns = Counter()\nfile_changes = defaultdict(list)\n\nfor f_data in data['files']:\n    path = f_data['path']\n    \n    if 'hunks' not in f_data:\n        continue\n        \n    for hunk in f_data['hunks']:\n        if 'body' not in hunk:\n            continue\n            \n        body = hunk['body']\n        \n        # Find lines that are removed (start with -)\n        for line in body.split('\\n'):\n            if line.startswith('-') and not line.startswith('---'):\n                removed_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '-'\n                if '@' in removed_line or 'ref:' in removed_line:\n                    removal_patterns[removed_line.strip()] += 1\n                    file_changes[path].append(('remove', removed_line.strip()))\n                    \n            elif line.startswith('+') and not line.startswith('+++'):\n                added_line = line[1:]  # Strip the '+'\n                if '@' in added_line:\n                    addition_patterns[added_line.strip()] += 1\n                    file_changes[path].append(('add', added_line.strip()))\n\nprint(\"=== Top Removals ===\")\nfor pattern, count in removal_patterns.most_common(15):\n    print(f\"[{count:2d}] {pattern[:100]}\")\n\nprint(\"\\n=== Top Additions ===\")\nfor pattern, count in addition_patterns.most_common(15):\n    print(f\"[{count:2d}] {pattern[:100]}\")\n\nprint(f\"\\n=== Files with ref: changes ===\")\nfor path, changes in list(file_changes.items())[:5]:\n    has_ref = any('ref:' in ch[1] for ch in changes)\n    if has_ref:\n        print(f\"{path}\")\n        for op, line in changes:\n            if 'ref:' in line:\n                print(f\"  {op}: {line[:80]}\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}

  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\n\nwith open('/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/b6ec2735-e7f2-4093-acb1-67b55f7ecbd4/tool-results/mcp-sourcegraph-sg_compare_revisions-1772417294614.txt', 'r') as f:\n    data = json.loads(f.read())\n\n# Get all files that need to be changed\nfiles_to_update = [f_data['path'] for f_data in data['files']]\n\n# Save this to a file for processing\nwith open('/tmp/files_to_update.txt', 'w') as f:\n    for file_path in sorted(files_to_update):\n        f.write(file_path + '\\n')\n\nprint(f\"Total files to update: {len(files_to_update)}\")\nfor path in sorted(files_to_update):\n    print(f\"  {path}\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}

Input
{
  "description": "Process 100 PyTorch release workflow files in parallel",
  "prompt": "You need to process 100 files from the PyTorch 2.10 release branch update. The files are listed in /tmp/files_to_update.txt.\n\nFor EACH file, you must:\n1. Use sg_read_file to get content from: github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78 (repo parameter)\n2. Apply these transformations:\n   - Replace ALL instances of `@main` with `@release/2.10` in lines that contain `uses:`\n   - Remove the entire line: `ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}`\n   - Update branch references from 'main' to 'r2.10' in specific contexts\n   - Pin disabled/unstable jobs URLs for release 2.10\n3. Write the modified content to /workspace/<file_path>\n\nKey changes from the pattern:\n- All `uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/...@main` becomes `@release/2.10`\n- All `uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/...@main` becomes `@release/2.10`\n- Remove git ref lines for pull_request head.sha\n- Update xla clone: add `-b r2.10` to git clone\n- Update xla.txt commit hash to just `r2.10`\n- Pin S3 URLs with version IDs in filter_test_configs.py\n\nProcess ALL files from /tmp/files_to_update.txt. Make the directory structure in /workspace as needed. Read each file, apply transformations, and write back.\n\nStart by reading /tmp/files_to_update.txt to get the list, then process each file in the list.",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}

24. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".ci/pytorch/common_utils.sh",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: #!/bin/bash\\n2: \\n3: # Common util **functions** that can be sourced in other scripts.\\n4: \\n5: # note: printf is used instead of echo to avoid backslash\\n6: # processing and to properly handle values that begin with a '-'.\\n7: \\n8: log() { printf '%s\\\\n' \\\"$*\\\"; }\\n9: error() { log \\\"ERROR: $*\\\" >&2; }\\n10: fatal() { error \\\"$@\\\"; exit 1; }\\n11: \\n12: retry () {\\n13:     \\\"$@\\\" || (sleep 10 && \\\"$@\\\") || (sleep 20 && \\\"$@\\\") || (sleep 40 && \\\"$@\\\")\\n14: }\\n15: \\n16: # compositional trap taken from https://stackoverflow.com/a/7287873/23845\\n17: # appends a command to a trap\\n18: #\\n19: # - 1st arg:  code to add\\n20: # - remaining args:  names of traps to modify\\n21: #\\n22: trap_add() {\\n23:     trap_add_cmd=$1; shift || fatal \\\"${FUNCNAME[0]} usage error\\\"\\n24:     for trap_add_name in \\\"$@\\\"; do\\n25:         trap -- \\\"$(\\n26:             # helper fn to get existing trap command from output\\n27:             # of trap -p\\n28:             extract_trap_cmd() { printf '%s\\\\n' \\\"$3\\\"; }\\n29:             # print existing trap command with newline\\n30:             eval \\\"extract_trap_cmd $(trap -p \\\"${trap_add_name}\\\")\\\"\\n31:             # print the new trap command\\n32:             printf '%s\\\\n' \\\"${trap_add_cmd}\\\"\\n33:         )\\\" \\\"${trap_add_name}\\\" \\\\\\n34:             || fatal \\\"unable to add to trap ${trap_add_name}\\\"\\n35:     done\\n36: }\\n37: # set the trace attribute for the above function.  this is\\n38: # required to modify DEBUG or RETURN traps because functions don't\\n39: # inherit them unless the trace attribute is set\\n40: declare -f -t trap_add\\n41: \\n42: function assert_git_not_dirty() {\\n43:     # TODO: we should add an option to `build_amd.py` that reverts the repo to\\n44:     #       an unmodified state.\\n45:     if [[ \\\"$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\\\" != *rocm* ]] && [[ \\\"$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\\\" != *xla* ]] ; then\\n46:         git_status=$(git status --porcelain | grep -v '?? third_party' || true)\\n47:         if [[ $git_status ]]; then\\n48:             echo \\\"Build left local git repository checkout dirty\\\"\\n49:             echo \\\"git status --porcelain:\\\"\\n50:             echo \\\"${git_status}\\\"\\n51:             exit 1\\n52:         fi\\n53:     fi\\n54: }\\n55: \\n56: function pip_install_whl() {\\n57:   # This is used to install PyTorch and other build artifacts wheel locally\\n58:   # without using any network connection\\n59: \\n60:   # Convert the input arguments into an array\\n61:   local args=(\\\"$@\\\")\\n62: \\n63:   # Check if the first argument contains multiple paths separated by spaces\\n64:   if [[ \\\"${args[0]}\\\" == *\\\" \\\"* ]]; then\\n65:     # Split the string by spaces into an array\\n66:     IFS=' ' read -r -a paths <<< \\\"${args[0]}\\\"\\n67:     # Loop through each path and install individually\\n68:     for path in \\\"${paths[@]}\\\"; do\\n69:       echo \\\"Installing $path\\\"\\n70:       python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps \\\"$path\\\"\\n71:     done\\n72:   else\\n73:     # Loop through each argument and install individually\\n74:     for path in \\\"${args[@]}\\\"; do\\n75:       echo \\\"Installing $path\\\"\\n76:       python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps \\\"$path\\\"\\n77:     done\\n78:   fi\\n79: }\\n80: \\n81: function pip_build_and_install() {\\n82:   local build_target=$1\\n83:   local wheel_dir=$2\\n84: \\n85:   local found_whl=0\\n86:   for file in \\\"${wheel_dir}\\\"/*.whl\\n87:   do\\n88:     if [[ -f \\\"${file}\\\" ]]; then\\n89:       found_whl=1\\n90:       break\\n91:     fi\\n92:   done\\n93: \\n94:   # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist\\n95:   if [ \\\"${found_whl}\\\" == \\\"0\\\" ]; then\\n96:     python3 -m pip wheel \\\\\\n97:       --no-build-isolation \\\\\\n98:       --no-deps \\\\\\n99:       -w \\\"${wheel_dir}\\\" \\\\\\n100:       \\\"${build_target}\\\"\\n101:   fi\\n102: \\n103:   for file in \\\"${wheel_dir}\\\"/*.whl\\n104:   do\\n105:     pip_install_whl \\\"${file}\\\"\\n106:   done\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: function pip_install() {\\n110:   # retry 3 times\\n111:   pip_install_pkg=\\\"python3 -m pip install --progress-bar off\\\"\\n112:   ${pip_install_pkg} \\\"$@\\\" || \\\\\\n113:     ${pip_install_pkg} \\\"$@\\\" || \\\\\\n114:     ${pip_install_pkg} \\\"$@\\\"\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: function pip_uninstall() {\\n118:   # uninstall 2 times\\n119:   pip3 uninstall -y \\\"$@\\\" || pip3 uninstall -y \\\"$@\\\"\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: function get_exit_code() {\\n123:   set +e\\n124:   \\\"$@\\\"\\n125:   retcode=$?\\n126:   set -e\\n127:   return $retcode\\n128: }\\n129: \\n130: function get_bazel() {\\n131:   # Download and use the cross-platform, dependency-free Python\\n132:   # version of Bazelisk to fetch the platform specific version of\\n133:   # Bazel to use from .bazelversion.\\n134:   retry curl --location --output tools/bazel \\\\\\n135:     https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bazelbuild/bazelisk/v1.23.0/bazelisk.py\\n136:   shasum --algorithm=1 --check \\\\\\n137:     <(echo '01df9cf7f08dd80d83979ed0d0666a99349ae93c  tools/bazel')\\n138:   chmod u+x tools/bazel\\n139: }\\n140: \\n141: function install_monkeytype {\\n142:   # Install MonkeyType\\n143:   pip_install MonkeyType\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: \\n147: function get_pinned_commit() {\\n148:   cat .github/ci_commit_pins/\\\"${1}\\\".txt\\n149: }\\n150: \\n151: function detect_cuda_arch() {\\n152:   if [[ \\\"${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}\\\" == *cuda* ]]; then\\n153:     if command -v nvidia-smi; then\\n154:       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=$(nvidia-smi --query-gpu=compute_cap --format=csv | tail -n 1)\\n155:     elif [[ \\\"${TEST_CONFIG}\\\" == *nogpu* ]]; then\\n156:       # There won't be nvidia-smi in nogpu tests, so just set TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST to the default\\n157:       # minimum supported value here\\n158:       TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=8.0\\n159:     fi\\n160:     export TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST\\n161:   fi\\n162: }\\n163: \\n164: function install_torchaudio() {\\n165:   local commit\\n166:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit audio)\\n167:   pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/audio.git@${commit}\\\" dist/audio\\n168: }\\n169: \\n170: function install_torchtext() {\\n171:   local data_commit\\n172:   local text_commit\\n173:   data_commit=$(get_pinned_commit data)\\n174:   text_commit=$(get_pinned_commit text)\\n175:   pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/data.git@${data_commit}\\\" dist/data\\n176:   pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/text.git@${text_commit}\\\" dist/text\\n177: }\\n178: \\n179: function install_torchvision() {\\n180:   local orig_preload\\n181:   local commit\\n182:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit vision)\\n183:   orig_preload=${LD_PRELOAD}\\n184:   if [ -n \\\"${LD_PRELOAD}\\\" ]; then\\n185:     # Silence dlerror to work-around glibc ASAN bug, see https://sourceware.org/bugzilla/show_bug.cgi?id=27653#c9\\n186:     echo 'char* dlerror(void) { return \\\"\\\";}'|gcc -fpic -shared -o \\\"${HOME}/dlerror.so\\\" -x c -\\n187:     LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}:${HOME}/dlerror.so\\n188:   fi\\n189: \\n190:   if [[ \\\"${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}\\\" == *cuda* ]]; then\\n191:     # Not sure if both are needed, but why not\\n192:     export FORCE_CUDA=1\\n193:     export WITH_CUDA=1\\n194:   fi\\n195:   pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/vision.git@${commit}\\\" dist/vision\\n196: \\n197:   if [ -n \\\"${LD_PRELOAD}\\\" ]; then\\n198:     LD_PRELOAD=${orig_preload}\\n199:   fi\\n200: }\\n201: \\n202: function install_torchrec_and_fbgemm() {\\n203:   local torchrec_commit\\n204:   torchrec_commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchrec)\\n205:   local fbgemm_commit\\n206:   fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm)\\n207:   if [[ \\\"$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\\\" == *rocm* ]] ; then\\n208:     fbgemm_commit=$(get_pinned_commit fbgemm_rocm)\\n209:   fi\\n210:   pip_uninstall torchrec-nightly\\n211:   pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly\\n212:   pip_install setuptools-git-versioning scikit-build pyre-extensions\\n213: \\n214:   if [[ \\\"$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\\\" == *rocm* ]] ; then\\n215:     # install torchrec first because it installs fbgemm nightly on top of rocm fbgemm\\n216:     pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}\\\" dist/torchrec\\n217:     pip_uninstall fbgemm-gpu-nightly\\n218: \\n219:     # Set ROCM_HOME isn't available, use ROCM_PATH if set or /opt/rocm\\n220:     ROCM_HOME=\\\"${ROCM_HOME:-${ROCM_PATH:-/opt/rocm}}\\\"\\n221: \\n222:     # Find rocm_version.h header file for ROCm version extract\\n223:     rocm_version_h=\\\"${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm-core/rocm_version.h\\\"\\n224:     if [ ! -f \\\"$rocm_version_h\\\" ]; then\\n225:         rocm_version_h=\\\"${ROCM_HOME}/include/rocm_version.h\\\"\\n226:     fi\\n227: \\n228:     # Error out if rocm_version.h not found\\n229:     if [ ! -f \\\"$rocm_version_h\\\" ]; then\\n230:         echo \\\"Error: rocm_version.h not found in expected locations.\\\" >&2\\n231:         exit 1\\n232:     fi\\n233: \\n234:     # Extract major, minor and patch ROCm version numbers\\n235:     MAJOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MAJOR' \\\"$rocm_version_h\\\" | awk '{print $3}')\\n236:     MINOR_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_MINOR' \\\"$rocm_version_h\\\" | awk '{print $3}')\\n237:     PATCH_VERSION=$(grep 'ROCM_VERSION_PATCH' \\\"$rocm_version_h\\\" | awk '{print $3}')\\n238:     ROCM_INT=$((MAJOR_VERSION * 10000 + MINOR_VERSION * 100 + PATCH_VERSION))\\n239:     echo \\\"ROCm version: $ROCM_INT\\\"\\n240:     export BUILD_ROCM_VERSION=\\\"$MAJOR_VERSION.$MINOR_VERSION\\\"\\n241: \\n242:     pip_install tabulate  # needed for newer fbgemm\\n243:     pip_install patchelf  # needed for rocm fbgemm\\n244: \\n245:     local wheel_dir=dist/fbgemm_gpu\\n246:     local found_whl=0\\n247:     for file in \\\"${wheel_dir}\\\"/*.whl\\n248:     do\\n249:       if [[ -f \\\"${file}\\\" ]]; then\\n250:         found_whl=1\\n251:         break\\n252:       fi\\n253:     done\\n254: \\n255:     # Build the wheel if it doesn't exist\\n256:     if [ \\\"${found_whl}\\\" == \\\"0\\\" ]; then\\n257:       git clone --recursive https://github.com/pytorch/fbgemm\\n258:       pushd fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu\\n259:       git checkout \\\"${fbgemm_commit}\\\" --recurse-submodules\\n260:       # until the fbgemm_commit includes the tbb patch\\n261:       patch <<'EOF'\\n262: --- a/FbgemmGpu.cmake\\n263: +++ b/FbgemmGpu.cmake\\n264: @@ -184,5 +184,6 @@ gpu_cpp_library(\\n265:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_cache\\n266:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_optimizers\\n267:      fbgemm_gpu_tbe_utils\\n268: +    tbb\\n269:    DESTINATION\\n270:      fbgemm_gpu)\\n271: EOF\\n272:       python setup.py bdist_wheel --build-variant=rocm\\n273:       popd\\n274: \\n275:       # Save the wheel before cleaning up\\n276:       mkdir -p dist/fbgemm_gpu\\n277:       cp fbgemm/fbgemm_gpu/dist/*.whl dist/fbgemm_gpu\\n278:     fi\\n279: \\n280:     for file in \\\"${wheel_dir}\\\"/*.whl\\n281:     do\\n282:       pip_install_whl \\\"${file}\\\"\\n283:     done\\n284: \\n285:     rm -rf fbgemm\\n286:   else\\n287:     pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/torchrec.git@${torchrec_commit}\\\" dist/torchrec\\n288:     # Skip fbgemm for CUDA 13 as it's not compatible yet\\n289:     if [[ \\\"$BUILD_ENVIRONMENT\\\" != *cuda13* ]]; then\\n290:       pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/FBGEMM.git@${fbgemm_commit}#subdirectory=fbgemm_gpu\\\" dist/fbgemm_gpu\\n291:     fi\\n292:   fi\\n293: }\\n294: \\n295: function clone_pytorch_xla() {\\n296:   if [[ ! -d ./xla ]]; then\\n297:     git clone --recursive -b r2.10 https://github.com/pytorch/xla.git\\n298:     pushd xla\\n299:     # pin the xla hash so that we don't get broken by changes to xla\\n300:     git checkout \\\"$(cat ../.github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt)\\\"\\n301:     git submodule sync\\n302:     git submodule update --init --recursive\\n303:     popd\\n304:   fi\\n305: }\\n306: \\n307: function install_torchao() {\\n308:   local commit\\n309:   commit=$(get_pinned_commit torchao)\\n310:   pip_build_and_install \\\"git+https://github.com/pytorch/ao.git@${commit}\\\" dist/ao\\n311: }\\n312: \\n313: function install_flash_attn_cute() {\\n314:   echo \\\"Installing FlashAttention CuTe from GitHub...\\\"\\n315:   # Grab latest main til we have a pinned commit\\n316:   local flash_attn_commit\\n317:   flash_attn_commit=$(git ls-remote https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git HEAD | cut -f1)\\n318: \\n319:   # Clone the repo to a temporary directory\\n320:   rm -rf flash-attention-build\\n321:   git clone --depth 1 --recursive https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention.git flash-attention-build\\n322: \\n323:   pushd flash-attention-build\\n324:   git checkout \\\"${flash_attn_commit}\\\"\\n325: \\n326:   # Install only the 'cute' sub-directory\\n327:   pip_install -e flash_attn/cute/\\n328:   popd\\n329: \\n330:   # remove the local repo\\n331:   rm -rf flash-attention-build\\n332:   echo \\\"FlashAttention CuTe installation complete.\\\"\\n333: }\\n334: \\n335: function print_sccache_stats() {\\n336:   echo 'PyTorch Build Statistics'\\n337:   sccache --show-stats\\n338: \\n339:   if [[ -n \\\"${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}\\\" ]]; then\\n340:     sccache --show-stats --stats-format json | jq .stats \\\\\\n341:       > \\\"sccache-stats-${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT}-${OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID}.json\\\"\\n342:   else\\n343:     echo \\\"env var OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID not set, will not write sccache stats to json\\\"\\n344:   fi\\n345: }\\n346: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.ci/pytorch/common_utils.sh\"}"

Input
{
  "path": ".github/scripts/filter_test_configs.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: #!/usr/bin/env python3\\n2: # ruff: noqa: LOG015\\n3: \\n4: import json\\n5: import logging\\n6: import os\\n7: import re\\n8: import subprocess\\n9: import sys\\n10: import warnings\\n11: from collections.abc import Callable\\n12: from enum import Enum\\n13: from functools import cache\\n14: from logging import info\\n15: from typing import Any, Optional\\n16: from urllib.request import Request, urlopen\\n17: \\n18: import yaml\\n19: \\n20: \\n21: REENABLE_TEST_REGEX = \\\"(?i)(Close(d|s)?|Resolve(d|s)?|Fix(ed|es)?) (#|https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/)([0-9]+)\\\"\\n22: MAIN_BRANCH = \\\"main\\\"\\n23: \\n24: PREFIX = \\\"test-config/\\\"\\n25: \\n26: logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)\\n27: \\n28: \\n29: def is_cuda_or_rocm_job(job_name: Optional[str]) -> bool:\\n30:     if not job_name:\\n31:         return False\\n32: \\n33:     return \\\"cuda\\\" in job_name or \\\"rocm\\\" in job_name\\n34: \\n35: \\n36: # Supported modes when running periodically. Only applying the mode when\\n37: # its lambda condition returns true\\n38: SUPPORTED_PERIODICAL_MODES: dict[str, Callable[[Optional[str]], bool]] = {\\n39:     # Memory leak check is only needed for CUDA and ROCm jobs which utilize GPU memory\\n40:     \\\"mem_leak_check\\\": is_cuda_or_rocm_job,\\n41:     \\\"rerun_disabled_tests\\\": lambda job_name: True,\\n42: }\\n43: \\n44: # The link to the published list of disabled jobs. Pinned for release 2.10\\n45: DISABLED_JOBS_URL = \\\"https://ossci-metrics.s3.amazonaws.com/disabled-jobs.json?versionId=7uzr7psTR98QQyLkjIjI93OHKaAlii.q\\\"\\n46: # and unstable jobs\\n47: UNSTABLE_JOBS_URL = \\\"https://ossci-metrics.s3.amazonaws.com/unstable-jobs.json?versionId=JkNCBYVBZUEzYfRfUzvJ_eZbvkL_pXFX\\\"\\n48: \\n49: # Some constants used to handle disabled and unstable jobs\\n50: JOB_NAME_SEP = \\\"/\\\"\\n51: BUILD_JOB_NAME = \\\"build\\\"\\n52: TEST_JOB_NAME = \\\"test\\\"\\n53: BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME = \\\"build-and-test\\\"\\n54: JOB_NAME_CFG_REGEX = re.compile(r\\\"(?P<job>[\\\\w-]+)\\\\s+\\\\((?P<cfg>[\\\\w-]+)\\\\)\\\")\\n55: EXCLUDED_BRANCHES = [\\\"nightly\\\"]\\n56: MEM_LEAK_LABEL = \\\"enable-mem-leak-check\\\"\\n57: \\n58: \\n59: class IssueType(Enum):\\n60:     DISABLED = \\\"disabled\\\"\\n61:     UNSTABLE = \\\"unstable\\\"\\n62: \\n63: \\n64: def parse_args() -> Any:\\n65:     from argparse import ArgumentParser\\n66: \\n67:     parser = ArgumentParser(\\n68:         \\\"Filter all test configurations and keep only requested ones\\\"\\n69:     )\\n70:     parser.add_argument(\\n71:         \\\"--test-matrix\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"the original test matrix\\\"\\n72:     )\\n73:     parser.add_argument(\\n74:         \\\"--selected-test-configs\\\",\\n75:         type=str,\\n76:         default=\\\"\\\",\\n77:         help=\\\"a comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep\\\",\\n78:     )\\n79:     parser.add_argument(\\n80:         \\\"--workflow\\\", type=str, help=\\\"the name of the current workflow, i.e. pull\\\"\\n81:     )\\n82:     parser.add_argument(\\n83:         \\\"--job-name\\\",\\n84:         type=str,\\n85:         help=\\\"the name of the current job, i.e. linux-jammy-py3.8-gcc7 / build\\\",\\n86:     )\\n87:     parser.add_argument(\\\"--pr-number\\\", type=str, help=\\\"the pull request number\\\")\\n88:     parser.add_argument(\\\"--tag\\\", type=str, help=\\\"the associated tag if it exists\\\")\\n89:     parser.add_argument(\\n90:         \\\"--event-name\\\",\\n91:         type=str,\\n92:         help=\\\"name of the event that triggered the job (pull, schedule, etc)\\\",\\n93:     )\\n94:     parser.add_argument(\\n95:         \\\"--schedule\\\",\\n96:         type=str,\\n97:         help=\\\"cron schedule that triggered the job\\\",\\n98:     )\\n99:     parser.add_argument(\\n100:         \\\"--branch\\\",\\n101:         type=str,\\n102:         default=MAIN_BRANCH,\\n103:         help=\\\"the branch name\\\",\\n104:     )\\n105:     return parser.parse_args()\\n106: \\n107: \\n108: @cache\\n109: def get_pr_info(pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n110:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n111:     Dynamically get PR information\\n112:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n113:     # From https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/environment-variables\\n114:     pytorch_repo = os.environ.get(\\\"GITHUB_REPOSITORY\\\", \\\"pytorch/pytorch\\\")\\n115:     pytorch_github_api = f\\\"https://api.github.com/repos/{pytorch_repo}\\\"\\n116:     github_token = os.environ[\\\"GITHUB_TOKEN\\\"]\\n117: \\n118:     headers = {\\n119:         \\\"Accept\\\": \\\"application/vnd.github.v3+json\\\",\\n120:         \\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"token {github_token}\\\",\\n121:     }\\n122:     json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(\\n123:         url=f\\\"{pytorch_github_api}/issues/{pr_number}\\\",\\n124:         headers=headers,\\n125:     )\\n126: \\n127:     if not json_response:\\n128:         warnings.warn(f\\\"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}\\\")\\n129:         return {}\\n130: \\n131:     return json_response\\n132: \\n133: \\n134: def get_labels(pr_number: int) -> set[str]:\\n135:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n136:     Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request\\n137:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n138:     pr_info = get_pr_info(pr_number)\\n139:     return {\\n140:         label.get(\\\"name\\\") for label in pr_info.get(\\\"labels\\\", []) if label.get(\\\"name\\\")\\n141:     }\\n142: \\n143: \\n144: def filter_labels(labels: set[str], label_regex: Any) -> set[str]:\\n145:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n146:     Return the list of matching labels\\n147:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n148:     return {l for l in labels if re.match(label_regex, l)}\\n149: \\n150: \\n151: def filter(test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], labels: set[str]) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n152:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n153:     Select the list of test config to run from the test matrix. The logic works\\n154:     as follows:\\n155: \\n156:     If the PR has one or more test-config labels as specified, only these test configs\\n157:     will be selected.  This also works with ciflow labels, for example, if a PR has both\\n158:     ciflow/trunk and test-config/functorch, only trunk functorch builds and tests will\\n159:     be run.\\n160: \\n161:     If the PR has none of the test-config label, all tests are run as usual.\\n162:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n163:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {\\\"include\\\": []}\\n164: \\n165:     for entry in test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\", []):\\n166:         config_name = entry.get(\\\"config\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n167:         if not config_name:\\n168:             continue\\n169: \\n170:         label = f\\\"{PREFIX}{config_name.strip()}\\\"\\n171:         if label in labels:\\n172:             msg = f\\\"Select {config_name} because label {label} is present in the pull request by the time the test starts\\\"\\n173:             info(msg)\\n174:             filtered_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"].append(entry)\\n175: \\n176:     test_config_labels = filter_labels(labels, re.compile(f\\\"{PREFIX}.+\\\"))\\n177:     if not filtered_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"] and not test_config_labels:\\n178:         info(\\\"Found no test-config label on the PR, so all test configs are included\\\")\\n179:         # Found no test-config label and the filtered test matrix is empty, return the same\\n180:         # test matrix as before so that all tests can be run normally\\n181:         return test_matrix\\n182:     else:\\n183:         msg = f\\\"Found {test_config_labels} on the PR so only these test configs are run\\\"\\n184:         info(msg)\\n185:         # When the filter test matrix contain matches or if a valid test config label\\n186:         # is found in the PR, return the filtered test matrix\\n187:         return filtered_test_matrix\\n188: \\n189: \\n190: def filter_selected_test_configs(\\n191:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], selected_test_configs: set[str]\\n192: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n193:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:     Keep only the selected configs if the list if not empty. Otherwise, keep all test configs.\\n195:     This filter is used when the workflow is dispatched manually.\\n196:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n197:     if not selected_test_configs:\\n198:         return test_matrix\\n199: \\n200:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {\\\"include\\\": []}\\n201:     for entry in test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\", []):\\n202:         config_name = entry.get(\\\"config\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n203:         if not config_name:\\n204:             continue\\n205: \\n206:         if config_name in selected_test_configs:\\n207:             filtered_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"].append(entry)\\n208: \\n209:     return filtered_test_matrix\\n210: \\n211: \\n212: def set_periodic_modes(\\n213:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]], job_name: Optional[str]\\n214: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n215:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n216:     Apply all periodic modes when running under a schedule\\n217:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n218:     scheduled_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {\\n219:         \\\"include\\\": [],\\n220:     }\\n221: \\n222:     for config in test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\", []):\\n223:         for mode, cond in SUPPORTED_PERIODICAL_MODES.items():\\n224:             if not cond(job_name):\\n225:                 continue\\n226: \\n227:             cfg = config.copy()\\n228:             cfg[mode] = mode\\n229:             scheduled_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"].append(cfg)\\n230: \\n231:     return scheduled_test_matrix\\n232: \\n233: \\n234: def mark_unstable_jobs(\\n235:     workflow: str, job_name: str, test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]]\\n236: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n237:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n238:     Check the list of unstable jobs and mark them accordingly. Note that if a job\\n239:     is unstable, all its dependents will also be marked accordingly\\n240:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n241:     return process_jobs(\\n242:         workflow=workflow,\\n243:         job_name=job_name,\\n244:         test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n245:         issue_type=IssueType.UNSTABLE,\\n246:         url=UNSTABLE_JOBS_URL,\\n247:     )\\n248: \\n249: \\n250: def remove_disabled_jobs(\\n251:     workflow: str, job_name: str, test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]]\\n252: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n253:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n254:     Check the list of disabled jobs, remove the current job and all its dependents\\n255:     if it exists in the list\\n256:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n257:     return process_jobs(\\n258:         workflow=workflow,\\n259:         job_name=job_name,\\n260:         test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n261:         issue_type=IssueType.DISABLED,\\n262:         url=DISABLED_JOBS_URL,\\n263:     )\\n264: \\n265: \\n266: def _filter_jobs(\\n267:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],\\n268:     issue_type: IssueType,\\n269:     target_cfg: Optional[str] = None,\\n270: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n271:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n272:     An utility function used to actually apply the job filter\\n273:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n274:     # The result will be stored here\\n275:     filtered_test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]] = {\\\"include\\\": []}\\n276: \\n277:     # This is an issue to disable a CI job\\n278:     if issue_type == IssueType.DISABLED:\\n279:         # If there is a target config, disable (remove) only that\\n280:         if target_cfg:\\n281:             # Remove the target config from the test matrix\\n282:             filtered_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"] = [\\n283:                 r for r in test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"] if r.get(\\\"config\\\", \\\"\\\") != target_cfg\\n284:             ]\\n285: \\n286:         return filtered_test_matrix\\n287: \\n288:     if issue_type == IssueType.UNSTABLE:\\n289:         for r in test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"]:\\n290:             cpy = r.copy()\\n291: \\n292:             if (target_cfg and r.get(\\\"config\\\", \\\"\\\") == target_cfg) or not target_cfg:\\n293:                 # If there is a target config, only mark that as unstable, otherwise,\\n294:                 # mark everything as unstable\\n295:                 cpy[IssueType.UNSTABLE.value] = IssueType.UNSTABLE.value\\n296: \\n297:             filtered_test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"].append(cpy)\\n298: \\n299:         return filtered_test_matrix\\n300: \\n301:     # No matching issue, return everything\\n302:     return test_matrix\\n303: \\n304: \\n305: def process_jobs(\\n306:     workflow: str,\\n307:     job_name: str,\\n308:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],\\n309:     issue_type: IssueType,\\n310:     url: str,\\n311: ) -> dict[str, list[Any]]:\\n312:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n313:     Both disabled and unstable jobs are in the following format:\\n314: \\n315:     {\\n316:         \\\"WORKFLOW / PLATFORM / JOB (CONFIG)\\\": [\\n317:             AUTHOR,\\n318:             ISSUE_NUMBER,\\n319:             ISSUE_URL,\\n320:             WORKFLOW,\\n321:             PLATFORM,\\n322:             JOB (CONFIG),\\n323:         ],\\n324:         \\\"pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / test (dynamo)\\\": [\\n325:             \\\"pytorchbot\\\",\\n326:             \\\"94861\\\",\\n327:             \\\"https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/94861\\\",\\n328:             \\\"pull\\\",\\n329:             \\\"linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9\\\",\\n330:             \\\"test (dynamo)\\\",\\n331:         ],\\n332:     }\\n333:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n334:     try:\\n335:         # The job name from github is in the PLATFORM / JOB (CONFIG) format, so breaking\\n336:         # it into its two components first\\n337:         current_platform, _ = (n.strip() for n in job_name.split(JOB_NAME_SEP, 1) if n)\\n338:     except ValueError:\\n339:         warnings.warn(f\\\"Invalid job name {job_name}, returning\\\")\\n340:         return test_matrix\\n341: \\n342:     for record in download_json(url=url, headers={}).values():\\n343:         (\\n344:             author,\\n345:             _,\\n346:             target_url,\\n347:             target_workflow,\\n348:             target_platform,\\n349:             target_job_cfg,\\n350:         ) = record\\n351: \\n352:         if target_workflow != workflow:\\n353:             # The current workflow doesn't match this record\\n354:             continue\\n355: \\n356:         cleanup_regex = rf\\\"(-{BUILD_JOB_NAME}|-{TEST_JOB_NAME})$\\\"\\n357:         # There is an exception here for binary build workflows in which the platform\\n358:         # names have the build and test suffix. For example, we have a build job called\\n359:         # manywheel-py3-cuda11_8-build / build and its subsequent test job called\\n360:         # manywheel-py3-cuda11_8-test / test. So they are linked, but their suffixes\\n361:         # are different\\n362:         target_platform_no_suffix = re.sub(cleanup_regex, \\\"\\\", target_platform)\\n363:         current_platform_no_suffix = re.sub(cleanup_regex, \\\"\\\", current_platform)\\n364: \\n365:         if (\\n366:             target_platform != current_platform\\n367:             and target_platform_no_suffix != current_platform_no_suffix\\n368:         ):\\n369:             # The current platform doesn't match this record\\n370:             continue\\n371: \\n372:         # The logic after this is fairly complicated:\\n373:         #\\n374:         # - If the target record doesn't have the optional job (config) name,\\n375:         #   i.e. pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9, all build and test jobs will\\n376:         #   be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as unstable if it's\\n377:         #   an unstable record\\n378:         #\\n379:         # - If the target record has the job name and it's a build job, i.e.\\n380:         #   pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / build, all build and test jobs\\n381:         #   will be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as unstable if\\n382:         #   it's an unstable record, because the latter requires the former\\n383:         #\\n384:         # - If the target record has the job name and it's a test job without\\n385:         #   the config part, i.e. pull / linux-bionic-py3.8-clang9 / test, all\\n386:         #   test jobs will be skipped if it's a disabled record or marked as\\n387:         #   unstable if it's an unstable record\\n388:         #\\n389:         # - If the target record has the job (config) name, only that test config\\n390:         #   will be skipped or marked as unstable\\n391:         if not target_job_cfg:\\n392:             msg = (\\n393:                 f\\\"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} \\\"\\n394:                 + f\\\"all CI jobs for {workflow} / {job_name}\\\"\\n395:             )\\n396:             info(msg)\\n397:             return _filter_jobs(\\n398:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n399:                 issue_type=issue_type,\\n400:             )\\n401: \\n402:         if target_job_cfg == BUILD_JOB_NAME:\\n403:             msg = (\\n404:                 f\\\"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} \\\"\\n405:                 + f\\\"the build job for {workflow} / {job_name}\\\"\\n406:             )\\n407:             info(msg)\\n408:             return _filter_jobs(\\n409:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n410:                 issue_type=issue_type,\\n411:             )\\n412: \\n413:         if target_job_cfg in (TEST_JOB_NAME, BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME):\\n414:             msg = (\\n415:                 f\\\"Issue {target_url} created by {author} has {issue_type.value} \\\"\\n416:                 + f\\\"all the test jobs for {workflow} / {job_name}\\\"\\n417:             )\\n418:             info(msg)\\n419:             return _filter_jobs(\\n420:                 test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n421:                 issue_type=issue_type,\\n422:             )\\n423: \\n424:         m = JOB_NAME_CFG_REGEX.match(target_job_cfg)\\n425:         if m:\\n426:             target_job = m.group(\\\"job\\\")\\n427:             # Make sure that the job name is a valid test job name first before checking the config\\n428:             if target_job in (TEST_JOB_NAME, BUILD_AND_TEST_JOB_NAME):\\n429:                 target_cfg = m.group(\\\"cfg\\\")\\n430: \\n431:                 # NB: There can be multiple unstable configurations, i.e. inductor, inductor_huggingface\\n432:                 test_matrix = _filter_jobs(\\n433:                     test_matrix=test_matrix,\\n434:                     issue_type=issue_type,\\n435:                     target_cfg=target_cfg,\\n436:                 )\\n437:         else:\\n438:             warnings.warn(\\n439:                 f\\\"Found a matching {issue_type.value} issue {target_url} for {workflow} / {job_name}, \\\"\\n440:                 + f\\\"but the name {target_job_cfg} is invalid\\\"\\n441:             )\\n442: \\n443:     # Found no matching target, return the same input test matrix\\n444:     return test_matrix\\n445: \\n446: \\n447: def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:\\n448:     for _ in range(num_retries):\\n449:         try:\\n450:             req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)\\n451:             content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n452:             return json.loads(content)\\n453:         except Exception as e:\\n454:             warnings.warn(f\\\"Could not download {url}: {e}\\\")\\n455: \\n456:     warnings.warn(f\\\"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed\\\")\\n457:     return {}\\n458: \\n459: \\n460: def set_output(name: str, val: Any) -> None:\\n461:     print(f\\\"Setting output {name}={val}\\\")\\n462:     if os.getenv(\\\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"):\\n463:         with open(str(os.getenv(\\\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\")), \\\"a\\\") as env:\\n464:             print(f\\\"{name}={val}\\\", file=env)\\n465:     else:\\n466:         print(f\\\"::set-output name={name}::{val}\\\")\\n467: \\n468: \\n469: def parse_reenabled_issues(s: Optional[str]) -> list[str]:\\n470:     # NB: When the PR body is empty, GitHub API returns a None value, which is\\n471:     # passed into this function\\n472:     if not s:\\n473:         return []\\n474: \\n475:     # The regex is meant to match all *case-insensitive* keywords that\\n476:     # GitHub has delineated would link PRs to issues, more details here:\\n477:     # https://docs.github.com/en/issues/tracking-your-work-with-issues/linking-a-pull-request-to-an-issue.\\n478:     # E.g., \\\"Close #62851\\\", \\\"fixES #62851\\\" and \\\"RESOLVED #62851\\\" would all match, but not\\n479:     # \\\"closes  #62851\\\" --> extra space, \\\"fixing #62851\\\" --> not a keyword, nor \\\"fix 62851\\\" --> no #\\n480:     issue_numbers = [x[5] for x in re.findall(REENABLE_TEST_REGEX, s)]\\n481:     return issue_numbers\\n482: \\n483: \\n484: def get_reenabled_issues(pr_body: str = \\\"\\\") -> list[str]:\\n485:     default_branch = f\\\"origin/{os.environ.get('GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH', 'main')}\\\"\\n486:     try:\\n487:         commit_messages = subprocess.check_output(\\n488:             f\\\"git cherry -v {default_branch}\\\".split(\\\" \\\")\\n489:         ).decode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n490:     except Exception as e:\\n491:         warnings.warn(f\\\"failed to get commit messages: {e}\\\")\\n492:         commit_messages = \\\"\\\"\\n493:     return parse_reenabled_issues(pr_body) + parse_reenabled_issues(commit_messages)\\n494: \\n495: \\n496: def check_for_setting(labels: set[str], body: str, setting: str) -> bool:\\n497:     return setting in labels or f\\\"[{setting}]\\\" in body\\n498: \\n499: \\n500: def perform_misc_tasks(\\n501:     labels: set[str],\\n502:     test_matrix: dict[str, list[Any]],\\n503:     job_name: str,\\n504:     pr_body: str,\\n505:     branch: Optional[str] = None,\\n506:     tag: Optional[str] = None,\\n507: ) -> None:\\n508:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n509:     In addition to apply the filter logic, the script also does the following\\n510:     misc tasks to set keep-going and is-unstable variables\\n511:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n512:     set_output(\\n513:         \\\"keep-going\\\",\\n514:         branch == MAIN_BRANCH\\n515:         or bool(tag and re.match(r\\\"^trunk/[a-f0-9]{40}$\\\", tag))\\n516:         # Pattern for tags created via manual run on HUD\\n517:         or bool(tag and re.match(r\\\"^ciflow/[^/]+/[a-f0-9]{40}$\\\", tag))\\n518:         or check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"keep-going\\\"),\\n519:     )\\n520:     set_output(\\n521:         \\\"ci-verbose-test-logs\\\",\\n522:         check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"ci-verbose-test-logs\\\"),\\n523:     )\\n524:     set_output(\\n525:         \\\"ci-test-showlocals\\\", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"ci-test-showlocals\\\")\\n526:     )\\n527:     set_output(\\n528:         \\\"ci-no-test-timeout\\\", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"ci-no-test-timeout\\\")\\n529:     )\\n530:     set_output(\\\"ci-no-td\\\", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"ci-no-td\\\"))\\n531:     # Only relevant for the one linux distributed cuda job, delete this when TD\\n532:     # is rolled out completely\\n533:     set_output(\\n534:         \\\"ci-td-distributed\\\", check_for_setting(labels, pr_body, \\\"ci-td-distributed\\\")\\n535:     )\\n536: \\n537:     # Obviously, if the job name includes unstable, then this is an unstable job\\n538:     is_unstable = job_name and IssueType.UNSTABLE.value in job_name\\n539:     if not is_unstable and test_matrix and test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\"):\\n540:         # Even when the job name doesn't mention unstable, we will also mark it as\\n541:         # unstable when the test matrix only includes unstable jobs. Basically, this\\n542:         # logic allows build or build-and-test jobs to be marked as unstable too.\\n543:         #\\n544:         # Basically, when a build job is unstable, all the subsequent test jobs are\\n545:         # also unstable. And when all test jobs are unstable, we will also treat the\\n546:         # build job as unstable. It's simpler this way\\n547:         is_unstable = all(IssueType.UNSTABLE.value in r for r in test_matrix[\\\"include\\\"])\\n548: \\n549:     set_output(\\n550:         \\\"is-unstable\\\",\\n551:         is_unstable,\\n552:     )\\n553: \\n554:     set_output(\\\"reenabled-issues\\\", \\\",\\\".join(get_reenabled_issues(pr_body=pr_body)))\\n555: \\n556:     if MEM_LEAK_LABEL in labels:\\n557:         # Enable mem leak check if label is added\\n558:         for config in test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\", []):\\n559:             if is_cuda_or_rocm_job(job_name):\\n560:                 config[\\\"mem_leak_check\\\"] = \\\"mem_leak_check\\\"\\n561: \\n562: \\n563: def main() -> None:\\n564:     args = parse_args()\\n565:     # Load the original test matrix set by the workflow. Its format, however,\\n566:     # doesn't follow the strict JSON format, so we load it using yaml here for\\n567:     # its more relaxed syntax\\n568:     test_matrix = yaml.safe_load(args.test_matrix)\\n569: \\n570:     if test_matrix is None:\\n571:         warnings.warn(f\\\"Invalid test matrix input '{args.test_matrix}', exiting\\\")\\n572:         # We handle invalid test matrix gracefully by marking it as empty\\n573:         set_output(\\\"is-test-matrix-empty\\\", True)\\n574:         sys.exit(0)\\n575: \\n576:     pr_number = args.pr_number\\n577:     tag = args.tag\\n578: \\n579:     # If the tag matches, we can get the PR number from it, this is from ciflow\\n580:     # workflow dispatcher\\n581:     tag_regex = re.compile(r\\\"^ciflow/[\\\\w\\\\-]+/(?P<pr_number>\\\\d+)$\\\")\\n582: \\n583:     labels = set()\\n584:     if pr_number:\\n585:         # If a PR number is set, query all the labels from that PR\\n586:         labels = get_labels(int(pr_number))\\n587:         # Then filter the test matrix and keep only the selected ones\\n588:         filtered_test_matrix = filter(test_matrix, labels)\\n589: \\n590:     elif tag:\\n591:         m = tag_regex.match(tag)\\n592: \\n593:         if m:\\n594:             pr_number = m.group(\\\"pr_number\\\")\\n595: \\n596:             # The PR number can also come from the tag in ciflow tag event\\n597:             labels = get_labels(int(pr_number))\\n598:             # Filter the test matrix and keep only the selected ones\\n599:             filtered_test_matrix = filter(test_matrix, labels)\\n600: \\n601:         else:\\n602:             # There is a tag but it isn't ciflow, so there is nothing left to do\\n603:             filtered_test_matrix = test_matrix\\n604: \\n605:     else:\\n606:         # No PR number, no tag, we can just return the test matrix as it is\\n607:         filtered_test_matrix = test_matrix\\n608: \\n609:     if args.selected_test_configs:\\n610:         selected_test_configs = {\\n611:             v.strip().lower()\\n612:             for v in args.selected_test_configs.split(\\\",\\\")\\n613:             if v.strip()\\n614:         }\\n615:         filtered_test_matrix = filter_selected_test_configs(\\n616:             filtered_test_matrix, selected_test_configs\\n617:         )\\n618: \\n619:     if args.event_name == \\\"schedule\\\" and args.schedule == \\\"29 8 * * *\\\":\\n620:         # we don't want to run the mem leak check or disabled tests on normal\\n621:         # periodically scheduled jobs, only the ones at this time\\n622:         filtered_test_matrix = set_periodic_modes(filtered_test_matrix, args.job_name)\\n623: \\n624:     if args.workflow and args.job_name and args.branch not in EXCLUDED_BRANCHES:\\n625:         # If both workflow and job name are available, we will check if the current job\\n626:         # is disabled and remove it and all its dependants from the test matrix\\n627:         filtered_test_matrix = remove_disabled_jobs(\\n628:             args.workflow, args.job_name, filtered_test_matrix\\n629:         )\\n630: \\n631:         filtered_test_matrix = mark_unstable_jobs(\\n632:             args.workflow, args.job_name, filtered_test_matrix\\n633:         )\\n634: \\n635:     pr_body = get_pr_info(int(pr_number)).get(\\\"body\\\", \\\"\\\") if pr_number else \\\"\\\"\\n636: \\n637:     perform_misc_tasks(\\n638:         labels=labels,\\n639:         test_matrix=filtered_test_matrix,\\n640:         job_name=args.job_name,\\n641:         pr_body=pr_body if pr_body else \\\"\\\",\\n642:         branch=args.branch,\\n643:         tag=tag,\\n644:     )\\n645: \\n646:     # Set the filtered test matrix as the output\\n647:     set_output(\\\"test-matrix\\\", json.dumps(filtered_test_matrix))\\n648: \\n649:     filtered_test_matrix_len = len(filtered_test_matrix.get(\\\"include\\\", []))\\n650:     # and also put a flag if the test matrix is empty, so subsequent jobs can\\n651:     # quickly check it without the need to parse the JSON string\\n652:     set_output(\\\"is-test-matrix-empty\\\", filtered_test_matrix_len == 0)\\n653: \\n654: \\n655: if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n656:     main()\\n657: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/scripts/filter_test_configs.py\"}"

28. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-build\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       docker-image-name:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\\n14:       build-generates-artifacts:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: boolean\\n17:         default: true\\n18:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\\n19:       sync-tag:\\n20:         required: false\\n21:         type: string\\n22:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n23:         description: |\\n24:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n25:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n26:       cuda-arch-list:\\n27:         required: false\\n28:         type: string\\n29:         default: \\\"5.2\\\"\\n30:         description: |\\n31:           List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.\\n32:       runner_prefix:\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n35:         type: string\\n36:         description: Prefix for runner label\\n37:       runner:\\n38:         required: false\\n39:         type: string\\n40:         default: \\\"linux.c7i.2xlarge\\\"\\n41:         description: |\\n42:           Label of the runner this job should run on.\\n43:       test-matrix:\\n44:         required: false\\n45:         type: string\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\\n48:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\\n49:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\\n50:       selected-test-configs:\\n51:         description: |\\n52:           A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,\\n53:           The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults\\n54:         required: false\\n55:         type: string\\n56:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n57:       s3-bucket:\\n58:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n59:         required: false\\n60:         type: string\\n61:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n62:       aws-role-to-assume:\\n63:         description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n64:         required: false\\n65:         type: string\\n66:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n67:       disable-monitor:\\n68:         description: |\\n69:           Disable utilization monitoring for build job\\n70:         required: false\\n71:         type: boolean\\n72:         default: false\\n73:       monitor-log-interval:\\n74:         description: |\\n75:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n76:         required: false\\n77:         type: number\\n78:         default: 5\\n79:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n80:         description: |\\n81:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n82:         required: false\\n83:         type: number\\n84:         default: 1\\n85:       allow-reuse-old-whl:\\n86:         description: |\\n87:           If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a\\n88:           commit with no cpp changes from this commit\\n89:         required: false\\n90:         type: boolean\\n91:         default: true\\n92:       build-additional-packages:\\n93:         description: |\\n94:           If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their\\n95:           wheels as artifacts\\n96:         required: false\\n97:         type: string\\n98:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n99:       build-external-packages:\\n100:         description: |\\n101:           If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts\\n102:           use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.\\n103:         required: false\\n104:         type: string\\n105:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n106: \\n107:     secrets:\\n108:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n109:         required: false\\n110:         description: |\\n111:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n112:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\\n113:         required: false\\n114:         description: |\\n115:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\\n116: \\n117:     outputs:\\n118:       docker-image:\\n119:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n120:         description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.\\n121:       test-matrix:\\n122:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n123:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\\n124:       build-environment:\\n125:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n126:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n127: \\n128: jobs:\\n129:   build:\\n130:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\\n131:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n132:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n133:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}\\n134:     timeout-minutes: 480\\n135:     outputs:\\n136:       docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n137:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n138:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n139:     steps:\\n140:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n141:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n142:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n143:         with:\\n144:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n145:           instructions: |\\n146:             Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\\n147:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\\n148: \\n149:       # [pytorch repo ref]\\n150:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local\\n151:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local\\n152:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.\\n153:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n154:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n155:         with:\\n156:           no-sudo: true\\n157: \\n158:       - name: Setup Linux\\n159:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n160:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n161: \\n162:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n163:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n164:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n165:         with:\\n166:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\\n167:           role-session-name: gha-linux-build\\n168:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n169: \\n170:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n171:         id: get-job-id\\n172:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n173:         if: always()\\n174:         with:\\n175:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n176: \\n177:       - name: Check if can use old whl build\\n178:         id: use-old-whl\\n179:         uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl\\n180:         if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}\\n181:         with:\\n182:           build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n183:           run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n184:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n185:           job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n186:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n187: \\n188:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n189:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n191:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n192:         with:\\n193:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\\n194: \\n195:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n196:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n197:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n198:         env:\\n199:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n200:         shell: bash\\n201:         run: |\\n202:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n203:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n204: \\n205:       - name: Pull docker image\\n206:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n207:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n208:         with:\\n209:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n210: \\n211:       - name: Parse ref\\n212:         id: parse-ref\\n213:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n214: \\n215:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\\n216:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\\n217:         id: filter\\n218:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n219:         with:\\n220:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n221:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n222:           selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}\\n223:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n224: \\n225:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n226:         id: monitor-script\\n227:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n228:         shell: bash\\n229:         continue-on-error: true\\n230:         env:\\n231:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n232:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n233:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n234:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n235:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n236:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n237:         run: |\\n238:           mkdir -p ../../usage_logs\\n239:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\\n240:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \\\\\\n241:           --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" \\\\\\n242:           --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" \\\\\\n243:           > \\\"../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt\\\" 2>&1 &\\n244:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n245: \\n246:       - name: Download pytest cache\\n247:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\\n248:         continue-on-error: true\\n249:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n250:         with:\\n251:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n252:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n253:           s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n254: \\n255:       - name: Build\\n256:         if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n257:         id: build\\n258:         env:\\n259:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n260:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n261:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n262:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n263:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\\n264:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\\n265:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\\n266:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\\n267:           PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}\\n268:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\\n269:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n270:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\\n271:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\\n272:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n273:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n274:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\\n275:           BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}\\n276:           RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}\\n277:         run: |\\n278:           START_TIME=$(date +%s)\\n279:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n280:             JENKINS_USER=\\n281:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\\\"\\n282:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\\n283:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\\n284:             # when job is cancelled\\n285:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\\"sleep 12h\\\"\\n286: \\n287:             # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here\\n288:             env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n289:             env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n290:           else\\n291:             JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\\n292:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n293:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\n294:           fi\\n295: \\n296:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\\n297:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \\\"%.3f \\\\n\\\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\\n298:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\\n299:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\\n300:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\\n301: \\n302:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"riscv64\\\"* ]]; then\\n303:             # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation\\n304:             # Ensure binfmt_misc is available\\n305:             echo \\\"Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem\\\"\\n306:             sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true\\n307: \\n308:             echo \\\"QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static\\\"\\n309:             docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true\\n310: \\n311:             # Final verification\\n312:             echo \\\"Checking binfmt_misc status:\\\"\\n313:             ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo \\\"Cannot access binfmt_misc directory\\\"\\n314: \\n315:             if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then\\n316:               echo \\\"qemu-riscv64 registration successful\\\"\\n317:             else\\n318:               echo \\\"qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation\\\"\\n319:               echo \\\"This may cause RISC-V builds to fail\\\"\\n320:             fi\\n321: \\n322:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\\\"--privileged\\\"\\n323:           else\\n324:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\\n325:           fi\\n326: \\n327:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n328:           # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty\\n329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086\\n330:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n331:             ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \\\\\\n332:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n333:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n334:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n335:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n336:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n337:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n338:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n339:             -e XLA_CUDA \\\\\\n340:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\\\\n341:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\\\\n342:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\\\\n343:             -e PR_LABELS \\\\\\n344:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\\\\n345:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n346:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\\\\n347:             -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \\\\\\n348:             -e RUNNER \\\\\\n349:             --memory=\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\\\" \\\\\\n350:             --memory-swap=\\\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\\\" \\\\\\n351:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n352:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n353:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n354:             --tty \\\\\\n355:             --detach \\\\\\n356:             ${JENKINS_USER} \\\\\\n357:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n358:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n359:             \\\"${USED_IMAGE}\\\" \\\\\\n360:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\n361:           )\\n362: \\n363:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n364:             docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt\\\"\\n365:           fi\\n366: \\n367:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\\n368: \\n369:           END_TIME=$(date +%s)\\n370:           echo \\\"build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n371: \\n372:       - name: Build external packages\\n373:         id: build-external-packages\\n374:         if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n375:         uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages\\n376:         with:\\n377:           build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}\\n378:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n379:           cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\\n380:           output-dir: external\\n381: \\n382:       - name: Move external packages to dist\\n383:         if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'\\n384:         shell: bash\\n385:         run: |\\n386:           src=\\\"${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}\\\"\\n387:           if [ -d \\\"$src\\\" ]; then\\n388:             mkdir -p \\\"dist/$(dirname \\\"$src\\\")\\\"\\n389:             mv \\\"$src\\\" \\\"dist/$(dirname \\\"$src\\\")/\\\"\\n390:           fi\\n391: \\n392:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n393:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n394:         shell: bash\\n395:         continue-on-error: true\\n396:         env:\\n397:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n398:         run: |\\n399:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n400: \\n401:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip\\n402:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n403:         run: |\\n404:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files\\n405: \\n406:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3\\n407:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n408:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n409:         with:\\n410:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n411:           retention-days: 14\\n412:           if-no-files-found: error\\n413:           path: artifacts.zip\\n414:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n415: \\n416:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x\\n417:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n418:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n419:         with:\\n420:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n421:           retention-days: 14\\n422:           if-no-files-found: error\\n423:           path: artifacts.zip\\n424: \\n425:       - name: copy logs\\n426:         shell: bash\\n427:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\\n428:         continue-on-error: true\\n429:         run: |\\n430:           rm -f ./usage_logs\\n431:           mkdir -p ./usage_logs\\n432:           cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/\\n433: \\n434:       - name: Upload raw usage log to s3\\n435:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\\n436:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\\n437:         with:\\n438:           s3-prefix: |\\n439:             ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact\\n440:           retention-days: 14\\n441:           if-no-files-found: warn\\n442:           path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt\\n443: \\n444:       - name: Upload sccache stats\\n445:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n446:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats\\n447:         with:\\n448:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n449:           build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}\\n450: \\n451:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n452:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n453:         continue-on-error: true\\n454:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n455:         with:\\n456:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n457:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n458:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n459:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n460:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n461:           artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n462: \\n463:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n465:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n466: \\n467:       - name: Cleanup docker\\n468:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n469:         shell: bash\\n470:         run: |\\n471:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\\n472:           docker stop -a || true\\n473:           docker kill -a || true\\n474: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\"}"
29. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "mkdir -p .ci/pytorch .github/ci_commit_pins .github/scripts .github/templates .github/workflows && ls -la",
  "description": "Create directory structure for output files"
}
Output
"total 24\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 claude claude 4096 Mar  2 02:09 .\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 root   root   4096 Mar  2 02:07 ..\ndrwxr-xr-x 3 claude claude 4096 Mar  2 02:09 .ci\ndrwxr-xr-x 1 claude claude 4096 Mar  2 02:04 .git\ndrwxr-xr-x 6 claude claude 4096 Mar  2 02:09 .github"
30. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/process_files.py << 'EOF'\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nProcess PyTorch 2.10 release branch files.\nReads from Sourcegraph and applies transformations.\n\"\"\"\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport os\nimport sys\n\n# List of files to process\nfiles = [\n    \".ci/pytorch/common_utils.sh\",\n    \".github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt\",\n    \".github/scripts/filter_test_configs.py\",\n    \".github/templates/common.yml.j2\",\n    \".github/templates/linux_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\",\n    \".github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\",\n    \".github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\",\n    \".github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_docs.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_link_check.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_mac-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_mac-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_win-build.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_win-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-libtorch-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-manywheel-images-s390x.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-manywheel-images.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-triton-wheel.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/build-vllm-wheel.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/check-labels.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/close-nonexistent-disable-issues.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/create_release.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-builds.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-cache-rocm.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/docker-release.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/dynamo-unittest.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel-nightly.yml\",\n    \".github/workflows/generated-linux-binary-libtorch-nightly.yml\",\n
