50. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-build.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-build\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       docker-image-name:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\\n14:       build-generates-artifacts:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: boolean\\n17:         default: true\\n18:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\\n19:       sync-tag:\\n20:         required: false\\n21:         type: string\\n22:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n23:         description: |\\n24:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n25:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n26:       cuda-arch-list:\\n27:         required: false\\n28:         type: string\\n29:         default: \\\"5.2\\\"\\n30:         description: |\\n31:           List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.\\n32:       runner_prefix:\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n35:         type: string\\n36:         description: Prefix for runner label\\n37:       runner:\\n38:         required: false\\n39:         type: string\\n40:         default: \\\"linux.c7i.2xlarge\\\"\\n41:         description: |\\n42:           Label of the runner this job should run on.\\n43:       test-matrix:\\n44:         required: false\\n45:         type: string\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\\n48:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\\n49:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\\n50:       selected-test-configs:\\n51:         description: |\\n52:           A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,\\n53:           The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults\\n54:         required: false\\n55:         type: string\\n56:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n57:       s3-bucket:\\n58:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n59:         required: false\\n60:         type: string\\n61:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n62:       aws-role-to-assume:\\n63:         description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n64:         required: false\\n65:         type: string\\n66:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n67:       disable-monitor:\\n68:         description: |\\n69:           Disable utilization monitoring for build job\\n70:         required: false\\n71:         type: boolean\\n72:         default: false\\n73:       monitor-log-interval:\\n74:         description: |\\n75:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n76:         required: false\\n77:         type: number\\n78:         default: 5\\n79:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n80:         description: |\\n81:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n82:         required: false\\n83:         type: number\\n84:         default: 1\\n85:       allow-reuse-old-whl:\\n86:         description: |\\n87:           If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a\\n88:           commit with no cpp changes from this commit\\n89:         required: false\\n90:         type: boolean\\n91:         default: true\\n92:       build-additional-packages:\\n93:         description: |\\n94:           If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their\\n95:           wheels as artifacts\\n96:         required: false\\n97:         type: string\\n98:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n99:       build-external-packages:\\n100:         description: |\\n101:           If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts\\n102:           use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.\\n103:         required: false\\n104:         type: string\\n105:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n106: \\n107:     secrets:\\n108:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n109:         required: false\\n110:         description: |\\n111:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n112:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\\n113:         required: false\\n114:         description: |\\n115:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\\n116: \\n117:     outputs:\\n118:       docker-image:\\n119:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n120:         description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.\\n121:       test-matrix:\\n122:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n123:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\\n124:       build-environment:\\n125:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n126:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n127: \\n128: jobs:\\n129:   build:\\n130:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\\n131:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n132:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n133:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}\\n134:     timeout-minutes: 480\\n135:     outputs:\\n136:       docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n137:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n138:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n139:     steps:\\n140:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n141:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n142:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n143:         with:\\n144:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n145:           instructions: |\\n146:             Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\\n147:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\\n148: \\n149:       # [pytorch repo ref]\\n150:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local\\n151:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local\\n152:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.\\n153:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n154:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n155:         with:\\n156:           no-sudo: true\\n157: \\n158:       - name: Setup Linux\\n159:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n160:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n161: \\n162:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n163:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n164:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n165:         with:\\n166:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\\n167:           role-session-name: gha-linux-build\\n168:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n169: \\n170:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n171:         id: get-job-id\\n172:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n173:         if: always()\\n174:         with:\\n175:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n176: \\n177:       - name: Check if can use old whl build\\n178:         id: use-old-whl\\n179:         uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl\\n180:         if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}\\n181:         with:\\n182:           build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n183:           run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n184:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n185:           job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n186:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n187: \\n188:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n189:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n190:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n191:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n192:         with:\\n193:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\\n194: \\n195:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n196:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n197:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n198:         env:\\n199:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n200:         shell: bash\\n201:         run: |\\n202:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n203:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n204: \\n205:       - name: Pull docker image\\n206:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n207:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n208:         with:\\n209:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n210: \\n211:       - name: Parse ref\\n212:         id: parse-ref\\n213:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n214: \\n215:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\\n216:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\\n217:         id: filter\\n218:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n219:         with:\\n220:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n221:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n222:           selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}\\n223:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n224: \\n225:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n226:         id: monitor-script\\n227:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n228:         shell: bash\\n229:         continue-on-error: true\\n230:         env:\\n231:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n232:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n233:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n234:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n235:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n236:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n237:         run: |\\n238:           mkdir -p ../../usage_logs\\n239:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\\n240:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \\\\\\n241:           --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" \\\\\\n242:           --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" \\\\\\n243:           > \\\"../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt\\\" 2>&1 &\\n244:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n245: \\n246:       - name: Download pytest cache\\n247:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\\n248:         continue-on-error: true\\n249:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n250:         with:\\n251:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n252:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n253:           s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n254: \\n255:       - name: Build\\n256:         if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n257:         id: build\\n258:         env:\\n259:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n260:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n261:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n262:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n263:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\\n264:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\\n265:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\\n266:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\\n267:           PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}\\n268:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\\n269:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n270:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\\n271:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\\n272:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n273:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n274:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\\n275:           BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}\\n276:           RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}\\n277:         run: |\\n278:           START_TIME=$(date +%s)\\n279:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n280:             JENKINS_USER=\\n281:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\\\"\\n282:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\\n283:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\\n284:             # when job is cancelled\\n285:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\\"sleep 12h\\\"\\n286: \\n287:             # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here\\n288:             env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n289:             env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n290:           else\\n291:             JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\\n292:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n293:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\n294:           fi\\n295: \\n296:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\\n297:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \\\"%.3f \\\\n\\\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\\n298:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\\n299:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\\n300:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\\n301: \\n302:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"riscv64\\\"* ]]; then\\n303:             # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation\\n304:             # Ensure binfmt_misc is available\\n305:             echo \\\"Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem\\\"\\n306:             sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true\\n307: \\n308:             echo \\\"QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static\\\"\\n309:             docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true\\n310: \\n311:             # Final verification\\n312:             echo \\\"Checking binfmt_misc status:\\\"\\n313:             ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo \\\"Cannot access binfmt_misc directory\\\"\\n314: \\n315:             if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then\\n316:               echo \\\"qemu-riscv64 registration successful\\\"\\n317:             else\\n318:               echo \\\"qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation\\\"\\n319:               echo \\\"This may cause RISC-V builds to fail\\\"\\n320:             fi\\n321: \\n322:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\\\"--privileged\\\"\\n323:           else\\n324:             RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\\n325:           fi\\n326: \\n327:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n328:           # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty\\n329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086\\n330:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n331:             ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \\\\\\n332:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n333:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n334:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n335:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n336:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n337:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n338:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n339:             -e XLA_CUDA \\\\\\n340:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\\\\n341:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\\\\n342:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\\\\n343:             -e PR_LABELS \\\\\\n344:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\\\\n345:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n346:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\\\\n347:             -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \\\\\\n348:             -e RUNNER \\\\\\n349:             --memory=\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\\\" \\\\\\n350:             --memory-swap=\\\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\\\" \\\\\\n351:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n352:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n353:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n354:             --tty \\\\\\n355:             --detach \\\\\\n356:             ${JENKINS_USER} \\\\\\n357:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n358:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n359:             \\\"${USED_IMAGE}\\\" \\\\\\n360:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\n361:           )\\n362: \\n363:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n364:             docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt\\\"\\n365:           fi\\n366: \\n367:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\\n368: \\n369:           END_TIME=$(date +%s)\\n370:           echo \\\"build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n371: \\n372:       - name: Build external packages\\n373:         id: build-external-packages\\n374:         if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n375:         uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages\\n376:         with:\\n377:           build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}\\n378:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n379:           cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\\n380:           output-dir: external\\n381: \\n382:       - name: Move external packages to dist\\n383:         if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'\\n384:         shell: bash\\n385:         run: |\\n386:           src=\\\"${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}\\\"\\n387:           if [ -d \\\"$src\\\" ]; then\\n388:             mkdir -p \\\"dist/$(dirname \\\"$src\\\")\\\"\\n389:             mv \\\"$src\\\" \\\"dist/$(dirname \\\"$src\\\")/\\\"\\n390:           fi\\n391: \\n392:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n393:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n394:         shell: bash\\n395:         continue-on-error: true\\n396:         env:\\n397:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n398:         run: |\\n399:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n400: \\n401:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip\\n402:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\\n403:         run: |\\n404:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files\\n405: \\n406:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3\\n407:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n408:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n409:         with:\\n410:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n411:           retention-days: 14\\n412:           if-no-files-found: error\\n413:           path: artifacts.zip\\n414:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n415: \\n416:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x\\n417:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n418:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n419:         with:\\n420:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n421:           retention-days: 14\\n422:           if-no-files-found: error\\n423:           path: artifacts.zip\\n424: \\n425:       - name: copy logs\\n426:         shell: bash\\n427:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\\n428:         continue-on-error: true\\n429:         run: |\\n430:           rm -f ./usage_logs\\n431:           mkdir -p ./usage_logs\\n432:           cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/\\n433: \\n434:       - name: Upload raw usage log to s3\\n435:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\\n436:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\\n437:         with:\\n438:           s3-prefix: |\\n439:             ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact\\n440:           retention-days: 14\\n441:           if-no-files-found: warn\\n442:           path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt\\n443: \\n444:       - name: Upload sccache stats\\n445:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n446:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats\\n447:         with:\\n448:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n449:           build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}\\n450: \\n451:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n452:         if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n453:         continue-on-error: true\\n454:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n455:         with:\\n456:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n457:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n458:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n459:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n460:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n461:           artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n462: \\n463:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n465:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n466: \\n467:       - name: Cleanup docker\\n468:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n469:         shell: bash\\n470:         run: |\\n471:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\\n472:           docker stop -a || true\\n473:           docker kill -a || true\\n474: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\"}"
51. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       test-matrix:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n14:       docker-image:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n18:       sync-tag:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: |\\n23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n25:       timeout-minutes:\\n26:         required: false\\n27:         type: number\\n28:         default: 240\\n29:         description: |\\n30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n31:       use-gha:\\n32:         required: false\\n33:         type: string\\n34:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n35:         description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\\n36:       dashboard-tag:\\n37:         required: false\\n38:         type: string\\n39:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n40:       s3-bucket:\\n41:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: string\\n44:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n45:       aws-role-to-assume:\\n46:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n47:         required: false\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n50:       disable-monitor:\\n51:         description: |\\n52:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n53:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n54:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n55:         required: false\\n56:         type: boolean\\n57:         default: false\\n58:       monitor-log-interval:\\n59:         description: |\\n60:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n61:         required: false\\n62:         type: number\\n63:         default: 5\\n64:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n65:         description: |\\n66:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n67:         required: false\\n68:         type: number\\n69:         default: 1\\n70:     secrets:\\n71:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n72:         required: false\\n73:         description: |\\n74:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n75:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:\\n76:         required: false\\n77:         description: |\\n78:           HF Auth token to test vllm\\n79:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\\n80:         required: false\\n81:         description: |\\n82:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\\n83: \\n84: env:\\n85:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n86: \\n87: jobs:\\n88:   test:\\n89:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n90:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n91:     strategy:\\n92:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n93:       fail-fast: false\\n94:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\\n95:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n96:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n97:     permissions:\\n98:       id-token: write\\n99:       contents: read\\n100:     steps:\\n101:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n102:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n103:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n104:         with:\\n105:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n106:           instructions: |\\n107:             All testing is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\\n108:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\\n109: \\n110:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n111:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n112:         with:\\n113:           no-sudo: true\\n114: \\n115:       - name: Setup Python\\n116:         if: contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n117:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n118:         with:\\n119:           python-version: '3.12'\\n120:           cache: pip\\n121: \\n122:       - name: Setup Linux\\n123:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n124:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n125: \\n126:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n127:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n128:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n129:         with:\\n130:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\\n131:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test\\n132:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n133: \\n134:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR\\n135:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n136:         id: login-ecr\\n137:         continue-on-error: true\\n138:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\\n139: \\n140:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n141:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n142:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n143:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n144:         with:\\n145:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n146: \\n147:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n148:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n149:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n150:         env:\\n151:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n152:         shell: bash\\n153:         run: |\\n154:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n155:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n156: \\n157:       - name: Pull docker image\\n158:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n159:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n160:         with:\\n161:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n162: \\n163:       - name: Check if in a container runner\\n164:         shell: bash\\n165:         id: check_container_runner\\n166:         run: echo \\\"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n167: \\n168:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\\n169:         id: install-nvidia-driver\\n170:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\\n171:         with:\\n172:           driver-version: ${{ matrix.config == 'legacy_nvidia_driver' && '525.105.17' || '580.82.07' }}\\n173:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n174: \\n175:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\\n176:         id: setup-gpu-flag\\n177:         run: echo \\\"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n178:         if: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && (steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' || contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')) }}\\n179: \\n180:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\\n181:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag\\n182:         run: echo \\\"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n183:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n184: \\n185:       - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\\n186:         run: |\\n187:           sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\\n188:           sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\\n189:           nvidia-smi\\n190:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\\n191: \\n192:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n193:         id: get-job-id\\n194:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n195:         if: always()\\n196:         with:\\n197:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n198: \\n199:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n200:         id: monitor-script\\n201:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n202:         shell: bash\\n203:         continue-on-error: true\\n204:         env:\\n205:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n206:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n207:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n208:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n209:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n210:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n211:         run: |\\n212:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\\n213:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n214:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n215: \\n216:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n217:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n218:         with:\\n219:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n220:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n221:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\\n222: \\n223:       - name: Download TD artifacts\\n224:         continue-on-error: true\\n225:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\\n226: \\n227:       - name: Download Windows torch wheel for cross-compilation\\n228:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\\n229:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\\n230:         with:\\n231:           name: ${{ matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact }}\\n232:           path: win-torch-wheel\\n233: \\n234:       - name: Extract Windows wheel and setup CUDA libraries\\n235:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\\n236:         shell: bash\\n237:         run: |\\n238:           set -x\\n239: \\n240:           # Find the wheel file\\n241:           WHEEL_FILE=$(find win-torch-wheel -name \\\"*.whl\\\" -type f | head -n 1)\\n242:           if [ -z \\\"$WHEEL_FILE\\\" ]; then\\n243:             echo \\\"Error: No wheel file found in win-torch-wheel directory\\\"\\n244:             exit 1\\n245:           fi\\n246:           echo \\\"Found wheel file: $WHEEL_FILE\\\"\\n247: \\n248:           # Unzip the wheel file\\n249:           unzip -q \\\"$WHEEL_FILE\\\" -d win-torch-wheel-extracted\\n250:           echo \\\"Extracted wheel contents\\\"\\n251: \\n252:           # Setup CUDA libraries (cuda.lib and cudart.lib) directory\\n253:           mkdir -p win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64\\n254:           if [ -f \\\"win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib\\\" ]; then\\n255:             mv win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\n256:             echo \\\"Moved cuda.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\\"\\n257:           fi\\n258:           if [ -f \\\"win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib\\\" ]; then\\n259:             mv win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\n260:             echo \\\"Moved cudart.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\\"\\n261:           fi\\n262: \\n263:           # Verify CUDA libraries are present\\n264:           echo \\\"CUDA libraries:\\\"\\n265:           ls -la win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/ || echo \\\"No CUDA libraries found\\\"\\n266: \\n267:       - name: Parse ref\\n268:         id: parse-ref\\n269:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n270: \\n271:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n272:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n273:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n274:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n275:         id: keep-going\\n276:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n277:         with:\\n278:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n279:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n280:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n281: \\n282:       - name: Set Test step time\\n283:         id: test-timeout\\n284:         shell: bash\\n285:         env:\\n286:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n287:         run: |\\n288:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n289: \\n290:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\\n291:         shell: bash\\n292:         run: |\\n293:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n294:           env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n295: \\n296:       - name: Test\\n297:         id: test\\n298:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n299:         env:\\n300:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n301:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n302:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n303:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n304:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n305:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n306:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n307:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n308:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n309:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n310:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n311:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n312:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\\n313:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n314:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n315:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n316:           EXTRA_FLAGS: ${{ matrix.extra_flags || '' }}\\n317:           OP_BENCHMARK_TESTS: ${{ matrix.op_benchmark_tests }}\\n318:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n319:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n320:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n321:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n322:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n323:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n324:           TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\\n325:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\\n326:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2' || '' }}\\n327:           SCCACHE_REGION: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'us-east-1' || '' }}\\n328:           SHM_SIZE: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && '2g' || '1g' }}\\n329:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n330:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n331:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\\n332:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\\n333:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n334:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n335:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\\n336:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}\\n337:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n338:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\\n339:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n340:         run: |\\n341:           set -x\\n342: \\n343:           if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\\n344:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\\n345:           elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\\n346:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\\n347:           else\\n348:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\\n349:           fi\\n350: \\n351:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\\n352:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \\\"%.3f \\\\n\\\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\\n353:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\\n354:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\\n355:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\\n356: \\n357:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n358:             SHM_OPTS=\\n359:             JENKINS_USER=\\n360:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\\n361:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\\n362:             # when job is cancelled\\n363:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\\"sleep 12h\\\"\\n364:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\\\"\\n365:           else\\n366:             SHM_OPTS=\\\"--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}\\\"\\n367:             JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\\n368:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\n369:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n370:           fi\\n371: \\n372:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n373:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\\n374:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice\\n375:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\\n376:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n377:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n378:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n379:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n380:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n381:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n382:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\\\\n383:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\\\\n384:             -e GITHUB_JOB \\\\\\n385:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\\\\n386:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\\\\n387:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\\\\n388:             -e JOB_ID \\\\\\n389:             -e JOB_NAME \\\\\\n390:             -e BASE_SHA \\\\\\n391:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n392:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n393:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\\\\n394:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\\\\n395:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\\\\n396:             -e TEST_CONFIG \\\\\\n397:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\\\\n398:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\\\\n399:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\\\\n400:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\\\\n401:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\\\\n402:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\\\\n403:             -e NO_TD \\\\\\n404:             -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\\\\n405:             -e PR_LABELS \\\\\\n406:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n407:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n408:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n409:             -e XLA_CUDA \\\\\\n410:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\\\\n411:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\\\\n412:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\\\\n413:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\\\\n414:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n415:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \\\\\\n416:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\\\\n417:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\\\\n418:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \\\\\\n419:             --memory=\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\\\" \\\\\\n420:             --memory-swap=\\\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\\\" \\\\\\n421:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n422:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n423:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n424:             --ipc=host \\\\\\n425:             ${SHM_OPTS} \\\\\\n426:             --tty \\\\\\n427:             --detach \\\\\\n428:             --name=\\\"${container_name}\\\" \\\\\\n429:             ${JENKINS_USER} \\\\\\n430:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n431:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n432:             \\\"${USED_IMAGE}\\\" \\\\\\n433:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\n434:           )\\n435:           echo \\\"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n436: \\n437:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n438:             docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install -r .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\\\"\\n439:           fi\\n440: \\n441:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\\\"\\n442: \\n443:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\\n444:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\\n445:         continue-on-error: true\\n446:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n447:         with:\\n448:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n449:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n450:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n451:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n452:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n453: \\n454:       - name: Authenticate with AWS\\n455:         if: ${{ always() && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n456:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n457:         with:\\n458:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\\n459:           # The max duration enforced by the server side\\n460:           role-duration-seconds: 18000\\n461:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n462: \\n463:       - name: Upload the benchmark results\\n464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\\n465:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n466:         with:\\n467:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\\n468:           dry-run: false\\n469:           schema-version: v3\\n470:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n471: \\n472:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n473:         shell: bash\\n474:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n475:         run: |\\n476:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n477: \\n478:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n479:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n480:         shell: bash\\n481:         continue-on-error: true\\n482:         env:\\n483:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n484:         run: |\\n485:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n486: \\n487:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n488:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n489:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n490:         with:\\n491:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n492:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\\n493:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n494: \\n495:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\\n496:         if: always()\\n497:         run: |\\n498:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156\\n499:           find . -iname \\\"core.[1-9]*\\\" -exec docker exec \\\"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\\\" sh -c \\\"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\\\" \\\\;\\n500: \\n501:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3\\n502:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n503:         if: failure()\\n504:         with:\\n505:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\\n506:           retention-days: 14\\n507:           if-no-files-found: ignore\\n508:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\\n509: \\n510:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n511:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n512:         continue-on-error: true\\n513:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n514:         with:\\n515:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n516:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n517:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n518:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n519:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n520: \\n521:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n522:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n523:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\\n524: \\n525:       # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\\n526:       # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\\n527:       # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\\n528:       #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\\n529:       #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\\n530:       #     unknown error\\n531:       #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\\n532:       #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\\n533:       #\\n534:       # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\\n535:       # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\\n536:       - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\\n537:         if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n538:         shell: bash\\n539:         run: |\\n540:           set +e\\n541:           set -x\\n542: \\n543:           nvidia-smi\\n544:           # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\\n545:           # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\\n546:           # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\\n547:           # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\\n548:           #\\n549:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n550:           # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\\n551:           # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\\n552:           # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\\n553:           # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\\n554:           # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\\n555:           # |===============================+======================+======================|\\n556:           # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\\n557:           # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\\n558:           # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\\n559:           # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\\n560:           #\\n561:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n562:           # | Processes:                                                                  |\\n563:           # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\\n564:           # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\\n565:           # |=============================================================================|\\n566:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n567:           #\\n568:           # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\\n569:           # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\\n570:           #\\n571:           # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\\n572:           # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\\n573:           nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\\n574:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\\n575: \\n576:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\\n577:           if [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 0 ] && [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 14 ]; then\\n578:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\\\"\\n579:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n580:           fi\\n581: \\n582:           # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\\n583:           # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\\n584:           # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\\n585:           GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\\n586:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\\n587: \\n588:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\\n589:           if [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 0 ] && [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 14 ]; then\\n590:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\\\"\\n591:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n592:           fi\\n593: \\n594:           # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\\n595:           if [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -le 8 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 1 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 2 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 4 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 8 ]; then\\n596:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\\\"\\n597:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n598:           fi\\n599: \\n600:       - name: Cleanup docker\\n601:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n602:         shell: bash\\n603:         run: |\\n604:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\\n605:           docker stop -a || true\\n606:           docker kill -a || true\\n607: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\"}"
52. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

  "path": ".github/workflows/pull.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: pull\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     branches-ignore:\\n6:       - nightly\\n7:   push:\\n8:     branches:\\n9:       - main\\n10:       - release/*\\n11:       - landchecks/*\\n12:     tags:\\n13:       - ciflow/pull/*\\n14:   workflow_dispatch:\\n15:   schedule:\\n16:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\\n17: \\n18: concurrency:\\n19:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n20:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n21: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n47:     with:\\n48:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n49:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n50:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n51: \\n52:   linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build:\\n53:     name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\\n54: }}linux.2xlarge\\\" },\\n69:           { config: \\\"backwards_compat\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\\\" },\\n70:           { \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/pull.yml\"}"
53. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: linux-build\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      docker-image-name:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\n      build-generates-artifacts:\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n        description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      cuda-arch-list:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"5.2\"\n        description: |\n          List of CUDA architectures CI build should target.\n      runner_prefix:\n        required: false\n        default: \"\"\n        type: string\n        description: Prefix for runner label\n      runner:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"linux.c7i.2xlarge\"\n        description: |\n          Label of the runner this job should run on.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\n          is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\n          logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\n      selected-test-configs:\n        description: |\n          A comma-separated list of test configurations from the test matrix to keep,\n          The empty list means we are going to keep every configurations by defaults\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      s3-bucket:\n        description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n      aws-role-to-assume:\n        description: Role to assume for downloading artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          Disable utilization monitoring for build job\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n      allow-reuse-old-whl:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build try to pull an old wheel from s3 that was built on a\n          commit with no cpp changes from this commit\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      build-additional-packages:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build job will also builds these packages and saves their\n          wheels as artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      build-external-packages:\n        description: |\n          If set, the build external packages and saves their wheels as artifacts\n          use command separated list of packages to build ex: 'vllm,transformers'.\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n      SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\n\n    outputs:\n      docker-image:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n        description: The docker image containing the built PyTorch.\n      test-matrix:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n        description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\n      build-environment:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n\njobs:\n  build:\n    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\n    # Don't run on forked repos\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: 480\n    outputs:\n      docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          instructions: |\n            Build is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\n              docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\n\n      # [pytorch repo ref]\n      # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local\n      # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local\n      # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n          role-session-name: gha-linux-build\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Check if can use old whl build\n        id: use-old-whl\n        uses: ./.github/actions/reuse-old-whl\n        if: ${{ inputs.allow-reuse-old-whl }}\n        with:\n          build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          run-id: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          job-id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\n      - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.selected-test-configs }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          mkdir -p ../../usage_logs\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor \\\n          --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" \\\n          --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" \\\n          > \"../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_${JOB_ID}.txt\" 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download pytest cache\n        uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\n        continue-on-error: true\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        with:\n          cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n          job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          s3_bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Build\n        if: (steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == '') && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        id: build\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\n          SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\n          SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\n          XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n          PR_LABELS: ${{ toJson(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name) }}\n          TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\n          XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n          OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n          SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n          BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES: ${{ inputs.build-additional-packages }}\n          RUNNER: ${{ inputs.runner }}\n        run: |\n          START_TIME=$(date +%s)\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            JENKINS_USER=\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\"\n            # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\n            # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\n            # when job is cancelled\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\"sleep 12h\"\n\n            # since some steps are skipped on s390x, if they are necessary, run them here\n            env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n            env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          else\n            JENKINS_USER=\"--user jenkins\"\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\n          fi\n\n          # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\n          TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \"%.3f \\n\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\n          # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\n          # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\n          TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"riscv64\"* ]]; then\n            # EC2 specific setup for RISC-V emulation\n            # Ensure binfmt_misc is available\n            echo \"Mounting binfmt_misc filesystem\"\n            sudo mount binfmt_misc -t binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc 2>/dev/null || true\n\n            echo \"QEMU registration: multiarch/qemu-user-static\"\n            docker run --rm --privileged multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes || true\n\n            # Final verification\n            echo \"Checking binfmt_misc status:\"\n            ls -la /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ 2>/dev/null || echo \"Cannot access binfmt_misc directory\"\n\n            if [ -f /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/qemu-riscv64 ]; then\n              echo \"qemu-riscv64 registration successful\"\n            else\n              echo \"qemu-riscv64 registration failed - proceeding without emulation\"\n              echo \"This may cause RISC-V builds to fail\"\n            fi\n\n            RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\"--privileged\"\n          else\n            RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS=\n          fi\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # Used for JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD, which can be empty\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${RISCV_DOCKER_ARGS} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e XLA_CUDA \\\n            -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n            -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n            -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\n            -e PR_LABELS \\\n            -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n            -e BUILD_ADDITIONAL_PACKAGES \\\n            -e RUNNER \\\n            --memory=\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\" \\\n            --memory-swap=\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\" \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            ${JENKINS_USER} \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${USED_IMAGE}\" \\\n            ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\\n          )\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt\"\n          fi\n\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\n\n          END_TIME=$(date +%s)\n          echo \"build_time=$((END_TIME - START_TIME))\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Build external packages\n        id: build-external-packages\n        if: inputs.build-external-packages != '' &&  steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/build-external-packages\n        with:\n          build-targets: ${{ inputs.build-external-packages }}\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          cuda-arch-list: ${{ inputs.cuda-arch-list }}\n          output-dir: external\n\n      - name: Move external packages to dist\n        if: steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir != '' && steps.build-external-packages.outcome != 'skipped'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          src=\"${{ steps.build-external-packages.outputs.output_dir }}\"\n          if [ -d \"$src\" ]; then\n            mkdir -p \"dist/$(dirname \"$src\")\"\n            mv \"$src\" \"dist/$(dirname \"$src\")/\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Archive artifacts into zip\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse != 'true'\n        run: |\n          zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/custom_test_artifacts build/lib build/bin .additional_ci_files\n\n      - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on S3\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: artifacts.zip\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts for s390x\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && (steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' || steps.use-old-whl.outputs.reuse == 'true') && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: artifacts.zip\n\n      - name: copy logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          rm -f ./usage_logs\n          mkdir -p ./usage_logs\n          cp ../../usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt ./usage_logs/\n\n      - name: Upload raw usage log to s3\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'}}\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@v5\n        with:\n          s3-prefix: |\n            ${{ github.repository }}/${{ github.run_id }}/${{ github.run_attempt }}/artifact\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: warn\n          path: usage_logs/usage_log_build_*.txt\n\n      - name: Upload sccache stats\n        if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          build-time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.build_time }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n          artifact_prefix: usage_log_build_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always() && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n\n      - name: Cleanup docker\n        if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\n          docker stop -a || true\n          docker kill -a || true\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml"
