55. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen\\n2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to\\n3: # just use a different workflow altogether\\n4: \\n5: name: xpu-test\\n6: \\n7: on:\\n8:   workflow_call:\\n9:     inputs:\\n10:       build-environment:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n14:       test-matrix:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n18:       docker-image:\\n19:         required: true\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n22:       sync-tag:\\n23:         required: false\\n24:         type: string\\n25:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n26:         description: |\\n27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n29:       timeout-minutes:\\n30:         required: false\\n31:         type: number\\n32:         default: 390\\n33:         description: |\\n34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n35:       tests-to-include:\\n36:         required: false\\n37:         type: string\\n38:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n39:         description: |\\n40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)\\n41:       dashboard-tag:\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: string\\n44:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n45:       disable-monitor:\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n50:         required: false\\n51:         type: boolean\\n52:         default: true\\n53:       monitor-log-interval:\\n54:         description: |\\n55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n56:         required: false\\n57:         type: number\\n58:         default: 5\\n59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n60:         description: |\\n61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n62:         required: false\\n63:         type: number\\n64:         default: 1\\n65:     secrets:\\n66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n67:         required: false\\n68:         description: |\\n69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n70: permissions:\\n71:   id-token: write\\n72:   contents: read\\n73: \\n74: env:\\n75:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n76: \\n77: jobs:\\n78:   test:\\n79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n81:     strategy:\\n82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n83:       fail-fast: false\\n84:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n85:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n86:     steps:\\n87:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n88:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n89:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n90: \\n91:       - name: Setup XPU\\n92:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-xpu\\n93: \\n94:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n95:         id: aws_creds\\n96:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n97:         with:\\n98:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n99:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n100: \\n101:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR\\n102:         id: login-ecr\\n103:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\\n104: \\n105:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n106:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n108:         with:\\n109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n110: \\n111:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n112:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n113:         env:\\n114:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n115:         shell: bash\\n116:         run: |\\n117:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n118:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n119: \\n120:       - name: Pull docker image\\n121:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n122:         with:\\n123:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n124: \\n125:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n126:         id: get-job-id\\n127:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n128:         if: always()\\n129:         with:\\n130:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n131: \\n132:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n133:         id: monitor-script\\n134:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n135:         shell: bash\\n136:         continue-on-error: true\\n137:         env:\\n138:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n139:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n140:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n141:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n142:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n143:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n144:         run: |\\n145:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\\n146:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n147:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n148: \\n149:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n150:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n151:         with:\\n152:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n153: \\n154:       - name: Parse ref\\n155:         id: parse-ref\\n156:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n157: \\n158:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n159:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n160:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n161:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n162:         id: keep-going\\n163:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n164:         with:\\n165:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n166:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n167:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n168: \\n169:       - name: Set Test step time\\n170:         id: test-timeout\\n171:         shell: bash\\n172:         env:\\n173:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n174:         run: |\\n175:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n176: \\n177:       - name: Test\\n178:         id: test\\n179:         env:\\n180:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n181:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n182:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n183:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n184:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n185:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n186:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n187:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n188:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n189:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n190:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n191:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n192:           PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES: 1\\n193:           PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL: 1\\n194:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n195:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n196:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n197:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n198:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n200:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n201:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n202:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n203:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n204:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\\n205:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n206:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n207:           TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}\\n208:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\\n209:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n210:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n211:         run: |\\n212:           # Fetch aws credential from IMDs\\n213:           eval \\\"$(python3 .github/scripts/get_aws_session_tokens.py)\\\"\\n214:           set -x\\n215: \\n216:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\\n217: \\n218:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n219:           # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\\n220:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\\n221:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n222:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n223:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n224:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n225:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n226:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\\\\n227:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\\\\n228:             -e GITHUB_JOB \\\\\\n229:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\\\\n230:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\\\\n231:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\\\\n232:             -e JOB_ID \\\\\\n233:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n234:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n235:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\\\\n236:             -e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID \\\\\\n237:             -e AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY \\\\\\n238:             -e AWS_SESSION_TOKEN \\\\\\n239:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\\\\n240:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\\\\n241:             -e TEST_CONFIG \\\\\\n242:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\\\\n243:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\\\\n244:             -e PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES \\\\\\n245:             -e PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL \\\\\\n246:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\\\\n247:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\\\\n248:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\\\\n249:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\\\\n250:             -e NO_TD \\\\\\n251:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n252:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n253:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n254:             -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\\\\n255:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\\\\n256:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\\\\n257:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\\\\n258:             -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \\\\\\n259:             -e ZE_AFFINITY_MASK \\\\\\n260:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n261:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\\\\n262:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n263:             --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \\\\\\n264:             --ulimit core=0 \\\\\\n265:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n266:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n267:             --shm-size=\\\"8g\\\" \\\\\\n268:             --tty \\\\\\n269:             --detach \\\\\\n270:             --name=\\\"${container_name}\\\" \\\\\\n271:             --user jenkins \\\\\\n272:             --privileged \\\\\\n273:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n274:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n275:             \\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n276:           )\\n277:           # save container name for later step\\n278:           echo \\\"CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_ENV\\\"\\n279:           # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home\\n280:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}\\\"\\n281: \\n282:       - name: Save test results\\n283:         if: always()\\n284:         run: |\\n285:           # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct\\n286:           docker exec -t \\\"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\\\" sh -c \\\"cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test\\\"\\n287: \\n288:       - name: Change permissions\\n289:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion }}\\n290:         run: |\\n291:           docker exec -t \\\"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\\\" sh -c \\\"sudo chown -R 1000:1000 test\\\"\\n292: \\n293:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n294:         shell: bash\\n295:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n296:         run: |\\n297:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n298: \\n299:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n300:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n301:         shell: bash\\n302:         continue-on-error: true\\n303:         env:\\n304:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n305:         run: |\\n306:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n307: \\n308:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n309:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n310:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n311:         with:\\n312:           use-gha: true\\n313:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n314: \\n315:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n316:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n317:         continue-on-error: true\\n318:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n319:         with:\\n320:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n321:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n322:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n323:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n324:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n325: \\n326:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\\n327:         if: always()\\n328:         run: |\\n329:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156\\n330:           find . -iname \\\"core.[1-9]*\\\" -exec docker exec \\\"${CONTAINER_NAME}\\\" sh -c \\\"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\\\" \\\\;\\n331: \\n332:       - name: Stop container before exit\\n333:         if: always()\\n334:         run: |\\n335:           # Workaround for multiple runners on same IDC node\\n336:           docker stop \\\"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\\\"\\n337: \\n338:       - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub\\n339:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n340:         if: failure()\\n341:         with:\\n342:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\\n343:           retention-days: 14\\n344:           if-no-files-found: ignore\\n345:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\\n346: \\n347:       - name: Authenticate with AWS\\n348:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n349:         with:\\n350:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\\n351:           # The max duration enforced by the server side\\n352:           role-duration-seconds: 18000\\n353:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n354: \\n355:       - name: Upload the benchmark results\\n356:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\\n357:         with:\\n358:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\\n359:           dry-run: false\\n360:           schema-version: v3\\n361:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n362: \\n363:       - name: Teardown XPU\\n364:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu\\n365: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml\"}"
56. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen\n# places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to\n# just use a different workflow altogether\n\nname: xpu-test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 390\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      tests-to-include:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)\n      dashboard-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n          Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n          since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    steps:\n      # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Setup XPU\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-xpu\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        id: aws_creds\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n        id: login-ecr\n        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n        # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n        # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n        # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n        id: keep-going\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES: 1\n          PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL: 1\n          CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n          TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n          VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n          NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n          NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n          TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n          SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n          NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n          PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n          TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}\n          DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        run: |\n          # Fetch aws credential from IMDs\n          eval \"$(python3 .github/scripts/get_aws_session_tokens.py)\"\n          set -x\n\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # Used for GPU_FLAG since that doesn't play nice\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n            -e AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID \\\n            -e AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY \\\n            -e AWS_SESSION_TOKEN \\\n            -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n            -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n            -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n            -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n            -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n            -e PYTORCH_RETRY_TEST_CASES \\\n            -e PYTORCH_OVERRIDE_FLAKY_SIGNAL \\\n            -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n            -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n            -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\n            -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n            -e NO_TD \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX \\\n            -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n            -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \\\n            -e ZE_AFFINITY_MASK \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \\\n            --ulimit core=0 \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --shm-size=\"8g\" \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n            --user jenkins \\\n            --privileged \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\\\n          )\n          # save container name for later step\n          echo \"CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}\" >> \"$GITHUB_ENV\"\n          # jenkins user does not have write permission to mounted workspace; work-around by copying within container to jenkins home\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n      - name: Save test results\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # copy test results back to the mounted workspace, needed sudo, resulting permissions were correct\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test\"\n\n      - name: Change permissions\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion }}\n        run: |\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"sudo chown -R 1000:1000 test\"\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          use-gha: true\n          file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n\n      - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n          find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${CONTAINER_NAME}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n      - name: Stop container before exit\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # Workaround for multiple runners on same IDC node\n          docker stop \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\"\n\n      - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: failure()\n        with:\n          name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: ignore\n          path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n      - name: Authenticate with AWS\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\n          # The max duration enforced by the server side\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Upload the benchmark results\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\n        with:\n          benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\n          dry-run: false\n          schema-version: v3\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Teardown XPU\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-xpu\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml"
57. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

false\\n300:       test-matrix: |\\n301:         { include: [\\n302:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\\n303:         ]}\\n304:     secrets: inherit\\n305: \\n306: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/pull.yml\"}"
58. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: pull\n\non:\n  pull_request:\n    branches-ignore:\n      - nightly\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n      - landchecks/*\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/pull/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n  schedule:\n    - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  llm-td:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  target-determination:\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    needs: llm-td\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 5, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"docs_test\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1,  runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"jit_legacy\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"backwards_compat\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"distributed\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"distributed\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"numpy_2_x\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"libtorch_agnostic_targetting\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-docs:\n    name: linux-docs\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_docs.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-no-ops:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-no-ops\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-no-ops\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-pch:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-pch\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-pch\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner: linux.2xlarge.memory\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang18-asan\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 5, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 6, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 7, num_shards: 7, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"openreg\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n      sync-tag: asan-build\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang18-asan\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang18-asan-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      sync-tag: asan-test\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3-clang12-onnx\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12-onnx\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs:\n      - linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build\n      - target-determination\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-onnx-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-clang12\n      test-matrix: |\n        { num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-clang12-test:\n    name: needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.14-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-py3_14-clang12-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-cudnn9-py3_10-clang12-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-clang12\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cpu-py3_10-gcc11-bazel-test:\n    name: linux-jammy-cpu-py3.10-gcc11-bazel-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-bazel-test\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\n      cuda-version: cpu\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-py3_10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11-mobile-lightweight-dispatch-build\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\n      build-generates-artifacts: false\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1 },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build:\n    # don't run build twice on main\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'\n    name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\n      sync-tag: rocm-build\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.2\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\n      cuda-arch-list: '7.5'\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"pr_time_benchmarks\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.g4dn.metal.nvidia.gpu\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-test:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda13_0-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build:\n    name: cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda13.0-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\n      cuda-arch-list: '7.5'\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"pr_time_benchmarks\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.g4dn.metal.nvidia.gpu\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-cuda13_0-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-test:\n    name: cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: linux-jammy-cuda13_0-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm75\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda13_0-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda13_0-py3_10-gcc11-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-jammy-xpu-n-py3_10-build:\n    name: linux-jammy-xpu-n-py3.10\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      # This should sync with the build in xpu.yml but xpu uses a larger runner\n      # sync-tag: linux-xpu-n-build\n      runner_prefix: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\n      build-environment: linux-noble-xpu-n-py3.10\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-xpu-n-py3\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 1, num_shards: 4, runner: \"linux.idc.xpu\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 2, num_shards: 4, runner: \"linux.idc.xpu\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 3, num_shards: 4, runner: \"linux.idc.xpu\" },\n          { config: \"default\", shard: 4, num_shards: 4, runner: \"linux.idc.xpu\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/pull.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/pull.yml"
