61. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       test-matrix:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n14:       docker-image:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n18:       sync-tag:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: |\\n23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n25:       timeout-minutes:\\n26:         required: false\\n27:         type: number\\n28:         default: 240\\n29:         description: |\\n30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n31:       use-gha:\\n32:         required: false\\n33:         type: string\\n34:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n35:         description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\\n36:       dashboard-tag:\\n37:         required: false\\n38:         type: string\\n39:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n40:       s3-bucket:\\n41:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: string\\n44:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n45:       aws-role-to-assume:\\n46:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n47:         required: false\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n50:       disable-monitor:\\n51:         description: |\\n52:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n53:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n54:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n55:         required: false\\n56:         type: boolean\\n57:         default: false\\n58:       monitor-log-interval:\\n59:         description: |\\n60:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n61:         required: false\\n62:         type: number\\n63:         default: 5\\n64:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n65:         description: |\\n66:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n67:         required: false\\n68:         type: number\\n69:         default: 1\\n70:     secrets:\\n71:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n72:         required: false\\n73:         description: |\\n74:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n75:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:\\n76:         required: false\\n77:         description: |\\n78:           HF Auth token to test vllm\\n79:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\\n80:         required: false\\n81:         description: |\\n82:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\\n83: \\n84: env:\\n85:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n86: \\n87: jobs:\\n88:   test:\\n89:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n90:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n91:     strategy:\\n92:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n93:       fail-fast: false\\n94:     environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\\n95:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n96:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n97:     permissions:\\n98:       id-token: write\\n99:       contents: read\\n100:     steps:\\n101:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n102:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n103:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n104:         with:\\n105:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n106:           instructions: |\\n107:             All testing is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\\n108:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\\n109: \\n110:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n111:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n112:         with:\\n113:           no-sudo: true\\n114: \\n115:       - name: Setup Python\\n116:         if: contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n117:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n118:         with:\\n119:           python-version: '3.12'\\n120:           cache: pip\\n121: \\n122:       - name: Setup Linux\\n123:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n124:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n125: \\n126:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n127:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n128:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n129:         with:\\n130:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\\n131:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test\\n132:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n133: \\n134:       - name: Login to Amazon ECR\\n135:         if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n136:         id: login-ecr\\n137:         continue-on-error: true\\n138:         uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\\n139: \\n140:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n141:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n142:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n143:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n144:         with:\\n145:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n146: \\n147:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n148:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n149:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n150:         env:\\n151:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n152:         shell: bash\\n153:         run: |\\n154:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n155:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n156: \\n157:       - name: Pull docker image\\n158:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n159:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n160:         with:\\n161:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n162: \\n163:       - name: Check if in a container runner\\n164:         shell: bash\\n165:         id: check_container_runner\\n166:         run: echo \\\"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n167: \\n168:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\\n169:         id: install-nvidia-driver\\n170:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\\n171:         with:\\n172:           driver-version: ${{ matrix.config == 'legacy_nvidia_driver' && '525.105.17' || '580.82.07' }}\\n173:         if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n174: \\n175:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\\n176:         id: setup-gpu-flag\\n177:         run: echo \\\"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n178:         if: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && (steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' || contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')) }}\\n179: \\n180:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\\n181:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag\\n182:         run: echo \\\"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n183:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n184: \\n185:       - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\\n186:         run: |\\n187:           sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\\n188:           sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\\n189:           nvidia-smi\\n190:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\\n191: \\n192:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n193:         id: get-job-id\\n194:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n195:         if: always()\\n196:         with:\\n197:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n198: \\n199:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n200:         id: monitor-script\\n201:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n202:         shell: bash\\n203:         continue-on-error: true\\n204:         env:\\n205:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n206:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n207:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n208:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n209:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n210:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n211:         run: |\\n212:           python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\\n213:           python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n214:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n215: \\n216:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n217:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n218:         with:\\n219:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n220:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n221:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\\n222: \\n223:       - name: Download TD artifacts\\n224:         continue-on-error: true\\n225:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\\n226: \\n227:       - name: Download Windows torch wheel for cross-compilation\\n228:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\\n229:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\\n230:         with:\\n231:           name: ${{ matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact }}\\n232:           path: win-torch-wheel\\n233: \\n234:       - name: Extract Windows wheel and setup CUDA libraries\\n235:         if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\\n236:         shell: bash\\n237:         run: |\\n238:           set -x\\n239: \\n240:           # Find the wheel file\\n241:           WHEEL_FILE=$(find win-torch-wheel -name \\\"*.whl\\\" -type f | head -n 1)\\n242:           if [ -z \\\"$WHEEL_FILE\\\" ]; then\\n243:             echo \\\"Error: No wheel file found in win-torch-wheel directory\\\"\\n244:             exit 1\\n245:           fi\\n246:           echo \\\"Found wheel file: $WHEEL_FILE\\\"\\n247: \\n248:           # Unzip the wheel file\\n249:           unzip -q \\\"$WHEEL_FILE\\\" -d win-torch-wheel-extracted\\n250:           echo \\\"Extracted wheel contents\\\"\\n251: \\n252:           # Setup CUDA libraries (cuda.lib and cudart.lib) directory\\n253:           mkdir -p win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64\\n254:           if [ -f \\\"win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib\\\" ]; then\\n255:             mv win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\n256:             echo \\\"Moved cuda.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\\"\\n257:           fi\\n258:           if [ -f \\\"win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib\\\" ]; then\\n259:             mv win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\n260:             echo \\\"Moved cudart.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\\\"\\n261:           fi\\n262: \\n263:           # Verify CUDA libraries are present\\n264:           echo \\\"CUDA libraries:\\\"\\n265:           ls -la win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/ || echo \\\"No CUDA libraries found\\\"\\n266: \\n267:       - name: Parse ref\\n268:         id: parse-ref\\n269:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n270: \\n271:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n272:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n273:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n274:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n275:         id: keep-going\\n276:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n277:         with:\\n278:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n279:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n280:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n281: \\n282:       - name: Set Test step time\\n283:         id: test-timeout\\n284:         shell: bash\\n285:         env:\\n286:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n287:         run: |\\n288:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n289: \\n290:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\\n291:         shell: bash\\n292:         run: |\\n293:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n294:           env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n295: \\n296:       - name: Test\\n297:         id: test\\n298:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n299:         env:\\n300:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n301:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n302:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n303:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n304:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n305:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n306:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n307:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n308:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n309:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n310:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n311:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n312:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\\n313:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n314:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n315:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n316:           EXTRA_FLAGS: ${{ matrix.extra_flags || '' }}\\n317:           OP_BENCHMARK_TESTS: ${{ matrix.op_benchmark_tests }}\\n318:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n319:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n320:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n321:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n322:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n323:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n324:           TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\\n325:           # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\\n326:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2' || '' }}\\n327:           SCCACHE_REGION: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'us-east-1' || '' }}\\n328:           SHM_SIZE: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && '2g' || '1g' }}\\n329:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n330:           DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n331:           XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\\n332:           XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\\n333:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n334:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n335:           DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\\n336:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}\\n337:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n338:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\\n339:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n340:         run: |\\n341:           set -x\\n342: \\n343:           if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\\n344:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\\n345:           elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\\n346:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\\n347:           else\\n348:             TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\\n349:           fi\\n350: \\n351:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\\n352:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \\\"%.3f \\\\n\\\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\\n353:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\\n354:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\\n355:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\\n356: \\n357:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n358:             SHM_OPTS=\\n359:             JENKINS_USER=\\n360:             # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\\n361:             # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\\n362:             # when job is cancelled\\n363:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\\"sleep 12h\\\"\\n364:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\\\"\\n365:           else\\n366:             SHM_OPTS=\\\"--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}\\\"\\n367:             JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\\n368:             DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\n369:             USED_IMAGE=\\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n370:           fi\\n371: \\n372:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n373:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\\n374:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice\\n375:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\\n376:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n377:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n378:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n379:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n380:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n381:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n382:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\\\\n383:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\\\\n384:             -e GITHUB_JOB \\\\\\n385:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\\\\n386:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\\\\n387:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\\\\n388:             -e JOB_ID \\\\\\n389:             -e JOB_NAME \\\\\\n390:             -e BASE_SHA \\\\\\n391:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n392:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n393:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\\\\n394:             -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\\\\n395:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\\\\n396:             -e TEST_CONFIG \\\\\\n397:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\\\\n398:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\\\\n399:             -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\\\\n400:             -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\\\\n401:             -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\\\\n402:             -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\\\\n403:             -e NO_TD \\\\\\n404:             -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\\\\n405:             -e PR_LABELS \\\\\\n406:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n407:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n408:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n409:             -e XLA_CUDA \\\\\\n410:             -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\\\\n411:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\\\\n412:             -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\\\\n413:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\\\\n414:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n415:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \\\\\\n416:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\\\\n417:             -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\\\\n418:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \\\\\\n419:             --memory=\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\\\" \\\\\\n420:             --memory-swap=\\\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\\\" \\\\\\n421:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n422:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n423:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n424:             --ipc=host \\\\\\n425:             ${SHM_OPTS} \\\\\\n426:             --tty \\\\\\n427:             --detach \\\\\\n428:             --name=\\\"${container_name}\\\" \\\\\\n429:             ${JENKINS_USER} \\\\\\n430:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n431:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n432:             \\\"${USED_IMAGE}\\\" \\\\\\n433:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\n434:           )\\n435:           echo \\\"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n436: \\n437:           if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\\\"s390x\\\"* ]]; then\\n438:             docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install -r .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\\\"\\n439:           fi\\n440: \\n441:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\\\"\\n442: \\n443:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\\n444:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\\n445:         continue-on-error: true\\n446:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n447:         with:\\n448:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n449:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n450:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n451:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n452:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n453: \\n454:       - name: Authenticate with AWS\\n455:         if: ${{ always() && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\\n456:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n457:         with:\\n458:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\\n459:           # The max duration enforced by the server side\\n460:           role-duration-seconds: 18000\\n461:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n462: \\n463:       - name: Upload the benchmark results\\n464:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\\n465:         if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n466:         with:\\n467:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\\n468:           dry-run: false\\n469:           schema-version: v3\\n470:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n471: \\n472:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n473:         shell: bash\\n474:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n475:         run: |\\n476:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n477: \\n478:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n479:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n480:         shell: bash\\n481:         continue-on-error: true\\n482:         env:\\n483:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n484:         run: |\\n485:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n486: \\n487:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n488:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n489:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n490:         with:\\n491:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n492:           use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\\n493:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n494: \\n495:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\\n496:         if: always()\\n497:         run: |\\n498:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156\\n499:           find . -iname \\\"core.[1-9]*\\\" -exec docker exec \\\"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\\\" sh -c \\\"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\\\" \\\\;\\n500: \\n501:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3\\n502:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n503:         if: failure()\\n504:         with:\\n505:           name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\\n506:           retention-days: 14\\n507:           if-no-files-found: ignore\\n508:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\\n509: \\n510:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n511:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n512:         continue-on-error: true\\n513:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n514:         with:\\n515:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n516:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n517:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n518:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n519:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n520: \\n521:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n522:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n523:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\\n524: \\n525:       # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\\n526:       # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\\n527:       # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\\n528:       #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\\n529:       #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\\n530:       #     unknown error\\n531:       #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\\n532:       #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\\n533:       #\\n534:       # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\\n535:       # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\\n536:       - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\\n537:         if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\\n538:         shell: bash\\n539:         run: |\\n540:           set +e\\n541:           set -x\\n542: \\n543:           nvidia-smi\\n544:           # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\\n545:           # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\\n546:           # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\\n547:           # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\\n548:           #\\n549:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n550:           # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\\n551:           # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\\n552:           # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\\n553:           # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\\n554:           # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\\n555:           # |===============================+======================+======================|\\n556:           # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\\n557:           # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\\n558:           # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\\n559:           # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\\n560:           #\\n561:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n562:           # | Processes:                                                                  |\\n563:           # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\\n564:           # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\\n565:           # |=============================================================================|\\n566:           # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\\n567:           #\\n568:           # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\\n569:           # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\\n570:           #\\n571:           # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\\n572:           # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\\n573:           nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\\n574:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\\n575: \\n576:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\\n577:           if [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 0 ] && [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 14 ]; then\\n578:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\\\"\\n579:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n580:           fi\\n581: \\n582:           # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\\n583:           # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\\n584:           # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\\n585:           GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\\n586:           NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\\n587: \\n588:           # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\\n589:           if [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 0 ] && [ \\\"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\\\" -ne 14 ]; then\\n590:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\\\"\\n591:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n592:           fi\\n593: \\n594:           # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\\n595:           if [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -le 8 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 1 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 2 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 4 ] && [ \\\"$GPU_COUNT\\\" -ne 8 ]; then\\n596:             echo \\\"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\\\"\\n597:             .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\\n598:           fi\\n599: \\n600:       - name: Cleanup docker\\n601:         if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n602:         shell: bash\\n603:         run: |\\n604:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\\n605:           docker stop -a || true\\n606:           docker kill -a || true\\n607: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\"}"
62. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: linux-test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 240\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      use-gha:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: If set to any value, upload to GHA. Otherwise upload to S3.\n      dashboard-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      s3-bucket:\n        description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n      aws-role-to-assume:\n        description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n          Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n          since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n      VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to test vllm\n      SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\n\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    environment: ${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && 'scribe-protected' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release/') && 'scribe-protected' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'ci-scribe') && 'scribe-pr' || '' }}\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    steps:\n      - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\n        if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        with:\n          github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          instructions: |\n            All testing is done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\n              docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        if: contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\n        uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.12'\n          cache: pip\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\n\n      - name: configure aws credentials\n        if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\n          role-session-name: gha-linux-test\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Login to Amazon ECR\n        if: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\n        id: login-ecr\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: aws-actions/amazon-ecr-login@062b18b96a7aff071d4dc91bc00c4c1a7945b076 # v2.0.1\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Check if in a container runner\n        shell: bash\n        id: check_container_runner\n        run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\n        id: install-nvidia-driver\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\n        with:\n          driver-version: ${{ matrix.config == 'legacy_nvidia_driver' && '525.105.17' || '580.82.07' }}\n        if: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\n\n      - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n        id: setup-gpu-flag\n        run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        if: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && (steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' || contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')) }}\n\n      - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n        id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n        run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\n\n      - name: Lock NVIDIA A100 40GB Frequency\n        run: |\n          sudo nvidia-smi -pm 1\n          sudo nvidia-smi -ac 1215,1410\n          nvidia-smi\n        if: ${{ contains(matrix.runner, 'a100') && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false' }}\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n          use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n\n      - name: Download TD artifacts\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n      - name: Download Windows torch wheel for cross-compilation\n        if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\n        uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\n        with:\n          name: ${{ matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact }}\n          path: win-torch-wheel\n\n      - name: Extract Windows wheel and setup CUDA libraries\n        if: matrix.win_torch_wheel_artifact != ''\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          set -x\n\n          # Find the wheel file\n          WHEEL_FILE=$(find win-torch-wheel -name \"*.whl\" -type f | head -n 1)\n          if [ -z \"$WHEEL_FILE\" ]; then\n            echo \"Error: No wheel file found in win-torch-wheel directory\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n          echo \"Found wheel file: $WHEEL_FILE\"\n\n          # Unzip the wheel file\n          unzip -q \"$WHEEL_FILE\" -d win-torch-wheel-extracted\n          echo \"Extracted wheel contents\"\n\n          # Setup CUDA libraries (cuda.lib and cudart.lib) directory\n          mkdir -p win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64\n          if [ -f \"win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib\" ]; then\n            mv win-torch-wheel/cuda.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\n            echo \"Moved cuda.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\"\n          fi\n          if [ -f \"win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib\" ]; then\n            mv win-torch-wheel/cudart.lib win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\n            echo \"Moved cudart.lib to win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/\"\n          fi\n\n          # Verify CUDA libraries are present\n          echo \"CUDA libraries:\"\n          ls -la win-torch-wheel-extracted/lib/x64/ || echo \"No CUDA libraries found\"\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n        # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\n        # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\n        # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\n        id: keep-going\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n          TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n          SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n          NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n          EXTRA_FLAGS: ${{ matrix.extra_flags || '' }}\n          OP_BENCHMARK_TESTS: ${{ matrix.op_benchmark_tests }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n          VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n          TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n          NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n          NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n          TD_DISTRIBUTED: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-td-distributed }}\n          # Do not set SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX to share the cache between all build jobs\n          SCCACHE_BUCKET: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2' || '' }}\n          SCCACHE_REGION: ${{ !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') && 'us-east-1' || '' }}\n          SHM_SIZE: ${{ steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && '2g' || '1g' }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          XLA_CUDA: ${{ contains(inputs.build-environment, 'xla') && '0' || '' }}\n          XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME: ossci-compiler-clang-cache-circleci-xla\n          PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n          DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n          VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n          SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n          ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        run: |\n          set -x\n\n          if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n          elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/onnx/test.sh\n          else\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n          fi\n\n          # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\n          TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \"%.3f \\n\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\n          # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\n          # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\n          TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            SHM_OPTS=\n            JENKINS_USER=\n            # ensure that docker container cleanly exits in 12 hours\n            # if for some reason cleanup action doesn't stop container\n            # when job is cancelled\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\"sleep 12h\"\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE_S390X}\"\n          else\n            SHM_OPTS=\"--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}\"\n            JENKINS_USER=\"--user jenkins\"\n            DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\n            USED_IMAGE=\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n          fi\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n          # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e JOB_NAME \\\n            -e BASE_SHA \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n            -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n            -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n            -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n            -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n            -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n            -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n            -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n            -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\n            -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n            -e NO_TD \\\n            -e TD_DISTRIBUTED \\\n            -e PR_LABELS \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\n            -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\n            -e XLA_CUDA \\\n            -e XLA_CLANG_CACHE_S3_BUCKET_NAME \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n            -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \\\n            -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n            -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n            -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \\\n            --memory=\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\" \\\n            --memory-swap=\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\" \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --ipc=host \\\n            ${SHM_OPTS} \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n            ${JENKINS_USER} \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${USED_IMAGE}\" \\\n            ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\\n          )\n          echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n          if [[ ${BUILD_ENVIRONMENT} == *\"s390x\"* ]]; then\n            docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"python3 -m pip install -r .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\"\n          fi\n\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n      - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\n        uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\n        continue-on-error: true\n        if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure' && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          cache_dir: .pytest_cache\n          shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n          sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\n          job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n      - name: Authenticate with AWS\n        if: ${{ always() && contains(matrix.runner, 'b200') }}\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\n          # The max duration enforced by the server side\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Upload the benchmark results\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\n        if: inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        with:\n          benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\n          dry-run: false\n          schema-version: v3\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          use-gha: ${{ inputs.use-gha }}\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n          find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n      - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: failure()\n        with:\n          name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: ignore\n          path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor && inputs.build-environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n\n      # NB: We are currently having an intermittent GPU-related issue on G5 runners with\n      # A10G GPU. Once this happens, trying to reset the GPU as done in setup-nvidia does\n      # not seem to help. Here are some symptoms:\n      #   * Calling nvidia-smi timeouts after 60 second\n      #   * Fail to run nvidia-smi with an unable to determine the device handle for GPU\n      #     unknown error\n      #   * Test fails with a missing CUDA GPU error when initializing CUDA in PyTorch\n      #   * Run docker --gpus all fails with error response from daemon\n      #\n      # As both the root cause and recovery path are unclear, let's take the runner out of\n      # service so that it doesn't get any more jobs\n      - name: Check NVIDIA driver installation step\n        if: failure() && steps.install-nvidia-driver.outputs.has-nvidia == 'true' && !contains(matrix.runner, 'b200')\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          set +e\n          set -x\n\n          nvidia-smi\n          # NB: Surprisingly, nvidia-smi command returns successfully with return code 0 even in\n          # the case where the driver has already crashed as it still can get the driver version\n          # and some basic information like the bus ID.  However, the rest of the information\n          # would be missing (ERR!), for example:\n          #\n          # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n          # | NVIDIA-SMI 525.89.02    Driver Version: 525.89.02    CUDA Version: 12.0     |\n          # |-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n          # | GPU  Name        Persistence-M| Bus-Id        Disp.A | Volatile Uncorr. ECC |\n          # | Fan  Temp  Perf  Pwr:Usage/Cap|         Memory-Usage | GPU-Util  Compute M. |\n          # |                               |                      |               MIG M. |\n          # |===============================+======================+======================|\n          # |   0  ERR!                Off  | 00000000:00:1E.0 Off |                 ERR! |\n          # |ERR!  ERR! ERR!    ERR! / ERR! |   4184MiB / 23028MiB |    ERR!      Default |\n          # |                               |                      |                 ERR! |\n          # +-------------------------------+----------------------+----------------------+\n          #\n          # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n          # | Processes:                                                                  |\n          # |  GPU   GI   CI        PID   Type   Process name                  GPU Memory |\n          # |        ID   ID                                                   Usage      |\n          # |=============================================================================|\n          # +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+\n          #\n          # This should be reported as a failure instead as it will guarantee to fail when\n          # Docker tries to run with --gpus all\n          #\n          # So, the correct check here is to query one of the missing piece of info like\n          # GPU name, so that the command can fail accordingly\n          nvidia-smi --query-gpu=gpu_name --format=csv,noheader --id=0\n          NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n          # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n          if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n            echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n            .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n          fi\n\n          # For runner with multiple GPUs, we also want to confirm that the number of GPUs are the\n          # power of 2, i.e. 1, 2, 4, or 8. This is to avoid flaky test issue when one GPU fails\n          # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/4000\n          GPU_COUNT=$(nvidia-smi --list-gpus | wc -l)\n          NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS=$?\n\n          # These are acceptable return code from nvidia-smi as copied from setup-nvidia GitHub action\n          if [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 0 ] && [ \"$NVIDIA_SMI_STATUS\" -ne 14 ]; then\n            echo \"NVIDIA driver installation has failed, shutting down the runner...\"\n            .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n          fi\n\n          # Check the GPU count to be a power of 2\n          if [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -le 8 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 1 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 2 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 4 ] && [ \"$GPU_COUNT\" -ne 8 ]; then\n            echo \"NVIDIA driver detects $GPU_COUNT GPUs. The runner has a broken GPU, shutting it down...\"\n            .github/scripts/stop_runner_service.sh\n          fi\n\n      - name: Cleanup docker\n        if: always() && inputs.build-environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\n          docker stop -a || true\n          docker kill -a || true\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml"

{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# Read the list of files to update\nwith open('/tmp/files_to_update.txt', 'r') as f:\n    all_files = [line.strip() for line in f if line.strip()]\n\n# Files already done\ndone_files = {\n    '.ci/pytorch/common_utils.sh',\n    '.github/ci_commit_pins/xla.txt',\n    '.github/scripts/filter_test_configs.py',\n    '.github/templates/common.yml.j2',\n    '.github/templates/linux_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2',\n    '.github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2',\n    '.github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2',\n    '.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml',\n    '.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml',\n    '.github/workflows/_xpu-test.yml',\n    '.github/workflows/pull.yml',\n}\n\nremaining = [f for f in all_files if f not in done_files]\n\nprint(f\"Total files: {len(all_files)}\")\nprint(f\"Done: {len(done_files)}\")\nprint(f\"Remaining: {len(remaining)}\")\n\n# Save remaining files to a file for batch processing\nwith open('/tmp/remaining_files.txt', 'w') as f:\n    for file in remaining:\n        f.write(file + '\\n')\n\nprint(\"\\nRemaining files saved to /tmp/remaining_files.txt\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}

65. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'PYTHON'\nimport subprocess\nimport json\n\n# Read remaining files\nwith open('/tmp/remaining_files.txt', 'r') as f:\n    files = [line.strip() for line in f if line.strip()]\n\n# Process files in chunks\nchunk_size = 10\nfor i in range(0, len(files), chunk_size):\n    chunk = files[i:i+chunk_size]\n    print(f\"\\nProcessing files {i+1}-{min(i+chunk_size, len(files))}\")\n    \n    # Create a batch script\n    script = \"#!/bin/bash\\nset -e\\n\\n\"\n    \n    for file_path in chunk:\n        # Create directory structure\n        safe_dir = '/workspace/' + '/'.join(file_path.split('/')[:-1])\n        safe_file = '/workspace/' + file_path\n        \n        script += f\"\"\"\n# Processing {file_path}\nmkdir -p {safe_dir}\n\"\"\"\n    \n    with open(f'/tmp/batch_{i}.sh', 'w') as f:\n        f.write(script)\n\nprint(f\"Created {(len(files) + chunk_size - 1) // chunk_size} batch scripts\")\n\nPYTHON\n"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Processing files 1-10\n\nProcessing files 11-20\n\nProcessing files 21-30\n\nProcessing files 31-40\n\nProcessing files 41-50\n\nProcessing files 51-60\n\nProcessing files 61-70\n\nProcessing files 71-80\n\nProcessing files 81-89\nCreated 9 batch scripts"
}

Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: bazel\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       docker-image-name:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Name of the base docker image to build with.\\n14:       cuda-version:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: What CUDA version to build with (i.e. \\\"11.7\\\"), \\\"cpu\\\" for none.\\n18:       sync-tag:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: |\\n23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n25:       test-matrix:\\n26:         required: true\\n27:         type: string\\n28:         description: |\\n29:           A JSON description of what configs to run later on.\\n30:       runner:\\n31:         required: false\\n32:         type: string\\n33:         default: \\\"linux.large\\\"\\n34:         description: Runner type\\n35: \\n36: env:\\n37:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n38: \\n39: jobs:\\n40:   filter:\\n41:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n42:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}\\n43:     outputs:\\n44:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n45:       is-test-matrix-empty: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty }}\\n46:       keep-going: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.keep-going }}\\n47:       reenabled-issues: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n48:     steps:\\n49:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n50:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n51:         with:\\n52:           fetch-depth: 1\\n53:           submodules: false\\n54: \\n55:       - name: Select all requested test configurations\\n56:         id: filter\\n57:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n58:         with:\\n59:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n60:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n61: \\n62:   build-and-test:\\n63:     needs: filter\\n64:     # Don't run on forked repos.\\n65:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && needs.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False'\\n66:     strategy:\\n67:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(needs.filter.outputs.test-matrix) }}\\n68:       fail-fast: false\\n69:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n70:     steps:\\n71:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n72:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n73:         with:\\n74:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n75: \\n76:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n77:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n78:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n79: \\n80:       - name: Setup Linux\\n81:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n82: \\n83:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n84:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n85:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n86:         with:\\n87:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image-name }}\\n88: \\n89:       - name: Pull docker image\\n90:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n91:         with:\\n92:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n93: \\n94:       - name: Check if in a container runner\\n95:         shell: bash\\n96:         id: check_container_runner\\n97:         run: echo \\\"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n98: \\n99:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\\n100:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\\n101: \\n102:       - name: Output disk space left\\n103:         run: |\\n104:           sudo df -H\\n105: \\n106:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\\n107:         run: |\\n108:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n109:           env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n110: \\n111:       - name: Parse ref\\n112:         id: parse-ref\\n113:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n114: \\n115:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n116:         id: get-job-id\\n117:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n118:         if: always()\\n119:         with:\\n120:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n121: \\n122:       - name: Build\\n123:         env:\\n124:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n125:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n126:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n127:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n128:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n129:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n130:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n131:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n132:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n133:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n134:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ needs.filter.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n135:           # TODO duplicated\\n136:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n137:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n138:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\\n139:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\\n140:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: 5.2\\n141:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n142:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n143:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n144:         run: |\\n145:           export SHARD_NUMBER=0\\n146:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n147:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\\n148:           # Make sure we copy test results from bazel-testlogs symlink to\\n149:           # a regular directory ./test/test-reports\\n150:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086\\n151:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n152:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n153:             -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\\\\n154:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n155:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n156:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\\\\n157:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\\\\n158:             -e GITHUB_JOB \\\\\\n159:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\\\\n160:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\\\\n161:             -e JOB_ID \\\\\\n162:             -e GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH=\\\"$GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH\\\" \\\\\\n163:             -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\\\\n164:             -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\\\\n165:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n166:             -e SCCACHE_BUCKET \\\\\\n167:             -e SCCACHE_REGION \\\\\\n168:             -e SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1 \\\\\\n169:             -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\\\\n170:             -e TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST \\\\\\n171:             -e OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID \\\\\\n172:             -e CUDA_VERSION \\\\\\n173:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n174:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n175:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n176:             --shm-size=\\\"1g\\\" \\\\\\n177:             --tty \\\\\\n178:             --detach \\\\\\n179:             --user jenkins \\\\\\n180:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n181:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n182:             \\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n183:           )\\n184:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/build.sh'\\n185:           echo \\\"container_id=${container_name}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n186: \\n187:       - name: Test\\n188:         id: test\\n189:         # Time out the test phase after 3.5 hours\\n190:         timeout-minutes: 120\\n191:         run: |\\n192:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_id}\\\" sh -c '.ci/pytorch/test.sh && cp -Lr ./bazel-testlogs ./test/test-reports'\\n193: \\n194:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n195:         shell: bash\\n196:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n197:         run: |\\n198:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n199: \\n200:       - name: Chown workspace\\n201:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n202:         if: always()\\n203: \\n204:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n205:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n206:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n207:         with:\\n208:           file-suffix: bazel-${{ github.job }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n209: \\n210:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n212:         if: always()\\n213: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_bazel-build-test.yml\"}"

  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-binary-build\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build_name:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: The build's name\\n10:       build_environment:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: The build environment\\n14:       runner_prefix:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n17:         type: string\\n18:         description: prefix for runner label\\n19:       runs_on:\\n20:         required: false\\n21:         default: linux.12xlarge.memory.ephemeral\\n22:         type: string\\n23:         description: Hardware to run this \\\"build\\\" job on, linux.12xlarge or linux.arm64.2xlarge.\\n24:       timeout-minutes:\\n25:         required: false\\n26:         default: 240\\n27:         type: number\\n28:         description: timeout for the job\\n29:       ALPINE_IMAGE:\\n30:         required: false\\n31:         type: string\\n32:         default: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n33:         description: Alpine image to use\\n34:       PYTORCH_ROOT:\\n35:         required: true\\n36:         type: string\\n37:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository\\n38:       PACKAGE_TYPE:\\n39:         required: true\\n40:         type: string\\n41:         description: Package type\\n42:       DESIRED_CUDA:\\n43:         required: true\\n44:         type: string\\n45:         description: Desired Cuda version\\n46:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\\n47:         required: false\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         description: GPU Arch version\\n50:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\\n51:         required: true\\n52:         type: string\\n53:         description: GPU Arch type\\n54:       DOCKER_IMAGE:\\n55:         required: true\\n56:         type: string\\n57:         description: Docker image to use\\n58:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\\n59:         required: true\\n60:         type: string\\n61:         description: Docker image tag to use\\n62:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\\n63:         required: false\\n64:         type: string\\n65:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\\n66:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\\n67:         required: false\\n68:         type: string\\n69:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\\n70:       DESIRED_PYTHON:\\n71:         required: false\\n72:         type: string\\n73:         description: Desired python version\\n74:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS:\\n75:         required: false\\n76:         type: string\\n77:         description: Extra install requirements\\n78:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n79:     secrets:\\n80:       github-token:\\n81:         required: true\\n82:         description: Github Token\\n83: \\n84: permissions:\\n85:   id-token: write\\n86: \\n87: jobs:\\n88:   build:\\n89:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}\\n90:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n91:     env:\\n92:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\\n93:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\\n94:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n95:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n96:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\n97:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\\n98:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\\n99:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\n100:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n101:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\\n102:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\\n103:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\\n104:       PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}\\n105:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n106:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n107:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\\n108:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}\\n109:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\\n110:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n111:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\\n112:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n113:     steps:\\n114:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)\\n115:         shell: bash\\n116:         run: |\\n117:           {\\n118:             echo \\\"PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\\\"\\n119:             echo \\\"PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\\\"\\n120:             echo \\\"DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\\"\\n121:             echo \\\"GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\\\"\\n122:             echo \\\"GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\\\"\\n123:             echo \\\"DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\\"\\n124:             echo \\\"SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}\\\"\\n125:             echo \\\"LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\\\"\\n126:             echo \\\"LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\\\"\\n127:             echo \\\"DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\\\"\\n128:             echo \\\"PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS=${{ env.PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS }}\\\"\\n129:             echo \\\"ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\\"\\n130:             echo \\\"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\"BUILD_NAME=${{ env.BUILD_NAME }}\\\"\\n134:             echo \\\"PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}\\\"\\n135:             echo \\\"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\\\"\\n136:             echo \\\"SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}\\\"\\n137:           } >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV} }}\\\"\\n138: \\n139:       - name: List the env\\n140:         shell: bash\\n141:         run: env\\n142: \\n143:       - name: \\\"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\\\"\\n144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n145:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n146:         continue-on-error: true\\n147:         with:\\n148:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\\n149: \\n150:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n151:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n152:         with:\\n153:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment ==
