'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n154: \\n155:       - name: Setup Linux\\n156:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n157:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n158: \\n159:       - name: Chown workspace\\n160:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n161:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n162:         with:\\n163:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n164: \\n165:       - name: Clean workspace\\n166:         shell: bash\\n167:         run: |\\n168:           set -eux\\n169: \\n170:           rm -rf \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"\\n171:           mkdir \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"\\n172: \\n173:           if [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' ]] || [[ ${{ inputs.build_environment }} == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' ]] ; then\\n174:             rm -rf \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\\\"\\n175:             mkdir \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\\\"\\n176:           fi\\n177: \\n178:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir\\n179:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\\n180:         with:\\n181:           submodules: recursive\\n182:           path: pytorch\\n183:           show-progress: false\\n184: \\n185:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\\n186:         run: |\\n187:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\\n188:           git clean -fxd\\n189:         working-directory: pytorch\\n190: \\n191:       - name: Check if the job is disabled\\n192:         id: filter\\n193:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n194:         with:\\n195:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n196:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the\\n197:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled\\n198:           test-matrix: |\\n199:             { include: [\\n200:               { config: \\\"default\\\" },\\n201:             ]}\\n202: \\n203:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n204:         id: aws_creds\\n205:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\\n206:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n207:         with:\\n208:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n209:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n210:           role-duration-seconds: 18000\\n211: \\n212:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n213:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n214:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n215:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n216:         with:\\n217:           # If doing this in release/2.10 or release branch, use docker.io. Otherwise\\n218:           # use ECR\\n219:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\\n220:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\n221:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}\\n222:           # The build.sh script in this folder is not actually the correct one,\\n223:           # this is just needed for sha calculation\\n224:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\\n225:           working-directory: pytorch\\n226: \\n227:       - name: Pull Docker image\\n228:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n229:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n230:         with:\\n231:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n232: \\n233:       - name: Build PyTorch binary\\n234:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\\n235:         env:\\n236:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}\\n237:         run: |\\n238:           set -x\\n239:           mkdir -p artifacts/\\n240:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n241:             -e BINARY_ENV_FILE \\\\\\n242:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n243:             -e DESIRED_CUDA \\\\\\n244:             -e DESIRED_PYTHON \\\\\\n245:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n246:             -e GPU_ARCH_TYPE \\\\\\n247:             -e GPU_ARCH_VERSION \\\\\\n248:             -e LIBTORCH_VARIANT \\\\\\n249:             -e PACKAGE_TYPE \\\\\\n250:             -e PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR \\\\\\n251:             -e PYTORCH_ROOT \\\\\\n252:             -e SKIP_ALL_TESTS \\\\\\n253:             -e PYTORCH_EXTRA_INSTALL_REQUIREMENTS \\\\\\n254:             --tty \\\\\\n255:             --detach \\\\\\n256:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/pytorch:/pytorch\\\" \\\\\\n257:             -v \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/artifacts\\\" \\\\\\n258:             -w / \\\\\\n259:             \\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n260:           )\\n261:           docker exec -t -w \\\"${PYTORCH_ROOT}\\\" \\\"${container_name}\\\" bash -c \\\"bash .circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\\\"\\n262:           # Unified build script for all architectures (x86_64, aarch64, s390x)\\n263:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" bash -c \\\"source ${BINARY_ENV_FILE} && bash /pytorch/.ci/${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}/build.sh\\\"\\n264: \\n265:       - name: Chown artifacts\\n266:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n267:         shell: bash\\n268:         run: |\\n269:           # Ensure the working directory gets chowned back to the current user\\n270:           docker run --rm -v \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts:/v\\\" -w /v \\\"${ALPINE_IMAGE}\\\" chown -R \\\"$(id -u):$(id -g)\\\" .\\n271: \\n272:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@50769540e7f4bd5e21e526ee35c689e35e0d6874 # v4.4.0\\n273:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\\n274:         with:\\n275:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\\n276:           if-no-files-found: error\\n277:           path:\\n278:             ${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/*\\n279: \\n280:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n281:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n282:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n283: \\n284:       - name: Chown workspace\\n285:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n286:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n287:         with:\\n288:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n289: \\n290:       - name: Cleanup docker\\n291:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n292:         shell: bash\\n293:         run: |\\n294:           # on s390x stop the container for clean worker stop\\n295:           # ignore expansion of \\\"docker ps -q\\\" since it could be empty\\n296:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046\\n297:           docker stop $(docker ps -q) || true\\n298: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\"}"
70. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: linux-binary-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build_name:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: The build's name\\n10:       build_environment:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: The build environment\\n14:       ALPINE_IMAGE:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         default: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n18:       PYTORCH_ROOT:\\n19:         required: true\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository\\n22:       PACKAGE_TYPE:\\n23:         required: true\\n24:         type: string\\n25:         description: Package type\\n26:       DESIRED_CUDA:\\n27:         required: true\\n28:         type: string\\n29:         description: Desired Cuda version\\n30:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\\n31:         required: false\\n32:         type: string\\n33:         description: GPU Arch version\\n34:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\\n35:         required: true\\n36:         type: string\\n37:         description: GPU Arch type\\n38:       DOCKER_IMAGE:\\n39:         required: true\\n40:         type: string\\n41:         description: Docker image to use\\n42:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\\n43:         required: true\\n44:         type: string\\n45:         description: Docker image tag to use\\n46:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\\n47:         required: false\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\\n50:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\\n51:         required: false\\n52:         type: string\\n53:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\\n54:       DESIRED_PYTHON:\\n55:         required: false\\n56:         type: string\\n57:         description: Desired python version\\n58:       runner_prefix:\\n59:         required: false\\n60:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n61:         type: string\\n62:         description: prefix for runner label\\n63:       runs_on:\\n64:         required: true\\n65:         type: string\\n66:         description: Hardware to run this job on. Valid values are linux.4xlarge, linux.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu, linux.arm64.2xlarge, and linux.rocm.gpu\\n67:     secrets:\\n68:       github-token:\\n69:         required: true\\n70:         description: Github Token\\n71: \\n72: permissions:\\n73:   id-token: write\\n74: \\n75: jobs:\\n76:   test:\\n77:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix}}${{ inputs.runs_on }}\\n78:     timeout-minutes: 240\\n79:     env:\\n80:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ inputs.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\\n81:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\\n82:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n83:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n84:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\n85:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\\n86:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\\n87:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\n88:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n89:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\\n90:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\\n91:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\\n92:       ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n93:       AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n94:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\\n95:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build_environment }}\\n96:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n97:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n98:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\\n99:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n100:     steps:\\n101:       - name: Make the env permanent during this workflow (but not the secrets)\\n102:         shell: bash\\n103:         run: |\\n104:           {\\n105:             echo \\\"PYTORCH_ROOT=${{ env.PYTORCH_ROOT }}\\\"\\n106:             echo \\\"PACKAGE_TYPE=${{ env.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\\\"\\n107: \\n108:             echo \\\"DESIRED_CUDA=${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\\"\\n109:             echo \\\"GPU_ARCH_VERSION=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\\\"\\n110:             echo \\\"GPU_ARCH_TYPE=${{ env.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\\\"\\n111:             echo \\\"DOCKER_IMAGE=${{ env.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\\"\\n112:             echo \\\"SKIP_ALL_TESTS=${{ env.SKIP_ALL_TESTS }}\\\"\\n113:             echo \\\"LIBTORCH_CONFIG=${{ env.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\\\"\\n114:             echo \\\"LIBTORCH_VARIANT=${{ env.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\\\"\\n115:             echo \\\"DESIRED_PYTHON=${{ env.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\\\"\\n116: \\n117:             echo \\\"ALPINE_IMAGE=${{ env.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\\"\\n118:             echo \\\"AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=${{ env.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION }}\\\"\\n119:             echo \\\"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${{ env.BINARY_ENV_FILE }}\\\"\\n120:             echo \\\"BUILD_ENVIRONMENT=${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}\\\"\\n121:             echo \\\"PR_NUMBER=${{ env.PR_NUMBER }}\\\"\\n122:             echo \\\"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\\\"\\n123:             echo \\\"SHA1=${{ env.SHA1 }}\\\"\\n124:           } >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV} }}\\\"\\n125: \\n126:       - name: \\\"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\\\"\\n127:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n128:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n129:         continue-on-error: true\\n130:         with:\\n131:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\\n132: \\n133:         # Setup the environment\\n134:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n135:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n136:         with:\\n137:           no-sudo: ${{ inputs.build_environment == 'linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel' || inputs.build_environment == 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n138: \\n139:       - name: Setup Linux\\n140:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n141:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n142: \\n143:       - name: Chown workspace\\n144:         if: inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n145:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n146:         with:\\n147:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n148: \\n149:       - name: Clean workspace\\n150:         shell: bash\\n151:         run: |\\n152:           rm -rf \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"\\n153:           mkdir \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"\\n154: \\n155:       - name: Checkout PyTorch to pytorch dir\\n156:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\\n157:         with:\\n158:           submodules: recursive\\n159:           show-progress: false\\n160:           path: pytorch\\n161: \\n162:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\\n163:         run: |\\n164:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\\n165:           git clean -fxd\\n166:         working-directory: pytorch\\n167: \\n168:       - name: Check if the job is disabled\\n169:         id: filter\\n170:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n171:         with:\\n172:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n173:           # NB: Use a mock test matrix with a default value here. After filtering, if the\\n174:           # returned matrix is empty, it means that the job is disabled\\n175:           test-matrix: |\\n176:             { include: [\\n177:               { config: \\\"default\\\" },\\n178:             ]}\\n179: \\n180:       - name: Download Build Artifacts\\n181:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\\n182:         uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7\\n183:         with:\\n184:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\\n185:           path: \\\"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\\\"\\n186: \\n187:       - name: Install nvidia driver, nvidia-docker runtime, set GPU_FLAG\\n188:         id: install-nvidia-driver\\n189:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-nvidia@release/2.10\\n190:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\\n191: \\n192:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n193:         id: aws_creds\\n194:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\\n195:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n196:         with:\\n197:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n198:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n199:           role-duration-seconds: 18000\\n200: \\n201:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n202:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n203:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n204:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n205:         with:\\n206:           docker-registry: ${{ startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') && '308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com' || 'docker.io' }}\\n207:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\n208:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX }}\\n209:           docker-build-dir: .ci/docker\\n210:           working-directory: pytorch\\n211: \\n212:       - name: Pull Docker image\\n213:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel' }}\\n214:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n215:         with:\\n216:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n217: \\n218:       - name: Test Pytorch binary\\n219:         if: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' }}\\n220:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/test-pytorch-binary\\n221:         env:\\n222:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image || format('{0}:{1}', inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE, inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX) }}\\n223: \\n224:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n225:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n226:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n227: \\n228:       - name: Chown workspace\\n229:         if: always() && inputs.build_environment != 'linux-s390x-binary-manywheel'\\n230:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n231:         with:\\n232:           ALPINE_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.ALPINE_IMAGE }}\\n233: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-test-linux.yml\"}"
71. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: upload\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build_name:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: The build's name\\n10:       use_s3:\\n11:         type: boolean\\n12:         default: true\\n13:         description: If true, will download artifacts from s3. Otherwise will use the default GitHub artifact download action\\n14:       PYTORCH_ROOT:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: Root directory for the pytorch/pytorch repository. Not actually needed, but currently passing it in since we pass in the same inputs to the reusable workflows of all binary builds\\n18:       PACKAGE_TYPE:\\n19:         required: true\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         description: Package type\\n22:       DESIRED_CUDA:\\n23:         required: true\\n24:         type: string\\n25:         description: Desired CUDA version\\n26:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION:\\n27:         required: false\\n28:         type: string\\n29:         description: GPU Arch version\\n30:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE:\\n31:         required: true\\n32:         type: string\\n33:         description: GPU Arch type\\n34:       DOCKER_IMAGE:\\n35:         required: false\\n36:         type: string\\n37:         description: Docker image to use\\n38:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX:\\n39:         required: false\\n40:         type: string\\n41:         description: Docker image tag to use\\n42:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG:\\n43:         required: false\\n44:         type: string\\n45:         description: Desired libtorch config (for libtorch builds only)\\n46:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT:\\n47:         required: false\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         description: Desired libtorch variant (for libtorch builds only)\\n50:       DESIRED_PYTHON:\\n51:         required: false\\n52:         type: string\\n53:         description: Desired python version\\n54:     secrets:\\n55:       github-token:\\n56:         required: true\\n57:         description: Github Token\\n58: \\n59: jobs:\\n60:   upload:\\n61:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04\\n62:     container:\\n63:       image: continuumio/miniconda3:4.12.0\\n64:     env:\\n65:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n66:       PACKAGE_TYPE: ${{ inputs.PACKAGE_TYPE }}\\n67:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n68:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n69:       DESIRED_CUDA: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\n70:       GPU_ARCH_VERSION: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_VERSION }}\\n71:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: ${{ inputs.GPU_ARCH_TYPE }}\\n72:       DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.DOCKER_IMAGE }}\\n73:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n74:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_CONFIG }}\\n75:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: ${{ inputs.LIBTORCH_VARIANT }}\\n76:       DESIRED_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.DESIRED_PYTHON }}\\n77:       BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\\n78:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.github-token }}\\n79:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n80:       PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\\n81:       SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n82:     steps:\\n83:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n84:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n85:         with:\\n86:           no-sudo: true\\n87: \\n88:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for nightly\\n89:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' }}\\n90:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n91:         with:\\n92:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_nightly_build_wheels\\n93:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n94: \\n95:       - name: Configure AWS credentials(PyTorch account) for RC builds\\n96:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' &&  (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/')) }}\\n97:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n98:         with:\\n99:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::749337293305:role/gha_workflow_test_build_wheels\\n100:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n101: \\n102:       - name: Download Build Artifacts\\n103:         id: download-artifacts\\n104:         # NB: When the previous build job is skipped, there won't be any artifacts and\\n105:         # this step will fail. Binary build jobs can only be skipped on CI, not nightly\\n106:         continue-on-error: true\\n107:         uses: actions/download-artifact@65a9edc5881444af0b9093a5e628f2fe47ea3b2e # v4.1.7\\n108:         with:\\n109:           name: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\\n110:           path: \\\"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts/\\\"\\n111: \\n112:       - name: Set DRY_RUN (only for tagged pushes)\\n113:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.event.ref == 'refs/heads/nightly' || (startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/'))) }}\\n114:         run: |\\n115:           echo \\\"DRY_RUN=disabled\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_ENV\\\"\\n116: \\n117:       - name: Set UPLOAD_CHANNEL (only for tagged pushes)\\n118:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/') && !startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/ciflow/') }}\\n119:         shell: bash -e -l {0}\\n120:         run: |\\n121:           # reference ends with an RC suffix\\n122:           if [[ \\\"${GITHUB_REF_NAME}\\\" = *-rc[0-9]* ]]; then\\n123:             echo \\\"UPLOAD_CHANNEL=test\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_ENV\\\"\\n124:           fi\\n125: \\n126:       - name: Upload binaries\\n127:         if: steps.download-artifacts.outcome && steps.download-artifacts.outcome == 'success'\\n128:         shell: bash\\n129:         env:\\n130:           PKG_DIR: \\\"${{ runner.temp }}/artifacts\\\"\\n131:           UPLOAD_SUBFOLDER: \\\"${{ env.DESIRED_CUDA }}\\\"\\n132:           BUILD_NAME: ${{ inputs.build_name }}\\n133:         run: |\\n134:             set -ex\\n135:             bash .circleci/scripts/binary_upload.sh\\n136: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\"}"
72. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_docs.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: build docs\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       docker-image:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n14:       push:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: boolean\\n17:         default: false\\n18:         description: If set, push the docs to the docs website.\\n19:       run-doxygen:\\n20:         required: false\\n21:         type: boolean\\n22:         default: false\\n23:         description: If set, will enable C++ API doc generation using doxygen / breathe / exhale.\\n24:       sync-tag:\\n25:         required: false\\n26:         type: string\\n27:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n28:         description: |\\n29:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n30:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n31:       s3-bucket:\\n32:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         type: string\\n35:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n36:       aws-role-to-assume:\\n37:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n38:         required: false\\n39:         type: string\\n40:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n41:       upload-aws-role-to-assume:\\n42:         description: role to assume for downloading artifacts\\n43:         required: false\\n44:         type: string\\n45:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n46:       runner_prefix:\\n47:         description: prefix for runner label\\n48:         type: string\\n49:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n50:     secrets:\\n51:       GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN:\\n52:         required: false\\n53:         description: Permissions for pushing to the docs site.\\n54: \\n55: jobs:\\n56:   build-docs:\\n57:     # Don't run on forked repos.\\n58:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n59:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n60:     environment: ${{ (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'pytorchbot-env' || '' }}\\n61:     strategy:\\n62:       fail-fast: false\\n63:       matrix:\\n64:         include:\\n65:           - docs_type: cpp\\n66:             # We recently seeing lots of exit code 137 running this in Docker indicating\\n67:             # an OOM issue when running the job, so this upgrades the runner from 4xlarge\\n68:             # to the next available tier of 12xlarge. So much memory just to generate cpp\\n69:             # doc\\n70:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.12xlarge.memory\\n71:             # TODO: Nightly cpp docs take longer and longer to finish (more than 3h now)\\n72:             # Let's try to figure out how this can be improved\\n73:             timeout-minutes: 360\\n74:           - docs_type: python\\n75:             runner: ${{ inputs.runner_prefix }}linux.c7i.2xlarge\\n76:             # It takes less than 30m to finish python docs unless there are issues\\n77:             timeout-minutes: 30\\n78:     # Set a fixed name for this job instead of using the current matrix-generated name, i.e. build-docs (cpp, linux.12xlarge, 180)\\n79:     # The current name requires updating the database last docs push query from test-infra every time the matrix is updated\\n80:     name: build-docs-${{ matrix.docs_type }}-${{ inputs.push }}\\n81:     steps:\\n82:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n83:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n84:         with:\\n85:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n86:           instructions: |\\n87:             All builds are done inside the container, to start an interactive session run:\\n88:               docker exec -it $(docker container ps --format '{{.ID}}') bash\\n89:             To start Python docs build type:\\n90:               cd docs && make html && make coverage\\n91: \\n92:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n93:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n94:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n95: \\n96:       - name: Setup Linux\\n97:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n98: \\n99:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n100:         if : ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\\n101:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n102:         with:\\n103:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.aws-role-to-assume }}\\n104:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test\\n105:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n106: \\n107:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n108:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n109:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n110:         with:\\n111:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n112: \\n113:       - name: Pull docker image\\n114:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n115:         with:\\n116:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n117: \\n118:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n119:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n120:         with:\\n121:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n122:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n123: \\n124:       - name: Generate netrc (only for docs-push)\\n125:         if: inputs.push\\n126:         env:\\n127:           GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}\\n128:         run: |\\n129:           # sometimes .netrc exists as a directory even though this is the temp folder\\n130:           rm -rf \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\\\"\\n131:           # set credentials for https pushing\\n132:           echo \\\"machine github.com\\\" > \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\\\"\\n133:           echo \\\"login pytorchbot\\\" >> \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\\\"\\n134:           echo \\\"password ${GITHUB_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN}\\\" >> \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\\\"\\n135: \\n136:       - name: Build ${{ matrix.docs_type }} docs\\n137:         timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.timeout-minutes }}\\n138:         id: build-docs\\n139:         env:\\n140:           # After https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/88373, pull workflow can now be run periodically,\\n141:           # so using a schedule event to determine if the docs should be pushed or not doesn't hold true\\n142:           # anymore\\n143:           WITH_PUSH: ${{ inputs.push }}\\n144:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n145:           DOCS_TYPE: ${{ matrix.docs_type }}\\n146:           RUN_DOXYGEN: ${{ inputs.run-doxygen }}\\n147:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n148:         run: |\\n149:           set -ex\\n150:           # Convert refs/tags/v1.12.0rc3 into 1.12\\n151:           if [[ \\\"${GITHUB_REF}\\\" =~ ^refs/tags/v([0-9]+\\\\.[0-9]+)\\\\.* ]]; then\\n152:             target=\\\"${BASH_REMATCH[1]}\\\"\\n153:           else\\n154:             target=\\\"main\\\"\\n155:           fi\\n156:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n157:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n158:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n159:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n160:             -e SHA1=\\\"$GITHUB_SHA\\\" \\\\\\n161:             -e DOCS_VERSION=\\\"${target}\\\" \\\\\\n162:             -e DOCS_TYPE \\\\\\n163:             -e RUN_DOXYGEN \\\\\\n164:             -e WITH_PUSH \\\\\\n165:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n166:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n167:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n168:             --tty \\\\\\n169:             --detach \\\\\\n170:             --user jenkins \\\\\\n171:             -v \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/.netrc\\\":/var/lib/jenkins/.netrc \\\\\\n172:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n173:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n174:             \\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\"\\n175:           )\\n176:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" bash -c \\\"sudo chown -R jenkins . && pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ./.ci/pytorch/${DOCS_TYPE}_doc_push_script.sh\\\"\\n177: \\n178:       - name: Chown workspace\\n179:         uses: ./.github/actions/chown-workspace\\n180:         if: always()\\n181: \\n182:       - name: configure aws credentials\\n183:         if : ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume != '' }}\\n184:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n185:         with:\\n186:           role-to-assume: ${{ inputs.upload-aws-role-to-assume }}\\n187:           role-session-name: gha-linux-test\\n188:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n189: \\n190:       - name: Upload Python Docs Preview\\n191:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n192:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'python' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}\\n193:         with:\\n194:           retention-days: 14\\n195:           s3-bucket: doc-previews\\n196:           if-no-files-found: error\\n197:           path: pytorch_docs/main/\\n198:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n199: \\n200:       - name: Upload C++ Docs Preview\\n201:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n202:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && matrix.docs_type == 'cpp' && steps.build-docs.outcome == 'success' }}\\n203:         with:\\n204:           retention-days: 14\\n205:           if-no-files-found: error\\n206:           s3-bucket: doc-previews\\n207:           path: cppdocs/\\n208:           s3-prefix: pytorch/pytorch/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/cppdocs\\n209: \\n210:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n211:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n212:         if: always()\\n213: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_docs.yml\"}"
73. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_link_check.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: on:\\n2:   workflow_call:\\n3:     inputs:\\n4:       runner:\\n5:         type: string\\n6:         required: true\\n7:       ref:\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         required: true\\n10: \\n11: jobs:\\n12:   lint-urls:\\n13:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-url-lint') }}\\n14:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n15:     with:\\n16:       job-name: lint-urls\\n17:       timeout: 120\\n18:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge\\n19:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n20:       fetch-depth: 0\\n21:       submodules: false\\n22:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}\\n23:       script: |\\n24:         ./scripts/lint_urls.sh $(\\n25:           if [ \\\"${{ github.event_name }}\\\" = \\\"pull_request\\\" ]; then\\n26:             echo \\\"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\\\" \\\"${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\\\"\\n27:           else\\n28:             echo \\\"${{ github.event.before }}\\\" \\\"${{ github.sha }}\\\"\\n29:           fi\\n30:         ) || {\\n31:           echo\\n32:           echo \\\"URL lint failed.\\\"\\n33:           echo \\\"If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \\\\`skip-url-lint\\\\` label to your PR.\\\"\\n34:           echo \\\"Or add \\\\`@lint-ignore\\\\` somewhere on the same line as the URL you want to skip checking.\\\"\\n35:           exit 1\\n36:         }\\n37: \\n38:   lint-xrefs:\\n39:     if: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' || !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-xref-lint') }}\\n40:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n41:     with:\\n42:       job-name: lint-xrefs\\n43:       timeout: 60\\n44:       runner: ${{ inputs.runner }}linux.2xlarge\\n45:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n46:       fetch-depth: 0\\n47:       submodules: false\\n48:       ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}\\n49:       script: |\\n50:         ./scripts/lint_xrefs.sh $(\\n51:           if [ \\\"${{ github.event_name }}\\\" = \\\"pull_request\\\" ]; then\\n52:             echo \\\"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\\\" \\\"${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\\\"\\n53:           else\\n54:             echo \\\"${{ github.event.before }}\\\" \\\"${{ github.sha }}\\\"\\n55:           fi\\n56:         ) || {\\n57:           echo\\n58:           echo \\\"Xref lint failed.\\\"\\n59:           echo \\\"If this is a transient outage, you can bypass it by adding the \\\\`skip-xref-lint\\\\` label to your PR.\\\"\\n60:           echo \\\"Or add \\\\`@lint-ignore\\\\` somewhere on the same line as the reference you want to skip checking.\\\"\\n61:           exit 1\\n62:         }\\n63: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_link_check.yml\"}"
74. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # The point of this workflow is to test that a FA3 wheel that was built based off the\\n2: # stable ABI as of torch nightly 20250830 can still run on the newer torch.\\n3: #\\n4: # This workflow is very similar to the _linux-test.yml workflow, with the following\\n5: # differences:\\n6: #   1. It is simpler (there is no test matrix)\\n7: #   2. It pulls flash-attention as a secondary repository in order to access the tests.\\n8: #      Note that it does not BUILD anything from flash-attention, as we have a prebuilt\\n9: #      wheel. We pull flash-attention only to run a few tests.\\n10: #   3. It runs only FA3 tests. No PyTorch tests are run.\\n11: name: linux-test-stable-fa3\\n12: \\n13: on:\\n14:   workflow_call:\\n15:     inputs:\\n16:       build-environment:\\n17:         required: true\\n18:         type: string\\n19:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n20:       docker-image:\\n21:         required: true\\n22:         type: string\\n23:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n24:       timeout-minutes:\\n25:         required: false\\n26:         type: number\\n27:         default: 30\\n28:         description: |\\n29:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n30:       s3-bucket:\\n31:         description: S3 bucket to download artifact\\n32:         required: false\\n33:         type: string\\n34:         default: \\\"gha-artifacts\\\"\\n35:     secrets:\\n36:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n37:         required: false\\n38:         description: |\\n39:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n40:       VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:\\n41:         required: false\\n42:         description: |\\n43:           HF Auth token to test vllm\\n44:       SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\\n45:         required: false\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\\n48: \\n49: env:\\n50:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n51: \\n52: jobs:\\n53:   test:\\n54:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n55:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n56:     runs-on: linux.aws.h100\\n57:     timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes || 30 }}\\n58:     permissions:\\n59:       id-token: write\\n60:       contents: read\\n61:     steps:\\n62:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n63:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n64:         with:\\n65:           no-sudo: true\\n66: \\n67:       - name: Checkout flash-attention as a secondary repository\\n68:         uses: actions/checkout@v4\\n69:         with:\\n70:           repository: Dao-AILab/flash-attention\\n71:           path: flash-attention\\n72: \\n73:       - name: Setup Linux\\n74:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n75: \\n76:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n77:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n78:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n79:         with:\\n80:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n81: \\n82:       - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\\n83:         id: print-ghcr-mirror\\n84:         env:\\n85:           ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n86:         shell: bash\\n87:         run: |\\n88:           tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\\n89:           echo \\\"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\\\"\\n90: \\n91:       - name: Pull docker image\\n92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n93:         with:\\n94:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n95: \\n96:       - name: Check if in a container runner\\n97:         shell: bash\\n98:         id: check_container_runner\\n99:         run: echo \\\"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n100: \\n101:       - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\\n102:         id: setup-gpu-flag\\n103:         run: echo \\\"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n104: \\n105:       - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\\n106:         id: setup-sscache-port-flag\\n107:         run: echo \\\"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n108:         if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\\n109: \\n110:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n111:         id: get-job-id\\n112:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n113:         if: always()\\n114:         with:\\n115:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n116: \\n117:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n118:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n119:         with:\\n120:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n121:           s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\\n122: \\n123:       - name: Parse ref\\n124:         id: parse-ref\\n125:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n126: \\n127:       - name: Set Test step time\\n128:         id: test-timeout\\n129:         shell: bash\\n130:         env:\\n131:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n132:         run: |\\n133:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n134: \\n135:       - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\\n136:         shell: bash\\n137:         run: |\\n138:           env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n139:           env | grep '^CI' >> \\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\"\\n140: \\n141:       - name: Test\\n142:         id: test\\n143:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n144:         env:\\n145:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n146:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n147:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n148:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n149:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n150:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n151:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n152:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n153:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n154:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n155:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n156:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n157:           BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\\n158:           SHM_SIZE: '2g'\\n159:           DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n160:           VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}\\n161:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n162:           SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\\n163:           ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n164:         run: |\\n165:           set -x\\n166: \\n167:           TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test_fa3_abi_stable.sh\\n168: \\n169:           # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\\n170:           TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \\\"%.3f \\\\n\\\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\\n171:           # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\\n172:           # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\\n173:           TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\\n174: \\n175: \\n176:           SHM_OPTS=\\\"--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}\\\"\\n177:           JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\\n178:           DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\\n179: \\n180:           # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\\n181:           # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\\n182:           # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice\\n183:           # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\\n184:           container_name=$(docker run \\\\\\n185:             ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n186:             ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\\\\n187:             -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\\\\n188:             -e PR_NUMBER \\\\\\n189:             -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\\\\n190:             -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\\\\n191:             -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\\\\n192:             -e GITHUB_JOB \\\\\\n193:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\\\\n194:             -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\\\\n195:             -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\\\\n196:             -e JOB_ID \\\\\\n197:             -e JOB_NAME \\\\\\n198:             -e BASE_SHA \\\\\\n199:             -e BRANCH \\\\\\n200:             -e SHA1 \\\\\\n201:             -e MAX_JOBS=\\\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\\\" \\\\\\n202:             -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\\\\n203:             -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \\\\\\n204:             -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\\\\n205:             -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \\\\\\n206:             --memory=\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\\\" \\\\\\n207:             --memory-swap=\\\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\\\" \\\\\\n208:             --env-file=\\\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\\\" \\\\\\n209:             --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\\\\n210:             --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\\\\n211:             --ipc=host \\\\\\n212:             ${SHM_OPTS} \\\\\\n213:             --tty \\\\\\n214:             --detach \\\\\\n215:             --name=\\\"${container_name}\\\" \\\\\\n216:             ${JENKINS_USER} \\\\\\n217:             -v \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\\\" \\\\\\n218:             -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\\\\n219:             \\\"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\\\" \\\\\\n220:             ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\\n221:           )\\n222: \\n223:           echo \\\"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n224: \\n225:           docker exec -t \\\"${container_name}\\\" sh -c \\\"python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\\\"\\n226: \\n227:       - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\\n228:         if: always()\\n229:         run: |\\n230:           # shellcheck disable=SC2156\\n231:           find . -iname \\\"core.[1-9]*\\\" -exec docker exec \\\"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\\\" sh -c \\\"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\\\" \\\\;\\n232: \\n233:       - name: Store Core dumps on S3\\n234:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n235:         if: failure()\\n236:         with:\\n237:           name: coredumps-fa3-stable-abi-smoke-tests\\n238:           retention-days: 14\\n239:           if-no-files-found: ignore\\n240:           path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\\n241: \\n242:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n243:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' }}\\n244:         continue-on-error: true\\n245:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n246:         with:\\n247:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n248:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n249:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n250:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n251:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n252: \\n253:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n254:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n255:         if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\\n256: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml\"}"
75. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_mac-build.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: mac-build\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       runner-type:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Name of the GitHub-managed runner type to use for the build.\\n14:       build-generates-artifacts:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: boolean\\n17:         description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\\n18:       xcode-version:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: What xcode version to build with.\\n23:       sync-tag:\\n24:         required: false\\n25:         type: string\\n26:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n27:         description: |\\n28:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n29:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n30:       python-version:\\n31:         required: false\\n32:         type: string\\n33:         default: \\\"3.12\\\"\\n34:         description: |\\n35:           The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default\\n36:       test-matrix:\\n37:         required: false\\n38:         type: string\\n39:         description: |\\n40:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\\n41:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\\n42:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\\n43:       sccache-use-gha:\\n44:         required: false\\n45:         type: boolean\\n46:         default: false\\n47:         description: If true, use the Github cache as the storage option for sccache instead of S3.\\n48: \\n49:     outputs:\\n50:       test-matrix:\\n51:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n52:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\\n53:       build-outcome:\\n54:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-outcome }}\\n55:         description: The outcome of the build step. This is used to influence test filtering logic later on.\\n56:       build-environment:\\n57:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n58:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n59: \\n60: jobs:\\n61:   build:\\n62:     # Don't run on forked repos.\\n63:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n64:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner-type }}\\n65:     env:\\n66:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n67:       SCCACHE_USE_GHA: ${{ inputs.sccache-use-gha }}  # this is placed here instead of the sccache step to appease actionlint\\n68:     outputs:\\n69:       build-outcome: ${{ steps.build.outcome }}\\n70:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n71:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n72:     steps:\\n73:       - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow\\n74:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\\n75: \\n76:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n77:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n78:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n79: \\n80:       - name: Set xcode version\\n81:         env:\\n82:           XCODE_VERSION: ${{ inputs.xcode-version }}\\n83:         run: |\\n84:           if [ -n \\\"${XCODE_VERSION}\\\" ]; then\\n85:             echo \\\"DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode_${XCODE_VERSION}.app/Contents/Developer\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n86:           fi\\n87: \\n88:       - name: Setup Python\\n89:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@release/2.10\\n90:         with:\\n91:           python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}\\n92:           pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\\n93: \\n94:       - name: Install sccache (only for non-forked PRs, and pushes to trunk)\\n95:         uses: nick-fields/retry@7152eba30c6575329ac0576536151aca5a72780e # v3.0.0\\n96:         if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository }}\\n97:         with:\\n98:           timeout_minutes: 5\\n99:           max_attempts: 3\\n100:           retry_wait_seconds: 90\\n101:           command: |\\n102:             set -ex\\n103: \\n104:             DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=0\\n105:             SCCACHE_VERSION=\\\"0.4.1\\\"\\n106:             LOCAL_PATH=\\\"/usr/local/bin\\\"\\n107: \\n108:             if [ ! -f \\\"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\\\" ]; then\\n109:               DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1\\n110:             else\\n111:               LOCAL_VERSION=$(\\\"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\\\" --version | cut -d\\\" \\\" -f2)\\n112: \\n113:               if [ \\\"${LOCAL_VERSION}\\\" != \\\"${SCCACHE_VERSION}\\\" ]; then\\n114:                 DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1\\n115:               fi\\n116:             fi\\n117: \\n118:             if [ \\\"${DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE}\\\" == \\\"1\\\" ]; then\\n119:               sudo curl --retry 3 --retry-all-errors \\\"https://s3.amazonaws.com/ossci-macos/sccache/sccache-v0.4.1-${RUNNER_ARCH}\\\" --output \\\"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\\\"\\n120:               sudo chmod +x \\\"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\\\"\\n121:             fi\\n122: \\n123:             if [[ \\\"${SCCACHE_USE_GHA}\\\" == \\\"true\\\" ]]; then\\n124:               echo \\\"ACTIONS_CACHE_URL=${ACTIONS_CACHE_URL}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n125:               echo \\\"ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN=${ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n126:               echo \\\"SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=on\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n127:             else\\n128:               # The runner has access to the S3 bucket via IAM profile without the need\\n129:               # for any credential\\n130:               echo \\\"SCCACHE_BUCKET=ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n131:               echo \\\"SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX=${GITHUB_WORKFLOW}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n132:             fi\\n133: \\n134:             # This is needed so that later build script could find sccache (which sccache)\\n135:             echo \\\"${LOCAL_PATH}\\\" >> $GITHUB_PATH\\n136: \\n137:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n138:         id: get-job-id\\n139:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n140:         if: always()\\n141:         with:\\n142:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n143: \\n144:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\\n145:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\\n146:         id: filter\\n147:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n148:         with:\\n149:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n150:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n151:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n152: \\n153:       - name: Build\\n154:         if: steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == ''\\n155:         id: build\\n156:         env:\\n157:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n158:         run: |\\n159:           # TODO: Remove me later, and properly activate venv\\n160:           PATH=\\\"$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH\\\"\\n161:           export PATH\\n162: \\n163:           # NB: Same trick as Linux, there is no need to initialize sccache with the risk of getting\\n164:           # it hangs or timeout at initialization. The cache will be started automatically\\n165:           export SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1\\n166:           .ci/pytorch/macos-build.sh\\n167: \\n168:       - name: Archive artifacts into zip\\n169:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n170:         run: |\\n171:           zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/.ninja_log build/compile_commands.json .additional_ci_files\\n172: \\n173:       - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on GHA\\n174:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n175:         if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n176:         with:\\n177:           name: ${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}\\n178:           retention-days: 14\\n179:           if-no-files-found: error\\n180:           path: artifacts.zip\\n181: \\n182:       - name: Upload sccache stats to GHA\\n183:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n184:         # Only if sccache is installed, see above\\n185:         if: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository) && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' }}\\n186:         with:\\n187:           name: sccache-stats-${{ inputs.build-environment }}-runattempt${{ github.run_attempt }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n188:           retention-days: 14\\n189:           if-no-files-found: warn\\n190:           path: sccache-stats-*.json\\n191: \\n192:       - name: Clean up disk space\\n193:         if: always()\\n194:         continue-on-error: true\\n195:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\\n196: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_mac-build.yml\"}"
76. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_mac-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: mac-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       test-matrix:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n14:       sync-tag:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n18:         description: |\\n19:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n20:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n21:       python-version:\\n22:         required: false\\n23:         type: string\\n24:         default: \\\"3.12\\\"\\n25:         description: |\\n26:           The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default\\n27:       timeout-minutes:\\n28:         required: false\\n29:         type: number\\n30:         default: 270\\n31:         description: |\\n32:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n33:       disable-monitor:\\n34:         description: |\\n35:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n36:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n37:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n38:         required: false\\n39:         type: boolean\\n40:         default: true\\n41:       monitor-log-interval:\\n42:         description: |\\n43:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n44:         required: false\\n45:         type: number\\n46:         default: 5\\n47:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n48:         description: |\\n49:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n50:         required: false\\n51:         type: number\\n52:         default: 1\\n53:     secrets:\\n54:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n55:         required: false\\n56:         description: |\\n57:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n58: \\n59: jobs:\\n60:   test:\\n61:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n62:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n63:     defaults:\\n64:       run:\\n65:         shell: bash -e -l {0}\\n66:     strategy:\\n67:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n68:       fail-fast: false\\n69:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n70:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n71:     env:\\n72:       GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n73:       BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n74:       TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n75:       SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n76:       NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n77:       PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}\\n78:     steps:\\n79:       - name: Print runner OS/HW info\\n80:         run: |\\n81:           sysctl machdep.cpu.brand_string kern.osproductversion\\n82: \\n83:       - name: Clean up leftover processes on MacOS pet runner\\n84:         continue-on-error: true\\n85:         run: |\\n86:           for PROCESS in \\\"python\\\" \\\"conda\\\" \\\"ninja\\\" \\\"clang\\\"; do\\n87:             echo \\\"Cleaning up all remaining ${PROCESS} process\\\"\\n88:             pkill \\\"${PROCESS}\\\" || true\\n89:           done\\n90: \\n91:       - name: Clean up brew miniconda, if installed\\n92:         continue-on-error: true\\n93:         run: |\\n94:           if brew list miniconda; then\\n95:             brew uninstall miniconda\\n96:             echo \\\"REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA=1\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n97:           fi\\n98: \\n99:       - name: Clean up leftover local python3 site-packages on MacOS pet runner\\n100:         continue-on-error: true\\n101:         run: |\\n102:           for dir in  ~/.local/lib/python3.*/site-packages; do\\n103:             echo \\\"Cleaning up ${dir}\\\"\\n104:             rm -rf \\\"${dir}\\\"\\n105:           done\\n106: \\n107:       - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow\\n108:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\\n109: \\n110:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n111:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n112:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n113: \\n114:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n115:         id: get-job-id\\n116:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n117:         if: always()\\n118:         with:\\n119:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n120: \\n121:       - name: Setup Python\\n122:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@release/2.10\\n123:         with:\\n124:           python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}\\n125:           pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\\n126: \\n127:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n128:         id: monitor-script\\n129:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n130:         continue-on-error: true\\n131:         env:\\n132:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n133:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n134:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n135:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n136:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n137:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n138:         run: |\\n139:           \\\"$VENV_PATH/bin/python3\\\" -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\\n140:           \\\"$VENV_PATH/bin/python3\\\" -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n141:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n142: \\n143:       - name: Download build artifacts\\n144:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\\n145:         with:\\n146:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n147:           use-gha: true\\n148: \\n149:       - name: Download TD artifacts\\n150:         continue-on-error: true\\n151:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\\n152:         with:\\n153:           use-gha: true\\n154: \\n155:       - name: Parse ref\\n156:         id: parse-ref\\n157:         run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n158: \\n159:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n160:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n161:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n162:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n163:         id: keep-going\\n164:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n165:         with:\\n166:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n167:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n168:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n169: \\n170:       - name: Set Test step time\\n171:         id: test-timeout\\n172:         shell: bash\\n173:         env:\\n174:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n175:         run: |\\n176:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n177: \\n178:       - name: Test\\n179:         id: test\\n180:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n181:         env:\\n182:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n183:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n184:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n185:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n186:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n187:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n188:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n189:           PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE: .github/requirements/pip-requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt\\n190:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n191:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n192:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n193:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n194:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n195:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n196:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n197:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n198:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\\n199:           HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\\n200:         run: |\\n201:           # shellcheck disable=SC1090\\n202:           set -ex\\n203: \\n204:           # TODO: Remove me later, and properly activate venv\\n205:           PATH=\\\"$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH\\\"\\n206:           export PATH\\n207: \\n208:           # Print out some information about the test environment\\n209:           for tool in python3 python; do\\n210:             which $tool\\n211:             $tool --version\\n212:           done\\n213: \\n214:           python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps dist/*.whl\\n215: \\n216:           set +e\\n217:           pushd \\\"${RUNNER_TEMP}\\\"\\n218:           # Install pip dependencies if they are not found. This is to mitigate a peculiar\\n219:           # flaky missing dependencies on MacOS\\n220:           python3 -c \\\"import torch\\\"\\n221:           RC=$?\\n222:           popd\\n223: \\n224:           if [ \\\"${RC}\\\" -ne 0 ]; then\\n225:             python3 -mpip install --ignore-installed -r \\\"${PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE}\\\"\\n226:           fi\\n227:           set -e\\n228: \\n229:           .ci/pytorch/macos-test.sh\\n230: \\n231:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n232:         shell: bash\\n233:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n234:         run: |\\n235:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n236: \\n237:       - name: Run OP benchmark\\n238:         shell: bash\\n239:         if: ${{ contains(steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name, 'mps') }}\\n240:         run: |\\n241:           python3 test/bench_mps_ops.py\\n242: \\n243: \\n244:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n245:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n246:         continue-on-error: true\\n247:         env:\\n248:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n249:         run: |\\n250:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n251: \\n252:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n253:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n254:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n255:         with:\\n256:           use-gha: true\\n257:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n258: \\n259:       - name: Upload the benchmark results\\n260:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\\n261:         with:\\n262:           benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\\n263:           dry-run: false\\n264:           schema-version: v3\\n265:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n266: \\n267:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n268:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n269:         continue-on-error: true\\n270:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n271:         with:\\n272:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n273:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n274:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n275:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n276:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n277:           local_path: usage_log.txt\\n278: \\n279:       - name: Reinstall brew miniconda, if was installed\\n280:         if: always()\\n281:         continue-on-error: true\\n282:         run: |\\n283:           if [[ -n \\\"$REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA\\\" ]]; then\\n284:               brew install --cask miniconda\\n285:           fi\\n286: \\n287:       - name: Clean up disk space\\n288:         if: always()\\n289:         continue-on-error: true\\n290:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\\n291: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_mac-test.yml\"}"
77. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # TODO: this looks sort of similar to _linux-test, but there are like a dozen\\n2: # places where you would have to insert an if statement. Probably it's better to\\n3: # just use a different workflow altogether\\n4: \\n5: name: test\\n6: \\n7: on:\\n8:   workflow_call:\\n9:     inputs:\\n10:       build-environment:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n14:       test-matrix:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n18:       docker-image:\\n19:         required: true\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         description: Docker image to run in.\\n22:       sync-tag:\\n23:         required: false\\n24:         type: string\\n25:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n26:         description: |\\n27:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n28:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n29:       timeout-minutes:\\n30:         required: false\\n31:         type: number\\n32:         default: 300\\n33:         description: |\\n34:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n35:       tests-to-include:\\n36:         required: false\\n37:         type: string\\n38:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n39:         description: |\\n40:           List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)\\n41:       dashboard-tag:\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: string\\n44:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n45:       disable-monitor:\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n48:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n49:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n50:         required: false\\n51:         type: boolean\\n52:         default: true\\n53:       monitor-log-interval:\\n54:         description: |\\n55:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n56:         required: false\\n57:         type: number\\n58:         default: 5\\n59:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n60:         description: |\\n61:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n62:         required: false\\n63:         type: number\\n64:         default: 1\\n65:     secrets:\\n66:       HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\\n67:         required: false\\n68:         description: |\\n69:           HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\\n70: env:\\n71:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n72: \\n73: permissions:\\n74:   id-token: write\\n75:   contents: read\\n76: \\n77: jobs:\\n78:   test:\\n79:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n80:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n81:     strategy:\\n82:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n83:       fail-fast: false\\n84:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n85:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n86:     steps:\\n87:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n88:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n89:         with:\\n90:           no-sudo: true\\n91: \\n92:       - name: Setup ROCm\\n93:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm\\n94: \\n95:       - name: Runner check GPU count (distributed jobs)\\n96:         if: ${{ contains(matrix.config, 'distributed') }}\\n97:         shell: bash\\n98:         run: |\\n99:           ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\\\\sgfx')\\n100:           if [[ $ngpu -lt 2 ]]; then #We are temporarily reducing this down to 2 from 4 so that we can run tests on nodes with less gpus.\\n101:             echo \\\"Error: only $ngpu GPU(s) detected, at least 2 GPUs are needed for distributed jobs\\\"\\n102:             exit 1\\n103:           fi\\n104: \\n105:       - name: Calculate docker image\\n106:         id: calculate-docker-image\\n107:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\\n108:         with:\\n109:           docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\\n110: \\n111:       - name: Pull docker image\\n112:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\\n113:         with:\\n114:           docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\\n115: \\n116:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n117:         id: get-job-id\\n118:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n119:         if: always()\\n120:         with:\\n121:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n122: \\n123:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n124:         id: monitor-script\\n125:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n126:         shell: bash\\n127:         continue-on-error: true\\n128:         env:\\n129:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n130:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n131:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n132:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n133:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n134:
