  "path": ".github/workflows/_win-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: win-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       cuda-version:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: What CUDA version to build with, \\\"cpu\\\" for none.\\n14:       test-matrix:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n18:       sync-tag:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: |\\n23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n25:       timeout-minutes:\\n26:         required: false\\n27:         type: number\\n28:         default: 240\\n29:         description: |\\n30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n31:       disable-monitor:\\n32:         description: |\\n33:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n34:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n35:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n36:         required: false\\n37:         type: boolean\\n38:         default: true\\n39:       monitor-log-interval:\\n40:         description: |\\n41:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: number\\n44:         default: 5\\n45:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n48:         required: false\\n49:         type: number\\n50:         default: 1\\n51: env:\\n52:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n53: \\n54: jobs:\\n55:   test:\\n56:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n57:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n58:     strategy:\\n59:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n60:       fail-fast: false\\n61:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n62:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n63:     defaults:\\n64:       run:\\n65:         shell: bash\\n66:     steps:\\n67:       # Duplicated in win-build because this MUST go before a checkout\\n68:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\\n69:         shell: bash\\n70:         run: |\\n71:           git config --global core.longpaths true\\n72:           git config --global core.symlinks true\\n73:           git config --global core.ignorecase false\\n74: \\n75:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\\n76:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\\n77:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\\n78:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false\\n79: \\n80:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n81:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n82:         with:\\n83:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n84:           instructions: |\\n85:             To forward remote desktop on your local machine ssh as follows:\\n86:               ssh -L 3389:localhost:3389 %%username%%@%%hostname%%\\n87:             And then change password using `passwd` command.\\n88: \\n89:             To start tests locally, change working folder to \\\\actions-runner\\\\_work\\\\pytorch\\\\pytorch\\\\test,\\n90:             Activate miniconda and Visual Studio environment and set PYTHON_PATH, by running:\\n91:               call C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\\\Scripts\\\\activate.bat C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\n92:               call \\\"C:\\\\Program Files (x86)\\\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\\\2022\\\\BuildTools\\\\VC\\\\Auxiliary\\\\Build\\\\vcvarsall.bat\\\" x64\\n93:               set PYTHONPATH=C:\\\\actions-runner\\\\_work\\\\pytorch\\\\pytorch\\\\build\\\\win_tmp\\\\build\\n94: \\n95:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n96:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n97:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n98:         with:\\n99:           no-sudo: true\\n100: \\n101:       - name: Setup Windows\\n102:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-win\\n103:         with:\\n104:           cuda-version: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n105: \\n106:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n107:         id: get-job-id\\n108:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n109:         if: always()\\n110:         with:\\n111:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n112: \\n113:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n114:         id: monitor-script\\n115:         env:\\n116:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n117:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n118:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n119:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n120:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n121:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n122:         shell: bash\\n123:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n124:         continue-on-error: true\\n125:         run: |\\n126:           # Windows conda doesn't have python3 binary, only python, but it's python3\\n127:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\\n128:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n129:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n130: \\n131:       - name: Download PyTorch Build Artifacts\\n132:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\\n133:         with:\\n134:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n135:           path: C:\\\\${{ github.run_id }}\\\\build-results\\n136: \\n137:       - name: Check build-results folder\\n138:         shell: powershell\\n139:         run: |\\n140:           tree /F C:\\\\$Env:GITHUB_RUN_ID\\\\build-results\\n141: \\n142:       - name: Download TD artifacts\\n143:         continue-on-error: true\\n144:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\\n145: \\n146:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n147:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n148:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n149:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n150:         id: keep-going\\n151:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n152:         with:\\n153:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n154:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n155:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n156: \\n157:       - name: Set Test step time\\n158:         id: test-timeout\\n159:         shell: bash\\n160:         env:\\n161:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n162:         run: |\\n163:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n164: \\n165:       - name: Test\\n166:         id: test\\n167:         shell: bash\\n168:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n169:         env:\\n170:           USE_CUDA: ${{ inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu' && '1' || '0' }}\\n171:           INSTALL_WINDOWS_SDK: 1\\n172:           PYTHON_VERSION: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n173:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n174:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n175:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n176:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n177:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n178:           VC_PRODUCT: \\\"BuildTools\\\"\\n179:           VC_VERSION: \\\"\\\"\\n180:           VS_VERSION: \\\"17.4.1\\\"\\n181:           VC_YEAR: \\\"2022\\\"\\n182:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n183:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n184:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n185:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n186:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n187:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n188:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n189:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n190:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n191:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n192:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n193:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n194:           PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n195:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n196:           ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n197:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n198:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n200:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ github.event.pull_request.reenabled-issues }}\\n201:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: \\\"8.6\\\"\\n202:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n203:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n204:         run: |\\n205:           pushd \\\"${PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR}\\\"\\n206:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046,SC2102\\n207:           python3 -mpip install $(echo *.whl)[opt-einsum,optree] optree==0.13.0\\n208:           popd\\n209: \\n210:           .ci/pytorch/win-test.sh\\n211: \\n212:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\\n213:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\\n214:         continue-on-error: true\\n215:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\\n216:         with:\\n217:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n218:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n219:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n220:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n221:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n222: \\n223:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n224:         shell: bash\\n225:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n226:         run: |\\n227:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n228: \\n229:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n230:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n231:         shell: bash\\n232:         continue-on-error: true\\n233:         env:\\n234:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n235:         run: |\\n236:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n237: \\n238:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n239:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n240:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n241:         with:\\n242:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n243: \\n244:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n245:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n246:         continue-on-error: true\\n247:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n248:         with:\\n249:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n250:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n251:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n252:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n253:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n254: \\n255:       - name: Parse ref\\n256:         id: parse-ref\\n257:         shell: bash\\n258:         run: python3 .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n259: \\n260:       - name: Teardown Windows\\n261:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-win\\n262:         if: always()\\n263:         timeout-minutes: 120\\n264: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_win-test.yml\"}"

push:\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       - ciflow/b200-distributed/*\\n11:   schedule:\\n12:     - cron: 46 8 * * *  # about 1:46am PDT\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n16:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n17: \\n18: permissions:\\n19:   id-token: write\\n20:   contents: read\\n21: \\n22: jobs:\\n23: \\n24:   get-label-type:\\n25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n26:     name: get-label-type\\n27:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n28:     with:\\n29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n31:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n32:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n33: \\n34:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200:\\n35:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n40:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge\\n41:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-distributed-b200\\n42:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n43:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\\n44:       test-matrix: |\\n45:         { include: [\\n46:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200.8\\\" },\\n47:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200.8\\\" },\\n48:         ]}\\n49:     secrets: inherit\\n50: \\n51:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-test-distributed-b200:\\n52:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-test-b200\\n53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n54:     needs:\\n55:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200\\n56:     with:\\n57:       timeout-minutes: 1200\\n58:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.build-environment }}\\n59:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.docker-image }}\\n60:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n61:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n62:     secrets: inherit\\n63: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: Limited CI for symmetric memory tests on B200\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     paths:\\n6:       - .github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8:   push:\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       - ciflow/b200-symm-mem/*\\n11:   schedule:\\n12:     - cron: 22 8 * * *  # about 1:22am PDT\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n16:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n17: \\n18: permissions:\\n19:   id-token: write\\n20:   contents: read\\n21: \\n22: jobs:\\n23: \\n24:   get-label-type:\\n25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n26:     name: get-label-type\\n27:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n28:     with:\\n29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n31:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n32:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n33: \\n34:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build-symm:\\n35:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100-symm\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n40:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge\\n41:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100-symm\\n42:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n43:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\\n44:       test-matrix: |\\n45:         { include: [\\n46:           { config: \\\"b200-symm-mem\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200.8\\\" },\\n47:         ]}\\n48:     secrets: inherit\\n49: \\n50:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-test:\\n51:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100-symm\\n52:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n53:     needs:\\n54:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build-symm\\n55:     with:\\n56:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build-symm.outputs.build-environment }}\\n57:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build-symm.outputs.docker-image }}\\n58:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build-symm.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n59:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n60:     secrets: inherit\\n61: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml\"}"

Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Build almalinux docker images\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_dispatch:\\n5:   push:\\n6:     branches:\\n7:       - main\\n8:       - release/*\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate or nightly builds\\n11:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n12:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n13:     paths:\\n14:       - .ci/docker/**\\n15:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\\n16:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\\n17:   pull_request:\\n18:     paths:\\n19:       - .ci/docker/**\\n20:       - .github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\\n21:       - .github/actions/binary-docker-build/**\\n22: \\n23: env:\\n24:   DOCKER_REGISTRY: \\\"docker.io\\\"\\n25:   DOCKER_BUILDKIT: 1\\n26:   WITH_PUSH: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) }}\\n27: \\n28: concurrency:\\n29:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n30:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n31: \\n32: jobs:\\n33:   build-docker:\\n34:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n35:     environment: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/heads/release') || startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')) && 'docker-build') || '' }}\\n36:     runs-on: linux.9xlarge.ephemeral\\n37:     strategy:\\n38:       matrix:\\n39:         tag: [\\\"cuda12.6\\\", \\\"cuda12.8\\\", \\\"cuda12.9\\\", \\\"cuda13.0\\\", \\\"rocm7.0\\\", \\\"rocm7.1\\\", \\\"cpu\\\"]\\n40:     steps:\\n41:       - name: Build docker image\\n42:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/binary-docker-build@release/2.10\\n43:         with:\\n44:           docker-image-name: almalinux-builder\\n45:           custom-tag-prefix: ${{matrix.tag}}\\n46:           docker-build-dir: almalinux\\n47:           DOCKER_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_TOKEN }}\\n48:           DOCKER_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCKER_ID }}\\n49: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/build-almalinux-images.yml\"}"
94. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: linux-test-stable-fa3\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 30\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      s3-bucket:\n        description: S3 bucket to download artifact\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"gha-artifacts\"\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n      VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to test vllm\n      SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          FB app token to write to scribe endpoint\n\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: linux.aws.h100\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes || 30 }}\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Checkout flash-attention as a secondary repository\n        uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          repository: Dao-AILab/flash-attention\n          path: flash-attention\n\n      - name: Setup Linux\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Use following to pull public copy of the image\n        id: print-ghcr-mirror\n        env:\n          ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          tag=${ECR_DOCKER_IMAGE##*:}\n          echo \"docker pull ghcr.io/pytorch/ci-image:${tag/:/-}\"\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Check if in a container runner\n        shell: bash\n        id: check_container_runner\n        run: echo \"IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER=$(if [ -f /.inarc ] || [ -f /.incontainer ]; then echo true ; else echo false; fi)\" >> \"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\"\n\n      - name: Setup GPU_FLAG for docker run\n        id: setup-gpu-flag\n        run: echo \"GPU_FLAG=--gpus all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=all\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n      - name: Setup SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT environment for docker run when on container\n        id: setup-sscache-port-flag\n        run: echo \"SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG=-e SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT=$((RUNNER_UID + 4226))\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n        if: ${{ steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'true' }}\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          s3-bucket: ${{ inputs.s3-bucket }}\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n          SHM_SIZE: '2g'\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n          SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN }}\n          ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX: ${{ github.job }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        run: |\n          set -x\n\n          TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test_fa3_abi_stable.sh\n\n          # Leaving 1GB for the runner and other things\n          TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB=$(awk '/MemTotal/ { printf \"%.3f \\n\", $2/1024/1024 - 1 }' /proc/meminfo)\n          # https://docs.docker.com/engine/containers/resource_constraints/#--memory-swap-details, the 3GB swap\n          # comes from https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/pull/6058\n          TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP=$((\\\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}\\\" + 3))\n\n\n          SHM_OPTS=\\\"--shm-size=${SHM_SIZE}\\\"\n          JENKINS_USER=\\\"--user jenkins\\\"\n          DOCKER_SHELL_CMD=\n\n          # detached container should get cleaned up by teardown_ec2_linux\n          # TODO: Stop building test binaries as part of the build phase\n          # Used for GPU_FLAG, SHM_OPTS, JENKINS_USER and DOCKER_SHELL_CMD since that doesn't play nice\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2086,SC2090\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            ${SCCACHE_SERVER_PORT_DOCKER_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e JOB_NAME \\\n            -e BASE_SHA \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e VLLM_TEST_HUGGING_FACE_TOKEN \\\n            -e SCRIBE_GRAPHQL_ACCESS_TOKEN \\\n            -e ARTIFACTS_FILE_SUFFIX \\\n            --memory=\"${TOTAL_AVAILABLE_MEMORY_IN_GB%.*}g\" \\\n            --memory-swap=\"${TOTAL_MEMORY_WITH_SWAP}g\" \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --ipc=host \\\n            ${SHM_OPTS} \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --name=\\\"${container_name}\\\" \\\n            ${JENKINS_USER} \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\" \\\n            ${DOCKER_SHELL_CMD}\n          )\n\n          echo \"DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID=${container_name}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"python3 -m pip install $(echo dist/*.whl)[opt-einsum] && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n      - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          # shellcheck disable=SC2156\n          find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${DOCKER_CONTAINER_ID}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n      - name: Store Core dumps on S3\n        uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\n        if: failure()\n        with:\n          name: coredumps-fa3-stable-abi-smoke-tests\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: ignore\n          path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n\n      - name: Teardown Linux\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\n        if: always() && steps.check_container_runner.outputs.IN_CONTAINER_RUNNER == 'false'\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_linux-test-stable-fa3.yml"
95. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: mac-build\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      runner-type:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Name of the GitHub-managed runner type to use for the build.\n      build-generates-artifacts:\n        required: true\n        type: boolean\n        description: If set, upload generated build artifacts.\n      xcode-version:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: What xcode version to build with.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      python-version:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"3.12\"\n        description: |\n          The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default\n      test-matrix:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        description: |\n          An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\n          is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\n          logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\n      sccache-use-gha:\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n        description: If true, use the Github cache as the storage option for sccache instead of S3.\n\n    outputs:\n      test-matrix:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n        description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\n      build-outcome:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-outcome }}\n        description: The outcome of the build step. This is used to influence test filtering logic later on.\n      build-environment:\n        value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n\njobs:\n  build:\n    # Don't run on forked repos.\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner-type }}\n    env:\n      BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n      SCCACHE_USE_GHA: ${{ inputs.sccache-use-gha }}\n    outputs:\n      build-outcome: ${{ steps.build.outcome }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Set xcode version\n        env:\n          XCODE_VERSION: ${{ inputs.xcode-version }}\n        run: |\n          if [ -n \"${XCODE_VERSION}\" ]; then\n            echo \"DEVELOPER_DIR=/Applications/Xcode_${XCODE_VERSION}.app/Contents/Developer\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@release/2.10\n        with:\n          python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}\n          pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\n\n      - name: Install sccache (only for non-forked PRs, and pushes to trunk)\n        uses: nick-fields/retry@7152eba30c6575329ac0576536151aca5a72780e # v3.0.0\n        if: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository }}\n        with:\n          timeout_minutes: 5\n          max_attempts: 3\n          retry_wait_seconds: 90\n          command: |\n            set -ex\n\n            DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=0\n            SCCACHE_VERSION=\"0.4.1\"\n            LOCAL_PATH=\"/usr/local/bin\"\n\n            if [ ! -f \"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\" ]; then\n              DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1\n            else\n              LOCAL_VERSION=$(\"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\" --version | cut -d\" \" -f2)\n\n              if [ \"${LOCAL_VERSION}\" != \"${SCCACHE_VERSION}\" ]; then\n                DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE=1\n              fi\n            fi\n\n            if [ \"${DOWNLOAD_SCCACHE}\" == \"1\" ]; then\n              sudo curl --retry 3 --retry-all-errors \"https://s3.amazonaws.com/ossci-macos/sccache/sccache-v0.4.1-${RUNNER_ARCH}\" --output \"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\"\n              sudo chmod +x \"${LOCAL_PATH}/sccache\"\n            fi\n\n            if [[ \"${SCCACHE_USE_GHA}\" == \"true\" ]]; then\n              echo \"ACTIONS_CACHE_URL=${ACTIONS_CACHE_URL}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n              echo \"ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN=${ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n              echo \"SCCACHE_GHA_ENABLED=on\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n            else\n              echo \"SCCACHE_BUCKET=ossci-compiler-cache-circleci-v2\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n              echo \"SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX=${GITHUB_WORKFLOW}\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n            fi\n\n            echo \"${LOCAL_PATH}\" >> $GITHUB_PATH\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\n        id: filter\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Build\n        if: steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == ''\n        id: build\n        env:\n          OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n        run: |\n          PATH=\"$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH\"\n          export PATH\n\n          export SKIP_SCCACHE_INITIALIZATION=1\n          .ci/pytorch/macos-build.sh\n\n      - name: Archive artifacts into zip\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\n        run: |\n          zip -1 -r artifacts.zip dist/ build/.ninja_log build/compile_commands.json .additional_ci_files\n\n      - name: Store PyTorch Build Artifacts on GHA\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: inputs.build-generates-artifacts && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          name: ${{ env.BUILD_ENVIRONMENT }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: error\n          path: artifacts.zip\n\n      - name: Upload sccache stats to GHA\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: ${{ (github.event_name == 'push' || github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository) && steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' }}\n        with:\n          name: sccache-stats-${{ inputs.build-environment }}-runattempt${{ github.run_attempt }}-${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: warn\n          path: sccache-stats-*.json\n\n      - name: Clean up disk space\n        if: always()\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_mac-build.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_mac-build.yml"
96. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: mac-test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      python-version:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"3.12\"\n        description: |\n          The python version to be used. Will be 3.9 by default\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 270\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n          Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n          since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\n    defaults:\n      run:\n        shell: bash -e -l {0}\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    env:\n      GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n      BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n      TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n      SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n      NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n      PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Print runner OS/HW info\n        run: |\n          sysctl machdep.cpu.brand_string kern.osproductversion\n\n      - name: Clean up leftover processes on MacOS pet runner\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          for PROCESS in \"python\" \"conda\" \"ninja\" \"clang\"; do\n            echo \"Cleaning up all remaining ${PROCESS} process\"\n            pkill \"${PROCESS}\" || true\n          done\n\n      - name: Clean up brew miniconda, if installed\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          if brew list miniconda; then\n            brew uninstall miniconda\n            echo \"REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA=1\" >> \"${GITHUB_ENV}\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Clean up leftover local python3 site-packages on MacOS pet runner\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          for dir in  ~/.local/lib/python3.*/site-packages; do\n            echo \"Cleaning up ${dir}\"\n            rm -rf \"${dir}\"\n          done\n\n      - name: Clean up disk space before running MacOS workflow\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-python@release/2.10\n        with:\n          python-version: ${{ inputs.python-version }}\n          pip-requirements-file: .ci/docker/requirements-ci.txt\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          \"$VENV_PATH/bin/python3\" -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\n          \"$VENV_PATH/bin/python3\" -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          use-gha: true\n\n      - name: Download TD artifacts\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n        with:\n          use-gha: true\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n        id: keep-going\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        env:\n          PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n          CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n          VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n          TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n          NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n          NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n          PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE: .github/requirements/pip-requirements-${{ runner.os }}.txt\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          set -ex\n\n          PATH=\"$VENV_PATH/bin:$PATH\"\n          export PATH\n\n          for tool in python3 python; do\n            which $tool\n            $tool --version\n          done\n\n          python3 -mpip install --no-index --no-deps dist/*.whl\n\n          set +e\n          pushd \"${RUNNER_TEMP}\"\n          python3 -c \"import torch\"\n          RC=$?\n          popd\n\n          if [ \"${RC}\" -ne 0 ]; then\n            python3 -mpip install --ignore-installed -r \"${PIP_REQUIREMENTS_FILE}\"\n          fi\n          set -e\n\n          .ci/pytorch/macos-test.sh\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Run OP benchmark\n        shell: bash\n        if: ${{ contains(steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name, 'mps') }}\n        run: |\n          python3 test/bench_mps_ops.py\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          use-gha: true\n          file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Upload the benchmark results\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\n        with:\n          benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\n          dry-run: false\n          schema-version: v3\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n          local_path: usage_log.txt\n\n      - name: Reinstall brew miniconda, if was installed\n        if: always()\n        continue-on-error: true\n        run: |\n          if [[ -n \"$REINSTALL_BREW_MINICONDA\" ]]; then\n              brew install --cask miniconda\n          fi\n\n      - name: Clean up disk space\n        if: always()\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/check-disk-space@release/2.10\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_mac-test.yml"
}
Output
