97. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: test\n\non:\n  workflow_call:\n    inputs:\n      build-environment:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\n      test-matrix:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\n      docker-image:\n        required: true\n        type: string\n        description: Docker image to run in.\n      sync-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\n          job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\n      timeout-minutes:\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 300\n        description: |\n          Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\n      tests-to-include:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n        description: |\n          List of tests to include (empty string implies default list)\n      dashboard-tag:\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: \"\"\n      disable-monitor:\n        description: |\n          [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\n          Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\n          since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      monitor-log-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 5\n      monitor-data-collect-interval:\n        description: |\n          Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\n        required: false\n        type: number\n        default: 1\n    secrets:\n      HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN:\n        required: false\n        description: |\n          HF Auth token to avoid rate limits when downloading models or datasets from hub\nenv:\n  GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  test:\n    # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\n    strategy:\n      matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\n      fail-fast: false\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\n    timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n    steps:\n      - name: Checkout PyTorch\n        uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\n        with:\n          no-sudo: true\n\n      - name: Setup ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/setup-rocm\n\n      - name: Runner check GPU count (distributed jobs)\n        if: ${{ contains(matrix.config, 'distributed') }}\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          ngpu=$(rocminfo | grep -c -E 'Name:.*\\sgfx')\n          if [[ $ngpu -lt 2 ]]; then\n            echo \"Error: only $ngpu GPU(s) detected, at least 2 GPUs are needed for distributed jobs\"\n            exit 1\n          fi\n\n      - name: Calculate docker image\n        id: calculate-docker-image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/calculate-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image-name: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Pull docker image\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/pull-docker-image@release/2.10\n        with:\n          docker-image: ${{ steps.calculate-docker-image.outputs.docker-image }}\n\n      - name: Get workflow job id\n        id: get-job-id\n        uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\n        if: always()\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Start monitoring script\n        id: monitor-script\n        if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\n          MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\n          MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\n        run: |\n          python3 -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7\n          python3 -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\" --data-collect-interval \"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\n          echo \"monitor-script-pid=${!}\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Download build artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-build-artifacts\n        with:\n          name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n\n      - name: Download TD artifacts\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\n\n      - name: Parse ref\n        id: parse-ref\n        run: .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\n\n      - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\n        id: keep-going\n        uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\n        with:\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n          test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\n          job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n\n      - name: Set Test step time\n        id: test-timeout\n        shell: bash\n        env:\n          JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\n        run: |\n          echo \"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\" >> \"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\"\n\n      - name: Preserve github env variables for use in docker\n        shell: bash\n        run: |\n          env | grep '^GITHUB' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n          env | grep '^CI' >> \"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\"\n\n      - name: Test\n        id: test\n        env:\n          BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\n          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n          GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\n          GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\n          GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\n          JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\n          SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\n          BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha || github.sha }}\n          TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\n          SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\n          NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\n          REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.reenabled-issues }}\n          CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\n          VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\n          TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\n          NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\n          NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\n          DOCKER_IMAGE: ${{ inputs.docker-image }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\n          PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\n          TESTS_TO_INCLUDE: ${{ inputs.tests-to-include }}\n          DASHBOARD_TAG: ${{ inputs.dashboard-tag }}\n          HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN }}\n        timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\n        run: |\n          set -x\n\n          if [[ $TEST_CONFIG == 'multigpu' ]]; then\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/multigpu-test.sh\n          elif [[ $BUILD_ENVIRONMENT == *onnx* ]]; then\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/caffe2/test.sh\n          else\n            TEST_COMMAND=.ci/pytorch/test.sh\n          fi\n\n          container_name=$(docker run \\\n            ${GPU_FLAG:-} \\\n            -e BUILD_ENVIRONMENT \\\n            -e PR_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_ACTIONS \\\n            -e GITHUB_REPOSITORY \\\n            -e GITHUB_WORKFLOW \\\n            -e GITHUB_JOB \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ID \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER \\\n            -e GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT \\\n            -e JOB_ID \\\n            -e JOB_NAME \\\n            -e BASE_SHA \\\n            -e BRANCH \\\n            -e SHA1 \\\n            -e AWS_DEFAULT_REGION \\\n            -e IN_WHEEL_TEST \\\n            -e SHARD_NUMBER \\\n            -e TEST_CONFIG \\\n            -e NUM_TEST_SHARDS \\\n            -e REENABLED_ISSUES \\\n            -e CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR \\\n            -e VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS \\\n            -e TEST_SHOWLOCALS \\\n            -e NO_TEST_TIMEOUT \\\n            -e NO_TD \\\n            -e MAX_JOBS=\"$(nproc --ignore=2)\" \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK \\\n            -e PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS \\\n            -e TESTS_TO_INCLUDE \\\n            -e HUGGING_FACE_HUB_TOKEN \\\n            -e DASHBOARD_TAG \\\n            --env-file=\"${RUNNER_TEMP}/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --ulimit stack=10485760:83886080 \\\n            --ulimit core=0 \\\n            --env-file=\"/tmp/github_env_${GITHUB_RUN_ID}\" \\\n            --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \\\n            --cap-add=SYS_PTRACE \\\n            --shm-size=\"8g\" \\\n            --tty \\\n            --detach \\\n            --name=\"${container_name}\" \\\n            --user jenkins \\\n            -v \"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}:/var/lib/jenkins/workspace\" \\\n            -w /var/lib/jenkins/workspace \\\n            \"${DOCKER_IMAGE}\"\n          )\n          echo \"CONTAINER_NAME=${container_name}\" >> \"$GITHUB_ENV\"\n          docker exec -t \"${container_name}\" sh -c \"cd .. && cp -R workspace pytorch && cd pytorch && pip install dist/*.whl && ${TEST_COMMAND}\"\n\n      - name: Save test results\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"cd ../pytorch && sudo cp -R test/test-reports ../workspace/test\"\n\n      - name: Change permissions (only needed for kubernetes runners for now)\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && (contains(matrix.runner, 'gfx942') || contains(matrix.runner, 'mi355')) }}\n        run: |\n          docker exec -t \"${{ env.CONTAINER_NAME }}\" sh -c \"sudo chown -R 1001:1001 test\"\n\n      - name: Print remaining test logs\n        shell: bash\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\n        run: |\n          cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\n\n      - name: Stop monitoring script\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        shell: bash\n        continue-on-error: true\n        env:\n          MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\n        run: |\n          kill \"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\"\n\n      - name: Upload test artifacts\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\n        if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\n        with:\n          use-gha: true\n          file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n\n      - name: Collect backtraces from coredumps (if any)\n        if: always()\n        run: |\n          find . -iname \"core.[1-9]*\" -exec docker exec \"${CONTAINER_NAME}\" sh -c \"gdb python {} -ex 'bt' -ex 'q'\" \\;\n\n      - name: Store Core dumps on GitHub\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\n        if: failure()\n        with:\n          name: coredumps-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}\n          retention-days: 14\n          if-no-files-found: ignore\n          path: ./**/core.[1-9]*\n\n      - name: Authenticate with AWS\n        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\n        with:\n          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_upload-benchmark-results\n          role-duration-seconds: 18000\n          aws-region: us-east-1\n\n      - name: Upload the benchmark results\n        uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/upload-benchmark-results@release/2.10\n        with:\n          benchmark-results-dir: test/test-reports\n          dry-run: false\n          schema-version: v3\n          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Upload utilization stats\n        if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\n        continue-on-error: true\n        uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\n        with:\n          job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\n          job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\n          workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\n          workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\n          workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\n\n      - name: Teardown ROCm\n        uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-rocm\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml"
}
Output
"File created successfully at: /workspace/.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml"
98. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Check whether the workflow owner can use ARC runners\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       check_experiments:\\n7:         required: false\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: |\\n10:           List of experiments for this workflow. If not defined, all default experiments are included.\\n11:       opt_out_experiments:\\n12:         required: false\\n13:         type: string\\n14:         description: Comma-separated list of experiments this workflow will opt-out of.\\n15:       triggering_actor:\\n16:         required: true\\n17:         type: string\\n18:         description: The triggering_actor for the workflow. Use github.triggering_actor\\n19:       issue_owner:\\n20:         required: true\\n21:         type: string\\n22:         description: The owner of the issue. Use github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login\\n23:       curr_branch:\\n24:         required: true\\n25:         type: string\\n26:         description: Current branch or tag.\\n27:       curr_ref_type:\\n28:         required: false\\n29:         type: string\\n30:         default: branch\\n31:         description: The value of \\\"github.ref_type\\\", \\\"branch\\\" or \\\"tag\\\"\\n32:       issue_number:\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         type: string\\n35:         default: \\\"5132\\\"\\n36:         description: |\\n37:           Fetch's GitHub Issue from pytorch/test-infra\\n38:           Example: https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\\n39: \\n40:     outputs:\\n41:       label-type:\\n42:         description: Type of runners to use\\n43:         value: ${{ jobs.runner-determinator.outputs.label-type }}\\n44: \\n45: jobs:\\n46:   runner-determinator:\\n47:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n48:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n49:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n50:     outputs:\\n51:       label-type: ${{ steps.set-condition.outputs.label-type }}\\n52:     env:\\n53:       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n54:       ISSUE_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.issue_number }}\\n55:       TRIGGERING_ACTOR: ${{ inputs.triggering_actor }}\\n56:       ISSUE_OWNER: ${{ inputs.issue_owner }}\\n57:       CHECK_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.check_experiments }}\\n58:       OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS: ${{ inputs.opt_out_experiments }}\\n59:       PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n60:     steps:\\n61:       # - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n62:       #   uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n63:       #   with:\\n64:       #     fetch-depth: 1\\n65:       #     submodules: true\\n66: \\n67:       # TODO: Remove the hardcoded step below\\n68:       # Hardcoding below is temporary for testing ALI runners\\n69:       # This file below should match the script found in .github/scripts/runner_determinator.py\\n70:       - name: Hardcode runner-determinator script\\n71:         id: hardcode-script\\n72:         run: |\\n73:           cat <<EOF > runner_determinator.py\\n74:           # flake8: noqa: G004\\n75: \\n76:           # Note: Copies of this script in runner_determinator.py and _runner-determinator.yml\\n77:           #       must be kept in sync. You can do it easily by running the following command:\\n78:           #           python .github/scripts/update_runner_determinator.py\\n79: \\n80:           \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n81:           This runner determinator is used to determine which set of runners to run a\\n82:           GitHub job on. It uses the first comment of a GitHub issue (by default\\n83:           https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132) to define the configuration\\n84:           of which runners should be used to run which job.\\n85: \\n86:           The configuration has two parts, the settings and a list of opted-in users,\\n87:           separated by a line containing \\\"---\\\".  If the line is not present, the\\n88:           settings are considered to be empty with only the second part, the user\\n89:           list, defined.\\n90: \\n91:           The first part is a YAML block that defines the rollout settings. This can be\\n92:           used to define any settings that are needed to determine which runners to use.\\n93:           It's fields are defined by the RolloutSettings class below.\\n94: \\n95:           The second part is a list of users who are explicitly opted in to the LF fleet.\\n96:           The user list is also a comma separated list of additional features or\\n97:           experiments which the user could be opted in to.\\n98: \\n99:           The user list has the following rules:\\n100: \\n101:           - Users are GitHub usernames, which must start with the @ prefix\\n102:           - Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable\\n103:           - A \\\"#\\\" prefix opts the user out of all experiments\\n104: \\n105:           Example config:\\n106:               # A list of experiments that can be opted into.\\n107:               # This defines the behavior they'll induce when opted into.\\n108:               # Expected syntax is:\\n109:               #   [experiment_name]: # Name of the experiment. Also used for the label prefix.\\n110:               #      rollout_perc: [int] # % of workflows to run with this experiment when users are not opted in.\\n111: \\n112:               experiments:\\n113:                 lf:\\n114:                   rollout_percent: 25\\n115:                   all_branches: false\\n116:                   default: true\\n117:               ---\\n118: \\n119:               # Opt-ins:\\n120:               # Users can opt into the LF fleet by adding their GitHub username to this list\\n121:               # and specifying experiments to enable in a comma-separated list.\\n122:               # To always opt out of an experiment, prefix it with a \\\"-\\\".\\n123:               # Experiments should be from the above list.\\n124: \\n125:               @User1,-lf,split_build\\n126:               @User2,lf\\n127:               @User3,split_build\\n128:           \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n129: \\n130:           import json\\n131:           import logging\\n132:           import os\\n133:           import random\\n134:           import re\\n135:           import sys\\n136:           from argparse import ArgumentParser\\n137:           from collections.abc import Iterable\\n138:           from functools import cache\\n139:           from logging import LogRecord\\n140:           from typing import Any, NamedTuple\\n141:           from urllib.request import Request, urlopen\\n142: \\n143:           import yaml\\n144:           from github import Auth, Github\\n145:           from github.Issue import Issue\\n146: \\n147: \\n148:           DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX = \\\"\\\"  # use meta runners\\n149:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF = \\\"lf.\\\"  # use runners from the linux foundation\\n150:           WORKFLOW_LABEL_LF_CANARY = \\\"lf.c.\\\"  # use canary runners from the linux foundation\\n151: \\n152:           GITHUB_OUTPUT = os.getenv(\\\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n153:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_AMI = \\\"runner-ami\\\"\\n154:           GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE = \\\"label-type\\\"\\n155:           OPT_OUT_LABEL = \\\"no-runner-experiments\\\"\\n156: \\n157:           SETTING_EXPERIMENTS = \\\"experiments\\\"\\n158: \\n159:           LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT = \\\"lf\\\"\\n160:           CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX = \\\".c\\\"\\n161: \\n162: \\n163:           class Experiment(NamedTuple):\\n164:               rollout_perc: float = (\\n165:                   0  # Percentage of workflows to experiment on when user is not opted-in.\\n166:               )\\n167:               all_branches: bool = (\\n168:                   False  # If True, the experiment is also enabled on the exception branches\\n169:               )\\n170:               default: bool = (\\n171:                   True  # If True, the experiment is enabled by default for all queries\\n172:               )\\n173: \\n174:               # Add more fields as needed\\n175: \\n176: \\n177:           class Settings(NamedTuple):\\n178:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n179:               Settings for the experiments that can be opted into.\\n180:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n181: \\n182:               experiments: dict[str, Experiment] = {}\\n183: \\n184: \\n185:           class ColorFormatter(logging.Formatter):\\n186:               \\\"\\\"\\\"Color codes the log messages based on the log level\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n187: \\n188:               COLORS = {\\n189:                   \\\"WARNING\\\": \\\"\\\\033[33m\\\",  # Yellow\\n190:                   \\\"ERROR\\\": \\\"\\\\033[31m\\\",  # Red\\n191:                   \\\"CRITICAL\\\": \\\"\\\\033[31m\\\",  # Red\\n192:                   \\\"INFO\\\": \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\",  # Reset\\n193:                   \\\"DEBUG\\\": \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\",  # Reset\\n194:               }\\n195: \\n196:               def format(self, record: LogRecord) -> str:\\n197:                   log_color = self.COLORS.get(record.levelname, \\\"\\\\033[0m\\\")  # Default to reset\\n198:                   record.msg = f\\\"{log_color}{record.msg}\\\\033[0m\\\"\\n199:                   return super().format(record)\\n200: \\n201: \\n202:           handler = logging.StreamHandler()\\n203:           handler.setFormatter(ColorFormatter(fmt=\\\"%(levelname)-8s: %(message)s\\\"))\\n204: \\n205:           log = logging.getLogger(os.path.basename(__file__))\\n206:           log.addHandler(handler)\\n207:           log.setLevel(logging.INFO)\\n208: \\n209: \\n210:           def set_github_output(key: str, value: str) -> None:\\n211:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n212:               Defines outputs of the github action that invokes this script\\n213:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n214:               if not GITHUB_OUTPUT:\\n215:                   # See https://github.blog/changelog/2022-10-11-github-actions-deprecating-save-state-and-set-output-commands/ for deprecation notice\\n216:                   log.warning(\\n217:                       \\\"No env var found for GITHUB_OUTPUT, you must be running this code locally. Falling back to the deprecated print method.\\\"\\n218:                   )\\n219:                   print(f\\\"::set-output name={key}::{value}\\\")\\n220:                   return\\n221: \\n222:               with open(GITHUB_OUTPUT, \\\"a\\\") as f:\\n223:                   log.info(f\\\"Setting output: {key}='{value}'\\\")\\n224:                   f.write(f\\\"{key}={value}\\\\n\\\")\\n225: \\n226: \\n227:           def _str_comma_separated_to_set(value: str) -> frozenset[str]:\\n228:               return frozenset(\\n229:                   filter(lambda itm: itm != \\\"\\\", map(str.strip, value.strip(\\\" \\\\n\\\\t\\\").split(\\\",\\\")))\\n230:               )\\n231: \\n232: \\n233:           def parse_args() -> Any:\\n234:               parser = ArgumentParser(\\\"Get dynamic rollout settings\\\")\\n235:               parser.add_argument(\\\"--github-token\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub token\\\")\\n236:               parser.add_argument(\\n237:                   \\\"--github-issue-repo\\\",\\n238:                   type=str,\\n239:                   required=False,\\n240:                   default=\\\"pytorch/test-infra\\\",\\n241:                   help=\\\"GitHub repo to get the issue\\\",\\n242:               )\\n243:               parser.add_argument(\\n244:                   \\\"--github-repo\\\",\\n245:                   type=str,\\n246:                   required=True,\\n247:                   help=\\\"GitHub repo where CI is running\\\",\\n248:               )\\n249:               parser.add_argument(\\n250:                   \\\"--github-issue\\\", type=int, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub issue number\\\"\\n251:               )\\n252:               parser.add_argument(\\n253:                   \\\"--github-actor\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub triggering_actor\\\"\\n254:               )\\n255:               parser.add_argument(\\n256:                   \\\"--github-issue-owner\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"GitHub issue owner\\\"\\n257:               )\\n258:               parser.add_argument(\\n259:                   \\\"--github-branch\\\", type=str, required=True, help=\\\"Current GitHub branch or tag\\\"\\n260:               )\\n261:               parser.add_argument(\\n262:                   \\\"--github-ref-type\\\",\\n263:                   type=str,\\n264:                   required=True,\\n265:                   help=\\\"Current GitHub ref type, branch or tag\\\",\\n266:               )\\n267:               parser.add_argument(\\n268:                   \\\"--eligible-experiments\\\",\\n269:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\\n270:                   required=False,\\n271:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n272:                   help=\\\"comma separated list of experiments to check, if omitted all experiments marked with default=True are checked\\\",\\n273:               )\\n274:               parser.add_argument(\\n275:                   \\\"--opt-out-experiments\\\",\\n276:                   type=_str_comma_separated_to_set,\\n277:                   required=False,\\n278:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n279:                   help=(\\n280:                       \\\"comma separated list of experiments to opt-out of. If unset, no opt-outs will occur. \\\"\\n281:                       \\\"If the same experiment is listed both here and in '--eligible-experiments' opt-out will take priority.\\\"\\n282:                   ),\\n283:               )\\n284:               parser.add_argument(\\n285:                   \\\"--pr-number\\\",\\n286:                   type=str,\\n287:                   required=False,\\n288:                   default=\\\"\\\",\\n289:                   help=\\\"the optional PR number where this is run\\\",\\n290:               )\\n291: \\n292:               return parser.parse_args()\\n293: \\n294: \\n295:           def get_gh_client(github_token: str) -> Github:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\\n296:               auth = Auth.Token(github_token)\\n297:               return Github(auth=auth)\\n298: \\n299: \\n300:           def get_issue(gh: Github, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> Issue:  # type: ignore[no-any-unimported]\\n301:               repo = gh.get_repo(repo)\\n302:               return repo.get_issue(number=issue_num)\\n303: \\n304: \\n305:           def get_potential_pr_author(\\n306:               github_token: str, repo: str, username: str, ref_type: str, ref_name: str\\n307:           ) -> str:\\n308:               # If the trigger was a new tag added by a bot, this is a ciflow case\\n309:               # Fetch the actual username from the original PR. The PR number is\\n310:               # embedded in the tag name: ciflow/<name>/<pr-number>\\n311: \\n312:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\\n313: \\n314:               if username == \\\"pytorch-bot[bot]\\\" and ref_type == \\\"tag\\\":\\n315:                   split_tag = ref_name.split(\\\"/\\\")\\n316:                   if (\\n317:                       len(split_tag) == 3\\n318:                       and split_tag[0] == \\\"ciflow\\\"\\n319:                       and split_tag[2].isnumeric()\\n320:                   ):\\n321:                       pr_number = split_tag[2]\\n322:                       try:\\n323:                           repository = gh.get_repo(repo)\\n324:                           pull = repository.get_pull(number=int(pr_number))\\n325:                       except Exception as e:\\n326:                           raise Exception(  # noqa: TRY002\\n327:                               f\\\"issue with pull request {pr_number} from repo {repository}\\\"\\n328:                           ) from e\\n329:                       return pull.user.login  # type: ignore[no-any-return]\\n330:               # In all other cases, return the original input username\\n331:               return username\\n332: \\n333: \\n334:           def is_exception_branch(branch: str) -> bool:\\n335:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n336:               Branches that get opted out of experiments by default, until they're explicitly enabled.\\n337:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n338:               return branch.split(\\\"/\\\")[0] in {\\\"main\\\", \\\"nightly\\\", \\\"release\\\", \\\"landchecks\\\"}\\n339: \\n340: \\n341:           def load_yaml(yaml_text: str) -> Any:\\n342:               try:\\n343:                   data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_text)\\n344:                   return data\\n345:               except yaml.YAMLError:\\n346:                   log.exception(\\\"Error loading YAML\\\")\\n347:                   raise\\n348: \\n349: \\n350:           def extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state: str) -> tuple[str, str]:\\n351:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n352:               Extracts the text with settings, if any, and the opted in users from the rollout state.\\n353: \\n354:               If the issue body contains \\\"---\\\" then the text above that is the settings\\n355:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\\n356: \\n357:               If it doesn't contain \\\"---\\\" then the settings are empty and the rest is the users.\\n358:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n359:               rollout_state_parts = rollout_state.split(\\\"---\\\")\\n360:               if len(rollout_state_parts) >= 2:\\n361:                   return rollout_state_parts[0], rollout_state_parts[1]\\n362:               else:\\n363:                   return \\\"\\\", rollout_state\\n364: \\n365: \\n366:           class UserOptins(dict[str, list[str]]):\\n367:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n368:               Dictionary of users with a list of features they have opted into\\n369:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n370: \\n371: \\n372:           def parse_user_opt_in_from_text(user_optin_text: str) -> UserOptins:\\n373:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n374:               Parse the user opt-in text into a key value pair of username and the list of features they have opted into\\n375: \\n376:               Users are GitHub usernames with the @ prefix. Each user is also a comma-separated list of features/experiments to enable.\\n377:                   - Example line: \\\"@User1,lf,split_build\\\"\\n378:                   - A \\\"#\\\" prefix indicates the user is opted out of all experiments\\n379: \\n380: \\n381:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n382:               optins = UserOptins()\\n383:               for user in user_optin_text.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\"):\\n384:                   user = user.strip(\\\"\\\\r\\\\n\\\\t -\\\")\\n385:                   if not user or not user.startswith(\\\"@\\\"):\\n386:                       # Not a valid user. Skip\\n387:                       continue\\n388: \\n389:                   if user:\\n390:                       usr_name = user.split(\\\",\\\")[0].strip(\\\"@\\\")\\n391:                       optins[usr_name] = [exp.strip(\\\" \\\") for exp in user.split(\\\",\\\")[1:]]\\n392: \\n393:               return optins\\n394: \\n395: \\n396:           def is_valid_experiment_name(experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n397:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n398:               Check if the experiment name is valid.\\n399:               A valid name:\\n400:                   - Contains only alphanumeric characters and the special characters \\\"_\\\" & \\\"-\\\"\\n401:                   - The special characters \\\"_\\\" & \\\"-\\\" shouldn't be the first or last characters\\n402:                   - Cannot contain spaces\\n403:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n404: \\n405:               valid_char_regex = r\\\"^[a-zA-Z0-9]([\\\\w-]*[a-zA-Z0-9])?$\\\"\\n406:               valid = bool(re.match(valid_char_regex, experiment_name))\\n407: \\n408:               if valid:\\n409:                   return True\\n410: \\n411:               log.error(\\n412:                   f\\\"Invalid experiment name: {experiment_name}. Experiment names should only contain alphanumeric characters, '_', and '-'. They cannot contain spaces, and the special characters '_' and '-' cannot be the first or last characters.\\\"\\n413:               )\\n414:               return False\\n415: \\n416: \\n417:           def parse_settings_from_text(settings_text: str) -> Settings:\\n418:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n419:               Parse the experiments from the issue body into a list of ExperimentSettings\\n420:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n421:               try:\\n422:                   if settings_text:\\n423:                       # Escape the backtick as well so that we can have the settings in a code block on the GH issue\\n424:                       # for easy reading\\n425:                       # Note: Using ascii for the backtick so that the cat step in _runner-determinator.yml doesn't choke on\\n426:                       #       the backtick character in shell commands.\\n427:                       backtick = chr(96)  # backtick character\\n428:                       settings_text = settings_text.strip(f\\\"\\\\r\\\\n\\\\t{backtick} \\\")\\n429:                       settings = load_yaml(settings_text)\\n430: \\n431:                       # For now we just load experiments. We can expand this if/when we add more settings\\n432:                       experiments = {}\\n433: \\n434:                       for exp_name, exp_settings in settings.get(SETTING_EXPERIMENTS).items():\\n435:                           if not is_valid_experiment_name(exp_name):\\n436:                               # Exclude invalid experiments from the list. We log an error, but don't raise an exception so that other experiments can still be processed.\\n437:                               continue\\n438: \\n439:                           valid_settings = {}\\n440:                           for setting in exp_settings:\\n441:                               if setting not in Experiment._fields:\\n442:                                   log.warning(\\n443:                                       f\\\"Unexpected setting in experiment: {setting} = {exp_settings[setting]}\\\"\\n444:                                   )\\n445:                               else:\\n446:                                   valid_settings[setting] = exp_settings[setting]\\n447: \\n448:                           experiments[exp_name] = Experiment(**valid_settings)\\n449:                       return Settings(experiments)\\n450: \\n451:               except Exception:\\n452:                   log.exception(\\\"Failed to parse settings\\\")\\n453: \\n454:               return Settings()\\n455: \\n456: \\n457:           def parse_settings(rollout_state: str) -> Settings:\\n458:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n459:               Parse settings, if any, from the rollout state.\\n460: \\n461:               If the issue body contains \\\"---\\\" then the text above that is the settings\\n462:               and the text below is the list of opted in users.\\n463: \\n464:               If it doesn't contain \\\"---\\\" then the settings are empty and the default values are used.\\n465:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n466:               settings_text, _ = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\\n467:               return parse_settings_from_text(settings_text)\\n468: \\n469: \\n470:           def parse_users(rollout_state: str) -> UserOptins:\\n471:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n472:               Parse users from the rollout state.\\n473: \\n474:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n475:               _, users_text = extract_settings_user_opt_in_from_text(rollout_state)\\n476:               return parse_user_opt_in_from_text(users_text)\\n477: \\n478: \\n479:           def is_user_opted_in(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n480:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n481:               Check if a user is opted into an experiment\\n482:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n483:               return experiment_name in user_optins.get(user, [])\\n484: \\n485: \\n486:           def is_user_opted_out(user: str, user_optins: UserOptins, experiment_name: str) -> bool:\\n487:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n488:               Check if a user explicitly opted out of an experiment\\n489:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n490:               # if the experiment is prefixed with a \\\"-\\\", then it's an opt-out\\n491:               experiment_optout = \\\"-\\\" + experiment_name\\n492:               if experiment_optout not in user_optins.get(user, []):\\n493:                   return False\\n494: \\n495:               if is_user_opted_in(user, user_optins, experiment_name):\\n496:                   log.warning(\\n497:                       f\\\"User {user} is opted into experiment {experiment_name}, but also opted out of it. Defaulting to opting out\\\"\\n498:                   )\\n499: \\n500:               return True\\n501: \\n502: \\n503:           def get_runner_prefix(\\n504:               rollout_state: str,\\n505:               workflow_requestors: Iterable[str],\\n506:               branch: str,\\n507:               eligible_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\\n508:               opt_out_experiments: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),\\n509:               is_canary: bool = False,\\n510:           ) -> str:\\n511:               settings = parse_settings(rollout_state)\\n512:               user_optins = parse_users(rollout_state)\\n513: \\n514:               fleet_prefix = \\\"\\\"\\n515:               prefixes = []\\n516:               for experiment_name, experiment_settings in settings.experiments.items():\\n517:                   if not experiment_settings.all_branches and is_exception_branch(branch):\\n518:                       log.info(\\n519:                           f\\\"Branch {branch} is an exception branch. Not enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n520:                       )\\n521:                       continue\\n522: \\n523:                   if opt_out_experiments:\\n524:                       if experiment_name in opt_out_experiments:\\n525:                           opt_out_exp_list = \\\", \\\".join(opt_out_experiments)\\n526:                           log.info(\\n527:                               f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as this workflow has opted-out (opted out experiments are: {opt_out_exp_list})\\\"\\n528:                           )\\n529:                           continue\\n530: \\n531:                   if eligible_experiments:\\n532:                       if experiment_name not in eligible_experiments:\\n533:                           exp_list = \\\", \\\".join(eligible_experiments)\\n534:                           log.info(\\n535:                               f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not in the eligible_experiments list: {exp_list}\\\"\\n536:                           )\\n537:                           continue\\n538:                   elif not experiment_settings.default:\\n539:                       log.info(\\n540:                           f\\\"Skipping experiment '{experiment_name}', as it is not a default experiment\\\"\\n541:                       )\\n542:                       continue\\n543: \\n544:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted out to this experiment?\\n545:                   opted_out_users = [\\n546:                       requestor\\n547:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\\n548:                       if is_user_opted_out(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\\n549:                   ]\\n550: \\n551:                   if opted_out_users:\\n552:                       log.info(\\n553:                           f\\\"{', '.join(opted_out_users)} have opted out of experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n554:                       )\\n555:                       continue\\n556: \\n557:                   # Is any workflow_requestor opted in to this experiment?\\n558:                   opted_in_users = [\\n559:                       requestor\\n560:                       for requestor in workflow_requestors\\n561:                       if is_user_opted_in(requestor, user_optins, experiment_name)\\n562:                   ]\\n563: \\n564:                   enabled = False\\n565:                   if opted_in_users:\\n566:                       log.info(\\n567:                           f\\\"{', '.join(opted_in_users)} have opted into experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n568:                       )\\n569:                       enabled = True\\n570: \\n571:                   elif experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\\n572:                       # If no user is opted in, then we randomly enable the experiment based on the rollout percentage\\n573:                       if random.uniform(0, 100) <= experiment_settings.rollout_perc:\\n574:                           log.info(\\n575:                               f\\\"Based on rollout percentage of {experiment_settings.rollout_perc}%, enabling experiment {experiment_name}.\\\"\\n576:                           )\\n577:                           enabled = True\\n578: \\n579:                   if enabled:\\n580:                       label = experiment_name\\n581:                       if experiment_name == LF_FLEET_EXPERIMENT:\\n582:                           # We give some special treatment to the \\\"lf\\\" experiment since determines the fleet we use\\n583:                           #  - If it's enabled, then we always list it's prefix first\\n584:                           #  - If we're in the canary branch, then we append \\\".c\\\" to the lf prefix\\n585:                           if is_canary:\\n586:                               label += CANARY_FLEET_SUFFIX\\n587:                           fleet_prefix = label\\n588:                       else:\\n589:                           prefixes.append(label)\\n590: \\n591:               if len(prefixes) > 1:\\n592:                   log.error(\\n593:                       f\\\"Only a fleet and one other experiment can be enabled for a job at any time. Enabling {prefixes[0]} and ignoring the rest, which are {', '.join(prefixes[1:])}\\\"\\n594:                   )\\n595:                   prefixes = prefixes[:1]\\n596: \\n597:               # Fleet always comes first\\n598:               if fleet_prefix:\\n599:                   prefixes.insert(0, fleet_prefix)\\n600: \\n601:               return \\\".\\\".join(prefixes) + \\\".\\\" if prefixes else \\\"\\\"\\n602: \\n603: \\n604:           def get_rollout_state_from_issue(github_token: str, repo: str, issue_num: int) -> str:\\n605:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n606:               Gets the first comment of the issue, which contains the desired rollout state.\\n607: \\n608:               The default issue we use - https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/issues/5132\\n609:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n610:               gh = get_gh_client(github_token)\\n611:               issue = get_issue(gh, repo, issue_num)\\n612:               return str(issue.get_comments()[0].body.strip(\\\"\\\\n\\\\t \\\"))\\n613: \\n614: \\n615:           def download_json(url: str, headers: dict[str, str], num_retries: int = 3) -> Any:\\n616:               for _ in range(num_retries):\\n617:                   try:\\n618:                       req = Request(url=url, headers=headers)\\n619:                       content = urlopen(req, timeout=5).read().decode(\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n620:                       return json.loads(content)\\n621:                   except Exception as e:\\n622:                       log.warning(f\\\"Could not download {url}: {e}\\\")\\n623: \\n624:               log.warning(f\\\"All {num_retries} retries exhausted, downloading {url} failed\\\")\\n625:               return {}\\n626: \\n627: \\n628:           @cache\\n629:           def get_pr_info(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n630:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n631:               Dynamically get PR information\\n632:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n633:               github_api = f\\\"https://api.github.com/repos/{github_repo}\\\"\\n634:               headers = {\\n635:                   \\\"Accept\\\": \\\"application/vnd.github.v3+json\\\",\\n636:                   \\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"token {github_token}\\\",\\n637:               }\\n638:               json_response: dict[str, Any] = download_json(\\n639:                   url=f\\\"{github_api}/issues/{pr_number}\\\",\\n640:                   headers=headers,\\n641:               )\\n642: \\n643:               if not json_response:\\n644:                   log.warning(f\\\"Failed to get the labels for #{pr_number}\\\")\\n645:                   return {}\\n646: \\n647:               return json_response\\n648: \\n649: \\n650:           def get_labels(github_repo: str, github_token: str, pr_number: int) -> set[str]:\\n651:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n652:               Dynamically get the latest list of labels from the pull request\\n653:               \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n654:               pr_info = get_pr_info(github_repo, github_token, pr_number)\\n655:               return {\\n656:                   label.get(\\\"name\\\") for label in pr_info.get(\\\"labels\\\", []) if label.get(\\\"name\\\")\\n657:               }\\n658: \\n659: \\n660:           def main() -> None:\\n661:               args = parse_args()\\n662: \\n663:               runner_label_prefix = DEFAULT_LABEL_PREFIX\\n664: \\n665:               # Check if the PR is opt-out\\n666:               if args.pr_number:\\n667:                   labels = get_labels(args.github_repo, args.github_token, int(args.pr_number))\\n668:                   if OPT_OUT_LABEL in labels:\\n669:                       log.info(\\n670:                           f\\\"Opt-out runner determinator because #{args.pr_number} has {OPT_OUT_LABEL} label\\\"\\n671:                       )\\n672:                       set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\\n673:                       sys.exit()\\n674: \\n675:               try:\\n676:                   rollout_state = get_rollout_state_from_issue(\\n677:                       args.github_token, args.github_issue_repo, args.github_issue\\n678:                   )\\n679: \\n680:                   username = get_potential_pr_author(\\n681:                       args.github_token,\\n682:                       args.github_repo,\\n683:                       args.github_actor,\\n684:                       args.github_ref_type,\\n685:                       args.github_branch,\\n686:                   )\\n687: \\n688:                   is_canary = args.github_repo == \\\"pytorch/pytorch-canary\\\"\\n689: \\n690:                   runner_label_prefix = get_runner_prefix(\\n691:                       rollout_state,\\n692:                       (args.github_issue_owner, username),\\n693:                       args.github_branch,\\n694:                       args.eligible_experiments,\\n695:                       args.opt_out_experiments,\\n696:                       is_canary,\\n697:                   )\\n698: \\n699:               except Exception as e:\\n700:                   log.error(\\n701:                       f\\\"Failed to get issue. Defaulting to Meta runners and no experiments. Exception: {e}\\\"\\n702:                   )\\n703: \\n704:               set_github_output(GH_OUTPUT_KEY_LABEL_TYPE, runner_label_prefix)\\n705: \\n706: \\n707:           if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n708:               main()\\n709: \\n710:           EOF\\n711: \\n712:           cat runner_determinator.py\\n713: \\n714:       - name: Install dependencies\\n715:         run: python3 -m pip install urllib3==1.26.18 PyGithub==2.3.0\\n716: \\n717:       - name: Get the workflow type for the current user\\n718:         id: set-condition\\n719:         run: |\\n720:           curr_branch=\\\"${{ inputs.curr_branch }}\\\"\\n721:           curr_ref_type=\\\"${{ inputs.curr_ref_type }}\\\"\\n722:           echo \\\"Current branch is '$curr_branch'\\\"\\n723: \\n724:           python3 runner_determinator.py \\\\\\n725:             --github-token \\\"$GITHUB_TOKEN\\\" \\\\\\n726:             --github-issue \\\"$ISSUE_NUMBER\\\" \\\\\\n727:             --github-branch \\\"$curr_branch\\\" \\\\\\n728:             --github-actor \\\"$TRIGGERING_ACTOR\\\" \\\\\\n729:             --github-issue-owner \\\"$ISSUE_OWNER\\\" \\\\\\n730:             --github-ref-type \\\"$curr_ref_type\\\" \\\\\\n731:             --github-repo \\\"$GITHUB_REPOSITORY\\\" \\\\\\n732:             --eligible-experiments \\\"$CHECK_EXPERIMENTS\\\" \\\\\\n733:             --opt-out-experiments \\\"$OPT_OUT_EXPERIMENTS\\\" \\\\\\n734:             --pr-number \\\"${PR_NUMBER}\\\"\\n735: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml\"}"
99. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_win-build.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: windows-build\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       cuda-version:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: What CUDA version to build with, \\\"cpu\\\" for none.\\n14:       use-xpu:\\n15:         required: false\\n16:         type: boolean\\n17:         default: false\\n18:         description: If set, build with XPU support.\\n19:       xpu-version:\\n20:         required: false\\n21:         type: string\\n22:         description: The version of XPU support package.\\n23:       vc-year:\\n24:         required: false\\n25:         type: string\\n26:         default: \\\"2022\\\"\\n27:         description: The Visual Studio year to use for building.\\n28:       build-with-debug:\\n29:         required: false\\n30:         type: boolean\\n31:         default: false\\n32:         description: If set, build in debug mode.\\n33:       sync-tag:\\n34:         required: false\\n35:         type: string\\n36:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n37:         description: |\\n38:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n39:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n40:       test-matrix:\\n41:         required: false\\n42:         type: string\\n43:         description: |\\n44:           An option JSON description of what test configs to run later on. This\\n45:           is moved here from the Linux test workflow so that we can apply filter\\n46:           logic using test-config labels earlier and skip unnecessary builds\\n47:       runner:\\n48:         required: false\\n49:         type: string\\n50:         default: \\\"windows.4xlarge.nonephemeral\\\"\\n51:         description: |\\n52:           Label of the runner this job should run on.\\n53: \\n54:     outputs:\\n55:       test-matrix:\\n56:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n57:         description: An optional JSON description of what test configs to run later on.\\n58:       build-environment:\\n59:         value: ${{ jobs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n60:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n61: \\n62: env:\\n63:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n64: \\n65: jobs:\\n66:   build:\\n67:     # Don't run on forked repos.\\n68:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n69:     runs-on: ${{ inputs.runner }}\\n70:     timeout-minutes: 240\\n71:     outputs:\\n72:       test-matrix: ${{ steps.filter.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n73:       build-environment: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n74:     defaults:\\n75:       run:\\n76:         shell: bash\\n77:     steps:\\n78:       # Duplicated in win-test because this MUST go before a checkout\\n79:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\\n80:         shell: bash\\n81:         run: |\\n82:           git config --global core.longpaths true\\n83:           git config --global core.symlinks true\\n84:           git config --global core.ignorecase false\\n85: \\n86:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\\n87:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\\n88:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\\n89:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false\\n90: \\n91:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n92:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n93:         with:\\n94:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n95:           instructions: |\\n96:             To forward remote desktop on your local machine ssh as follows:\\n97:               ssh -L 3389:localhost:3389 %%username%%@%%hostname%%\\n98:             And then change password using `passwd` command.\\n99: \\n100:             To start build locally, change working folder to \\\\actions-runner\\\\_work\\\\pytorch\\\\pytorch,\\n101:             Activate miniconda and Visual Studio environment, by running:\\n102:               call C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\\\Scripts\\\\activate.bat C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\n103:               call \\\"C:\\\\Program Files (x86)\\\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\\\2022\\\\BuildTools\\\\VC\\\\Auxiliary\\\\Build\\\\vcvarsall.bat\\\" x64\\n104: \\n105:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n106:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n107:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n108:         with:\\n109:           no-sudo: true\\n110: \\n111:       - name: Setup Windows\\n112:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-win\\n113:         with:\\n114:           cuda-version: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n115: \\n116:       - name: Parse ref\\n117:         id: parse-ref\\n118:         shell: bash\\n119:         run: python3 .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n120: \\n121:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n122:         id: get-job-id\\n123:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n124:         if: always()\\n125:         with:\\n126:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n127: \\n128:       # Apply the filter logic to the build step too if the test-config label is already there\\n129:       - name: Select all requested test configurations (if the test matrix is available)\\n130:         id: filter\\n131:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n132:         with:\\n133:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n134:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n135:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n136: \\n137:       - name: Download pytest cache\\n138:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\\n139:         continue-on-error: true\\n140:         with:\\n141:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n142:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n143: \\n144:       - name: Build\\n145:         if: steps.filter.outputs.is-test-matrix-empty == 'False' || inputs.test-matrix == ''\\n146:         id: build\\n147:         shell: bash\\n148:         env:\\n149:           PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n150:           BRANCH: ${{ steps.parse-ref.outputs.branch }}\\n151:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n152:           BUILD_WHEEL: 1\\n153:           MAX_JOBS: 8\\n154:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n155:           PYTHON_VERSION: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n156:           SCCACHE_BUCKET: \\\"ossci-compiler-cache\\\"\\n157:           SCCACHE_S3_KEY_PREFIX: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n158:           SCCACHE_REGION: us-east-1\\n159:           VC_PRODUCT: \\\"BuildTools\\\"\\n160:           VC_VERSION: \\\"\\\"\\n161:           VC_YEAR: \\\"${{ inputs.vc-year }}\\\"\\n162:           ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n163:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n164:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n165:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n166:           DEBUG: ${{ inputs.build-with-debug && '1' || '0' }}\\n167:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: \\\"8.6\\\"\\n168:           USE_CUDA: ${{ inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu' && '1' || '0' }}\\n169:           USE_XPU: ${{ inputs.use-xpu == true && '1' || '0' }}\\n170:           XPU_VERSION: \\\"${{ inputs.xpu-version }}\\\"\\n171:           OUR_GITHUB_JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n172:         run: |\\n173:           .ci/pytorch/win-build.sh\\n174: \\n175:       # Collect Windows torch libs and CUDA libs for cross-compilation\\n176:       - name: Collect Windows CUDA libs for cross-compilation\\n177:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped' && inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu'\\n178:         shell: bash\\n179:         run: |\\n180:           set -ex\\n181: \\n182:           # Create directory structure if does not exist\\n183:           mkdir -p /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results\\n184: \\n185:           # Copy CUDA libs\\n186:           CUDA_PATH=\\\"/c/Program Files/NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit/CUDA/v${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\\"\\n187: \\n188:           if [ -f \\\"${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cuda.lib\\\" ]; then\\n189:             cp \\\"${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cuda.lib\\\" /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n190:           fi\\n191: \\n192:           if [ -f \\\"${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cudart.lib\\\" ]; then\\n193:             cp \\\"${CUDA_PATH}/lib/x64/cudart.lib\\\" /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n194:           fi\\n195: \\n196:           # List collected files\\n197:           echo \\\"Collected CUDA libs:\\\"\\n198:           ls -lah /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/*.lib\\n199: \\n200:       # Upload to github so that people can click and download artifacts\\n201:       - name: Upload artifacts to s3\\n202:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n203:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n204:         with:\\n205:           retention-days: 14\\n206:           if-no-files-found: error\\n207:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n208:           path: C:\\\\${{ github.run_id }}\\\\build-results\\n209: \\n210:       - name: Upload sccache stats\\n211:         if: steps.build.outcome != 'skipped'\\n212:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-sccache-stats\\n213:         with:\\n214:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n215: \\n216:       - name: Teardown Windows\\n217:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-win\\n218:         if: always()\\n219:         timeout-minutes: 120\\n220:         with:\\n221:           extra-delete-dir: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n222: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_win-build.yml\"}"
100. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

{
  "path": ".github/workflows/_win-test.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: win-test\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5:     inputs:\\n6:       build-environment:\\n7:         required: true\\n8:         type: string\\n9:         description: Top-level label for what's being built/tested.\\n10:       cuda-version:\\n11:         required: true\\n12:         type: string\\n13:         description: What CUDA version to build with, \\\"cpu\\\" for none.\\n14:       test-matrix:\\n15:         required: true\\n16:         type: string\\n17:         description: JSON description of what test configs to run.\\n18:       sync-tag:\\n19:         required: false\\n20:         type: string\\n21:         default: \\\"\\\"\\n22:         description: |\\n23:           If this is set, our linter will use this to make sure that every other\\n24:           job with the same `sync-tag` is identical.\\n25:       timeout-minutes:\\n26:         required: false\\n27:         type: number\\n28:         default: 240\\n29:         description: |\\n30:           Set the maximum (in minutes) how long the workflow should take to finish\\n31:       disable-monitor:\\n32:         description: |\\n33:           [Experimental] Disable utilization monitoring for tests.\\n34:           Currently, by default we disable the monitor job and only look for specific tests,\\n35:           since we are investigating the behaviour of the monitor script with different tests.\\n36:         required: false\\n37:         type: boolean\\n38:         default: true\\n39:       monitor-log-interval:\\n40:         description: |\\n41:           Set the interval for the monitor script to log utilization.\\n42:         required: false\\n43:         type: number\\n44:         default: 5\\n45:       monitor-data-collect-interval:\\n46:         description: |\\n47:           Set the interval for the monitor script to collect data.\\n48:         required: false\\n49:         type: number\\n50:         default: 1\\n51: env:\\n52:   GIT_DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}\\n53: \\n54: jobs:\\n55:   test:\\n56:     # Don't run on forked repos or empty test matrix\\n57:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && toJSON(fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix).include) != '[]'\\n58:     strategy:\\n59:       matrix: ${{ fromJSON(inputs.test-matrix) }}\\n60:       fail-fast: false\\n61:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n62:     timeout-minutes: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n63:     defaults:\\n64:       run:\\n65:         shell: bash\\n66:     steps:\\n67:       # Duplicated in win-build because this MUST go before a checkout\\n68:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\\n69:         shell: bash\\n70:         run: |\\n71:           git config --global core.longpaths true\\n72:           git config --global core.symlinks true\\n73:           git config --global core.ignorecase false\\n74: \\n75:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\\n76:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\\n77:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\\n78:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false\\n79: \\n80:       - name: Setup SSH (Click me for login details)\\n81:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n82:         with:\\n83:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n84:           instructions: |\\n85:             To forward remote desktop on your local machine ssh as follows:\\n86:               ssh -L 3389:localhost:3389 %%username%%@%%hostname%%\\n87:             And then change password using `passwd` command.\\n88: \\n89:             To start tests locally, change working folder to \\\\actions-runner\\\\_work\\\\pytorch\\\\pytorch\\\\test,\\n90:             Activate miniconda and Visual Studio environment and set PYTHON_PATH, by running:\\n91:               call C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\\\Scripts\\\\activate.bat C:\\\\Jenkins\\\\Miniconda3\\n92:               call \\\"C:\\\\Program Files (x86)\\\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\\\2022\\\\BuildTools\\\\VC\\\\Auxiliary\\\\Build\\\\vcvarsall.bat\\\" x64\\n93:               set PYTHONPATH=C:\\\\actions-runner\\\\_work\\\\pytorch\\\\pytorch\\\\build\\\\win_tmp\\\\build\\n94: \\n95:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n96:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n97:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n98:         with:\\n99:           no-sudo: true\\n100: \\n101:       - name: Setup Windows\\n102:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-win\\n103:         with:\\n104:           cuda-version: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n105: \\n106:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n107:         id: get-job-id\\n108:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n109:         if: always()\\n110:         with:\\n111:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n112: \\n113:       - name: Start monitoring script\\n114:         id: monitor-script\\n115:         env:\\n116:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n117:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n118:           WORKFLOW_NAME: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n119:           WORKFLOW_RUN_ID: ${{github.run_id}}\\n120:           MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-log-interval }}\\n121:           MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL: ${{ inputs.monitor-data-collect-interval }}\\n122:         shell: bash\\n123:         if: ${{ !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n124:         continue-on-error: true\\n125:         run: |\\n126:           # Windows conda doesn't have python3 binary, only python, but it's python3\\n127:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m pip install psutil==5.9.8 dataclasses_json==0.6.7 nvidia-ml-py==11.525.84\\n128:           ${CONDA_RUN} python -m tools.stats.monitor --log-interval \\\"$MONITOR_LOG_INTERVAL\\\" --data-collect-interval \\\"$MONITOR_DATA_COLLECT_INTERVAL\\\" > usage_log.txt 2>&1 &\\n129:           echo \\\"monitor-script-pid=${!}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n130: \\n131:       - name: Download PyTorch Build Artifacts\\n132:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\\n133:         with:\\n134:           name: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n135:           path: C:\\\\${{ github.run_id }}\\\\build-results\\n136: \\n137:       - name: Check build-results folder\\n138:         shell: powershell\\n139:         run: |\\n140:           tree /F C:\\\\$Env:GITHUB_RUN_ID\\\\build-results\\n141: \\n142:       - name: Download TD artifacts\\n143:         continue-on-error: true\\n144:         uses: ./.github/actions/download-td-artifacts\\n145: \\n146:       - name: Check for keep-going label and re-enabled test issues\\n147:         # This uses the filter-test-configs action because it conveniently\\n148:         # checks for labels and re-enabled test issues.  It does not actually do\\n149:         # any filtering.  All filtering is done in the build step.\\n150:         id: keep-going\\n151:         uses: ./.github/actions/filter-test-configs\\n152:         with:\\n153:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n154:           test-matrix: ${{ inputs.test-matrix }}\\n155:           job-name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n156: \\n157:       - name: Set Test step time\\n158:         id: test-timeout\\n159:         shell: bash\\n160:         env:\\n161:           JOB_TIMEOUT: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check == 'mem_leak_check' && 600 || inputs.timeout-minutes }}\\n162:         run: |\\n163:           echo \\\"timeout=$((JOB_TIMEOUT-30))\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n164: \\n165:       - name: Test\\n166:         id: test\\n167:         shell: bash\\n168:         timeout-minutes: ${{ fromJson(steps.test-timeout.outputs.timeout) }}\\n169:         env:\\n170:           USE_CUDA: ${{ inputs.cuda-version != 'cpu' && '1' || '0' }}\\n171:           INSTALL_WINDOWS_SDK: 1\\n172:           PYTHON_VERSION: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n173:           CONTINUE_THROUGH_ERROR: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.keep-going }}\\n174:           VERBOSE_TEST_LOGS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-verbose-test-logs }}\\n175:           TEST_SHOWLOCALS: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-test-showlocals }}\\n176:           NO_TEST_TIMEOUT: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-test-timeout }}\\n177:           NO_TD: ${{ steps.keep-going.outputs.ci-no-td }}\\n178:           VC_PRODUCT: \\\"BuildTools\\\"\\n179:           VC_VERSION: \\\"\\\"\\n180:           VS_VERSION: \\\"17.4.1\\\"\\n181:           VC_YEAR: \\\"2022\\\"\\n182:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n183:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n184:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n185:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n186:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n187:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n188:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n189:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n190:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n191:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n192:           SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n193:           CUDA_VERSION: ${{ inputs.cuda-version }}\\n194:           PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /c/${{ github.run_id }}/build-results/\\n195:           BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: ${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n196:           ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n197:           SHARD_NUMBER: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n198:           NUM_TEST_SHARDS: ${{ matrix.num_shards }}\\n199:           TEST_CONFIG: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n200:           REENABLED_ISSUES: ${{ github.event.pull_request.reenabled-issues }}\\n201:           TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST: \\\"8.6\\\"\\n202:           PYTORCH_TEST_CUDA_MEM_LEAK_CHECK: ${{ matrix.mem_leak_check && '1' || '0' }}\\n203:           PYTORCH_TEST_RERUN_DISABLED_TESTS: ${{ matrix.rerun_disabled_tests && '1' || '0' }}\\n204:         run: |\\n205:           pushd \\\"${PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR}\\\"\\n206:           # shellcheck disable=SC2046,SC2102\\n207:           python3 -mpip install $(echo *.whl)[opt-einsum,optree] optree==0.13.0\\n208:           popd\\n209: \\n210:           .ci/pytorch/win-test.sh\\n211: \\n212:       - name: Upload pytest cache if tests failed\\n213:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-upload\\n214:         continue-on-error: true\\n215:         if: failure() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion == 'failure'\\n216:         with:\\n217:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n218:           shard: ${{ matrix.shard }}\\n219:           sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n220:           test_config: ${{ matrix.config }}\\n221:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}_${{ inputs.build-environment }}\\n222: \\n223:       - name: Print remaining test logs\\n224:         shell: bash\\n225:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion\\n226:         run: |\\n227:           cat test/**/*_toprint.log || true\\n228: \\n229:       - name: Stop monitoring script\\n230:         if: ${{ always() && steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n231:         shell: bash\\n232:         continue-on-error: true\\n233:         env:\\n234:           MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID: ${{ steps.monitor-script.outputs.monitor-script-pid }}\\n235:         run: |\\n236:           kill \\\"$MONITOR_SCRIPT_PID\\\"\\n237: \\n238:       - name: Upload test artifacts\\n239:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-test-artifacts\\n240:         if: always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped'\\n241:         with:\\n242:           file-suffix: ${{ github.job }}-${{ matrix.config }}-${{ matrix.shard }}-${{ matrix.num_shards }}-${{ matrix.runner }}_${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n243: \\n244:       - name: Upload utilization stats\\n245:         if: ${{ always() && steps.test.conclusion && steps.test.conclusion != 'skipped' && !inputs.disable-monitor }}\\n246:         continue-on-error: true\\n247:         uses: ./.github/actions/upload-utilization-stats\\n248:         with:\\n249:           job_id: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n250:           job_name: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n251:           workflow_name: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n252:           workflow_run_id: ${{github.run_id}}\\n253:           workflow_attempt: ${{github.run_attempt}}\\n254: \\n255:       - name: Parse ref\\n256:         id: parse-ref\\n257:         shell: bash\\n258:         run: python3 .github/scripts/parse_ref.py\\n259: \\n260:       - name: Teardown Windows\\n261:         uses: ./.github/actions/teardown-win\\n262:         if: always()\\n263:         timeout-minutes: 120\\n264: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/_win-test.yml\"}"
101. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: CI for distributed tests on B200\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     paths:\\n6:       - .github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8:   push:\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       - ciflow/b200-distributed/*\\n11:   schedule:\\n12:     - cron: 46 8 * * *  # about 1:46am PDT\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n16:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n17: \\n18: permissions:\\n19:   id-token: write\\n20:   contents: read\\n21: \\n22: jobs:\\n23: \\n24:   get-label-type:\\n25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n26:     name: get-label-type\\n27:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n28:     with:\\n29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n31:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n32:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n33: \\n34:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200:\\n35:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n40:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge\\n41:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-distributed-b200\\n42:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n43:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\\n44:       test-matrix: |\\n45:         { include: [\\n46:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200.8\\\" },\\n47:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200.8\\\" },\\n48:         ]}\\n49:     secrets: inherit\\n50: \\n51:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-test-distributed-b200:\\n52:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-test-b200\\n53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n54:     needs:\\n55:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200\\n56:     with:\\n57:       timeout-minutes: 1200\\n58:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.build-environment }}\\n59:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.docker-image }}\\n60:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-build-distributed-b200.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n61:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n62:     secrets: inherit\\n63: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/b200-distributed.yml\"}"
102. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/b200-symm-mem.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
