Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: periodic-rocm-mi300\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     # We have several schedules so jobs can check github.event.schedule to activate only for a fraction of the runs.\\n6:     # Also run less frequently on weekends.\\n7:     - cron: 45 0,8,16 * * 1-5\\n8:     - cron: 45 4 * * 0,6\\n9:     - cron: 45 4,12,20 * * 1-5\\n10:     - cron: 45 12 * * 0,6\\n11:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests\\n12:   push:\\n13:     tags:\\n14:       - ciflow/periodic/*\\n15:       - ciflow/periodic-rocm-mi300/*\\n16:     branches:\\n17:       - release/*\\n18:   workflow_dispatch:\\n19: \\n20: concurrency:\\n21:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}-${{ github.event.schedule }}\\n22:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n23: \\n24: permissions: read-all\\n25: \\n26: jobs:\\n27:   llm-td:\\n28:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n29:     name: before-test\\n30:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\\n31:     permissions:\\n32:       id-token: write\\n33:       contents: read\\n34: \\n35:   target-determination:\\n36:     name: before-test\\n37:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\\n38:     needs: llm-td\\n39:     permissions:\\n40:       id-token: write\\n41:       contents: read\\n42: \\n43:   get-label-type:\\n44:     name: get-label-type\\n45:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n46:     if: (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n47:     with:\\n48:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n49:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n50:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n51:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n52: \\n53:   linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build:\\n54:     name: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\\n55:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n56:     needs: get-label-type\\n57:     with:\\n58:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n59:       build-environment: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\\n60:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\\n61:       test-matrix: |\\n62:         { include: [\\n63:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.4.b\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n64:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.4.b\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n65:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.4.b\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n66:         ]}\\n67:     secrets: inherit\\n68: \\n69:   linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-test:\\n70:     permissions:\\n71:       id-token: write\\n72:       contents: read\\n73:     name: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\\n74:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n75:     needs:\\n76:       - linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build\\n77:       - target-determination\\n78:     with:\\n79:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n80: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi300.yml\"}"

default: true\\n16:       inference:\\n17:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\\n18:         required: false\\n19:         type: boolean\\n20:         default: true\\n21: default: false\\n51:       maxautotune:\\n52:         description: Run inductor_max_autotune?\\n53:         required: false\\n54:         type: boolean\\n55:         default: false\\n56: 219. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Retrieval PyTorch Tests for Target Determination\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5: \\n6: permissions:\\n7:   id-token: write\\n8:   contents: read\\n9: \\n10: jobs:\\n11:   get-label-type:\\n12:     name: get-label-type\\n13:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n14:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n15:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n16:     with:\\n17:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n18:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n19:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n20:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n21: \\n22:   llm-retrieval:\\n23:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n24:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n25:     runs-on: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.4xlarge\\\"\\n26:     continue-on-error: true\\n27:     needs: get-label-type\\n28:     steps:\\n29:       - name: Clone PyTorch\\n30:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\\n31:         with:\\n32:           repository: pytorch/pytorch\\n33:           fetch-depth: 0\\n34:           path: pytorch\\n35: \\n36:       - name: Setup Linux\\n37:         uses: ./pytorch/.github/actions/setup-linux\\n38: \\n39:       - name: Clone CodeLlama\\n40:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\\n41:         with:\\n42:           repository: osalpekar/codellama\\n43:           ref: main\\n44:           path: codellama\\n45: \\n46:       - name: Clone Target Determination Code\\n47:         uses: actions/checkout@11bd71901bbe5b1630ceea73d27597364c9af683 # v4.2.2\\n48:         with:\\n49:           repository: osalpekar/llm-target-determinator\\n50:           ref: v0.0.2\\n51:           path: llm-target-determinator\\n52: \\n53:       - name: Install requirements\\n54:         shell: bash\\n55:         run: |\\n56:           set -euxo pipefail\\n57:           python3 -m pip install -r llm-target-determinator/requirements.txt\\n58:           cd \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/codellama\\\"\\n59:           python3 -m pip install -e .\\n60: \\n61:       - name: Fetch CodeLlama Checkpoint\\n62:         shell: bash\\n63:         run: |\\n64:           set -euxo pipefail\\n65:           cd \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/codellama\\\"\\n66:           mkdir \\\"CodeLlama-7b-Python\\\"\\n67:           aws s3 cp \\\"s3://target-determinator-assets/CodeLlama-7b-Python\\\" \\\"CodeLlama-7b-Python\\\" --recursive --no-progress\\n68: \\n69:       - name: Fetch indexes\\n70:         uses: nick-fields/retry@7152eba30c6575329ac0576536151aca5a72780e # v3.0.0\\n71:         with:\\n72:           max_attempts: 3\\n73:           retry_wait_seconds: 10\\n74:           timeout_minutes: 5\\n75:           shell: bash\\n76:           command: |\\n77:             set -euxo pipefail\\n78:             python3 -m pip install awscli==1.29.40\\n79:             cd \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"/llm-target-determinator/assets\\n80:             aws s3 cp \\\"s3://target-determinator-assets/indexes/latest\\\" . --recursive\\n81: \\n82:             unzip -o indexer-files\\\\*.zip\\n83:             rm indexer-files*.zip\\n84: \\n85:       - name: Run Retriever\\n86:         id: run_retriever\\n87:         continue-on-error: true  # ghstack not currently supported due to problems getting git diff\\n88:         shell: bash\\n89:         run: |\\n90:           set -euxo pipefail\\n91:           cd \\\"${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}\\\"/llm-target-determinator\\n92:           export PATH=\\\"$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH\\\"\\n93:           torchrun \\\\\\n94:             --standalone \\\\\\n95:             --nnodes=1 \\\\\\n96:             --nproc-per-node=1 \\\\\\n97:             retriever.py \\\\\\n98:             --experiment-name indexer-files \\\\\\n99:             --pr-parse-format GITDIFF\\n100:           cd assets\\n101:           zip -r mappings.zip mappings\\n102: \\n103:       - name: Upload results to s3\\n104:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n105:         if: ${{ steps.run_retriever.outcome == 'success' }}\\n106:         with:\\n107:           name: llm_results\\n108:           retention-days: 14\\n109:           if-no-files-found: warn\\n110:           path: llm-target-determinator/assets/mappings.zip\\n111:         env:\\n112:           AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: \\\"\\\"\\n113:           AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: \\\"\\\"\\n114:           AWS_SESSION_TOKEN: \\\"\\\"\\n115:           AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: \\\"\\\"\\n116:           AWS_REGION: \\\"\\\"\\n117: \\n118:       - name: Teardown Linux\\n119:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/teardown-linux@release/2.10\\n120:         if: always()\\n121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\"}"
220. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": ".github/workflows/unstable.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: unstable\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   push:\\n5:     branches:\\n6:       - main\\n7:     tags:\\n8:       - ciflow/unstable/*\\n9:   workflow_dispatch:\\n10: \\n11: concurrency:\\n12:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n13:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n14: \\n15: permissions:\\n16:   id-token: write\\n17:   contents: read\\n18: \\n19: jobs:\\n20:   # There must be at least one job here to satisfy GitHub action workflow syntax\\n21:   introduction:\\n22:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n23:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n24:     continue-on-error: true\\n25:     steps:\\n26:       - name: Introduce PyTorch unstable workflow\\n27:         run: |\\n28:           echo \\\"PyTorch unstable workflow is used to host experimental or flaky jobs\\\"\\n29:           echo \\\" that needs to be run for every commit, but doesn't block PR merging\\\"\\n30:           echo \\\" as part of the stable pull or trunk workflows.\\\"\\n31:           echo\\n32:           echo \\\"In addition, a new label called ciflow/unstable can be attached to the\\\"\\n33:           echo \\\" PR to trigger this workflow. That can be done either manually or\\\"\\n34:           echo \\\" automatically using PyTorch auto-label bot.\\\"\\n35:           echo\\n36:           echo \\\"Once the jobs are deemed stable enough (% red signal < 5% and TTS < 3h),\\\"\\n37:           echo \\\" they can graduate and move back to pull or trunk.\\\"\\n38: \\n39:   target-determination:\\n40:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n41:     name: before-test\\n42:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\\n43:     permissions:\\n44:       id-token: write\\n45:       contents: read\\n46: \\n47:   get-label-type:\\n48:     name: get-label-type\\n49:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n50:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n51:     with:\\n52:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n53:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n54:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n55:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n56: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/unstable.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: test-scripts-and-ci-tools\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   push:\\n5:     branches:\\n6:       - main\\n7:     paths:\\n8:       - scripts/lumen_cli/**\\n9:       - .github/workflows/tools-unit-tests.yml\\n10:   pull_request:\\n11:     paths:\\n12:       - scripts/lumen_cli/**\\n13:       - .github/workflows/tools-unit-tests.yml\\n14: \\n15: concurrency:\\n16:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n17:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n18: \\n19: jobs:\\n20:   lumen-cli-unit-tests-python312:\\n21:     permissions:\\n22:       contents: read\\n23:       pull-requests: write\\n24:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n25:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n26:     steps:\\n27:       - name: Checkout pytorch\\n28:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n29:         with:\\n30:           submodules: true\\n31:           fetch-depth: 0\\n32:       - name: Setup Python\\n33:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n34:         with:\\n35:           python-version: '3.12'\\n36:           cache: pip\\n37: \\n38:       - name: Run tests\\n39:         continue-on-error: true\\n40:         run: |\\n41:           set -ex\\n42:           python3 -m venv /tmp/venv\\n43:           source /tmp/venv/bin/activate\\n44:           pip install -e .ci/lumen_cli/\\n45:           pytest -v -s .ci/lumen_cli/tests/*\\n46: \\n47:   lumen-cli-compatible-python39:\\n48:     permissions:\\n49:       contents: read\\n50:       pull-requests: write\\n51:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n52:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n53:     steps:\\n54:       - name: Checkout pytorch\\n55:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n56:         with:\\n57:           submodules: true\\n58:           fetch-depth: 0\\n59:       - name: Setup Python\\n60:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n61:         with:\\n62:           python-version: '3.10'\\n63:           cache: 'pip'\\n64:       - name: Run tests\\n65:         continue-on-error: true\\n66:         run: |\\n67:           set -ex\\n68:           python3 -m venv /tmp/venv\\n69:           source /tmp/venv/bin/activate\\n70:           pip install -e .ci/lumen_cli/\\n71: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/tools-unit-tests.yml\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # @generated DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\\n2: \\n3: # Template is at:    .github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\\n4: # Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\\n5: name: windows-arm64-binary-wheel\\n6: \\n7: on:\\n8:   push:\\n9:     # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\\n10:     branches:\\n11:       - nightly\\n12:     tags:\\n13:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\\n14:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n15:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n16:       - 'ciflow/binaries/*'\\n17:       - 'ciflow/binaries_wheel/*'\\n18:   workflow_dispatch:\\n19: \\n20: env:\\n21:   # Needed for conda builds\\n22:   ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n23:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n24:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: windows-arm64-binary-wheel\\n25:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n26:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n27:   SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n28:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n29:   OS: windows-arm64\\n30:   PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n31:   DOWNLOADS_DIR: c:\\\\temp\\\\downloads\\n32:   DEPENDENCIES_DIR: c:\\\\temp\\\\dependencies\\n33:   ENABLE_APL: 1\\n34:   ENABLE_OPENBLAS: 0\\n35:   MSVC_VERSION : 14.42\\n36: concurrency:\\n37:   group: windows-arm64-binary-wheel-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n38:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n39: \\n40: jobs:\\n41:   get-label-type:\\n42:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n43:     name: get-label-type\\n44:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n45:     with:\\n46:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n47:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n48:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n49:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n50:   wheel-py3_11-cpu-build:\\n51:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n52:     needs: get-label-type\\n53:     runs-on: \\\"windows-11-arm64-preview\\\"\\n54:     timeout-minutes: 360\\n55:     env:\\n56:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ github.workspace }}/pytorch\\n57:       PACKAGE_TYPE: wheel\\n58:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n59:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n60:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n61:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\\n62:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n63:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \\\"3.11\\\"\\n64:     steps:\\n65:       - name: Populate binary env\\n66:         shell: cmd\\n67:         run: |\\n68:           echo BINARY_ENV_FILE=%RUNNER_TEMP%/env>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n69:           echo PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%/artifacts>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n70:           echo WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n71:       - name: Bootstrap folders\\n72:         shell: cmd\\n73:         run: |\\n74:           mkdir \\\"%NIGHTLIES_PYTORCH_ROOT%\\\"\\n75:           mkdir \\\"%PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR%\\\"\\n76:       - name: Enable long paths\\n77:         shell: cmd\\n78:         run: |\\n79:           git config --system --get core.longpaths || echo \\\"core.longpaths is not set, setting it now\\\"\\n80:           git config --system core.longpaths true\\n81:       - name: Git checkout PyTorch\\n82:         uses: actions/checkout@v4\\n83:         with:\\n84:           path: \\\"pytorch\\\"\\n85:           submodules: recursive\\n86:       - name: Bootstrap Python\\n87:         shell: cmd\\n88:         run: |\\n89:           \\\"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_python.bat\\\"\\n90:       - name: Bootstrap APL\\n91:         shell: cmd\\n92:         run: |\\n93:           \\\"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_apl.bat\\\"\\n94:       - name: Bootstrap Rust\\n95:         shell: cmd\\n96:         run: |\\n97:           \\\"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_rust.bat\\\"\\n98:       - name: Bootstrap sccache\\n99:         shell: cmd\\n100:         run: |\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-wheel-nightly.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # Workflow: Inductor Unit Test\\n2: # 1. runs unit tests for inductor.\\n3: # 2. performs daily memory leak checks and reruns of disabled tests, scheduled at `29 8 * * *`.\\n4: name: inductor-unittest\\n5: \\n6: on:\\n7:   workflow_call:\\n8:   schedule:\\n9:     - cron: 29 8 * * * # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests.\\n10:   pull_request:\\n11:     paths:\\n12:       - .github/workflows/inductor-unittest.yml\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-unittest\\n16:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n17: \\n18: permissions:\\n19:   id-token: write\\n20:   contents: read\\n21: \\n22: jobs:\\n23:   get-label-type:\\n24:     name: get-label-type\\n25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n26:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n27:     with:\\n28:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n29:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n30:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n31:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n32:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n33: \\n34:   inductor-build:\\n35:     name: inductor-build\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm86\\n40:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n41:       cuda-arch-list: '8.6'\\n42:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n43:       test-matrix: |\\n44:         { include: [\\n45:           { config: \\\"inductor\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n46:           { config: \\\"inductor\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n47:           { config: \\\"inductor_distributed\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.12xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n48:           { config: \\\"inductor_cpp_wrapper\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n49:           { config: \\\"inductor_cpp_wrapper\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n50:         ]}\\n51:     secrets: inherit\\n52: \\n53:   inductor-test:\\n54:     name: inductor-test\\n55:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n56:     needs: inductor-build\\n57:     with:\\n58:       build-environment: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n59:       docker-image: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n60:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n61:     secrets: inherit\\n62: \\n63:   inductor-halide-build:\\n64:     name: inductor-halide-build\\n65:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n66:     needs: get-label-type\\n67:     with:\\n68:       build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.12-gcc11\\n69:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.12-halide\\n70:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n71:       test-matrix: |\\n72:         { include: [\\n73:           { config: \\\"inductor-halide\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.12xlarge\\\" },\\n74:         ]}\\n75:     secrets: inherit\\n76: \\n77:   inductor-halide-test:\\n78:     name: inductor-halide-test\\n79:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n80:     needs: inductor-halide-build\\n81:     with:\\n82: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-unittest.yml\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Limited CI for symmetric memory tests on H100\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     paths:\\n6:       - .github/workflows/h100-symm-mem.yml\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8:   push:\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       - ciflow/h100-symm-mem/*\\n11:   schedule:\\n12:     - cron: 22 8 * * *  # about 1:22am PDT\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n16:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n17: \\n18: permissions:\\n19:   id-token: write\\n20:   contents: read\\n21: \\n22: jobs:\\n23: \\n24:   get-label-type:\\n25:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n26:     name: get-label-type\\n27:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n28:     with:\\n29:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n30:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n31:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n32:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n33: \\n34:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-symm:\\n35:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-symm\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n40:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-symm\\n41:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n42:       cuda-arch-list: '9.0'\\n43:       test-matrix: |\\n44:         { include: [\\n45:           { config: \\\"h100-symm-mem\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100.4\\\" },\\n46:         ]}\\n47:     secrets: inherit\\n48: \\n49:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-test:\\n50:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-symm\\n51:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n52:     needs:\\n53:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-symm\\n54:     with:\\n55:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-symm.outputs.build-environment }}\\n56:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-symm.outputs.docker-image }}\\n57:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-symm.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n58:     secrets: inherit\\n59: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/h100-symm-mem.yml\"}"
225. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/nitpicker.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Nitpicker\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request_target:\\n5:     types: [opened, reopened]\\n6:     branches: [main]\\n7: \\n8:   pull_request:\\n9:     types: [opened, reopened]\\n10:     branches: [gh/**/base]\\n11: \\n12: jobs:\\n13:   triage:\\n14:     permissions:\\n15:       contents: read\\n16:       pull-requests: write\\n17:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n18:     # Do not comment on nightly builds PR\\n19:     if: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number != 26921 && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n20:     steps:\\n21:     - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n22:       uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n23:     - uses: ethanis/nitpicker@v1\\n24:       with:\\n25:         nitpicks: '.github/nitpicks.yml'\\n26:         token: \\\"${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\\"\\n27: \\n28: concurrency:\\n29:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n30:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n31: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/nitpicker.yml\"}"
226. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/lint-autoformat.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Apply lint suggestions\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled, unlabeled]\\n6: \\n7: jobs:\\n8:   lintrunner-autoformat:\\n9:     permissions:\\n10:       contents: read\\n11:       pull-requests: write\\n12:     runs-on: lf.linux.2xlarge\\n13:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'autoformat') }}\\n14:     steps:\\n15:       - name: Checkout pytorch\\n16:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n17:         with:\\n18:           submodules: true\\n19:           fetch-depth: 0\\n20:       - name: Run lintrunner (nonretryable)\\n21:         continue-on-error: true\\n22:         run: |\\n23:           set -ex\\n24:           python3 -m venv /tmp/venv\\n25:           source /tmp/venv/bin/activate\\n26:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"format --all-files\\\"\\n27:           bash .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh\\n28:       - name: Check for changes\\n29:         id: git-check\\n30:         continue-on-error: true\\n31:         run: |\\n32:           git diff --exit-code || echo \\\"changes=true\\\" >> \\\"$GITHUB_OUTPUT\\\"\\n33:       - name: Suggest changes\\n34:         if: steps.git-check.outputs.changes == 'true'\\n35:         continue-on-error: true\\n36:         uses: parkerbxyz/suggest-changes@a2ec1653b0c4cc8287d682f0066dba4a173cc7f3 # v1.0.8\\n37:         with:\\n38:           comment: \\\"Please commit the suggested changes from pytorch's linter.\\\"\\n39: \\n40: concurrency:\\n41:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n42:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n43: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/lint-autoformat.yml\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: Limited CI for CUTLASS backend on H100\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     paths:\\n6:       - .github/workflows/h100-cutlass-backend.yml\\n7:       - torch/_inductor/codegen/cuda/**\\n8:       - test/inductor/test_cutlass_backend.py\\n9:       - test/inductor/test_cutlass_evt.py\\n10:   workflow_dispatch:\\n11:   schedule:\\n12:     - cron: 22 9,21 * * *  # every 12 hours\\n13:   push:\\n14:     tags:\\n15:       - ciflow/h100-cutlass-backend/*\\n16: \\n17: concurrency:\\n18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n19:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n20: \\n21: permissions:\\n22:   id-token: write\\n23:   contents: read\\n24: \\n25: jobs:\\n26: \\n27:   get-label-type:\\n28:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n29:     name: get-label-type\\n30:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n31:     with:\\n32:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n33:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n34:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n35:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n36: \\n37:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-cutlass-backend:\\n38:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-cutlass-backend\\n39:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n40:     needs: get-label-type\\n41:     with:\\n42:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n43:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-cutlass-backend\\n44:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n45:       cuda-arch-list: '9.0'\\n46:       test-matrix: |\\n47:         { include: [\\n48:           { config: \\\"h100_cutlass_backend\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\", owners: [\\\"oncall:pt2\\\"] },\\n49:         ]}\\n50:     secrets: inherit\\n51: \\n52:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-test:\\n53:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90-cutlass-backend\\n54:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n55:     needs:\\n56:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-cutlass-backend\\n57:     with:\\n58:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-cutlass-backend.outputs.build-environment }}\\n59:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-cutlass-backend.outputs.docker-image }}\\n60:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build-cutlass-backend.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n61:     secrets: inherit\\n62: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/h100-cutlass-backend.yml\"}"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: operator_microbenchmark\\n2: # NOTE: When adding a new test, please update README: ../../benchmarks/operator_benchmark/README.md\\n3: \\n4: on:\\n5:   push:\\n6:     tags:\\n7:       - ciflow/op-benchmark/*\\n8:   workflow_dispatch:\\n9:   schedule:\\n10:     # Run at 06:00 UTC everyday\\n11:     - cron: 0 6 * * *\\n12: \\n13: concurrency:\\n14:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n15:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n16: \\n17: permissions:\\n18:   id-token: write\\n19:   contents: read\\n20: \\n21: jobs:\\n22:   get-label-type:\\n23:     name: get-label-type\\n24:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n25:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n26:     with:\\n27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n30:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n31: \\n32:   # H100 A100 runners\\n33:   opmicrobenchmark-build:\\n34:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n35:     name: opmicrobenchmark-build\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n37:     needs: get-label-type\\n38:     with:\\n39:       runner: linux.12xlarge.memory\\n40:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n41:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n42:       cuda-arch-list: '8.0 9.0'\\n43:       test-matrix: |\\n44:         { include: [\\n45:           { config: \\\"operator_microbenchmark_test\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\" },\\n46:           { config: \\\"operator_microbenchmark_test\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n47:         ]}\\n48:     secrets: inherit\\n49: \\n50:   opmicrobenchmark-test:\\n51:     name: opmicrobenchmark-test\\n52:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n53:     needs: opmicrobenchmark-build\\n54:     with:\\n55:       timeout-minutes: 500\\n56:       build-environment: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n57:       docker-image: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n58:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n59:     secrets: inherit\\n60: \\n61:   # B200 runner\\n62:   opmicrobenchmark-build-b200:\\n63:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n64:     name: opmicrobenchmark-build-b200\\n65:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n66:     needs: get-label-type\\n67:     with:\\n68:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n69:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge\\n70:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\\n71:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n72:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\\n73:       test-matrix: |\\n74:         { include: [\\n75:           { config: \\\"operator_microbenchmark_test\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200\\\" },\\n76:         ]}\\n77:     secrets: inherit\\n78: \\n79:   opmicrobenchmark-test-b200:\\n80:     name: opmicrobenchmark-test-b200\\n81:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n82:     needs: opmicrobenchmark-build-b200\\n83:     with:\\n84:       timeout-minutes: 500\\n85:       build-environment: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-b200.outputs.build-environment }}\\n86:       docker-image: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-b200.outputs.docker-image }}\\n87:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-b200.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n88:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n89:     secrets: inherit\\n90: \\n91:   # ROCM MI300 runner\\n92:   opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm:\\n93:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n94:     name: opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm\\n95:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n96:     with:\\n97:       build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10\\n98:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3-benchmarks\\n99:       test-matrix: |\\n100:         { include: [\\n101:           { config: \\\"operator_microbenchmark_test\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\\\" },\\n102:         ]}\\n103:     secrets: inherit\\n104: \\n105:   opmicrobenchmark-test-rocm:\\n106:     name: opmicrobenchmark-test-rocm\\n107:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n108:     needs: opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm\\n109:     with:\\n110:       timeout-minutes: 500\\n111:       build-environment: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm.outputs.build-environment }}\\n112:       docker-image: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm.outputs.docker-image }}\\n113:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.opmicrobenchmark-build-rocm.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n114:     secrets: inherit\\n115: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/operator_microbenchmark.yml\"}"

Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/nightly-s3-uploads.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Nightly Upload to s3\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     # Choose a random time near midnight PST because it may be delayed if there are high loads\\n6:     - cron:  37 7 * * *\\n7:   pull_request:\\n8:     paths:\\n9:       - 'tools/stats/upload_external_contrib_stats.py'\\n10:       - '.github/workflows/nightly-s3-uploads.yml'\\n11: \\n12: concurrency:\\n13:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n14:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n15: \\n16: permissions:\\n17:   id-token: write\\n18: \\n19: jobs:\\n20:   upload-stats-to-s3:\\n21:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n22:     runs-on: ubuntu-22.04\\n23:     environment: upload-stats\\n24:     steps:\\n25:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n26:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n27:         with:\\n28:           fetch-depth: 1\\n29:           submodules: false\\n30: \\n31:       - uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n32:         with:\\n33:           python-version: '3.11'\\n34:           cache: pip\\n35: \\n36:       - run: |\\n37:           pip3 install requests==2.32.2 boto3==1.35.42\\n38: \\n39:       - name: Authenticate with AWS\\n40:         uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@ececac1a45f3b08a01d2dd070d28d111c5fe6722 # v4.1.0\\n41:         with:\\n42:           role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_upload_external_contrib_stats\\n43:           aws-region: us-east-1\\n44: \\n45:       - name: Upload external contribution stats\\n46:         uses: nick-fields/retry@7152eba30c6575329ac0576536151aca5a72780e # v3.0.0\\n47:         env:\\n48:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n49:         with:\\n50:           timeout_minutes: 10\\n51:           max_attempts: 10\\n52:           retry_wait_seconds: 90\\n53:           command: |\\n54:             echo \\\"Uploading external contribution stats for 10 days starting on\\\" \\\"$(date -d '10 days ago' '+%Y-%m-%d')\\\"\\n55:             python3 -m tools.stats.upload_external_contrib_stats --startDate \\\"$(date -d '10 days ago' '+%Y-%m-%d')\\\" --length 10\\n56: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/nightly-s3-uploads.yml\"}"
231. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/rocm-mi355.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: rocm-mi355\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   push:\\n5:     tags:\\n6:       - ciflow/rocm-mi355/*\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8:   schedule:\\n9:     - cron: 30 11,1 * * *  # about 4:30am PDT and 6:30pm PDT\\n10: \\n11: concurrency:\\n12:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n13:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n14: \\n15: permissions: read-all\\n16: \\n17: jobs:\\n18:   target-determination:\\n19:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n20:     name: before-test\\n21:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\\n22:     permissions:\\n23:       id-token: write\\n24:       contents: read\\n25: \\n26:   get-label-type:\\n27:     name: get-label-type\\n28:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n29:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n30:     with:\\n31:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n32:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n33:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n34:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n35: \\n36:   linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build:\\n37:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n38:     name: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi355\\n39:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n40:     needs: get-label-type\\n41:     with:\\n42:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n43:       build-environment: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi355\\n44:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\\n45:       test-matrix: |\\n46:         { include: [\\n47:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n48:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n49:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 3, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n50:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 4, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n51:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 5, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n52:           { config: \\\"default\\\", shard: 6, num_shards: 6, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.mi355.1\\\" },\\n53:         ]}\\n54:     secrets: inherit\\n55: \\n56:   linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-test:\\n57:     permissions:\\n58:       id-token: write\\n59:       contents: read\\n60:     name: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi355\\n61:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n62:     needs:\\n63:       - linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build\\n64:       - target-determination\\n65:     with:\\n66:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n67:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n68:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n69:       tests-to-include: >-\\n70:                         ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' && 'test_nn test_torch test_cuda test_ops test_unary_ufuncs test_binary_ufuncs test_autograd inductor/test_torchinductor test_matmul_cuda test_scaled_matmul_cuda'\\n71:                            || '' }}\\n72:     secrets: inherit\\n73: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/rocm-mi355.yml\"}"
232. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/lint.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: Lint\\n2: # Workflow that runs lint checks and also unittests for tools, and scripts.\\n3: \\n4: on:\\n5:   pull_request:\\n6:     branches-ignore:\\n7:       - nightly\\n8:   push:\\n9:     branches:\\n10:       - main\\n11:       - release/*\\n12:       - landchecks/*\\n13:     tags:\\n14:       - ciflow/pull/*\\n15:       - ciflow/trunk/*\\n16:   workflow_dispatch:\\n17: \\n18: permissions: read-all\\n19: # The names of steps that actually test the code should be suffixed with `(nonretryable)`.\\n20: # When any other step fails, it's job will be retried once by retryBot.\\n21: jobs:\\n22:   get-label-type:\\n23:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n24:     name: get-label-type\\n25:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n26:     with:\\n27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n30: \\n31:   get-changed-files:\\n32:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n33:     name: Get changed files\\n34:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_get-changed-files.yml\\n35:     with:\\n36:       all_files: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'lint-all-files') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'Reverted') || github.event_name == 'push' }}\\n37: \\n38:   lintrunner-clang:\\n39:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n40:     # Needed to prevent deduping on HUD\\n41:     name: lintrunner-clang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}\\n42:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]\\n43:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to clangtidy / clangformat\\n44:     if: |\\n45:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (\\n46:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||\\n47:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.h') ||\\n48:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cpp') ||\\n49:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cc') ||\\n50:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cxx') ||\\n51:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hpp') ||\\n52:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.hxx') ||\\n53:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cu') ||\\n54:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.cuh') ||\\n55:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.mm') ||\\n56:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.metal')\\n57:       )\\n58:     with:\\n59:       timeout: 120\\n60:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n61:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3.10-linter\\n62:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout\\n63:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed\\n64:       fetch-depth: 0\\n65:       submodules: true\\n66:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n67:       script: |\\n68:         CHANGED_FILES=\\\"${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}\\\"\\n69:         if [ \\\"$CHANGED_FILES\\\" = \\\"*\\\" ]; then\\n70:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT --all-files\\\"\\n71:         else\\n72:           export ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"--take CLANGTIDY,CLANGFORMAT $CHANGED_FILES\\\"\\n73:         fi\\n74:         export CLANG=1\\n75:         .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh\\n76: \\n77:   # NOTE: mypy needs its own job because it depends on --all-files, without assessing all files it sometimes\\n78:   #       fails to find types when it should\\n79:   # NOTE: We should be able to disable this and consolidate with Pyrefly\\n80:   lintrunner-pyrefly:\\n81:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n82:     name: lintrunner-pyrefly-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}\\n83:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]\\n84:     # Only run if there are changed files relevant to pyrefly\\n85:     if: |\\n86:       github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (\\n87:         needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' ||\\n88:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.py') ||\\n89:         contains(needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files, '.pyi')\\n90:       )\\n91:     with:\\n92:       timeout: 120\\n93:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n94:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n95:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout\\n96:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed\\n97:       fetch-depth: 0\\n98:       submodules: true\\n99:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n100:       script: |\\n101:         CHANGED_FILES=\\\"${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}\\\"\\n102:         echo \\\"Running pyrefly\\\"\\n103:         ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"--take PYREFLY --all-files\\\" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh\\n104: \\n105:   lintrunner-noclang:\\n106:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n107:     name: lintrunner-noclang-${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files == '*' && 'all' || 'partial' }}\\n108:     needs: [get-label-type, get-changed-files]\\n109:     with:\\n110:       timeout: 120\\n111:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n112:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n113:       # NB: A shallow checkout won't work here because calculate-docker-image requires a full checkout\\n114:       # to run git rev-parse HEAD~:.ci/docker when a new image is needed\\n115:       fetch-depth: 0\\n116:       submodules: true\\n117:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n118:       script: |\\n119:         CHANGED_FILES=\\\"${{ needs.get-changed-files.outputs.changed-files }}\\\"\\n120:         echo \\\"Running all other linters\\\"\\n121:         if [ \\\"$CHANGED_FILES\\\" = '*' ]; then\\n122:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY --all-files\\\" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh\\n123:         else\\n124:           ADDITIONAL_LINTRUNNER_ARGS=\\\"--skip CLANGTIDY,CLANGTIDY_EXECUTORCH_COMPATIBILITY,CLANGFORMAT,PYREFLY ${CHANGED_FILES}\\\" .github/scripts/lintrunner.sh\\n125:         fi\\n126: \\n127:   quick-checks:\\n128:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n129:     needs: get-label-type\\n130:     with:\\n131:       timeout: 120\\n132:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n133:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n134:       fetch-depth: 0\\n135:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n136:       script: |\\n137:         # Ensure no non-breaking spaces\\n138:         # NB: We use 'printf' below rather than '\\\\u000a' since bash pre-4.2\\n139:         # does not support the '\\\\u000a' syntax (which is relevant for local linters)\\n140:         (! git --no-pager grep -In \\\"$(printf '\\\\xC2\\\\xA0')\\\" -- . || (echo \\\"The above lines have non-breaking spaces (U+00A0); please convert them to spaces (U+0020)\\\"; false))\\n141: \\n142:         # Ensure cross-OS compatible file names\\n143:         (! git ls-files | grep -E '([<>:\\\"|?*]|[ .]$)' || (echo \\\"The above file names are not valid across all operating systems. Please ensure they don't contain the characters '<>:\\\"\\\"|?*' and don't end with a white space or a '.' \\\"; false))\\n144: \\n145:         # Ensure no versionless Python shebangs\\n146:         (! git --no-pager grep -In '#!.*python$' -- . || (echo \\\"The above lines have versionless Python shebangs; please specify either python2 or python3\\\"; false))\\n147: \\n148:         # Ensure ciflow tags mentioned in config\\n149:         python3 .github/scripts/collect_ciflow_labels.py --validate-tags\\n150: \\n151:         # C++ docs check\\n152:         pushd docs/cpp/source\\n153:         ./check-doxygen.sh\\n154:         popd\\n155: \\n156:         # CUDA kernel launch check\\n157:         set -eux\\n158:         python3 torch/testing/_internal/check_kernel_launches.py |& tee cuda_kernel_launch_checks.txt\\n159: \\n160:   pr-sanity-checks:\\n161:     name: pr-sanity-checks\\n162:     needs: get-label-type\\n163:     runs-on: [self-hosted, \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.large\\\"]\\n164:     # Only run this on pull requests. This check is simple enough to be done without a Docker image\\n165:     if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && !contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'skip-pr-sanity-checks')\\n166:     steps:\\n167:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n168:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n169:         with:\\n170:           submodules: false\\n171:           fetch-depth: -1\\n172: \\n173:       - name: PR size check (nonretryable)\\n174:         env:\\n175:           BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\\n176:           HEAD: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\\n177:         run: |\\n178:           bash .github/scripts/pr-sanity-check.sh\\n179: \\n180:   workflow-checks:\\n181:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n182:     needs: get-label-type\\n183:     with:\\n184:       timeout: 120\\n185:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n186:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n187:       fetch-depth: -1\\n188:       submodules: true\\n189:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n190:       script: |\\n191:         # Regenerate workflows\\n192:         export RELEASE_VERSION_TAG=2.10\\n193:         .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\\n194: \\n195:         RC=0\\n196:         # Assert that regenerating the workflows didn't change them\\n197:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .github/workflows; then\\n198:           echo\\n199:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the committed .github/workflows'\\n200:           echo 'are not up to date according to .github/templates.'\\n201:           echo 'Please run this command, commit, and push again to your PR:'\\n202:           echo\\n203:           echo '    .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py'\\n204:           echo\\n205:           echo 'If running that command does nothing, you may need to rebase'\\n206:           echo 'onto a more recent commit from the PyTorch main branch.'\\n207:           RC=1\\n208:         fi\\n209: \\n210:         # Check that jobs will be cancelled\\n211:         .github/scripts/ensure_actions_will_cancel.py\\n212: \\n213:         exit $RC\\n214: \\n215:   toc:\\n216:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n217:     needs: get-label-type\\n218:     with:\\n219:       timeout: 120\\n220:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n221:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n222:       fetch-depth: 0\\n223:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n224:       script: |\\n225:         # Regenerate ToCs and check that they didn't change\\n226:         set -eu\\n227: \\n228:         export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:\\\"$PATH\\\"\\n229:         for FILE in $(git grep -Il '<!-- toc -->' -- '**.md'); do\\n230:           markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i \\\"$FILE\\\"\\n231:         done\\n232: \\n233:         if ! .github/scripts/report_git_status.sh .; then\\n234:           echo\\n235:           echo 'As shown by the above diff, the table of contents in one or'\\n236:           echo 'more Markdown files is not up to date with the file contents.'\\n237:           echo 'You can either apply that Git diff directly to correct the'\\n238:           echo 'table of contents, or if you have npm installed, you can'\\n239:           echo 'install the npm package markdown-toc and run the following'\\n240:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016\\n241:           echo 'command (replacing $FILE with the filename for which you want'\\n242:           echo 'to regenerate the table of contents):'\\n243:           echo\\n244:           # shellcheck disable=SC2016\\n245:           echo \\\"    markdown-toc --bullets='-' -i \\\\\\\"\\\\$FILE\\\\\\\"\\\"\\n246:           false\\n247:         fi\\n248: \\n249:   test-tools:\\n250:     name: Test tools\\n251:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}\\n252:     uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/workflows/linux_job_v2.yml@release/2.10\\n253:     needs: get-label-type\\n254:     with:\\n255:       timeout: 120\\n256:       runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n257:       docker-image: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-linter\\n258:       fetch-depth: 0\\n259:       ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n260:       script: |\\n261:         # Test tools\\n262:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/stats\\n263:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest tools/test -o \\\"python_files=test*.py\\\"\\n264:         PYTHONPATH=$(pwd) pytest .github/scripts -o \\\"python_files=test*.py\\\"\\n265: \\n266:   test_run_test:\\n267:     name: Test `run_test.py` is usable without boto3\\n268:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}\\n269:     runs-on: linux.24_04.4x\\n270:     steps:\\n271:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n272:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n273:         with:\\n274:           submodules: false\\n275:           fetch-depth: 1\\n276:       - name: Setup Python 3.10\\n277:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n278:         with:\\n279:           python-version: '3.10'\\n280:           architecture: x64\\n281:           cache: pip\\n282:       - name: Install dependencies\\n283:         run: |\\n284:           python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip\\n285:           pip install pytest-rerunfailures==11.1.* pytest-flakefinder==1.1.* pytest-xdist==3.3.* expecttest==0.3.* fbscribelogger==0.1.* numpy==1.24.*\\n286:           pip install torch --pre --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cpu/\\n287:       - name: Run run_test.py (nonretryable)\\n288:         run: |\\n289:           # Run test_vulkan, which is a fast noop on Linux\\n290:           python3 test/run_test.py --include test_vulkan --verbose\\n291: \\n292:   test_collect_env:\\n293:     if: ${{ github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch' }}\\n294:     name: Test collect_env\\n295:     runs-on: ${{ matrix.runner }}\\n296:     strategy:\\n297:       matrix:\\n298:         include:\\n299:           - test_type: with_torch\\n300:             runner: linux.24_04.4x\\n301:           - test_type: without_torch\\n302:             runner: linux.24_04.4x\\n303:           - test_type: older_python_version\\n304:             runner: linux.24_04.4x\\n305:     steps:\\n306:       # [see note: pytorch repo ref]\\n307:       # deep clone (fetch-depth 0) required, to allow us to use git log\\n308:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n309:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n310:         with:\\n311:           submodules: false\\n312:           fetch-depth: 1\\n313:       - name: Get min python version\\n314:         id: get-min-python-version\\n315:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'\\n316:         run: |\\n317:           set -eou pipefail\\n318:           # Generate PyTorch version to use\\n319:           echo \\\"MIN_PYTHON_VERSION=$(python3 .github/scripts/get_ci_variable.py --min-python-version)\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_OUTPUT}\\\"\\n320:       - name: Setup Old Python version\\n321:         if: matrix.test_type == 'older_python_version'\\n322:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n323:         with:\\n324:           python-version: 3.8\\n325:           architecture: x64\\n326:           check-latest: false\\n327:           cache: pip\\n328:           cache-dependency-path: |\\n329:             **/requirements-build.txt\\n330:             **/requirements.txt\\n331:       - name: Setup Min Python version\\n332:         if: matrix.test_type != 'older_python_version'\\n333:         uses: actions/setup-python@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065 # v5.6.0\\n334:         with:\\n335:           python-version: ${{ steps.get-min-python-version.outputs.MIN_PYTHON_VERSION }}\\n336:           architecture: x64\\n337:           check-latest: false\\n338:           cache: pip\\n339:           cache-dependency-path: |\\n340:             **/requirements-build.txt\\n341:             **/requirements.txt\\n342:       - name: Install torch\\n343:         if: matrix.test_type == 'with_torch'\\n344:         run: |\\n345:           pip install -r requirements.txt\\n346:           # Doesn't really matter what torch version, we just need ANY torch installed\\n347:           pip install 'torch==2.*'\\n348:       - name: Run collect_env.py (nonretryable)\\n349:         run: |\\n350:           # All we need to see is that it passes\\n351:           python3 torch/utils/collect_env.py\\n352: \\n353:   link-check:\\n354:     name: Link checks\\n355:     needs: get-label-type\\n356:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_link_check.yml\\n357:     with:\\n358:       runner: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\n359:       ref:    ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n360:     secrets: inherit\\n361: \\n362: concurrency:\\n363:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n364:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n365: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/lint.yml\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: inductor-rocm-mi200\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     - cron: 0 */3 * * *\\n6:   push:\\n7:     branches:\\n8:       - release/*\\n9:     tags:\\n10:       - ciflow/inductor-rocm-mi200/*\\n11:   workflow_dispatch:\\n12: \\n13: concurrency:\\n14:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n15:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n16: \\n17: permissions:\\n18:   id-token: write\\n19:   contents: read\\n20: \\n21: jobs:\\n22:   get-label-type:\\n23:     name: get-label-type\\n24:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n25:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n26:     with:\\n27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n30:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n31:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n32: \\n33:   linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-build:\\n34:     name: rocm-py3.10-inductor\\n35:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n36:     needs: get-label-type\\n37:     with:\\n38:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n39:       build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\\n40:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\\n41:       test-matrix: |\\n42:         { include: [\\n43:           { config: \\\"inductor\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.2\\\" },\\n44:           { config: \\\"inductor\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.2\\\" },\\n45:         ]}\\n46:     secrets: inherit\\n47: \\n48:   linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-test:\\n49:     permissions:\\n50:       id-token: write\\n51:       contents: read\\n52:     name: rocm-py3.10-inductor\\n53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n54:     needs: linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-build\\n55:     with:\\n56:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n57:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n58:       test-matrix:  ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n59:     secrets: inherit\\n60: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi200.yml\"}"

Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": ".github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # @generated DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\\n2: \\n3: # Template is at:    .github/templates/windows_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\\n4: # Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\\n5: name: windows-binary-libtorch-debug\\n6: \\n7: on:\\n8:   push:\\n9:     # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\\n10:     branches:\\n11:       - nightly\\n12:     tags:\\n13:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\\n14:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n15:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n16:       - 'ciflow/binaries/*'\\n17:       - 'ciflow/binaries_libtorch/*'\\n18:   workflow_dispatch:\\n19: \\n20: env:\\n21:   # Needed for conda builds\\n22:   ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n23:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n24:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: windows-binary-libtorch-debug\\n25:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n26:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n27:   SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n28:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n29:   OS: windows\\n30: concurrency:\\n31:   group: windows-binary-libtorch-debug-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n32:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n33: \\n34: jobs:\\n35:   get-label-type:\\n36:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n37:     name: get-label-type\\n38:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n39:     with:\\n40:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n41:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n42:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n43:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n44:   libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-debug-build:\\n45:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n46:     needs: get-label-type\\n47:     runs-on: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}windows.12xlarge\\\"\\n48:     timeout-minutes: 360\\n49:     env:\\n50:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ github.workspace }}/pytorch\\n51:       PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\\n52:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n53:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n54:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n55:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\\n56:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n57:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: debug\\n58:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\\n59:       # This is a dummy value for libtorch to work correctly with our batch scripts\\n60:       # without this value pip does not get installed for some reason\\n61:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n62:     steps:\\n63:       # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally\\n64:       #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the\\n65:       #       runner.temp variable, which we need.\\n66:       - name: Populate binary env\\n67:         shell: bash\\n68:         run: |\\n69:           echo \\\"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n70:           echo \\\"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n71:           echo \\\"WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}\\\"\\n72:       - name: Display EC2 information\\n73:         shell: bash\\n74:         run: |\\n75:           set -euo pipefail\\n76:           function get_ec2_metadata() {\\n77:             # Pulled from instance metadata endpoint for EC2\\n78:             # see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instancedata-data-retrieval.html\\n79:             category=$1\\n80:             curl -H \\\"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $(curl -s -X PUT \\\"http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token\\\" -H \\\"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 30\\\")\\\" -fsSL \\\"http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/${category}\\\"\\n81:           }\\n82:           echo \\\"ami-id: $(get_ec2_metadata ami-id)\\\"\\n83:           echo \\\"instance-id: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-id)\\\"\\n84:           echo \\\"instance-type: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-type)\\\"\\n85:           echo \\\"system info $(uname -a)\\\"\\n86:       - name: \\\"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\\\"\\n87:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n88:         continue-on-error: true\\n89:         with:\\n90:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n91:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\\n92:         shell: bash\\n93:         run: |\\n94:           git config --global core.longpaths true\\n95:           git config --global core.symlinks true\\n96: \\n97:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\\n98:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\\n99:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\\n100:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-debug-nightly.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n15:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n16:       - 'ciflow/binaries/*'\\n17:       - 'ciflow/binaries_libtorch/*'\\n18:   workflow_dispatch:\\n19: \\n20: env:\\n21:   # Needed for conda builds\\n22:   ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n23:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n24:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-release\\n25:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n26:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n27:   SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n28:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n29:   OS: windows-arm64\\n30:   PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n31:   DOWNLOADS_DIR: c:\\\\temp\\\\downloads\\n32:   DEPENDENCIES_DIR: c:\\\\temp\\\\dependencies\\n33:   ENABLE_APL: 1\\n34:   ENABLE_OPENBLAS: 0\\n35:   MSVC_VERSION : 14.42\\n36: concurrency:\\n37:   group: windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n38:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n39: \\n40: jobs:\\n41:   get-label-type:\\n42:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n43:     name: get-label-type\\n44:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n45:     with:\\n46:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n47:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n48:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n49:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n50:   libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release-build:\\n51:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n52:     needs: get-label-type\\n53:     runs-on: \\\"windows-11-arm64-preview\\\"\\n54:     timeout-minutes: 360\\n55:     env:\\n56:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ github.workspace }}/pytorch\\n57:       PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\\n58:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n59:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n60:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n61:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\\n62:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n63:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\\n64:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\\n65:       # This is a dummy value for libtorch to work correctly with our batch scripts\\n66:       # without this value pip does not get installed for some reason\\n67:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n68:     steps:\\n69:       - name: Populate binary env\\n70:         shell: cmd\\n71:         run: |\\n72:           echo BINARY_ENV_FILE=%RUNNER_TEMP%/env>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n73:           echo PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%/artifacts>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n74:           echo WIN_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=%RUNNER_TEMP%>> %GITHUB_ENV%\\n75:       - name: Bootstrap folders\\n76:         shell: cmd\\n77:         run: |\\n78:           mkdir \\\"%NIGHTLIES_PYTORCH_ROOT%\\\"\\n79:           mkdir \\\"%PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR%\\\"\\n80:       - name: Enable long paths\\n81:         shell: cmd\\n82:         run: |\\n83:           git config --system --get core.longpaths || echo \\\"core.longpaths is not set, setting it now\\\"\\n84:           git config --system core.longpaths true\\n85:       - name: Git checkout PyTorch\\n86:         uses: actions/checkout@v4\\n87:         with:\\n88:           path: \\\"pytorch\\\"\\n89:           submodules: recursive\\n90:       - name: Bootstrap Python\\n91:         shell: cmd\\n92:         run: |\\n93:           \\\"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_python.bat\\\"\\n94:       - name: Bootstrap APL\\n95:         shell: cmd\\n96:         run: |\\n97:           \\\"pytorch/.ci/pytorch/windows/arm64/bootstrap_apl.bat\\\"\\n98:       - name: Bootstrap Rust\\n99:         shell: cmd\\n100:         run: |\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/generated-windows-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

Pulled from instance metadata endpoint for EC2\\n78:             # see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/instancedata-data-retrieval.html\\n79:             category=$1\\n80:             curl -H \\\"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $(curl -s -X PUT \\\"http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token\\\" -H \\\"X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 30\\\")\\\" -fsSL \\\"http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/${category}\\\"\\n81:           }\\n82:           echo \\\"ami-id: $(get_ec2_metadata ami-id)\\\"\\n83:           echo \\\"instance-id: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-id)\\\"\\n84:           echo \\\"instance-type: $(get_ec2_metadata instance-type)\\\"\\n85:           echo \\\"system info $(uname -a)\\\"\\n86:       - name: \\\"[FB EMPLOYEES] Enable SSH (Click me for login details)\\\"\\n87:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/setup-ssh@release/2.10\\n88:         continue-on-error: true\\n89:         with:\\n90:           github-secret: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n91:       - name: Enable git long paths and symlinks on Windows and disable fsmonitor daemon\\n92:         shell: bash\\n93:         run: |\\n94:           git config --global core.longpaths true\\n95:           git config --global core.symlinks true\\n96: \\n97:           # https://git-scm.com/docs/git-fsmonitor--daemon.  The daemon could lock\\n98:           # the directory on Windows and prevent GHA from checking out as reported\\n99:           # in https://github.com/actions/checkout/issues/1018\\n100:           git config --global core.fsmonitor false\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/generated-windows-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

ciflow/h100/*\\n17: \\n18: concurrency:\\n19:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n20:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n21: \\n22: permissions:\\n23:   id-token: write\\n24:   contents: read\\n25: \\n26: jobs:\\n27: \\n28:   get-label-type:\\n29:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n30:     name: get-label-type\\n31:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n32:     with:\\n33:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n34:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n35:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n36:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n37: \\n38:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-build:\\n39:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90\\n40:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n41:     needs: get-label-type\\n42:     with:\\n43:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n44:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90\\n45:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n46:       cuda-arch-list: '9.0'\\n47:       test-matrix: |\\n48:         { include: [\\n49:           { config: \\\"smoke\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\" },\\n50:         ]}\\n51:     secrets: inherit\\n52: \\n53:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm90-test:\\n54:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90\\n55:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n56:     needs:\\n57:       -
