Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: nightly\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     - cron: 0 0 * * *\\n6:   push:\\n7:     tags:\\n8:       # NOTE: Doc build pipelines should only get triggered on:\\n9:       # Major or minor release candidates builds\\n10:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.0+-rc[0-9]+\\n11:       # Final RC for major, minor and patch releases\\n12:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+\\n13:       - ciflow/nightly/*\\n14:   workflow_dispatch:\\n15: \\n16: \\n17: concurrency:\\n18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}--${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n19:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n20: \\n21: jobs:\\n22:   get-label-type:\\n23:     name: get-label-type\\n24:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n25:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n26:     with:\\n27:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n28:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n29:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n30:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n31: \\n32:   link-check:\\n33:     name: Link checks\\n34:     needs: get-label-type\\n35:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_link_check.yml\\n36:     with:\\n37:       runner: ${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\n38:       ref:    ${{ github.sha }}\\n39:     secrets: inherit\\n40: \\n41:   docs-build:\\n42:     name: docs build\\n43:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n44:     needs: get-label-type\\n45:     with:\\n46:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n47:       build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\\n48:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\\n49:     secrets: inherit\\n50: \\n51:   docs-push:\\n52:     name: docs push\\n53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_docs.yml\\n54:     needs:\\n55:       - docs-build\\n56:       - get-label-type\\n57:     with:\\n58:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n59:       build-environment: linux-jammy-py3.10-gcc11\\n60:       docker-image: ${{ needs.docs-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n61:       push: ${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || startsWith(github.event.ref, 'refs/tags/v') }}\\n62:       run-doxygen: true\\n63:     secrets:\\n64:       GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GH_PYTORCHBOT_TOKEN }}\\n65: \\n66:   update-commit-hashes:\\n67:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n68:     environment: update-commit-hash\\n69:     strategy:\\n70:       matrix:\\n71:         include:\\n72:           - repo-name: vision\\n73:             repo-owner: pytorch\\n74:             branch: main\\n75:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins\\n76:           - repo-name: audio\\n77:             repo-owner: pytorch\\n78:             branch: main\\n79:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins\\n80:           # executorch jobs are disabled since it needs some manual work for the hash update\\n81:           # - repo-name: executorch\\n82:           #   repo-owner: pytorch\\n83:           #   branch: main\\n84:           #   pin-folder: .ci/docker/ci_commit_pins\\n85:           - repo-name: triton\\n86:             repo-owner: triton-lang\\n87:             branch: main\\n88:             pin-folder: .ci/docker/ci_commit_pins\\n89:           - repo-name: vllm\\n90:             repo-owner: vllm-project\\n91:             branch: main\\n92:             pin-folder: .github/ci_commit_pins\\n93:     # Allow this to be triggered on either a schedule or on workflow_dispatch to allow for easier testing\\n94:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' && (github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch')\\n95:     steps:\\n96:       - name: \\\"${{ matrix.repo-owner }}/${{ matrix.repo-name }} update-commit-hash\\\"\\n97:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/update-commit-hash@release/2.10\\n98:         with:\\n99:           repo-owner: ${{ matrix.repo-owner }}\\n100:           repo-name: ${{ matrix.repo-name }}\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/nightly.yml%3FL1-100\"}"

monitor in perf tests\\n116:       disable-monitor: false\\n117:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n118:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n119:     secrets: inherit\\n120: \\n121: "{\"content\":\"1: # This workflow is dedicated to host slow jobs that are run only periodically because\\n2: # they are too slow to run in every commit.  The list of slow tests can be found in\\n3: # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/blob/generated-stats/stats/slow-tests.json\\n4: name: slow-rocm-mi200\\n5: \\n6: on:\\n7:   push:\\n8:     branches:\\n9:       - release/*\\n10:     tags:\\n11:       - ciflow/slow/*\\n12:       - ciflow/slow-rocm-mi200/*\\n13:   schedule:\\n14:     - cron: 0 */3 * * *\\n15:   workflow_dispatch:\\n16: \\n17: concurrency:\\n18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}-${{ github.event.schedule }}\\n19:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n20: \\n21: permissions:\\n22:   id-token: write\\n23:   contents: read\\n24: \\n25: jobs:\\n26:   llm-td:\\n27:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n28:     name: before-test\\n29:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\\n30:     permissions:\\n31:       id-token: write\\n32:       contents: read\\n33: \\n34:   target-determination:\\n35:     name: before-test\\n36:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\\n37:     needs: llm-td\\n38:     permissions:\\n39:       id-token: write\\n40:       contents: read\\n41: \\n42:   get-label-type:\\n43:     name: get-label-type\\n44:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n45:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n46:     with:\\n47:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n48:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n49: [\\n63:           { config: \\\"slow\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.2\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\"] },\\n64:           { config: \\\"slow\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.2\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\"] },\\n65:         ]}\\n66:     secrets: inherit\\n67: \\n68:   linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-test:\\n69:     permissions:\\n70:       id-token: write\\n71:       contents: read\\n72:     name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\\n73:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n74:     needs:\\n75:       - linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build\\n76:       - needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n80:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n81:     secrets: inherit\\n82: Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # Workflow: Inductor\\n2: # runs all inductor tests, including both benchmark tests and unit tests.\\n3: # adding New Tests:\\n4: #  1. inductor-unittest.yml:\\n5: #      - Unit Tests\\n6: #      - Mixed Tests\\n7: #      - Flaky Benchmark tests\\n8: #  2. inductor.yml(this workflow):\\n9: #      - non-flaky benchmark tests\\n10: name: inductor\\n11: \\n12: on:\\n13:   push:\\n14:     branches:\\n15:       - main\\n16:       - release/*\\n17:     tags:\\n18:       - ciflow/inductor/*\\n19:   workflow_dispatch:\\n20: \\n21: concurrency:\\n22:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n23:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n24: \\n25: permissions:\\n26:   id-token: write\\n27:   contents: read\\n28: \\n29: jobs:\\n30:   unit-test:\\n31:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n32:     name: unit-test\\n33:     uses: ./.github/workflows/inductor-unittest.yml\\n34:     secrets: inherit\\n35: \\n36:   get-label-type:\\n37:     name: get-label-type\\n38:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n39:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n40:     with:\\n41:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n42:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n43:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n44:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n45:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n46: \\n47:   inductor-build:\\n48:     name: inductor-build\\n49:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n50:     needs: get-label-type\\n51:     with:\\n52:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm86\\n53:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n54:       cuda-arch-list: '8.6'\\n55:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n56:       test-matrix: |\\n57:         { include: [\\n58:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n59:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n60:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n61:           { config: \\\"inductor_torchbench\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n62:           { config: \\\"inductor_torchbench\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.g5.4xlarge.nvidia.gpu\\\" },\\n63:         ]}\\n64:       build-additional-packages: \\\"vision audio fbgemm torchao\\\"\\n65:     secrets: inherit\\n66: \\n67:   inductor-test:\\n68:     name: inductor-test\\n69:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n70:     needs: inductor-build\\n71:     with:\\n72:       build-environment: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n73:       docker-image: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n74:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n75:     secrets: inherit\\n76: \\n77:   inductor-build-cuda13:\\n78:     name: inductor-build-cuda13\\n79:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n80:     needs: get-label-type\\n81: 244. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: inductor-rocm-mi300\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm/*\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm-mi300/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  target-determination:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n      opt_out_experiments: lf\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build:\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-test:\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\n    needs: linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix:  ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/.github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi300.yml"
}

245. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "name: inductor-perf-b200\n\non:\n  schedule:\n    - cron: 0 7 * * 1-6\n    - cron: 0 7 * * 0\n  workflow_dispatch:\n    inputs:\n      training:\n        description: Run training (on by default)?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      inference:\n        description: Run inference (on by default)?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      default:\n        description: Run inductor_default?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      dynamic:\n        description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      cppwrapper:\n        description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      cudagraphs:\n        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      freezing_cudagraphs:\n        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      aotinductor:\n        description: Run aot_inductor for inference?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      maxautotune:\n        description: Run inductor_max_autotune?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      benchmark_configs:\n        description: The list of configs used the benchmark\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_b200,inductor_timm_perf_cuda_b200,inductor_torchbench_perf_cuda_b200\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n      opt_out_experiments: lf\n\n  build:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      # Use a bigger runner here because CUDA_ARCH 9.0 is only built for H100\n      # or newer GPUs, so it doesn't benefit much from existing compiler cache\n      # from trunk. Also use a memory-intensive runner here because memory is\n      # usually the bottleneck\n      runner: linux.12xlarge.memory\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\n      cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n          { config: \"inductor_timm_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n          { config: \"inductor_torchbench_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n        ]}\n      selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.benchmark_configs }}\n      build-additional-packages: \"vision audio fbgemm torchao\"\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  test-periodically:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: build\n    if: github.event.schedule == '0 7 * * 1-6'\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n      timeout-minutes: 720\n      disable-monitor: false\n      monitor-log-interval: 15\n      monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  test-weekly:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: build\n    if: github.event.schedule == '0 7 * * 0'\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      dashboard-tag: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      timeout-minutes: 1440\n      aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n      disable-monitor: false\n      monitor-log-interval: 15\n      monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\n    secrets: inherit\n\n dashboard-tag: training-${{ inputs.training }}-inference-${{ inputs.inference }}-default-${{ inputs.default }}-dynamic-${{ inputs.dynamic }}-cudagraphs-${{ inputs.cudagraphs }}-cppwrapper-${{ inputs.cppwrapper }}-aotinductor-${{ inputs.aotinductor }}-maxautotune-${{ inputs.maxautotune }}-freezing_cudagraphs-${{ inputs.freezing_cudagraphs }}\n      docker-image: ${{ 247. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /workspace/.github/workflows/inductor-rocm-mi300.yml << 'EOFFILE'\nname: inductor-rocm-mi300\n\non:\n  push:\n    branches:\n      - main\n      - release/*\n    tags:\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm/*\n      - ciflow/inductor-rocm-mi300/*\n  workflow_dispatch:\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  target-determination:\n    if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\n    name: before-test\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n      opt_out_experiments: lf\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build:\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      build-environment: linux-noble-rocm-py3.12-mi300\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-noble-rocm-n-py3\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n          { config: \"inductor\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \"linux.rocm.gpu.gfx942.1\" },\n        ]}\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-test:\n    permissions:\n      id-token: write\n      contents: read\n    name: rocm-py3.12-inductor-mi300\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\n    needs: linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix:  ${{ needs.linux-noble-rocm-py3_12-inductor-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n    secrets: inherit\nEOFFILE\n",
  "description": "Write first workflow file using heredoc"
}

248. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3 << 'EOFPYTHON'\nimport os\n\nfiles_data = {\n    '.github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-b200.yml': '''name: inductor-perf-b200\n\non:\n  schedule:\n    - cron: 0 7 * * 1-6\n    - cron: 0 7 * * 0\n  workflow_dispatch:\n    inputs:\n      training:\n        description: Run training (on by default)?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      inference:\n        description: Run inference (on by default)?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      default:\n        description: Run inductor_default?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      dynamic:\n        description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      cppwrapper:\n        description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      cudagraphs:\n        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: true\n      freezing_cudagraphs:\n        description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      aotinductor:\n        description: Run aot_inductor for inference?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      maxautotune:\n        description: Run inductor_max_autotune?\n        required: false\n        type: boolean\n        default: false\n      benchmark_configs:\n        description: The list of configs used the benchmark\n        required: false\n        type: string\n        default: inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_b200,inductor_timm_perf_cuda_b200,inductor_torchbench_perf_cuda_b200\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\npermissions:\n  id-token: write\n  contents: read\n\njobs:\n  get-label-type:\n    name: get-label-type\n    uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\n    if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\n    with:\n      triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\n      issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\n      curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\n      curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\n      opt_out_experiments: lf\n\n  build:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\n    needs: get-label-type\n    with:\n      runner_prefix: \"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\"\n      # Use a bigger runner here because CUDA_ARCH 9.0 is only built for H100\n      # or newer GPUs, so it doesn't benefit much from existing compiler cache\n      # from trunk. Also use a memory-intensive runner here because memory is\n      # usually the bottleneck\n      runner: linux.12xlarge.memory\n      build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n      docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\n      cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\n      test-matrix: |\n        { include: [\n          { config: \"inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n          { config: \"inductor_timm_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n          { config: \"inductor_torchbench_perf_cuda_b200\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \"linux.dgx.b200\" },\n        ]}\n      selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.benchmark_configs }}\n      build-additional-packages: \"vision audio fbgemm torchao\"\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  test-periodically:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: build\n    if: github.event.schedule == '0 7 * * 1-6'\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n      timeout-minutes: 720\n      disable-monitor: false\n      monitor-log-interval: 15\n      monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  test-weekly:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: build\n    if: github.event.schedule == '0 7 * * 0'\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true-maxautotune-true-freeze_autotune_cudagraphs-true\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      timeout-minutes: 1440\n      aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n      disable-monitor: false\n      monitor-log-interval: 15\n      monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\n    secrets: inherit\n\n  test:\n    name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\n    uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\n    needs: build\n    with:\n      build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\n      dashboard-tag: training-${{ inputs.training }}-inference-${{ inputs.inference }}-default-${{ inputs.default }}-dynamic-${{ inputs.dynamic }}-cudagraphs-${{ inputs.cudagraphs }}-cppwrapper-${{ inputs.cppwrapper }}-aotinductor-${{ inputs.aotinductor }}-maxautotune-${{ inputs.maxautotune }}-freezing_cudagraphs-${{ inputs.freezing_cudagraphs }}\n      docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\n      test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\n      aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\n      timeout-minutes: 720\n      disable-monitor: false\n      monitor-log-interval: 15\n      monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\n    secrets: inherit\n'''\n}\n\nos.chdir('/workspace')\nfor file_path, content in files_data.items():\n    dir_path = os.path.dirname(file_path)\n    os.makedirs(dir_path, exist_ok=True)\n    with open(file_path, 'w') as f:\n        f.write(content)\n    print(f\"Wrote: {file_path}\")\n\nprint(\"Successfully wrote files!\")\nEOFPYTHON\n",
  "description": "Write files 1-2 using Python script"
}

252. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat /tmp/small_files.txt | while read line; do\n  filepath=\".github/workflows/$line\"\n  if [ -f \"$filepath\" ]; then\n    lines=$(wc -l < \"$filepath\")\n    echo \"\u2713 $line ($lines lines)\"\n  else\n    echo \"\u2717 $line (MISSING)\"\n  fi\ndone | head -20 && echo \"...\" && cat /tmp/small_files.txt | while read line; do\n  filepath=\".github/workflows/$line\"\n  if [ ! -f \"$filepath\" ]; then\n    echo \"MISSING: $line\"\n  fi\ndone | wc -l && echo \"missing files total\"\n",
  "description": "Check which of the 60 files are missing"
}

"{\"content\":\"1: name: inductor-perf-nightly-h100\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     - cron: 15 0 * * 1-6\\n6:     - cron: 0 7 * * 0\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8:     inputs:\\n9:       training:\\n10:         description: Run training (on by default)?\\n11:         required: false\\n12:         type: boolean\\n13:         default: true\\n14:       inference:\\n15:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\\n16:         required: false\\n17:         type: boolean\\n18:         default: true\\n19:       default:\\n20:         description: Run inductor_default?\\n21:         required: false\\n22:         type: boolean\\n23:         default: false\\n24:       dynamic:\\n25:         description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\\n26:         required: false\\n27:         type: boolean\\n28:         default: false\\n29:       cppwrapper:\\n30:         description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\\n31:         required: false\\n32:         type: boolean\\n33:         default: false\\n34:       cudagraphs:\\n35:         description: Run inductor_cudagraphs?\\n36:         required: false\\n37:         type: boolean\\n38:         default: true\\n39:       freezing_cudagraphs:\\n40:         description: Run inductor_cudagraphs with freezing for inference?\\n41:         required: false\\n42:         type: boolean\\n43:         default: false\\n44:       aotinductor:\\n45:         description: Run aot_inductor for inference?\\n46:         required: false\\n47:         type: boolean\\n48:         default: false\\n49:       maxautotune:\\n50:         description: Run inductor_max_autotune?\\n51:         required: false\\n52:         type: boolean\\n53:         default: false\\n54:       benchmark_configs:\\n55:         description: The list of configs used the benchmark\\n56:         required: false\\n57:         type: string\\n58:         default: inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_h100,inductor_timm_perf_cuda_h100,inductor_torchbench_perf_cuda_h100\\n59:   pull_request:\\n60:     # Changing these files guarantees that this workflow needs to be run\\n61:     paths:\\n62:       - .github/workflows/inductor-perf-test-nightly-h100.yml\\n63:       - .ci/docker/ci_commit_pins/huggingface-requirements.txt\\n64: \\n65: concurrency:\\n66:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n67:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n68: \\n69: permissions:\\n70:   id-token: write\\n71:   contents: read\\n72: \\n73: jobs:\\n74:   get-label-type:\\n75:     name: get-label-type\\n76:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n77:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n78:     with:\\n79:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n80:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n81:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n82:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n83:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n84: \\n85:   build:\\n86:     name: build\\n87:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n88:     needs: get-label-type\\n89:     with:\\n90:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n91:       # Use a bigger runner here because CUDA_ARCH 9.0 is only built for H100\\n92:       # or newer GPUs, so it doesn't benefit much from existing compiler cache\\n93:       # from trunk. Also use a memory-intensive runner here because memory is\\n94:       # usually the bottleneck\\n95:       runner: linux.12xlarge.memory\\n96:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm90\\n97:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n98:       cuda-arch-list: '9.0'\\n99:       test-matrix: |\\n100:         { include: [\\n101:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_h100\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 5, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\" },\\n102:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_h100\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 5, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\" },\\n103:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface_perf_cuda_h100\\\", shard: 3, num_shards: 5, runner: \\\"linux.aws.h100\\\" },\\n122:         ]}\\n123:       selected-test-configs: ${{ inputs.benchmark_configs }}\\n124:       build-additional-packages: \\\"vision audio fbgemm torchao\\\"\\n125:     secrets: inherit\\n126: \\n127:   test-periodically:\\n128:     name: test-periodically\\n129:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n130:     needs: build\\n131:     if: github.event.schedule == '15 0 * * 1-6'\\n132:     with:\\n133:       build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n134:       dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true\\n135:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n136:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n137:       timeout-minutes: 720\\n138:       # disable monitor in perf tests, next step is to enable it\\n139:       disable-monitor: false\\n140:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n141:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n142:     secrets: inherit\\n143: \\n144:   test-weekly:\\n145:     name: test-weekly\\n146:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n147:     needs: build\\n148:     if: github.event.schedule == '0 7 * * 0'\\n149:     with:\\n150:       build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n151:       dashboard-tag: training-true-inference-true-default-true-dynamic-true-cudagraphs-true-cppwrapper-true-aotinductor-true-freezing_cudagraphs-true-maxautotune-true-freeze_autotune_cudagraphs-true\\n152:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n153:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n154:       timeout-minutes: 1440\\n155:       # disable monitor in perf tests, next step is to enable it\\n156:       disable-monitor: false\\n157:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n158:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n159:     secrets: inherit\\n160: \\n161:   test:\\n162:     name: test\\n163:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n164:     needs: build\\n165:     # The pull_request trigger is used in PR to bump transformers pin which always\\n166:     # needs one round of benchmark\\n167:     if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}\\n168:     with:\\n169:       build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n170:       dashboard-tag: training-${{ inputs.training || 'true' }}-inference-${{ inputs.inference || 'true' }}-default-${{ inputs.default || 'true' }}-dynamic-${{ inputs.dynamic || 'true' }}-cudagraphs-${{ inputs.cudagraphs || 'true' }}-cppwrapper-${{ inputs.cppwrapper || 'false' }}-aotinductor-${{ inputs.aotinductor || 'false' }}-maxautotune-${{ inputs.maxautotune || 'false' }}-freezing_cudagraphs-${{ inputs.freezing_cudagraphs || 'false' }}\\n171:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n172:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n173:       timeout-minutes: 720\\n174:       # disable monitor in perf tests for more investigation\\n175:       disable-monitor: false\\n176:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n177:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n178:     secrets: inherit\\n179: get-default-label-prefix\\n64:     with:\\n65:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-default-label-prefix.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n66:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n67:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda13.0-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n68:       cuda-arch-list: '8.0'\\n69:       test-matrix: |\\n70:         { include: [\\n71:           { config: \\\"inductor-micro-benchmark\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\", owners: [\\\"oncall:pt2\\\"] },\\n72:         ]}\\n73:     secrets: inherit\\n74: \\n75:   test-cuda13:\\n76:     name: cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n77:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n78:     needs: build-cuda13\\n79:     with:\\n80:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n81:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build-cuda13.outputs.docker-image }}\\n82:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build-cuda13.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n83:       timeout-minutes: 720\\n84:     secrets: inherit\\n85: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-micro-benchmark.yml\"}"

ciflow/rocm-mi300/*\\n11:   workflow_dispatch:\\n12:   schedule:\\n13:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT\\n14: \\n15: concurrency:\\n16:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ 258. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/test-b200.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # B200 Smoke Tests CI Workflow\\n2: #\\n3: # This workflow runs smoke tests on B200 hardware\\n4: #\\n5: # Flow:\\n6: # 1. Builds PyTorch with CUDA 12.8+ and sm100 architecture for B200\\n7: # 2. Runs smoke tests on linux.dgx.b200 runner\\n8: # 3. Tests executed are defined in .ci/pytorch/test.sh -> test_python_smoke_b200() function\\n9: #    - Includes matmul, scaled_matmul, FP8, and FlashAttention CuTe tests\\n10: #    - FlashAttention CuTe DSL is installed as part of test execution\\n11: #\\n12: # Triggered by:\\n13: # - Pull requests modifying this workflow file\\n14: # - Manual dispatch\\n15: # - Schedule (every 6 hours)\\n16: # - Adding ciflow/b200 label to a PR (creates ciflow/b200/* tag)\\n17: \\n18: name: B200 Smoke Tests\\n19: \\n20: on:\\n21:   pull_request:\\n22:     paths:\\n23:       - .github/workflows/test-b200.yml\\n24:   workflow_dispatch:\\n25:   schedule:\\n26:     - cron: 0 */2 * * *  # every 2 hours\\n27:   push:\\n28:     tags:\\n29:       - ciflow/b200/*\\n30: \\n31: concurrency:\\n32:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}\\n33:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n34: \\n35: permissions:\\n36:   id-token: write\\n37:   contents: read\\n38: \\n39: jobs:\\n40: \\n41:   get-label-type:\\n42:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n43:     name: get-label-type\\n44:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n45:     with:\\n46:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n47:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n48:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n49:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n50: \\n51:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build:\\n52:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\\n53:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n54:     needs: get-label-type\\n55:     with:\\n56:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n57:       runner: linux.r7i.4xlarge\\n58:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\\n59:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11\\n60:       cuda-arch-list: '10.0'\\n61:       test-matrix: |\\n62:         { include: [\\n63:           { config: \\\"smoke_b200\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.dgx.b200\\\" },\\n64:         ]}\\n65:       # config: \\\"smoke_b200\\\" maps to test_python_smoke_b200() in .ci/pytorch/test.sh\\n66:     secrets: inherit\\n67: \\n68:   linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-test:\\n69:     name: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm100\\n70:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n71:     needs:\\n72:       - linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build\\n73:     with:\\n74:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n75:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n76:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-cuda12_8-py3_10-gcc11-sm100-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n77:       aws-role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::308535385114:role/gha_workflow_s3_and_ecr_read_only\\n78:     secrets: inherit\\n79: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/test-b200.yml\"}"

jobs can check github.event.schedule to activate only for a fraction of the runs.\\n10:     # Also run less frequently on weekends.\\n11:     - cron: 45 */2 * * 1-5\\n12:     - cron: 45 4,12 * * 0,6\\n13: \\n14: concurrency:\\n15:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name ==   "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"

Output
"{\"content\":\"1: # @generated DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\\n2: \\n3: # Template is at:    .github/templates/macos_binary_build_workflow.yml.j2\\n4: # Generation script: .github/scripts/generate_ci_workflows.py\\n5: name: macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release\\n6: \\n7: on:\\n8: # TODO: Migrate to new ciflow trigger, reference https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/70321\\n9:   push:\\n10:     # NOTE: Meta Employees can trigger new nightlies using: https://fburl.com/trigger_pytorch_nightly_build\\n11:     branches:\\n12:       - nightly\\n13:     tags:\\n14:       # NOTE: Binary build pipelines should only get triggered on release candidate builds\\n15:       # Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n16:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n17:       - 'ciflow/binaries/*'\\n18:       - 'ciflow/binaries_libtorch/*'\\n19:   workflow_dispatch:\\n20: \\n21: env:\\n22:   ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"308535385114.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tool/alpine\\\"\\n23:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n24:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release\\n25:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n26:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n27:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0\\n28: concurrency:\\n29:   group: macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n30:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n31: \\n32: jobs:\\n33:   libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release-build:\\n34:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n35:     runs-on: macos-14-xlarge\\n36:     timeout-minutes: 240\\n37:     env:\\n38:       PYTORCH_ROOT: ${{ github.workspace }}/pytorch\\n39:       PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\\n40:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n41:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n42:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n43:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\\n44:       SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 1\\n45:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\\n46:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\\n47:       # This is a dummy value for libtorch to work correctly with our batch scripts\\n48:       # without this value pip does not get installed for some reason\\n49:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n50:     steps:\\n51:       # NOTE: These environment variables are put here so that they can be applied on every job equally\\n52:       #       They are also here because setting them at a workflow level doesn't give us access to the\\n53:       #       runner.temp variable, which we need.\\n54:       - name: Populate binary env\\n55:         shell: bash\\n56:         run: |\\n57:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129\\n58:           echo \\\"BINARY_ENV_FILE=${RUNNER_TEMP}/env\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n59:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129\\n60:           echo \\\"PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}/artifacts\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n61:           # shellcheck disable=SC2129\\n62:           echo \\\"MAC_PACKAGE_WORK_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP}\\\" >> \\\"${GITHUB_ENV}\\\"\\n63:       - name: Setup Python\\n64:         uses: actions/setup-python@v6\\n65:         with:\\n66:           # .4 version is min minor for 3.10, and also no-gil version of 3.13 needs at least 3.13.3\\n67:           python-version: \\\"3.10.4\\\"\\n68:           freethreaded: false\\n69:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n70:         uses: actions/checkout@v4\\n71:         with:\\n72:           submodules: recursive\\n73:           path: pytorch\\n74:           show-progress: false\\n75:       - name: Clean PyTorch checkout\\n76:         run: |\\n77:           # Remove any artifacts from the previous checkouts\\n78:           git clean -fxd\\n79:         working-directory: pytorch\\n80:       - name: Populate binary env\\n81:         run: |\\n82:           \\\"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.circleci/scripts/binary_populate_env.sh\\\"\\n83:       - name: Build PyTorch binary\\n84:         run: |\\n85:           set -eux -o pipefail\\n86:           # shellcheck disable=SC1090\\n87:           source \\\"${BINARY_ENV_FILE:-/Users/distiller/project/env}\\\"\\n88:           mkdir -p \\\"$PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR\\\"\\n89: \\n90:           # Build\\n91:           USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT=1\\n92:           USE_COREML_DELEGATE=1\\n93:           TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME=\\\"${TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME//-/_}\\\"\\n94:           export USE_PYTORCH_METAL_EXPORT\\n95:           export USE_COREML_DELEGATE\\n96:           export TORCH_PACKAGE_NAME\\n97:           \\\"${PYTORCH_ROOT}/.ci/wheel/build_wheel.sh\\\"\\n98:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4.4.0\\n99:         if: always()\\n100:         with:\\n101:           name: libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release\\n102:           retention-days: 14\\n103:           if-no-files-found: error\\n104:           path: \\\"${{ env.PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR }}\\\"\\n105:   libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release-upload:  # Uploading\\n106:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n107:     permissions:\\n108:       id-token: write\\n109:       contents: read\\n110:     needs: libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release-build\\n111:     with:\\n112:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n113:       PACKAGE_TYPE: libtorch\\n114:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n115:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n116:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n117:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu\\n118:       DOCKER_IMAGE: libtorch-cxx11-builder\\n119:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cpu\\n120:       LIBTORCH_CONFIG: release\\n121:       LIBTORCH_VARIANT: shared-with-deps\\n122:       build_name: libtorch-cpu-shared-with-deps-release\\n123:       use_s3: False\\n124:     secrets:\\n125:       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n126:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-upload.yml\\n127: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/generated-macos-arm64-binary-libtorch-release-nightly.yml\"}"

Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/lint-bc.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: BC Lint\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   pull_request:\\n5:     types:\\n6:       - opened\\n7:       - synchronize\\n8:       - reopened\\n9:     branches-ignore:\\n10:       - nightly\\n11:   push:\\n12:     tags:\\n13:       # TODO: Remove me later\\n14:       -  ciflow/inductor-cu126/*\\n15:   workflow_dispatch:\\n16: \\n17: jobs:\\n18:   bc_linter:\\n19:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n20:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n21:     steps:\\n22:       - name: Run BC Lint Action\\n23:         uses: pytorch/test-infra/.github/actions/bc-lint@release/2.10\\n24:         with:\\n25:           repo: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}\\n26:           base_sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\\n27:           head_sha: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\\n28:           suppression: ${{ contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'suppress-api-compatibility-check') || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'suppress-bc-linter') }}\\n29:           docs_link: 'https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/wiki/BC-Linter'\\n30: \\n31: concurrency:\\n32:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n33:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n34: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/lint-bc.yml\"}"
264. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/target_determination.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: target-determination\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   workflow_call:\\n5: \\n6: jobs:\\n7: \\n8:   get-label-type:\\n9:     name: get-label-type\\n10:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n11:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n12:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n13:     with:\\n14:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n15:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n16:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n17:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n18: \\n19:   target-determination:\\n20:     # Don't run on forked repos\\n21:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n22:     runs-on: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}linux.2xlarge\\\"\\n23:     needs: get-label-type\\n24:     steps:\\n25:       # [pytorch repo ref]\\n26:       # Use a pytorch/pytorch reference instead of a reference to the local\\n27:       # checkout because when we run this action we don't *have* a local\\n28:       # checkout. In other cases you should prefer a local checkout.\\n29:       - name: Checkout PyTorch\\n30:         uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/actions/checkout-pytorch@release/2.10\\n31:         with:\\n32:           submodules: false\\n33: \\n34:       - name: Setup Linux\\n35:         uses: ./.github/actions/setup-linux\\n36: \\n37:       - name: Get workflow job id\\n38:         id: get-job-id\\n39:         uses: ./.github/actions/get-workflow-job-id\\n40:         if: always()\\n41:         with:\\n42:           github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n43: \\n44:       - name: Download pytest cache\\n45:         uses: ./.github/actions/pytest-cache-download\\n46:         continue-on-error: true\\n47:         with:\\n48:           cache_dir: .pytest_cache\\n49:           job_identifier: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n50: \\n51:       - name: Download LLM Artifacts from S3\\n52:         uses: seemethere/download-artifact-s3@1da556a7aa0a088e3153970611f6c432d58e80e6 # v4.2.0\\n53:         continue-on-error: true\\n54:         with:\\n55:           name: llm_results\\n56:           path: .additional_ci_files/llm_results\\n57: \\n58:       - name: Do TD\\n59:         id: td\\n60:         continue-on-error: true\\n61:         env:\\n62:           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n63:           GITHUB_REPOSITORY: ${{ github.repository }}\\n64:           GITHUB_WORKFLOW: ${{ github.workflow }}\\n65:           GITHUB_JOB: ${{ github.job }}\\n66:           GITHUB_RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}\\n67:           GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER: ${{ github.run_number }}\\n68:           GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT: ${{ github.run_attempt }}\\n69:           GITHUB_REF: ${{ github.ref }}\\n70:           JOB_ID: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-id }}\\n71:           JOB_NAME: ${{ steps.get-job-id.outputs.job-name }}\\n72:           PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n73:         run: |\\n74:           unzip -o .additional_ci_files/llm_results/mappings.zip -d .additional_ci_files/llm_results || true\\n75:           python3 -m pip install boto3==1.35.42\\n76:           python3 tools/testing/do_target_determination_for_s3.py\\n77: \\n78:       - name: Upload TD results to s3\\n79:         uses: seemethere/upload-artifact-s3@baba72d0712b404f646cebe0730933554ebce96a # v5.1.0\\n80:         if: steps.td.outcome == 'success'\\n81:         with:\\n82:           name: td_results\\n83:           retention-days: 14\\n84:           if-no-files-found: error\\n85:           path: td_results.json\\n86: \\n87:       - name: Store TD results on GHA\\n88:         uses: actions/upload-artifact@ea165f8d65b6e75b540449e92b4886f43607fa02 # v4.6.2\\n89:         if: steps.td.outcome == 'success'\\n90:         with:\\n91:           name: td_results.json\\n92:           retention-days: 14\\n93:           if-no-files-found: error\\n94:           path: td_results.json\\n95: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\"}"
265. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": ".github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi200.yml",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78"
}
Output
"{\"content\":\"1: name: periodic-rocm-mi200\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     # We have several schedules so jobs can check github.event.schedule to activate only for a fraction of the runs.\\n6:     # Also run less frequently on weekends.\\n7:     - cron: 45 0,8,16 * * 1-5\\n8:     - cron: 45 4 * * 0,6\\n9:     - cron: 45 4,12,20 * * 1-5\\n10:     - cron: 45 12 * * 0,6\\n11:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests\\n12:   push:\\n13:     tags:\\n14:       - ciflow/periodic-rocm-mi200/*\\n15:     branches:\\n16:       - release/*\\n17:   workflow_dispatch:\\n18: \\n19: concurrency:\\n20:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}-${{ github.event_name == 'schedule' }}-${{ github.event.schedule }}\\n21:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n22: \\n23: permissions:\\n24:   id-token: write\\n25:   contents: read\\n26: \\n27: jobs:\\n28:   llm-td:\\n29:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n30:     name: before-test\\n31:     uses: ./.github/workflows/llm_td_retrieval.yml\\n32:     permissions:\\n33:       id-token: write\\n34:       contents: read\\n35: \\n36:   target-determination:\\n37:     name: before-test\\n38:     uses: ./.github/workflows/target_determination.yml\\n39:     needs: llm-td\\n40:     permissions:\\n41:       id-token: write\\n42:       contents: read\\n43: \\n44:   get-label-type:\\n45:     name: get-label-type\\n46:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n47:     if: (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n48:     with:\\n49:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n50:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n51:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n52:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n53: \\n54:   linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build:\\n55:     name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\\n56:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n57:     needs: get-label-type\\n58:     with:\\n59:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n60:       build-environment: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\\n61:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-rocm-n-py3\\n62:       test-matrix: |\\n63:         { include: [\\n64:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.4\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n65:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.4\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n66:           { config: \\\"distributed\\\", shard: 3, num_shards: 3, runner: \\\"linux.rocm.gpu.4\\\", owners: [\\\"module:rocm\\\", \\\"oncall:distributed\\\"] },\\n67:         ]}\\n68:     secrets: inherit\\n69: \\n70:   linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-test:\\n71:     permissions:\\n72:       id-token: write\\n73:       contents: read\\n74:     name: linux-jammy-rocm-py3.10\\n75:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_rocm-test.yml\\n76:     needs:\\n77:       - linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build\\n78:       - target-determination\\n79:     with:\\n80:       build-environment: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n81:       docker-image: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n82:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.linux-jammy-rocm-py3_10-build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n83:     secrets: inherit\\n84: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/periodic-rocm-mi200.yml\"}"

"{\"content\":\"1: name: inductor-A100-perf-compare\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   push:\\n5:     tags:\\n6:       - ciflow/inductor-perf-compare/*\\n7:   workflow_dispatch:\\n8: \\n9: concurrency:\\n10:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n11:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n12: \\n13: permissions:\\n14:   id-token: write\\n15:   contents: read\\n16: \\n17: jobs:\\n18:   get-default-label-prefix:\\n19:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n20:     name: get-default-label-prefix\\n21:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n22:     with:\\n23:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n24:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n25:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n26:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n27:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n28: \\n29:   build:\\n30:     name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n31:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n32:     needs:\\n33:       - get-default-label-prefix\\n34:     with:\\n35:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-default-label-prefix.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n36:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n37:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda12.8-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n38:       cuda-arch-list: '8.0'\\n39:       test-matrix: |\\n40:         { include: [\\n41:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n42:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n43:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm_perf_compare\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n44:           { config: \\\"inductor_torchbench_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n45:         ]}\\n46:       build-additional-packages: \\\"vision audio fbgemm torchao\\\"\\n47:     secrets: inherit\\n48: \\n49:   test:\\n50:     name: cuda12.8-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n51:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n52:     needs: build\\n53:     with:\\n54:       build-environment: ${{ needs.build.outputs.build-environment }}\\n55:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build.outputs.docker-image }}\\n56:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n57:       # disable monitor in perf tests for more investigation\\n58:       disable-monitor: false\\n59:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n60:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n61:     secrets: inherit\\n62: \\n63:   build-cuda13:\\n64:     name: cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n65:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n66:     needs:\\n67:       - get-default-label-prefix\\n68:     with:\\n69:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-default-label-prefix.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n70:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n71:       docker-image-name: ci-image:pytorch-linux-jammy-cuda13.0-cudnn9-py3-gcc11-inductor-benchmarks\\n72:       cuda-arch-list: '8.0'\\n73:       test-matrix: |\\n74:         { include: [\\n75:           { config: \\\"inductor_huggingface_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n76:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n77:           { config: \\\"inductor_timm_perf_compare\\\", shard: 2, num_shards: 2, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n78:           { config: \\\"inductor_torchbench_perf_compare\\\", shard: 1, num_shards: 1, runner: \\\"linux.aws.a100\\\" },\\n79:         ]}\\n80:       build-additional-packages: \\\"vision audio torchao\\\"\\n81:     secrets: inherit\\n82: \\n83:   test-cuda13:\\n84:     name: cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n85:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-test.yml\\n86:     needs: build-cuda13\\n87:     with:\\n88:       build-environment: linux-jammy-cuda13.0-py3.10-gcc11-sm80\\n89:       docker-image: ${{ needs.build-cuda13.outputs.docker-image }}\\n90:       test-matrix: ${{ needs.build-cuda13.outputs.test-matrix }}\\n91:       # disable monitor in perf tests for more investigation\\n92:       disable-monitor: false\\n93:       monitor-log-interval: 15\\n94:       monitor-data-collect-interval: 4\\n95:     secrets: inherit\\n96: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--863edc78@863edc787f5fa7950e0f3f86d4952641e1ef9c2c/-/blob/.github/workflows/inductor-perf-compare.yml\"}"

Release candidate tags look like: v1.11.0-rc1\\n16:       - v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+\\n17:       - 'ciflow/binaries/*'\\n18:       - 'ciflow/binaries_wheel/*'\\n19:   workflow_dispatch:\\n20: \\n21: permissions:\\n22:   id-token: write\\n23: \\n24: env:\\n25:   # Needed for conda builds\\n26:   ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"arm64v8/alpine\\\"\\n27:   AWS_DEFAULT_REGION: us-east-1\\n28:   BINARY_ENV_FILE: /tmp/env\\n29:   BUILD_ENVIRONMENT: linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel\\n30:   GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n31:   PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\\n32:   PYTORCH_FINAL_PACKAGE_DIR: /artifacts\\n33:   PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n34:   SHA1: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha || github.sha }}\\n35:   SKIP_ALL_TESTS: 0\\n36: concurrency:\\n37:   group: linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' }}\\n38:   cancel-in-progress: true\\n39: \\n40: jobs:\\n41:   get-label-type:\\n42:     if: github.repository_owner == 'pytorch'\\n43:     name: get-label-type\\n44:     uses: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n45:     with:\\n46:       triggering_actor: ${{ github.triggering_actor }}\\n47:       issue_owner: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login || github.event.issue.user.login }}\\n48:       curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n49:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n50:   manywheel-py3_10-cpu-aarch64-build:\\n51:     if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'pytorch' }}\\n52:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_binary-build-linux.yml\\n53:     needs: get-label-type\\n54:     with:\\n55:       PYTORCH_ROOT: /pytorch\\n56:       PACKAGE_TYPE: manywheel\\n57:       # TODO: This is a legacy variable that we eventually want to get rid of in\\n58:       #       favor of GPU_ARCH_VERSION\\n59:       DESIRED_CUDA: cpu\\n60:       GPU_ARCH_TYPE: cpu-aarch64\\n61:       DOCKER_IMAGE: manylinux2_28_aarch64-builder\\n62:       DOCKER_IMAGE_TAG_PREFIX: cpu-aarch64\\n63:       DESIRED_PYTHON: \\\"3.10\\\"\\n64:       runner_prefix: \\\"${{ needs.get-label-type.outputs.label-type }}\\\"\\n65:       runs_on: linux.arm64.r7g.12xlarge.memory\\n66:       ALPINE_IMAGE: \\\"arm64v8/alpine\\\"\\n67:       build_name: manywheel-py3_10-cpu-aarch64\\n68:       build_environment: linux-aarch64-binary-manywheel\\n69:     secrets:\\n70:       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\\n71: \\n72:   manywheel-py3_10-cpu-aarch64-test:  # "{\"content\":\"1: # This workflow is dedicated to host slow jobs that are run only periodically because\\n2: # they are too slow to run in every commit.  The list of slow tests can be found in\\n3: # https://github.com/pytorch/test-infra/blob/generated-stats/stats/slow-tests.json\\n4: name: slow\\n5: \\n6: on:\\n7:   push:\\n8:     branches:\\n9:       - main\\n10:       - release/*\\n11:     tags:\\n12:       - ciflow/slow/*\\n13:   schedule:\\n14:     - cron: 29 8 * * *  # about 1:29am PDT, for mem leak check and rerun disabled tests\\n15:   workflow_dispatch:\\n16: \\n17: concurrency:\\n18:   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}-${{ github.ref_type == 'branch' && github.sha }}-${{ "{\"content\":\"1: name: inductor-perf-nightly-aarch64\\n2: \\n3: on:\\n4:   schedule:\\n5:     # Does not perform max_autotune on CPU, so skip the weekly run setup\\n6:     - cron: 0 7 * * *\\n7:   # NB: GitHub has an upper limit of 10 inputs here\\n8:   workflow_dispatch:\\n9:     inputs:\\n10:       training:\\n11:         # CPU for training is not typical, but leave the option open here\\n12:         description: Run training (off by default)?\\n13:         required: false\\n14:         type: boolean\\n15:         default: false\\n16:       inference:\\n17:         description: Run inference (on by default)?\\n18:         required: false\\n19:         type: boolean\\n20:         default: true\\n21:       default:\\n22:         description: Run inductor_default?\\n23:         required: false\\n24:         type: boolean\\n25:         default: true\\n26:       dynamic:\\n27:         description: Run inductor_dynamic_shapes?\\n28:         required: false\\n29:         type: boolean\\n30:         default: false\\n31:       cppwrapper:\\n32:         description: Run inductor_cpp_wrapper?\\n33:         required: false\\n34:         type: boolean\\n35:         default: false\\n36:       aotinductor:\\n37:         description: Run aot_inductor for inference?\\n38:         required: false\\n39:         type: boolean\\n40:         default: false\\n41: pytorch/pytorch/.github/workflows/_runner-determinator.yml@release/2.10\\n59:     if: ${{ (github.event_name != 'schedule' || github.repository == 'pytorch/pytorch') && github.repository_owner ==    curr_branch: ${{ github.head_ref || github.ref_name }}\\n64:       curr_ref_type: ${{ github.ref_type }}\\n65:       opt_out_experiments: lf\\n66: \\n67:   linux-jammy-aarch64-py3_10-inductor-build:\\n68:     name: linux-jammy-aarch64-py3.10-inductor\\n69:     uses: ./.github/workflows/_linux-build.yml\\n70:     needs: get-label-type\\n71:
